Recipe name: Miso-Butter Roast Chicken With Acorn Squash Panzanella
Ingredients: '1 (-lb.) whole chicken', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided, plus more', '2 small acorn squash (about 3 lb. total)', '2 Tbsp. finely chopped sage', '1 Tbsp. finely chopped rosemary', '6 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted, plus 3 Tbsp. room temperature', ' tsp. ground allspice', 'Pinch of crushed red pepper flakes', 'Freshly ground black pepper', 'A loaf good-quality sturdy white bread, torn into 1" pieces (about cups)', '2 medium apples (such as Gala or Pink Lady; about oz. total), cored, cut into 1" pieces', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', ' small red onion, thinly sliced', '3 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar', '1 Tbsp. white miso', ' cup all-purpose flour', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature', ' cup dry white wine', '2 cups unsalted chicken broth', '2 tsp. white miso', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper'
Instructions:
1. Pat chicken dry with paper towels, season all over with 2 tsp. salt, and tie legs together with kitchen twine.
2. Let sit at room temperature 1 hour.
3. Meanwhile, halve squash and scoop out seeds.
4. Run a vegetable peeler along ridges of squash halves to remove skin.
5. Cut each half into "-thick wedges; arrange on a rimmed baking sheet.
6. Combine sage, rosemary, and 6 Tbsp. melted butter in a large bowl; pour half of mixture over squash on baking sheet.
7. Sprinkle squash with allspice, red pepper flakes, and tsp. salt and season with black pepper; toss to coat.
8. Add bread, apples, oil, and tsp. salt to remaining herb butter in bowl; season with black pepper and toss to combine.
9. Set aside.
10. Place onion and vinegar in a small bowl; season with salt and toss to coat.
11. Let sit, tossing occasionally, until ready to serve.
12. Place a rack in middle and lower third of oven; preheat to .
13. Mix miso and 3 Tbsp. room-temperature butter in a small bowl until smooth.
14. Pat chicken dry with paper towels, then rub or brush all over with miso butter.
15. Place chicken in a large cast-iron skillet and roast on middle rack until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of breast registers , minutes. (Temperature will climb to while chicken rests.) Let chicken rest in skillet at least 5 minutes, then transfer to a plate; reserve skillet.
16. Meanwhile, roast squash on lower rack until mostly tender, about minutes.
17. Remove from oven and scatter reserved bread mixture over, spreading into as even a layer as you can manage.
18. Return to oven and roast until bread is golden brown and crisp and apples are tender, about minutes.
19. Remove from oven, drain pickled onions, and toss to combine.
20. Transfer to a serving dish.
21. Using your fingers, mash flour and butter in a small bowl to combine.
22. Set reserved skillet with chicken drippings over medium heat.
23. You should have about cup, but a little over or under is all good. (If you have significantly more, drain off and set excess aside.) Add wine and cook, stirring often and scraping up any browned bits with a wooden spoon, until bits are loosened and wine is reduced by about half (you should be able to smell the wine), about 2 minutes.
24. Add butter mixture; cook, stirring often, until a smooth paste forms, about 2 minutes.
25. Add broth and any reserved drippings and cook, stirring constantly, until combined and thickened, 6A8 minutes.
26. Remove from heat and stir in miso.
27. Taste and season with salt and black pepper.
28. Serve chicken with gravy and squash panzanella alongside.

Ingredients: '2 large egg whites', '1 pound new potatoes (about 1 inch in diameter)', '2 teaspoons kosher salt', ' teaspoon finely ground black pepper', '1 teaspoon finely chopped rosemary', '1 teaspoon finely chopped thyme', '1 teaspoon finely chopped parsley'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to and line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment.
2. In a large bowl, whisk the egg whites until foamy (there shouldnAt be any liquid whites in the bowl).
3. Add the potatoes and toss until theyAre well coated with the egg whites, then transfer to a strainer or colander and let the excess whites drain.
4. Season the potatoes with the salt, pepper, and herbs.
5. Scatter the potatoes on the baking sheet (make sure theyAre not touching) and roast until the potatoes are very crispy and tender when poked with a knife, to minutes (depending on the size of the potatoes).
6. Transfer to a bowl and serve.

Ingredients: '1 cup evaporated milk', '1 cup whole milk', '1 tsp. garlic powder', '1 tsp. onion powder', '1 tsp. smoked paprika', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper', '1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '2 lb. extra-sharp cheddar, coarsely grated', '4 oz. full-fat cream cheese', '1 lb. elbow macaroni'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Bring evaporated milk and whole milk to a bare simmer in a large saucepan over medium heat.
3. Whisk in garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, pepper, and 1 tsp. salt.
4. Working in batches, whisk in three fourths of the cheddar, then all of the cream cheese.
5. Meanwhile, bring a large pot of generously salted water to a boil (it should have a little less salt than seawater).
6. Cook macaroni, stirring occasionally, until very al dente, about 4 minutes.
7. Drain in a colander.
8. Add macaroni to cheese sauce in pan and mix until well coated.
9. Evenly spread out half of macaroni mixture in a 13x9" baking dish.
10. Sprinkle half of remaining cheddar evenly over.
11. Layer remaining macaroni mixture on top and sprinkle with remaining cheddar.
12. Bake until all of the cheese is melted, about minutes.
13. Let cool slightly before serving.

Ingredients: '1 (- to 1-pound) round Italian loaf, cut into 1-inch cubes (8 cups)', '2 tablespoons olive oil, divided', '2 pounds sweet Italian sausage, casings removed, divided', '1 stick unsalted butter, cut into pieces', '3 medium onions, chopped', '4 large celery ribs, chopped', '5 garlic cloves, minced', '4 large eggs, lightly beaten', ' cup heavy cream, divided', ' cup turkey giblet stock or reduced-sodium chicken broth', '1 cup grated Parmigiano-Reggiano (2 ounces)', ' cup coarsely chopped flat-leaf parsley', '4-qt shallow ceramic or glass baking dish'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to with rack in middle.
2. Generously butter baking dish.
3. Put bread in 2 shallow baking pans and bake, switching position of pans halfway through baking, until just dried out, about minutes.
4. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a -inch heavy skillet over medium-high heat until it shimmers, then cook half of sausage, stirring and breaking it into small pieces, until golden brown, about 6 minutes.
5. Transfer with a slotted spoon to a large bowl.
6. Brown remaining sausage in remaining tablespoon oil, transferring to bowl.
7. Pour off fat from skillet and wipe clean.
8. Heat butter over medium heat until foam subsides, then cook onions, celery, garlic, and teaspoon each of salt and pepper, stirring occasionally, until golden, to minutes.
9. Add vegetables and bread to sausage.
10. Whisk together eggs, cup cream, turkey stock, cheese, and parsley, then stir into stuffing and cool completely, about minutes.
11. Reserve 5 cups stuffing to stuff turkey and spoon remainder into baking dish, then drizzle with remaining cup cream.
12. Cover stuffing and chill.
13. About 1 hour before stuffed turkey is finished roasting, bring dish of stuffing to room temperature.
14. When turkey is done, increase oven temperature to and bake stuffing, covered tightly with foil, until hot throughout, about minutes.
15. Remove foil and bake until top is golden and crisp, about minutes more.
16. Cooks' Note: Bread can be toasted 3 days ahead and kept (once cool) in a sealed bag at room temperature.
17. Stuffing can be prepared (but not baked) 4 hours before roasting turkey.
18. If baking stuffing at the same time as potatoes, put stuffing in upper third of oven and potatoes in bottom third (allow extra time).

Ingredients: '1 teaspoon dark brown sugar', '1 teaspoon hot water', '1 oz. bourbon', ' oz. fresh lemon juice', '2 teaspoons apple butter (storebought or homemade)', 'Garnish: orange twist and freshly grated or ground cinnamon'
Instructions:
1. Stir together brown sugar and hot water in a cocktail shaker to dissolve.
2. Let cool, then add bourbon, lemon juice, and apple butter and fill with ice.
3. Shake until well chilled, about seconds.
4. Strain into an ice-filled rocks glass.
5. Garnish with orange twist and cinnamon.

Ingredients: '2 chamomile tea bags', ' oz. reposado tequila', ' oz. fresh lemon juice', '1 Tbsp. agave nectar'
Instructions:
1. Place 2 chamomile tea bags in a heatsafe vessel, such as a large liquid measuring cup.
2. Pour in 1 cups boiling water, and let steep 5 minutes, then remove tea bags.
3. Add 1 oz. reposado tequila, oz. fresh lemon juice, and 1 Tbsp. agave nectar and stir until incorporated.
4. Pour into a -ounce insulated mug (or two smaller 8-ounce mugs) and drink hot.

Ingredients: '3 oz. Grand Marnier', '1 oz. Amaro Averna', 'Small pat salted butter (about teaspoon)', '1 cup hot apple cider', ' to 3 tsp. fresh lemon juice (to taste, depending on the sweetness of your cider)', 'Garnish: freshly ground pink peppercorns, plus 2 lemon wheels (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Add 3 oz.
2. Grand Marnier, 1 oz.
3. Amaro Averna, and a small pat of salted butter to a -ounce travel mug or large heatproof measuring cup.
4. Add 1 cup hot apple cider and stir until butter melts.
5. Add tsp. fresh lemon juice and taste for sweetness, adding additional if needed.
6. If serving in travel mug, garnish with ground pink peppercorns and seal.
7. If serving in mugs, divide drink between two warmed mugs and garnish each with a pink peppercorn-dusted lemon wheel.

Ingredients: ' cup granulated sugar', ' tsp. ground turmeric', '1 oz. Amontillado sherry', '1 oz. bourbon, aged rum, Scotch, mezcal, or gin', '1 oz. Turmeric Syrup', ' oz. fresh lemon juice', 'Garnish: Dehydrated lemon wheel (optional)'
Instructions:
1. For the turmeric syrup, combine cup hot water and cup sugar in a liquid measuring cup or mason jar.
2. Add tsp. ground turmeric and stirAor seal and shakeAuntil sugar is completely dissolved. (This makes enough syrup for 4 drinks.
3. Syrup can be used immediately or refrigerated, covered, up to days.
4. Shake or stir before using to reincorporate turmeric.) For the toddy, combine 1 oz.
5. Amontillado sherry, 1 oz. bourbon (or other spirit of choice), 1 oz.
6. Turmeric Syrup, and oz. fresh lemon juice in a sturdy pint glass or -ounce thermos.
7. Top with 8 oz. hot water and stir gently.
8. Garnish with dehydrated lemon wheel if desired.

Ingredients: ' cup assorted dals (such as chana dal, moong dal, masoor dal, andor urad dal)', ' cup white jasmine rice or other long-grain rice', ' cup pearl barley', ' lb. bone-in lamb stew meat', '3 tsp. kosher salt, divided, plus more', '1 6" piece fresh ginger', '3 medium shallots, thinly sliced', 'A cup ghee or vegetable oil', '4 garlic cloves, finely grated', '2 Tbsp. plus tsp. garam masala', '1 tsp. (or more) cayenne pepper', '1 tsp. ground turmeric', '3 green Thai chiles, stems removed (optional)', ' cup (lightly packed) chopped cilantro, plus more for serving', ' white onion, finely chopped', '2 limes, cut into wedges'
Instructions:
1. Combine dals, rice, and barley in a medium bowl.
2. Pour in water to cover and swish dals and grains around with your fingers.
3. Drain and repeat process until water runs clear enough to see your hands through.
4. Pour in water to cover and let dals and grains soak at room temperature at least 2 hours, or cover and chill up to hours.
5. Place lamb on a plate, pat dry with paper towels, and season all over with 2 tsp. salt; set aside.
6. Peel ginger by scraping off skin with a spoon.
7. Finely grate half of ginger; set aside.
8. Thinly slice remaining ginger.
9. Stack slices and cut crosswise into matchsticks; set aside.
10. If using an electric pressure cooker, combine shallots and ghee in pot and set to sautA function or high.
11. Cook, stirring often and adjusting heat as needed if your cooker has that option, until shallots are just beginning to brown, about 8 minutes (or about 6 minutes if cooking in oil).
12. Reduce to normal function or medium if possible, or, if your cooker doesnAt have a lower setting, turn off for a minute if mixture is getting too hot.
13. Add reserved ginger matchsticks and cook, stirring often, until shallots are brown and crisp and ginger is frizzled, minutes, depending on your cooker.
14. Using a slotted spoon, transfer shallots and ginger to a plate, spreading out into a single layer.
15. Season lightly with salt; let cool.
16. Add garlic, garam masala, cayenne, turmeric, and reserved grated ginger, and cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about seconds.
17. Add lamb and stir to coat in spices.
18. Cook, stirring often with a wooden spoon and repeatedly adding splashes of water as spices begin to stick to bottom of pot and scraping up browned bits, until flavors come together, about minutes.(Bhuna, the process of repeated sticking and scraping, is a Southeast Asian cooking technique that brings out the flavor of the spices and ensures they donAt taste raw and grainy.) Drain dal mixture and add to pot along with 1 tsp. salt and 5 cups water.
19. Secure lid and bring to full pressure according to manufacturerAs directions.
20. Cook hours.
21. Let sit minutes, then manually release pressure and open up.
22. Taste haleem and add more salt and cayenne if needed.
23. Add chiles if using. (These are for flavor, not heat; feel free to leave them out.) Simmer on normal function or medium, stirring often, 5 minutes.
24. Stir in cup cilantro.
25. Serve haleem with white onion, lime wedges, reserved crispy shallots and ginger, and more chopped cilantro.
26. If using a medium Dutch oven or stovetop pressure cooker, cook shallots and ghee over high heat, stirring often, until shallots are just beginning to brown, about 8 minutes (or about 6 minutes if cooking in oil).
27. Add reserved ginger matchsticks and cook, stirring often, until shallots are brown and crisp and ginger is frizzled, 5A8 minutes.
28. Using a slotted spoon, transfer shallots and ginger to a plate, spreading out in a single layer.
29. Season lightly with salt; let cool.
30. Reduce heat to medium, add garlic, garam masala, cayenne, turmeric, and reserved grated ginger, and cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about seconds.
31. Add lamb and stir to coat in spices.
32. Cook, stirring often with a wooden spoon and repeatedly adding splashes of water as spices begin to stick to bottom of pot and scraping up browned bits, until flavors come together, about minutes. (Bhuna, the process of repeated sticking and scraping, is a Southeast Asian cooking technique that brings out the flavor of the spices and ensures they donAt taste raw and grainy.) Drain dal mixture and add to pot along with 1 tsp. salt and cups water (if using a Dutch oven) or 5 cups water (for pressure cooker).
33. Increase heat to high and bring to a boil, then reduce heat to the low, cover pot, and cook, stirring and scraping bottom of pot every minutes, until meat is falling off the bone and dals and grains have nearly melted into the stew, hours. (If using a stovetop cooker, increase heat to high and bring to a boil.
34. Secure lid and bring to full pressure according to manufacturerAs directions.
35. Cook hours.
36. Let sit minutes, then manually release pressure and open up.) Taste haleem and add more salt and cayenne if needed.
37. Add chiles if using. (These are for flavor, not heat; feel free to leave them out.) Simmer over medium heat, stirring often, 5 minutes.
38. Stir in cup cilantro.
39. Serve haleem with white onion, lime wedges, reserved crispy shallots and ginger, and more chopped cilantro.
40. Do ahead: Haleem can be made 1 day ahead.
41. Let cool; cover and chill.
42. Reheat over medium until warmed through.

Recipe name: Spiced Lentil And Caramelized Onion Baked Eggs
Ingredients: '1 (.5-ounce) can basic lentil soup, like AmyAs', '1 large onion, thinly sliced', ' tsp. turmeric', '1 tsp. cumin', ' tsp. Aleppo pepper or A tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '2 Tbsp. tomato paste', '3 large eggs', '2 Tbsp. ghee, unsalted butter, or olive oil', ' tsp. whole cumin seeds', 'Olive oil', 'Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper', 'Parsley, for finishing'
Instructions:
1. Place an oven rack in the center of the oven, then preheat to .
2. In a medium, oven-safe pan, heat 1 Tbsp. olive oil over medium heat.
3. Add 1 large, thinly sliced onion and tsp.
4. Kosher salt.
5. Cook, stirring often, until golden brown, about minutes.
6. Add tsp. turmeric, 1 tsp. cumin, tsp.
7. Aleppo pepper (or A tsp. crushed red pepper flakes), and 2 Tbsp. tomato paste.
8. Cook and stir constantly until the onions are coated and the tomato paste has darkened slightly, about 2 minutes.
9. Add A cup water; stir and scrape up all the browned bits on the bottom of the pan for 1 to 2 minutes, or until the liquid looks thickened and saucy.
10. Add one -oz. can of lentil soup; cook, stirring to combine, 1 to 2 minutes.
11. Turn off the heat and season with salt, pepper, and more Aleppo pepper or red pepper flakes to taste.
12. Using a spoon, create 3 wells in the lentil mixture.
13. Carefully crack 1 egg into each well.
14. Transfer the pan to the oven and bake until the whites of the eggs are just set, to minutes.
15. While the eggs bake, in a small pot or butter warmer, heat 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, ghee, or olive oil over medium heat.
16. Add tsp. cumin seeds; swirl the pan until the seeds start to sizzle and brown, seconds to 1 minute.
17. Remove from the heat.
18. Drizzle the finished eggs with the butter mixture, season with salt and pepper, and garnish with parsley before serving.

Ingredients: '1 (7 oz. g) can chipotle in adobo sauce', ' garlic clove, minced', 'Kosher salt', ' tsp. cornstarch', '1 (5 fl oz. ml) can evaporated milk (about cup plus 2 Tbsp)', '8 oz. ( g) sharp or extra-sharp Cheddar cheese, coarsely grated (about 2 cups)', '2 oz. ( g) cream cheese, roughly diced, at room temperature', ' cup ( g) mayonnaise', '1 (4 oz. g) jar diced pimento peppers, drained', ' tsp. Chipotle Pepper PurAe'
Instructions:
1. Put the chipotle peppers and adobo sauce in a small food processor or blender and blend until the mixture turns into a smooth purAe.
2. Set aside.
3. The chipotle purAe can be made in advance, stored in an airtight container, and refrigerated for up to 2 months.
4. On a cutting board, sprinkle the garlic with a large pinch of salt and gather it into a small mound.
5. Holding the blunt side of the knife with both hands, press and scrape the knifeAs sharp end, holding it at a slight angle, across the garlic mound to flatten it.
6. Repeat, dragging it across the garlic, until you have a smooth paste.
7. Set aside.
8. In a small bowl, mix the cornstarch and tsp. of the evaporated milk into a slurry.
9. Pour the rest of the evaporated milk into a medium saucepan and stir in the slurry.
10. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, whisking constantly.
11. Turn the heat to low and add the Cheddar gradually by the handful, stirring until the Cheddar is melted and the mixture is smooth.
12. Add the cream cheese and whisk until it melts.
13. Stir in the mayonnaise, pimento peppers, tsp. of the chipotle purAe, and the garlic paste.
14. Season with salt.
15. Transfer to a serving bowl or keep it warm in a slow cooker and serve immediately.
16. To reheat the sauce, microwave it, stirring every seconds, until fully melted.

Recipe name: Spiral Ham In The Slow Cooker
Ingredients: '1 (6- to 7-pound) bone-in, hickory-smoked, uncured fully cooked ham', '5 medium parsnips, peeled and halved lengthwise', '5 medium carrots, peeled and halved lengthwise', '1 cup maple syrup', '2 cups unsweetened apple juice', ' cup packed dark brown sugar', ' cup smooth Dijon mustard', ' cup grainy Dijon mustard', '2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar', 'Kosher salt (if needed)'
Instructions:
1. Pour 3 cups of water into a 7-quart slow cooker.
2. Add the ham, cut-side up, as well as the parsnips and carrots.
3. Pour the maple syrup and apple juice over the ham, and sprinkle the brown sugar over the top.
4. Cook on high, uncovered, for to minutes, then cover the slow cooker and cook on low until the ham is tender, 3 to 4 hours.
5. Hams vary in size and tenderness, so be patient.
6. When itAs tender, the meat should come off the bone with very little resistance.
7. Taste a small piece for tenderness.
8. Transfer the ham and vegetables to a deep serving platter (reserve the liquid in the pot).
9. Use a pair of kitchen shears or scissors to cut the slices of ham off the bone and let them fall onto the platter.
10. Stir both kinds of mustard and the vinegar into the cooking liquid left in the slow cooker and taste for seasoningAthe sauce may or may not need salt.
11. Pour the liquid over the ham and vegetables, and serve.

Ingredients: '6 slices bacon, cut crosswise into -inch pieces', '2 celery ribs, chopped', '1 carrot, chopped', '1 medium onion, chopped', ' teaspoon cinnamon', ' pound boiling potatoes', '2 medium Granny Smith apples (about pound total)', '1 pounds butternut squash, peeled, seeded, and cut into -inch pieces ( to 4 cups)', '2 cups reduced-sodium chicken stock or broth', '2 to cups water', 'Sour cream'
Instructions:
1. Cook bacon in a 6-quart heavy pot over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until crisp, then transfer with a slotted spoon to paper towels to drain, reserving 2 tablespoons fat in pot. (Add vegetable oil if your bacon is very lean and doesn't yield enough fat.) Cook celery, carrot, and onion in fat in pot over low heat, covered, stirring occasionally, until softened but not browned, to minutes.
2. Add cinnamon and cook, uncovered, stirring, 1 minute.
3. While vegetables are cooking, peel potatoes and coarsely chop.
4. Peel and core 1 apple and coarsely chop.
5. Stir squash, potato, chopped apple, stock, 2 cups water, 1 teaspoon salt and teaspoon pepper into onion mixture, then simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are very tender, to minutes.
6. Puree soup in 4 batches in a blender (use caution when blending hot liquids), then heat in cleaned pot over medium low heat, stirring occasionally. (Add additional cup water to thin, if necessary.) While soup is heating, cut enough of remaining apple into thin matchsticks (about 1 -inches long) to measure about cup.
7. Serve soup topped with sour cream, bacon, and apple matchsticks.
8. Cooks' Notes: Soup can be made 2 days ahead and chilled, covered once cooled.
9. Cooked bacon can be kept refrigerated in an airtight containerAreheat in a oven in a small baking pan.

Ingredients: '8 anchovies, mashed to a paste', '8 garlic cloves, finely grated', '6 Tbsp. mayonnaise, divided', '1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard, plus more for serving', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper, plus more', '1 (-lb.) whole chicken or 4 chicken legs (thigh and drumstick; about 3 lb.)', 'Kosher salt', '8 medium shallots, unpeeled, halved lengthwise', '2 lemons, divided', '1 oz. Parmesan, finely grated', '4 oz. country-style bread, torn into (") pieces', '2 romaine hearts, leaves separated, torn'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in lower third of oven; preheat to .
2. Whisk anchovies, garlic, 3 Tbsp. mayonnaise, 1 Tbsp. mustard, 1 Tbsp. oil, and tsp. pepper in a small bowl.
3. Set aside 1 Tbsp. anchovy mayo in another small bowl.
4. Pat chicken dry; season outside and inside all over with salt.
5. Arrange breast side up in a cast-iron skillet and tuck wings underneath.
6. Arrange shallots around (if using legs, nestle under and around); season with salt and pepper.
7. Brush remaining anchovy mayo all over chicken, making sure to get it into the nooks and crannies, then brush shallots with any leftover anchovy mayo.
8. Place chicken in oven so legs are facing toward the back (this is the hottest part of the oven and will help the legs cook before the breast dries out) and roast until some anchovy mayo and fat begin to drip onto shallots, about minutes.
9. Remove from oven and, using tongs, turn shallots to coat in drippings.
10. Return skillet to oven and continue to roast chicken, stirring shallots once or twice, until golden brown and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of breast registers , minutes.
11. If skin starts to get too dark on the top before chicken is done, tent area with foil, leaving the rest of the bird exposed.
12. If using chicken legs, start checking at minutes (a thermometer inserted right at the joint should register ).
13. Transfer chicken and shallots to a cutting board, leaving behind any juices and fat in skillet.
14. If shallots need more time to soften and darken, roast a bit longer without chicken before proceeding.
15. Reserve skillet.
16. Reduce oven temperature to .
17. Finely grate half of zest of 1 lemon into a large bowl; cut lemon in half and squeeze in juice.
18. Add reserved 1 Tbsp. anchovy mayo, remaining 3 Tbsp. mayo, and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil and whisk to combine, then stir in Parmesan.
19. Season with salt and pepper.
20. Set dressing aside.
21. Add bread to reserved skillet with fat and turn to coat.
22. Return skillet to oven and toast bread, tossing halfway through, until golden brown and crisp, minutes.
23. Transfer croutons to bowl with reserved dressing.
24. Add romaine and gently toss until lettuce is well coated.
25. Season salad with salt and pepper.
26. Slice remaining lemon into wedges.
27. Carve chicken and nestle back into skillet or transfer to a platter; arrange shallots and lemon wedges around.
28. Serve with salad and more mustard alongside.

Recipe name: Chicken And Rice With Leeks And Salsa Verde
Ingredients: ' lb. skinless, boneless chicken thighs (4A8 depending on size)', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter, divided', '2 large or 3 medium leeks, white and pale green parts only, halved lengthwise, thinly sliced', 'Zest and juice of 1 lemon, divided', ' cups long-grain white rice, rinsed until water runs clear', ' cups low-sodium chicken broth', '1 oil-packed anchovy fillet', '2 garlic cloves', '1 Tbsp. drained capers', 'Crushed red pepper flakes', '1 cup tender herb leaves (such as parsley, cilantro, andor mint)', '4A5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil'
Instructions:
1. Season chicken with salt and pepper.
2. Melt 2 Tbsp. butter in a large high-sided skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Add leeks and half of lemon zest, season with salt and pepper, and mix to coat leeks in butter.
4. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and cook, stirring occasionally, until leeks are somewhat tender, about 5 minutes.
5. Remove lid, increase heat to medium-high, and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender and just starting to take on color, about 3 minutes.
6. Add rice and cook, stirring often, 3 minutes, then add broth, scraping up any browned bits.
7. Tuck short sides of each chicken thigh underneath so they are touching and nestle seam side down into rice mixture.
8. Bring to a simmer.
9. Cover, reduce heat to medium-low, and cook until rice is tender and chicken is cooked through, about minutes.
10. Remove from heat.
11. Cut remaining 1 Tbsp. butter into small pieces and scatter over mixture.
12. Re-cover and let sit minutes.
13. Meanwhile, pulse anchovy, garlic, capers, a few pinches of red pepper flakes, and remaining lemon zest in a food processor until finely chopped.
14. Add herbs; process until a paste forms.
15. With motor running, gradually stream in oil until loosened to a thick sauce.
16. Add half of lemon juice; season salsa verde with salt.
17. Drizzle remaining lemon juice over chicken and rice.
18. Serve with salsa verde.

Ingredients: '1A cups instant corn masa flour (such as Maseca)', '5 Tbsp. lard, melted, or vegetable oil, divided', ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt, divided, plus more', '4 guajillo or dried New Mexico chiles, stems and seeds removed', '2 morita chiles, stems removed, or 2 canned chipotle chiles in adobo', '1 tsp. ground coriander or cumin', ' lb. medium shrimp, peeled, deveined', '4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '2 sprigs thyme', '1 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar', '1 tsp. agave syrup or honey', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces', 'Sliced onion, sliced avocado, cilantro leaves with tender stems, and lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Stir masa, 1 Tbsp. lard, 1 tsp.
2. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
3. Morton salt, and 1 cup water in a medium bowl until a soft and slightly sticky dough forms.
4. If dry, mix in another 1 Tbsp. water.
5. Cover and let sit at least minutes and up to 3 hours.
6. Meanwhile, bring guajillo chiles, morita chiles, coriander, and cups water to a boil in a small saucepan.
7. Cover, remove from heat, and let sit, stirring occasionally, minutes to allow chiles to soften.
8. Transfer to a blender and purAe until smooth; set aside.
9. Place rack in lowest position in oven; preheat to .
10. Brush a rimmed baking sheet with 2 Tbsp. lard.
11. Divide dough into 4 pieces and form into balls.
12. Set on work surface and cover with a damp kitchen towel.
13. Place 1 ball between 2 sheets of parchment or wax paper.
14. Using a small skillet or saucepan, gently press ball into a 5"-diameter disk.
15. Carefully peel away parchment; place on prepared baking sheet.
16. Repeat with remaining balls.
17. Bake gorditas until outer edges are lightly golden, minutes.
18. Turn over and bake until golden brown and crisp, minutes.
19. Transfer to plates and let cool minutes.
20. Insert a paring knife into side of gordita and, using a slow sawing motion, cut open along one side (keep other side intact).
21. Heat remaining 2 Tbsp. lard in a large skillet over high.
22. Cook shrimp until lightly browned and just cooked through, about 1 minute per side.
23. Transfer to a medium bowl.
24. Reduce heat to medium and cook garlic in same skillet, tossing often, until beginning to brown, about 1 minute.
25. Add thyme sprigs, vinegar, agave, tsp.
26. Diamond Crystal or 1 tsp.
27. Morton kosher salt, and reserved chile purAe.
28. Cook, stirring occasionally, until brick red and the consistency of a thick sauce, 6A8 minutes.
29. Add shrimp and any juices and cook 1 minute.
30. Remove from heat; add butter and stir constantly until melted and sauce is glossy.
31. Season with more salt if needed.
32. Remove and discard thyme.
33. Fill gorditas with shrimp mixture, onion, avocado, and cilantro.
34. Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

Ingredients: '5 Tbsp. vegetable oil, divided', '8 corn tortillas', ' oz. fresh chorizo', '3 garlic cloves, crushed', ' medium white onion, thinly sliced, plus more for serving', 'Kosher salt', '2 (-oz.) cans black beans, rinsed, or 3 cups Frijoles de la Olla, drained', ' cups low-sodium chicken broth', '6 oz. queso fresco or Cotija cheese, crumbled', 'Cilantro leaves with tender stems and sliced avocado (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Using 2 Tbsp. oil, brush both sides of each tortilla.
2. Heat a large skillet over medium-high.
3. Working in batches, cook until lightly browned and starting to crisp, about 1 minute per side.
4. Set aside.
5. Heat remaining 3 Tbsp. oil in same skillet over medium-high.
6. Cook chorizo, breaking up with a wooden spoon, until browned and cooked through, 7A9 minutes.
7. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to a medium bowl; set aside.
8. Add garlic and onion to same skillet, season with salt, and cook, tossing occasionally, until tender and beginning to brown, 6A8 minutes.
9. Using slotted spoon, transfer garlic mixture to a blender; reserve pan with oil.
10. Add beans and broth to blender and purAe until smooth (it should be the consistency of yogurt); season with salt.
11. Set reserved pan over medium-high and heat oil.
12. Transfer bean purAe to skillet and bring to a boil.
13. Reduce heat to low.
14. Working one at a time and using tongs, dip tortillas in bean purAe, turning to coat and leaving until softened, about 3 seconds per side (they will soften more as they sit).
15. Transfer to a baking sheet as you go.
16. Spoon 1 Tbsp. chorizo and 1 Tbsp. queso fresco across the center of each tortilla; fold over like a taco.
17. Divide among plates and spoon remaining bean purAe over.
18. Top with cilantro, avocado, more onion, remaining chorizo, and remaining queso.

Ingredients: '6 Tbsp. virgin coconut oil', '4 ripe (spotted) plAtanos manzanos or 2 large ripe (black) plantains, peeled, sliced in half lengthwise', 'Kosher salt', '3 cups plain unsweetened coconut milk yogurt or Greek yogurt', '2 ripe mangoes, thinly sliced', 'Toasted nuts, seeds, andor unsweetened shredded coconut (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
2. Cook plantains, turning once, until deep golden brown, about 3 minutes per side.
3. Transfer to paper towels to drain; season with salt.
4. Divide yogurt among bowls; top with plantains and mangoes, then nuts, seeds, andor coconut as desired.

Recipe name: Chicken And Potato Gratin With Brown Butter Cream
Ingredients: '3 lb. skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs and drumsticks', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', '1 lb. small potatoes (such as fingerling or baby Yukon Gold), halved lengthwise', '8 oz. small or medium shallots, halved through root ends', '5 Tbsp. unsalted butter, divided', ' cup heavy cream', '2 garlic cloves, finely grated', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper', ' tsp. freshly grated or ground nutmeg', '8 sprigs thyme', '8 sage leaves', ' oz. country-style bread, preferably day-old, torn into large pieces', '1 lemon, cut into wedges (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Season chicken all over with 4 tsp. salt.
2. Place in an airtight container and cover.
3. Chill at least 1 hour and up to 1 day.
4. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
5. Toss potatoes, shallots, and 2 tsp. salt in a large bowl.
6. Melt 4 Tbsp. butter in a small saucepan over medium heat and cook, stirring often, until butter foams, then browns, 5A8 minutes.
7. Immediately remove from heat (it will continue to darken) and stir in cream, garlic, pepper, nutmeg, and remaining tsp. salt.
8. Set saucepan over medium-low heat and cook, stirring, until salt is dissolved and mixture is warm to the touch.
9. Stir in thyme and sage; remove from heat.
10. Heat remaining 1 Tbsp. butter in a large stainless-steel skillet over medium-high until melted and bubbling.
11. Working in 2 batches, cook chicken, skin side down, until golden underneath, about 5 minutes.
12. Transfer chicken to a 13x9" or 3-qt. baking dish and arrange skin side up.
13. Set skillet with pan drippings aside.
14. Nestle potatoes and shallots around chicken (donAt place on top).
15. Pour brown butter cream over chicken and vegetables, making sure herbs and spices are evenly dispersed and tucking herbs into vegetables so they donAt get too crispy when they bake.
16. Bake gratin until cream is mostly reduced and chicken and vegetables are dark golden brown, about minutes.
17. Let gratin cool minutes.
18. Meanwhile, pulse bread in a food processor until pieces are about oat-size (you should have about cups).
19. Toss breadcrumbs in pan drippings in reserved skillet to coat.
20. Cook over medium heat, scraping up browned bits with a spatula, until deep golden brown, 5A7 minutes.
21. Let cool.
22. To serve, top gratin with breadcrumbs.
23. Serve lemon wedges alongside for squeezing over if desired.

Recipe name: Roasted Beets With Crispy Sunchokes And Pickled Orange-Ginger Puraae
Ingredients: 'Strips of zest (no pith) from 1 medium orange', ' ounces fresh ginger (about one 5-inch piece), peeled and thinly shaved (use a mandoline if you have one)', ' cup fresh lime juice', ' cup champagne vinegar', ' cup sugar', '2 tablespoons plus teaspoon kosher salt', '2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil', ' Thai chile, with seeds', ' pounds beets (use a mix of colors if possible), about 5 medium', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', ' cup white wine vinegar', ' cup water', 'Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper', '1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil', ' cup roughly chopped cashews', '4 large sunchokes, thinly shaved (use a mandoline if you have one)', 'Vegetable oil', 'Kosher salt', '1 medium orange', 'Roasted Beets', 'Extra-virgin olive oil', 'Kosher salt', ' cup Pickled Orange-Ginger Puree', 'Toasted Cashews', '1 tablespoon roughly chopped tarragon', '1 tablespoon roughly chopped dill fronds', 'Sunchoke Chips'
Instructions:
1. Place the zest strips in a small saucepan and cover with water.
2. Bring the water to a boil, then drain.
3. Repeat this process two more times (blanching the orange zest removes any bitter flavors).
4. Place the ginger and orange zest in a jar or heatproof container.
5. In a saucepan, bring the lime juice, vinegar, sugar, and teaspoon salt to a boil.
6. Pour over the ginger and orange.
7. Let cool to room temperature, then cover and refrigerate overnight.
8. The pickled ginger can be refrigerated for a couple of months.
9. Drain the pickled ginger and orange and save 3 tablespoons of the pickling liquid.
10. Add both to a blender or mini food processor, along with the olive oil, chile, and remaining 2 tablespoons salt.
11. Blend until very smooth, scraping down the side of the blender as needed.
12. Transfer to a container and refrigerate until ready to use.
13. The puree can be made a couple of days ahead. (Makes about 1 cup.) Preheat the oven to .
14. Place the beets in a baking dish (if using a mix of red and golden beets, separate them into two smaller dishes and divide the oil, vinegar, and water between the two dishes).
15. Drizzle with the oil and add the water and vinegar.
16. Season with salt and pepper.
17. Cover the pan with foil and roast until the beets are tender when pierced with a knife; this can take anywhere from minutes to an hour, depending on the thickness of the beets.
18. Let the beets cool, then peel and cut into 1-inch wedges.
19. Place each color of beet, along with any accumulated juices, into a small mixing bowl.
20. In a skillet, heat the oil over medium-low heat.
21. Add the cashews and cook, stirring constantly, until golden brown.
22. Transfer to a heat-proof bowl.
23. Pour 1 inch of oil into a small saucepan and add the sunchokes (there should be just enough oil to cover the sunchokes; add more oil if needed).
24. Turn the heat to medium and stir occasionally; when the sunchokes begin to sizzle and foam, increase the temperature to high and continue frying, stirring constantly, until the sunchokes are light golden brown.
25. At this point the sunchokes will rapidly go from golden brown to burnt, so quickly transfer the chips to paper towels to drain.
26. Lightly season with salt.
27. The sunchoke chips can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for a day or two, or until they start to soften. (Makes about cup.) Using a sharp knife, cut the peel and pith off the orange.
28. Working over a bowl, cut the orange between the membranes to release the segments.
29. Dice the segments, then return them to the bowl.
30. Drizzle the beet wedges with a little bit of olive oil, season with salt, and toss.
31. Spread the ginger puree on the bottom of a shallow serving bowl or plate.
32. Arrange the beets over the puree.
33. Sprinkle the chopped orange segments over the beets and grind some pepper over the dish.
34. Top with the cashews, herbs, and sunchoke chips.
35. Serve.

Recipe name: Kale And Pumpkin Falafels With Pickled Carrot Slaw
Ingredients: ' cup (120g or oz) toasted pepitas (pumpkin seeds)', '3 cups (90g or 3 oz) shredded kale, stems removed (about 3 large leaves)', '1 clove garlic, crushed', '1 ( oz or 400g ) can chickpeas (garbanzo beans), rinsed, drained', ' teaspoons ground turmeric', ' cup (6g or oz) coriander (cilantro) leaves', 'Sea salt and cracked black pepper', '3 cups (450g or 1 lb) firmly packed grated pumpkin', 'Extra virgin olive oil, for brushing', 'Plain Greek-style yogurt and hulled tahini, to serve', '6 carrots, shredded using a julienne peeler', '2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar', '2 tablespoons honey', '2 large red chiles, sliced', ' cup (6g or oz) coriander (cilantro) leaves'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to ().
2. Place the pepitas into the bowl of a food processor and process until finely chopped.
3. Add the kale, garlic, chickpeas, turmeric, coriander, salt and pepper and process until well combined.
4. Add the pumpkin and stir through to combine.
5. Shape large tablespoonfuls of pumpkin mixture into balls.
6. Place on a baking tray lined with non-stick baking paper and flatten slightly.
7. Brush well with oil, then bake for minutes or until golden and crisp.
8. To make the pickled carrot slaw, place the carrots, vinegar, honey, chile and coriander in a bowl and toss to combine.
9. Set aside for 5 minutes to pickle.
10. To serve, divide the pickled carrot slaw between bowls and top with pumpkin falafels and serve with yoghurt and tahini.

Recipe name: Maple And Chile Roasted Squash With Quinoa Tabouli
Ingredients: '1 (2kg or 4 lb or 4 oz) butternut squash or 2 (1kg or 2 lb or 3 oz) butternut squash, halved', '2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil', '1 tablespoon pure maple syrup', '1 teaspoon chile flakes', 'Sea salt and cracked black pepper', ' cup (40g or 1 oz) roasted almonds, chopped', '2 cups (320g or oz) cooked quinoa', ' cup (8g or oz) torn mint leaves', '60g (2 oz) wild rocket (arugula) leaves', ' cup (140g or 5 oz) hulled tahini', 'A cup (160ml or 5 fl oz) lemon juice', '1 cup (250ml or 8 fl oz) water', '1 clove garlic, crushed', 'Sea salt flakes'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to ().
2. Scoop the seeds from the squash and discard.
3. Place squash, cut side up, onto a baking tray.
4. Using the tip of a sharp knife, make some shallow slashes into the squash flesh.
5. Combine the oil, maple, chile, salt and pepper and brush over the squash.
6. Cover with aluminium foil and roast for minutes.
7. Remove the foil and roast for a further minutes or until the squash is golden and soft.
8. To make the quinoa tabouli, in a large bowl combine the quinoa, mint, and arugula.
9. To make the lemon tahini dressing, in a small bowl, place the tahini, lemon juice, water, garlic and salt.
10. Mix to combine.
11. To serve, place the squash onto a serving platter and top with the tabouli.
12. Drizzle with the lemon tahini dressing and sprinkle over almonds.

Ingredients: 'Ghee, for the cake pan', '8 ounces paneer, preferably homemade', ' cup confectionersA sugar', ' teaspoon ground cardamom', '2 tablespoons semolina', ' tablespoons ghee, store-bought or homemade', ' cup whole milk', '2 tablespoons roasted cashews', '2 tablespoons golden raisins', '3 tablespoons granulated sugar', 'Freshly whipped cream or sliced almonds (optional), for serving'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. Butter a 9-inch nonstick metal cake pan with some ghee.
3. In a large bowl, stir together the paneer, confectionersA sugar, and cardamom until incorporated but still slightly chunky.
4. Add the semolina, ghee, and milk and whisk until smooth.
5. Stir in the cashews and raisins and set aside.
6. Sprinkle the granulated sugar over the bottom of the prepared cake pan.
7. Set the pan on the stove over medium heat and once the sugar just begins to bubble and turn pale golden brown (the sugar will continue to caramelize in the oven, so donAt let it get any darker right now), after 8 to minutes, remove the pan from the heat using tongs. (This step is a bit tricky and you might be tempted to move the sugar around with a spoon while it is bubbling, but donAt touch itAjust let it caramelize on its own.) You will know itAs ready for the cake batter when itAs bubbling but is still slightly grainy and just turning a pale golden color.
8. Pour the batter into the pan, using a rubber spatula to scrape the bowl clean.
9. It should settle into an even layer on its own, but if it doesnAt, use the spatula to create a smooth surface.
10. Wearing oven mitts, carefully transfer the pan to the oven and bake until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean, to minutes.
11. Transfer to a wire rack and cool in the pan to room temperature.
12. Run a paring knife around the cakeAs edges and then place a serving platter larger than the diameter of the cake over the cake and gently flip the cake overAit should slide out, with caramel coming out with it like you would see in a flan (though some caramel will be left in the panAthis is fine).
13. Slice and serve with freshly whipped cream or sliced almonds, if desired.
14. The cake will keep in a covered container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
15. Note: To clean the pan, add some boiling water to the pan and let it sit for at least minutes to loosen up the browned sugar sticking to the surface of the pan before cleaning it.

Ingredients: '2 quarts whole milk', 'Juice of 2 lemons', ' teaspoon kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, heat the milk to over medium heat.
2. Using a wooden spoon, stir frequently during the heating process to prevent the milk from scalding.
3. Be sure to scrape the bottom of the pan when you stir because this is where the milk is most prone to sticking.
4. The milk will become foamy and bubbles will form on its surface once it reaches the desired temperature.
5. Remove from the heat and stir in the lemon juice until evenly distributed, which will cause the separation of curds from the whey.
6. The process should begin right away, but donAt fret if it takes a minute or two.
7. Cover the pan and set aside at room temperature for minutes to let the acid work its magic.
8. Check the mixture at this point: It should be slightly yellow and the curds should have completely separated from the whey.
9. DonAt panic if this hasnAt happened completely yet.
10. Simply add another teaspoon of acid and set aside for a few minutes more.
11. This should do the trick.
12. Line a fine-mesh sieve with cheesecloth and set the sieve over a bowl.
13. Ladle the curds and whey into the sieve.
14. Reserve the whey for another use.
15. Season the curds (still in the sieve) with salt, stirring it in using a wooden spoon, then gather up the edges of the cheesecloth into a tight bundle and squeeze the excess whey from the curds.
16. Place the cheesecloth and curds on a large plate and shape them into a square.
17. Wrap the cheesecloth edges around the paneer and place a second plate on top of it to compress it.
18. Set aside at room temperature for minutes.
19. At this point the paneer is ready to use or it can be refrigerated in a covered container for up to 3 days.

Recipe name: Sloppy Joe Shirred Eggs With Spinach
Ingredients: '1 medium carrot, top removed and cut into about 6 pieces', '1 (.5-ounce) can diced tomatoes, or 2 scant cups roughly chopped fresh tomatoes', '2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided', '3 garlic cloves, minced', '8 ounces fresh spinach (4 cups packed)', '3 teaspoons kosher salt, divided', '1 pound ground turkey', '2 teaspoons ground cumin', '1 teaspoon smoked paprika', ' teaspoon ground black pepper', ' cup R-Rated Onions', '2 tablespoons red wine vinegar', '4 large eggs Parmigiano Reggiano, for serving (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to .
2. Combine the carrot, tomatoes plus their juice, and 1 cup water in a blender.
3. Blitz till itAs as smooth as your blender allows.
4. In an ovenproof -inch sautA pan or skillet set over medium heat, cook 1 tablespoon of the olive oil and the minced garlic.
5. Watch for the garlic to start sizzling, then stir in the spinach and teaspoon of the salt.
6. Continue stirring for a minute, until all the spinach is wilted.
7. Remove it from the pan and set aside.
8. Add the final tablespoon olive oil to the pan, raise the heat to medium, and add the turkey plus 1 teaspoon salt.
9. Using a wooden spoon or spatula, break the turkey up into small morsels as it browns.
10. When you have browned turkey, add the cumin, paprika, and black pepper.
11. Stir to incorporate, toasting the spices for about seconds.
12. Add the carrot-tomato mixture, the R-Rated Onions, vinegar, and teaspoon salt.
13. Let this cook for about minutes, uncovered, stirring occasionally.
14. You may need to lower the heat to prevent Joe from sticking or splattering.
15. When the ragout is done it will be thick and juicy.
16. If it seems too dry, stir in a little water.
17. Taste the ragout for seasoning and adjust as you like.
18. Turn the heat off and make four indentions in the ragout.
19. Divide the spinach and put equal amounts in each indentation.
20. Top each section of spinach with a cracked egg.
21. Sprinkle the eggs with the remaining 1 teaspoon salt.
22. Slide the skillet onto the middle rack of your preheated oven.
23. Bake for to minutes, until the whites are set and the yolks are slightly runny.
24. To serve, scatter ribbons of Parmigiano Reggiano or another hard cheese over the top.
25. If bread is your thing, this is great with toast.

Ingredients: '4A5 large or 6A8 medium yellow or white onions', '1 Tbsp olive or vegetable oil', '1 tsp kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. Peel your onions and cut them in half through their stem endsAlongways, if you will.
2. Slice them thinly with the grain, following the line from root to stem rather than cutting the onion across its belly.
3. This is actually important because slicing it the other way makes the path to silky onions a longer one.
4. Once your onions are sliced, heat your skillet over medium-high heat and add the oil, then the onions and the salt.
5. Let the onions sizzle for a couple minutes.
6. Stir with a wooden spoon or a heatproof spatula and watch as they wilt for about 3 more minutes.
7. At this point, reduce the heat to medium low.
8. Put a lid on and step away for a few minutes.
9. Give the onions a stir every now and then.
10. You donAt have to stand over it like risotto, but donAt go for a walk around the block either.
11. Caramelized bits will accumulate at the bottom and sides of the pan, and thatAs good.
12. Just scrape them up and stir them in.
13. A little color building as you go is okay, but donAt rush to brown them.
14. The point is to cook the onions gently, coaxing them through stages of raw, wilted, sweaty, soft, light brown, and finally deeply caramelized About minutes in, remove the lid for the last time.
15. They should be a light caramel color.
16. Now, with the lid off, you will need to watch more closely and stir more frequently.
17. At some point you may find that despite your best efforts some of the caramelized bits, verging on burnt, cling to the pan and threaten over all onion ruin.
18. Do not fret!
19. Just add a cup or so of water and use its energy to help scrape up the stubborn but tasty film.
20. Let the water cook out of course.
21. When youAre smiling over a soft, creamy, fragrant pile of mahogany onions, youAre done.
22. Note: R-Rated Onions will keep in the fridge for a week and in the freezer for 3 months.

Ingredients: '1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 Tbsp. white miso', '2 tsp. pure maple syrup or brown sugar', '1 tsp. regular soy sauce or tamari', '1 small kabocha squash (about 2 lb.), peeled, cut into 1"-thick wedges, or 1 small butternut squash (about 2 lb.), peeled, cut into 1" pieces', '1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 (1") piece ginger, coarsely chopped', '2 garlic cloves, coarsely chopped', '4 cups low-sodium vegetable broth', '2 Tbsp. white miso', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', '1 large bunch broccolini ( oz.), halved crosswise, thicker stalks halved lengthwise', '4 (5-oz.) packages wavy ramen noodles, preferably fresh', 'Handful of cilantro leaves with tender stems'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Whisk oil, miso, maple syrup, and soy sauce in a small bowl until a thick paste forms.
3. Place squash on a rimmed baking sheet and scrape dressing over; toss to coat.
4. Arrange squash in a single layer and roast until fork-tender (it will be a little caramelized in spots), minutes.
5. Heat oil in a medium pot over medium-high.
6. Add ginger and garlic and cook, stirring, 1 minute.
7. Add broth and bring to a boil.
8. Remove from heat.
9. Set 8 roasted squash wedges (or about 2 cups if using butternut) aside for serving.
10. Add miso and remaining squash to pot and blend with an immersion blender until mostly smooth; season with salt and pepper. (Alternatively, you can let broth cool slightly, then purAe with sqush and miso in a regular blender.) Meanwhile, bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook broccolini until crisp-tender, about 3 minutes.
11. Using tongs, transfer to a plate.
12. Add noodles to same pot and cook according to packet directions.
13. Drain and divide noodles among bowls.
14. Ladle broth over noodles and arrange broccolini and reserved squash (cut in half if they are very big) on top.
15. Scatter cilantro over.

Ingredients: '2 tablespoons coconut oil', '1 pound calabaza pumpkin, seeded, peeled, and cut into chunks', '1 small onion, minced', '3 garlic cloves, minced', '1 ajA dulce pepper, seeded and minced', '1 teaspoon dark brown sugar', ' teaspoon ground cumin', '2 cups coconut milk', '1 cup chicken broth or vegetable broth', 'Salt and freshly ground black pepper', '2 tablespoons chopped fresh cilantro'
Instructions:
1. Heat the coconut oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add the pumpkin, stir well, and cook, covered, for minutes.
3. Add the onion, garlic, and ajA dulce and stir well.
4. SautA until the onion becomes translucent, 3 to 5 minutes, then add the brown sugar and cumin.
5. Mix well.
6. Cook until the sugar melts, 2 to 3 minutes.
7. Stir in the coconut milk and broth.
8. Bring to a simmer and cook, uncovered, until the pumpkin is very soft, about minutes.
9. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup into a smooth purAe.
10. Alternatively, remove the solids with a slotted spoon and place in a blender or food processor.
11. PurAe until smooth and return to the pan.
12. Bring back to a simmer and cook gently for 5 minutes so the flavors combine.
13. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
14. Serve hot, garnished with the cilantro.

Ingredients: '2 large Italian eggplants', '1 tablespoon canola oil', ' medium onion, chopped', '2 cloves garlic, finely chopped', '1 small tomato, chopped', ' teaspoon coarse salt, or to taste', 'Freshly ground black pepper to taste', '1 tablespoon coarsely chopped cilantro', 'Roti, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Prepare a hot grill or preheat the broiler.
2. With a fork, pierce the eggplants all over, and place on the grill or under the broiler.
3. Grill or broil until completely charred and soft, about minutes, turning frequently (the eggplants will brown and blister quickly).
4. Remove and allow to cool.
5. Once cool, cut open the eggplants and scrape out the flesh.
6. The flesh should be soft to the touch and pulpy, and should easily come away from the skin.
7. Set aside.
8. Heat the canola oil in a frying pan.
9. Add the onion and sautA until translucent.
10. Add the garlic and fry until the garlic turns a dark golden brown, then add the tomato and fry for 1 to 2 minutes.
11. Stir in the mashed eggplant and cook for about 2 minutes.
12. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
13. Garnish with the cilantro and serve with roti.

Ingredients: '1 (4-pound) chicken, cut into 8 pieces, skin removed', '1 tablespoon freshly squeezed lime juice', '2 tablespoons Green Seasoning', '1 teaspoon coarse salt', '1 Scotch bonnet pepper or other hot red chili pepper, finely chopped', '2 cloves garlic, crushed', ' cup Trinidad Curry Powder', '2 tablespoons canola oil', '1 medium onion, chopped', '1 cup chicken stock or water', 'Roti or white rice, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Place the chicken pieces in a bowl and toss with the lime juice.
2. Drain, rinse the chicken with cold water, and drain again.
3. Remove the chicken from the bowl and pat dry with paper towels.
4. Place the chicken in another bowl with the Green Seasoning, salt, chili pepper, and garlic.
5. Toss well to coat and refrigerate for at least minutes but ideally overnight.
6. Combine the curry powder with just enough water to form a thick paste.
7. Heat the oil in a heavy-bottomed pot.
8. Add the curry paste and onion.
9. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 to 2 minutes over medium-low heatAdo not allow the curry to scorch.
10. Add the chicken pieces and stir well to coat.
11. Add the stock or water, cover, and simmer for to minutes, or until the chicken is tender.
12. Remove the lid and simmer for 5 minutes more over medium-high heat, until the sauce reduces by one-third.
13. Serve with roti or white rice.

Ingredients: '6 cardamom pods', '1 cup coriander seeds', ' cup cumin seeds', '1 Tbsp. mustard seeds', '1 Tbsp. fenugreek seeds', '- curry leaves (optional)', ' cup whole black peppercorns', '6 whole cloves', 'A cup plus 1 Tbsp. ground turmeric'
Instructions:
1. Break open the cardamom pods, remove the seeds, and discard the pods.
2. Place the cardamom seeds, coriander, seeds, cumin seeds, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, and curry leaves in a heavy frying pan and toast, swirling for about 5 minutes, until the spices begin to release their aromas.
3. Place the toasted spices in a food processor or spice grinder and add the peppercorns and cloves.
4. Grind the mixture to a find powder.
5. Stir in the turmeric.
6. Store in an airtight container.
7. If stored properly, curry powder will keep for at least two months.

Ingredients: '3 garlic cloves, minced', '2 large onions, chopped fine', '1 cup finely chopped celery (about 2 stalks)', '1 red bell pepper, cut into -inch dice', ' green bell pepper, cut into -inch dice', '2 tablespoons olive oil', '2 cups chicken broth', '1 (-ounce) can whole tomatoes, drained and chopped', '1 bay leaf', '1 teaspoon ground cumin', '1 teaspoon salt, or to taste', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', ' pounds large shrimp (about ), shelled and deveined', 'Accompaniment: 1 cup uncooked rice, prepared according to package instructions but without butter or margarine'
Instructions:
1. In a large heavy kettle cook garlic, onions, celery, and bell peppers in oil over moderately low heat, stirring occasionally, until softened.
2. Add all remaining ingredients except shrimp and simmer, uncovered, minutes, or until thickened.
3. Stir in shrimp and cook, covered, over moderately high heat, stirring occasionally, until shrimp are cooked through, about 5 minutes.
4. Serve shrimp and sauce over coked rice.

Ingredients: '3 Tbsp. chopped fresh chives', '1 Tbsp. chopped fresh shado beni or cilantro leaves', '2 Tbsp. chopped fresh thyme', '1 Tbsp. chopped fresh oregano', '1 Tbsp. chopped fresh parsley', '4 cloves garlic'
Instructions: 1. Process all the ingredients in a food processor until the mixture forms a thick paste. (Alternatively, process in a blender with 2 Tbsp. vinegar.) Use immediately, or store in a tightly sealed glass jar in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

Ingredients: '1 cup dry or 1 (-ounce) can pigeon peas, pinto beans, or black-eyed peas', '2 cups long-grain rice', '3 tablespoons canola oil', ' cup sugar (white or brown)', '1 (3-pound) chicken, cut into 8 pieces, skin removed', '1 small onion, chopped', '1 clove garlic, finely chopped', '1 cup coconut milk', '1 bay leaf', '2 teaspoons Green Seasoning', ' cup chopped parsley', '1 sprig thyme', '2 carrots, peeled and chopped', '5 scallions, chopped (white and green parts)', 'Kosher salt', '2 cups cubed fresh calabaza or butternut squash', '1 small whole Scotch bonnet pepper', ' cup ketchup', '1 tablespoon butter'
Instructions:
1. If using dried peas, soak them overnight in 3 cups of water.
2. Drain.
3. Bring 3 fresh cups of water to a boil in a saucepan and add the peas.
4. Simmer for minutes, or until cooked almost completely through.
5. Drain and set aside.
6. If using canned beans, drain, rinse with cold water, drain again, and set aside.
7. Wash the rice by placing it in a colander or fine-mesh sieve and running cold water over it until the water runs clear, about 1 minute.
8. Drain well and set aside.
9. Heat the oil over medium heat in a Dutch oven or other heavy, deep pot.
10. Add the sugar and swirl in the pot, stirring constantly; allow it to caramelize to a dark brown color.
11. Add the chicken and stir well to coat.
12. Add the onion and garlic and cook for 1 to 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
13. Stir in 2 cups of water, the coconut milk, bay leaf, Green Seasoning, parsley, thyme, carrots, and scallions.
14. Season with salt.
15. Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for minutes.
16. Stir the rice, squash, peas, hot pepper, ketchup, and butter into the chicken.
17. Cover and cook for minutes, or until the peas and vegetables are tender.
18. Remove lid and fluff the rice.
19. The rice should be moist but not sticky.
20. Remove bay leaf, thyme sprig, and hot pepper.
21. Note: Pigeon peas are traditional in this dish, but pinto beans or black-eyes peas also work well because, like pigeon peas, they hold their shape well after cooking.
22. Brown sugar is preferable for deeper flavor and color.
23. Chicken is the most common meat in pelau, but cuts of stew beef or lamb work just as well.
24. If using meat, up the cooking time before adding the rice to minutes to allow the meat to get tender.
25. In Tobago, pelau is often made with crab.
26. West Indian pumpkin (also called calabaza) is ideal for this recipe, although the pie or "cheese" pumpkin common in the U.S., if in season, makes a very good substitute.
27. Butternut squash will also work, although it will be slightly less sweet.

Ingredients: '4 pounds Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled, cut into 2" pieces', '6 large garlic cloves, peeled', '1 tablespoon plus 2 teaspoons kosher salt, divided, plus more', '1 cups whole milk', '4 thyme sprigs', ' cup (1 sticks) plus 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, divided', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, plus more', ' cup sour cream', 'A potato ricer or food mill'
Instructions:
1. Cover potatoes, garlic, and 1 Tbsp. salt with cold water in a large pot.
2. Bring to a low boil, then reduce heat and simmer (do not boil) until potatoes are very tender when pierced with the tip of a paring knife but not falling apart, minutes.
3. Meanwhile, heat milk, thyme, and cup butter in a small pot over medium, stirring, until butter is melted.
4. Remove from heat; set aside.
5. Drain potatoes and garlic; return to pot.
6. Toss over low heat until moisture evaporates, 1A2 minutes.
7. Using potato ricer or food mill, immediately press potatoes and garlic into a large bowl (do not let cool).
8. Discard thyme from milk mixture and gradually stir into potatoes, reserving about cup if you plan to make in advance (see Do Ahead).
9. Season with 2 tsp. salt and tsp. pepper.
10. Fold in sour cream and stir with a spoon until incorporated and very smooth (do not overmix or potatoes will become gummy).
11. Taste and adjust seasonings.
12. Serve topped with remaining 2 Tbsp. butter and more pepper.
13. Mashed potatoes can be made 2 hours ahead; cover and store at room temperature.
14. Or chill, covered, up to 8 hours; reheat over medium with reserved cup milk mixture.

Ingredients: ' oz ( ml) London Dry gin', ' oz ( ml) Cynar', ' oz ( ml) Fino sherry', 'Orange twist, to garnish'
Instructions:
1. Combine all the ingredients in a chilled rocks glass.
2. Add one large ice cube and stir for seconds.
3. Garnish with an orange twist.

Recipe name: Creole Cream Cheesecake With Caramel-Apple Topping
Ingredients: '2 cups graham cracker crumbs (about whole crackers)', '3 tablespoons granulated sugar', '3 tablespoons firmly packed light brown sugar', '1A4 teaspoon kosher salt', '1A2 cup unsalted butter, melted', '2 pounds cream cheese, at room temperature', '1 cup granulated sugar', '1A cups Creole Cream Cheese', '1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste', '2 eggs, at room temperature', 'Ingredient Info: You can substitute 1 cup sour cream mixed with 1A4 cup buttermilk for the 1A cups Creole Cream Cheese.', ' tablespoons unsalted butter', '3A4 cup granulated sugar', '1A4 cup firmly packed light brown sugar', ' tablespoons honey', '4 cups peeled, cored, and evenly chopped Granny Smith apples (about 7)', ' teaspoons finely grated lemon zest', '1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice', '3A4 teaspoon kosher salt', '3A4 teaspoon ground cinnamon', '1A3 teaspoon ground cardamom', '1A3 teaspoon ground ginger', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract'
Instructions:
1. Make the crust.
2. Preheat the oven to .
3. In a medium bowl, stir together the graham cracker crumbs, granulated sugar, brown sugar, salt, and butter to combine.
4. Pat the mixture into a -inch springform pan, pressing the crumbs firmly in an even layer on the bottom and making sure they come at least halfway up the side of the pan.
5. Bake for 7 to minutes, until the crust is fragrant, dry, and toasty.
6. Let cool, then wrap the entire outside of the pan tightly in two layers of foil, all the way to the top.
7. In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment or in a large bowl using a handheld mixer, cream the plain cream cheese and sugar on medium-high speed for about 4 minutes, until incredibly smooth and light.
8. Stop the mixer and scrape down the bottom and sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula.
9. Add the Creole Cream Cheese and vanilla paste and beat on medium-high speed for about 2 minutes, scraping down the bowl once more.
10. Decrease the speed to medium and beat in the eggs, one at a time, incorporating each one before adding the next.
11. Mix just until the batter is smooth and uniform.
12. Continuing to mix past that point, even though the batter wonAt look different, only incorporates extra air, which will cause the cheesecake to bubble excessively and the surface to crack as that air tries to escape during the baking process.
13. Decrease the oven temperature to .
14. Pour the batter into the prepared crust and tap the pan gently on the counter a few times to remove any large air bubbles.
15. Place the springform pan in a large baking or roasting pan (just big enough for the springform pan to fit in) and set the pan on the middle rack in the oven.
16. Fill the pan with enough hot water to reach the rim of the springform pan.
17. Bake for about 1 hour, or until the sides of the custard are set and the middle is still just slightly wobbly.
18. Generally, if the entirety of the custard is fully set, your cheesecake is overbaked.
19. DonAt panicAitAll still be delicious, and youAll do better next time!
20. Let the cheesecake cool in the water until it is about room temperature. (You donAt want to have to try to grab the pan out of hot water if you can help it.) However, if youAre worried that your cheesecake might be slightly overbaked, youAll want to pull it out rather quickly, and let it cool at room temperature.
21. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours before slicing.
22. The cake can be refrigerated, covered, for up to 4 days.
23. In a large heavy-bottomed sautA pan, heat the butter until melted.
24. Stir in the granulated sugar, brown sugar, and honey and let cook until bubbling.
25. Add the apples and toss to coat.
26. Stir in the lemon zest, lemon juice, salt, cinnamon, cardamom, and ginger; stir to combine.
27. Cook for about 4 minutes, or until the apples just start to soften.
28. Remove from the heat, stir in the vanilla, and transfer to a bowl to cool completely before use.
29. The filling can be made up to 3 days ahead of time and stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

Ingredients: '2 quarts skim milk', '1A4 cup buttermilk', '1 rennet tablet'
Instructions:
1. In a large heavy-bottomed saucepot over medium heat with a kitchen thermometer attached to the rim, warm the milk and buttermilk to .
2. Add one-quarter of the rennet tablet and transfer the mixture to a plastic container or bowl.
3. Cover with plastic wrap and then poke a few holes in the plastic to allow the steam to escape.
4. Let stand at room temperature in a cool spot in your house for hours.
5. Use a slotted spoon to transfer the solids to a double layer of cheesecloth set over a bowl and discard the liquid.
6. Wrap the solids in the cheesecloth and hang it over the bowl (to catch the whey) in the refrigerator for hours.
7. Discard the whey.
8. Pack the cheese into an airtight plastic container and store in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

Ingredients: ' tsp. nonfat dry milk powder', '2 tsp. baking powder', '2 tsp. granulated sugar or light brown sugar', '2 cups ( g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', '1 Tbsp. vegetable oil, plus more', '2 Tbsp. vegetable shortening, room temperature', '1 Tbsp. ghee, unsalted butter, or vegan butter, room temperature'
Instructions:
1. Whisk milk powder, baking powder, granulated sugar, and 2 cups ( g) flour in a large shallow bowl.
2. Add about a fourth of cup warm water () and mix in a wide circular motion with your fingers to gently combine (do not knead).
3. As mixture becomes dry, add more of the water, little by little, and keep mixing gently until a shaggy dough forms; it should be soft and sticky, without dry spots. (Depending on the humidity, you may not need all of the water.) Drizzle 1 Tbsp. oil over dough and gently press with your knuckles so that the top of dough is covered with oil.
4. Cover bowl with a damp kitchen towel or plastic wrap and let rest in a warm spot minutes.
5. Meanwhile, mix shortening and ghee in a small bowl until smooth.
6. Turn out dough onto a lightly floured surface.
7. Flour your hands and gently fold dough over on itself once or twice, then roll into a fat cylinder.
8. Working quickly, without handling the dough too much, divide dough into four equal pieces and gently form each piece into a ball.
9. Cover balls with a damp kitchen towel or plastic wrap and let rest minutes.
10. Roll out a dough ball on a lightly floured surface into a 5-6"Adiameter round.
11. Brush with a quarter of shortening mixture and sprinkle evenly with flour.
12. Cut a slit halfway across the dough, from the center to the edge.
13. Starting on one side of slit, fold cut edge of dough at about a " interval.
14. Continue folding process until you reach the other side of the slit and have formed a cone.
15. Pinch together edges at wide end of cone to seal, then bring both point ends together at the top and gently push in toward center of cone to create a dimple (you donAt need to push in too far).
16. Push point at narrow end of cone in toward the center.
17. You should now have a ball with a dimple on each end.
18. Transfer to a lightly floured surface and repeat with remaining balls of dough.
19. Cover balls with a damp kitchen towel or plastic wrap and let rest minutes.
20. Heat a tawa or griddle over medium until a drop of water immediately sizzles on surface, about 2 minutes.
21. Meanwhile, place a ball of dough on a dimpled side on a lightly floured surface.
22. Gently flatten into a round with your hands, turning once if desired, then roll, rotating often, to a A"-thick round, about ".
23. Brush tawa with a thin layer of oil.
24. Cook roti until it begins to bubble slightly across the surface, about seconds.
25. Brush surface of roti with oil and continue to cook until bubbles across surface increase and roti is stiff enough on bottom side to turn, about 1 minute more.
26. Turn roti over and brush surface with oil (bubbles may deflate; thatAs okay).
27. Using a spatula, move roti around to avoid hot spots and ensure even cooking, pressing on edges and uncooked spots to increase contact with pan.
28. Continue to cook, turning every seconds or so, until cooked through and both sides are speckled with golden brown spots, 1A2 minutes more.
29. Using 2 spatulas, push opposite edges of roti toward the center.
30. Smack down on the bunched roti with the ends of the spatulas to break up and flake, then turn roti over.
31. Repeat process, scrunching and smacking roti from different directions, until flaky and lightly shredded.
32. Transfer to a clean kitchen towel set inside a bowl or basket and wrap up to keep warm.
33. Repeat with remaining dough balls and more oil, adding finished roti to bowl and covering tightly with towel as you go.

Ingredients: '6 large eggs', ' cup mayonnaise', '1 teaspoon Dijon mustard', ' teaspoon cayenne', 'Garnishes: paprika and chopped fresh chives', 'Pastry bag fitted with -inch star tip (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Cover eggs with cold water by 1 inches in a 3-quart heavy saucepan and bring to a rolling boil, partially covered.
2. Reduce heat to low and cook eggs, covered completely, seconds.
3. Remove from heat and let stand, covered, minutes.
4. Transfer eggs with a slotted spoon to a bowl of ice and cold water to stop cooking and let stand 5 minutes.
5. Peel eggs and halve lengthwise.
6. Carefully remove yolks and mash in a bowl with a fork.
7. Add mayonnaise, mustard, and cayenne and stir with fork until smooth, then season with salt and pepper.
8. Fill pastry bag with yolk mixture and pipe into egg whites.

Ingredients: '2 green, unripe mangoes, peeled and sliced (2 cups)', '4 shallots, thinly sliced (about cup)', '4 Indian or Thai green chiles, halved lengthwise', '1 (1-inch) piece ginger, peeled and cut into thin matchsticks', '3 cloves garlic, cut into thin matchsticks', '3 Tbsp coconut vinegar or white vinegar', ' tsp salt', '3 cups coconut milk', '1 Tbsp coconut oil', ' tsp black mustard seeds', '4 dried red chiles, snapped in half', ' curry leaves', 'Fried Onions for garnish (optional)', 'Basmati Rice to serve'
Instructions:
1. In a bowl, combine mangoes, shallots, green chiles, ginger, garlic, vinegar, and salt and mix well.
2. Set aside for 2 hours.
3. In a large saucepan, combine mango mixture and coconut milk and bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes, or until mangoes are softened.
5. Have a splash guard and measured spices nearby.
6. Heat oil in a small frying pan over medium-high heat until nearly smoking.
7. Immediately reduce heat to medium. (You can test the heat of the oil by dropping in a couple of seeds.
8. The oil is at the correct temperature when the seeds crackle, but do not burn.) Add mustard seeds and temper for a few seconds, until they stop popping. (Cover with the splash guard, if needed.) Add red chiles and curry leaves and cook for seconds.
9. Add the tempered spice mixture to the saucepan and stir to combine.
10. Garnish with fried onions (if using).
11. Serve with rice.
12. Tempering is a traditional method of extracting optimal flavor from Indian spices, and it is a skill learned with practice!
13. Reducing the heat a little before adding the spices prevents the spices from burning and adding a bitterness to your dish.
14. If they do burn, simply start again with fresh spices.

Ingredients: ' cup grated piloncillo or (packed) dark brown sugar', '1 (3") canela or cinnamon stick', '5 cardamom pods, cracked', '5 allspice berries', '1 tsp. black peppercorns', 'Pinch of kosher salt', '3 wide strips orange zest', '5A8 cups cold-brew coffee', 'Unsweetened coconut milk or half-and-half (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Bring piloncillo, canela, cardamom, allspice, peppercorns, salt, and 1 cup water to a boil in a small saucepan.
2. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer minutes.
3. Add orange zest; let cool.
4. Strain spiced syrup through a fine-mesh sieve into a measuring glass; discard spices. (You should have about 1 cup.) To make 1 drink, pour 1 cup cold-brew coffee into an ice-filled glass.
5. Stir in 2A3 Tbsp. spiced syrup; top with 2A3 Tbsp. coconut milk.
6. Do Ahead: Syrup can be made 1 week ahead.
7. Cover and chill.

Recipe name: Pumpkin Dutch Baby With Pumpkin Butter
Ingredients: '3 large eggs, room temperature', '1 (-ounce) can pumpkin purAe, divided', '1 cup milk, room temperature', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature, divided', '1 cup all-purpose flour', '1 tsp. cinnamon, divided', ' tsp. allspice', ' tsp. ground ginger', ' tsp. nutmeg', 'Kosher salt', ' cup apple cider or juice', '3 Tbsp. maple syrup', 'Powdered sugar, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Place an 8-inch ovenproof skillet or cast-iron pan in the oven and preheat to .
2. Once the oven reaches temperature, blend 3 large eggs in a blender until well combined and frothy, 1 minute.
3. Add cup of the pumpkin purAe, then 1 cup milk, and then 1 Tbsp. of the butter, blending for seconds after each addition.
4. Add 1 cup all-purpose flour, tsp. allspice, tsp. ground ginger, tsp. nutmeg, tsp. salt, and 1 tsp. of the cinnamon in one addition.
5. Blend again until combined and frothy, 1 minute (scraping down the sides of the blender if necessary).
6. Pull the hot pan from the oven and add the remaining 2 Tbsp. butter, swirling the pan so it melts evenly across the bottom (it will bubble and brown).
7. Once the butter is melted, immediately pour in the Dutch baby batter and return to the oven.
8. Bake until golden brown and slightly puffed, minutes.
9. While the Dutch baby bakes, combine cup apple cider, 3 Tbsp. maple syrup, the remaining tsp. cinnamon, a pinch of salt, and the rest of the can of pumpkin purAe (about 1 cups) in a small saucepan.
10. Cook over medium-high heat until the mixture starts to bubble, then reduce to medium and cook, stirring frequently and scraping down the sides of the pot with a spatula, until dark and thickened with no raw pumpkin flavor remaining, to minutes.
11. Slice the Dutch baby (it will start to sink as soon as you take it out of the oven) and serve with the pumpkin butter and powdered sugar.

Recipe name: Braised Chicken Legs With Grapes And Fennel
Ingredients: '4 chicken legs (thigh and drumstick; about lb.), patted dry', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '2 medium fennel bulbs (about 1 lb.), plus cup coarsely chopped fronds', '1 lb. Red Flame or green table grapes, stems removed (about 2 cups)', '1 cup unsalted chicken broth', ' cup honey', '4 tsp. Calabrian chile paste or chopped Calabrian chiles', '1 Tbsp. red wine vinegar'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Season chicken legs all over with 3 tsp. salt.
3. Swirl 1 Tbsp. oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot to coat and heat over medium-high.
4. Place 2 chicken legs, skin side down, in pot, making sure skin is in direct contact with pan.
5. Cook, undisturbed, until dark golden, about 5 minutes.
6. Turn over and cook until golden on other side, about 2 minutes.
7. Transfer to a large plate.
8. Repeat with remaining 2 chicken legs; reserve pot.
9. Meanwhile, working one at a time, stand a bulb, root end down, on a cutting board and slice off a short end to create a flat surface.
10. Turn cut side down and slice " thick, starting from the top end of bulb so the root end can hold the layers in place while you slice.
11. Discard last " (root end) of each bulb and pop out and discard tougher center from slices.
12. Transfer fennel to a large bowl and add half of the grapes, tsp. salt, and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil; toss to coat.
13. Set aside.
14. Reduce heat to medium and combine broth, honey, chile paste, and remaining 1 tsp. salt in reserved pot.
15. Stir to combine and dissolve salt.
16. Place remaining grapes in pot and arrange chicken legs, skin side up and overlapping if needed, over grapes.
17. Top with dressed fennel and grapes. (DonAt worry if it looks crowded: Fennel will soften as it cooks.) Transfer pot, uncovered, to oven and roast until fennel is slightly softened, with some crispy, caramelized edges, grapes on top are swollen and browned in spots, and chicken is cooked through, minutes.
18. Let cool 5 minutes.
19. Using a slotted spoon or tongs, transfer grapes and fennel to a bowl and chicken to a platter.
20. Place pot over medium-high heat and bring braising liquid to a simmer.
