21. Cook until reduced by half (you should have about 1 cup liquid), minutes.
22. Remove from heat and stir in vinegar and fennel fronds.
23. Spoon sauce over chicken and serve with fennel and grapes alongside.

Ingredients: '2 cups thinly sliced onion', '9 tablespoons unsalted butter', '6 tablespoons all-purpose flour', '3 cups milk', '2 pounds boiling potatoes', '1 cups coarsely grated sharp Cheddar (about 6 ounces)', ' cup dry bread crumbs'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. In a skillet, cook the onion and 2 tablespoons of the butter over moderately low heat, stirring, until it is very soft.
3. In a heavy saucepan, melt 6 tablespoons of the remaining butter over moderately low heat, whisk in the flour, and cook the roux, whisking, for 3 minutes.
4. Add the milk, scalded, in a stream, whisking, and bring the sauce to a boil.
5. Simmer the sauce, whisking, for 1 minute and add salt and pepper to taste.
6. Peel the potatoes and slice them A-inch thick.
7. Spread about one third of the sauce in the bottom of a well-buttered 3-quart gratin dish at least 2 -inches deep, cover it with a layer of potato slices, overlapping the slices slightly, and cover the potatoes with one third of the onions.
8. Sprinkle the onions with one third of the Cheddar and continue to layer the remaining sauce, potatoes, onions, and Cheddar in the same manner.
9. Sprinkle the top with bread crumbs, and dot it with the remaining 1 tablespoon butter, cut into bits.
10. Bake the mixture, covered with foil, in the middle of preheated oven for minutes.
11. Remove the foil and bake the potato mixture for minutes more, or until the top is golden and the potatoes are tender.

Ingredients: '500g skinless salmon fillet, cut into 4cm cubes', '4 tablespoons Greek yogurt', '1 tablespoon garlic granules', '1 heaped tablespoon rose harissa', '1 teaspoon ground turmeric', '1 teaspoon paprika', 'Finely grated zest of 1 unwaxed lime and a good squeeze of juice', '1 teaspoon olive oil', 'Generous amount of Maldon sea salt flakes and freshly ground black pepper', 'Tortilla wraps', 'Sliced tomatoes', 'Finely sliced onion', 'Coriander leaves', 'Greek yogurt'
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to its highest setting (with fan if it has one).
2. Line a baking tray with baking paper.
3. Mix all the marinade ingredients together in a mixing bowl.
4. Add the salmon and turn until well coated in the marinade.
5. Spread the salmon out on the prepared baking tray and roast for minutes until cooked through.
6. Remove from the oven and serve immediately with tortilla wraps, tomatoes, finely sliced onion, coriander leaves and Greek yogurt.

Ingredients: ' cups warm water', '1 teaspoon instant yeast', ' cups ( grams) bread flour, divided, plus more as needed', '2 tablespoons canola oil, plus more for your bowl', ' teaspoons Morton kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. Combine the water and yeast in a large mixing bowl (if you have a stand mixer, use that bowl) and let sit for 5 minutes.
2. Reserve cup ( grams) bread flour and add the remaining 4 cups to the mixing bowl along with the canola oil.
3. If you have a stand mixer, fit it with the dough hook and knead the mixture on low speed for about 3 minutes, until a sticky but cohesive dough starts to form.
4. Pause occasionally to scrape down the bowl if the flour is clinging to the sides and bottom or climbing up the hook.
5. If youAre making the dough without a stand mixer, mix it with a wooden spoon.
6. In either case, loosely cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a dish towel and let rest for minutes.
7. With your stand mixer on low or while stirring by hand in the mixing bowl, add the salt and, over the course of 2 or 3 minutes, add the remaining cup flour, 2 tablespoons at a time.
8. The dough should be more tense; it will feel soft but tacky, although it will pull from the sides of the bowl.
9. Flour your hands generously and use them to pull the dough onto a clean, unfloured work surface.
10. Cup your hands around the dough, rolling it in short, circular strokes and using the sides of your hands to nudge it into a relatively smooth ball.
11. Lightly wipe the inside of a large bowl with canola oil and place the dough inside, flipping it once or twice to coat.
12. Loosely cover the bowl and let the dough rise at a warm room temperature for 1 hour.
13. After 1 hour, the dough will be stretchy but very soft.
14. Leaving it inside the bowl, stretch opposite sides of the dough over the center.
15. Rotate the bowl a quarter- turn and stretch the dough in the same way, then flip the whole mound of dough upside down and cover again.
16. Let rise for 1 hour.
17. Repeat this series of folds one more time, allow it to rise at room temperature for 1 hour, then tightly cover the bowl and refrigerate overnight or up to 2 days.
18. The longer the dough is refrigerated, the more flavor it will have.
19. After this rise, itAs ready to be shaped; bear in mind that, once itAs shaped, it will need more time for a final rise, so plan ahead.
20. Wipe a bit of canola oil on a large baking sheet.
21. Dump the dough onto a clean, dry counter, and use a bench scraper or sharp knife to cut it into eight equal pieces; make clean, decisive cuts rather than use a sawing motion, so you donAt deflate all the air inside.
22. Lightly flour your hands and place one palm directly over the dough ball, with all your fingertips touching the counter to make a sort of AcageA around the dough.
23. Roll it in brisk, small circles on the countertop so it tightens itself into a smoother, more taut ball.
24. Space the rounds of dough a few inches apart, seam side down, on the sheet, and roll to coat them lightly in oil.
25. Tightly cover the sheet with plastic wrap so the dough doesnAt dry out, and let them rise at room temperature for 2 to 4 more hours, until theyAre pillowy.
26. Meanwhile, set a baking stone on the center rack of your oven and turn on the broiler.
27. YouAre emulating a wood- burning oven, so you need to give the stone a good long while to preheat before you bake.
28. When the dough is ready, lightly flour a work surface, and use a bench scraper or thin metal spatula to coax one piece into your palm; be sure you donAt manhandle it or youAll force out the pockets of air that formed while it rose.
29. Dust a little more flour on the top of the dough and onto your rolling pin.
30. With firm, even pressure, briskly roll the dough a few times along its length.
31. Flip it upside down, rotate it a quarter- turn, and roll it the same way, keeping it as round as possible.
32. Repeat, dusting a little extra flour as needed, until itAs about 6 inches across.
33. This next part happens fast and furiously, so make sure you have no distractionsAscreaming children and natural disasters will have to wait.
34. Use tongs or a good oven mitt to pull the oven rack with the baking stone partially out.
35. Carefully pick up the pita, drape it over your palm, and slap it down onto the stone as if youAre giving it a high- five (just be careful not to touch the hot stone!).
36. Set a timer for 1 minute, and close the oven.
37. Broilers vary in strength but all are quite hot, so donAt turn your back on the oven or the pita may burn.
38. Check on itA it should puff up and build in color, with some beautiful blistered spots.
39. If itAs still pale, close the oven and let it keep baking for - second intervals.
40. Use tongs to flip the pita, and let it finish baking with the oven door cracked so you can watch it finish.
41. Pull it out when the second side is as pretty as the first; this can take anywhere from seconds to 2 minutes, depending on your broiler.
42. Bake off the rest of the dough this way; as you get the hang of it, feel free to bake two pitas at a time.
43. Serve these hot or at room temperature.

Ingredients: '3 pounds ( to ) small beets, scrubbed thoroughly but not peeled', '9 tablespoons white vinegar', 'One 2-pound boneless pork butt, halved', '8 cups beef, chicken, or vegetable stock', '1 bay leaf', '1 teaspoon whole allspice berries', '1 tablespoon whole black peppercorns', '3 large carrots, peeled and sliced', '3 large celery stalks, sliced', '1 small head of green cabbage (about to 1 pound), shredded (about 4 cups)', '2 medium Idaho potatoes, peeled and cut into -inch dice', 'One -ounce can lima beans, drained and rinsed', 'Salt'
Instructions:
1. To make the Abeet water,A roughly chop 2 pounds of the beets (select the smaller ones), preferably in a food processor fitted with the metal blade.
2. Place the chopped beets in a large stockpot.
3. Add cups of water and 1 tablespoon vinegar.
4. Place the stockpot over high heat and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, and simmer for 2 hours. (If it seems like the liquid is evaporating too quickly, you may need to cover the pot partially with an offset lid.) The beets should be extremely soft and the liquid bright red.
5. Strain the liquid, pressing the cooked beets against the side of the strainer to extract as much liquid as possible.
6. Discard the pulp or reserve.
7. Set aside the beet water.
8. You should have just about 4 cups.
9. Meanwhile, place the remaining 1 pound of whole beets in a separate large stockpot.
10. Add water to cover and bring to a boil.
11. Reduce the heat to low, and simmer until the beets are tender-firm, about minutes.
12. When the beets are cooked, add 1 tablespoon white vinegar and set them aside to cool.
13. When the whole cooked beets are cool enough to handle, peel them; the skins should slip off easily.
14. Grate the peeled beets on the largest holes of a box grater or in a food processor fitted with the grating blade.
15. To make the broth, place the pork butt in a large stockpot and add the beef stock.
16. If necessary, add a little more stock or water to cover.
17. Add the bay leaf, allspice berries, and peppercorns.
18. Bring to a boil, reduce the heat to low, and simmer until the meat is tender and beginning to fall apart, about 2 hours.
19. Set the pork aside to cool.
20. When the pork is cool enough to handle, remove it from the pot and cut the meat into -inch cubes.
21. Strain the broth and discard the bay leaf, allspice berries, and peppercorns.
22. Reserve the cubed meat and 4 cups of the broth.
23. To cook the vegetables, place the carrots and celery in a large stockpot and pour the reserved meat broth over them.
24. Bring to a boil, reduce the heat, and simmer until the carrots and celery are just tender, about 8 minutes.
25. Add the cabbage and potatoes and continue to cook until the potatoes and carrots are easily pierced with a pairing knife but keep their shape, to additional minutes.
26. Add the lima beans and cook for 5 additional minutes, just to meld the flavors.
27. Gradually add the remaining 7 tablespoons white vinegar, tasting between additions and stopping when the flavor is to your liking.
28. Remove the soup from the heat and set aside.
29. To compose the soup, in a large soup pot combine the Abeet waterA and meat broth with the vegetables.
30. Add the cubed pork and the grated beets.
31. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer over low heat.
32. Season to taste with salt and serve immediately.

Recipe name: Papaya-And-Cubeb-Marinated Snapper With Baked Yam Chips
Ingredients: '4 5A6-oz. skin-on red snapper fillets, patted dry', ' tsp. okra salt or kosher salt, plus more', 'Alligator pepper or freshly ground black pepper', ' tsp. cubeb pepper or Ashanti pepper (uziza)', ' green or medium-ripe papaya, peeled, insides discarded', '1 small bunch cilantro', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 small red onion, coarsely chopped', '1 garlic clove, coarsely chopped', '1 1" piece ginger, peeled, coarsely chopped (unpeeled if organic)', ' tsp. ground dried bird chiles or other hot chile powder', '1 lemon, halved', '1 lb. puna, white yams, or sweet potatoes (2A3)', '1 tsp. coconut sugar or dark brown sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', 'Freshly ground black pepper', ' tsp. chile powder', 'Drunk Apricot Shito (for serving)', 'A spice mill or mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Using a sharp chefAs knife, score skin of each fish fillet in a crosshatch pattern, making cuts about " apart.
2. Season fillets on both sides with salt and alligator pepper.
3. Toast cubeb peppers in a dry small skillet over medium-low heat, shaking occasionally, until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
4. Transfer to a spice mill or mortar and pestle.
5. Let cool and coarsely grind; set aside.
6. Coarsely chop half of papaya into 1" pieces; set remaining half aside.
7. Coarsely chop half of cilantro (leaves and stems); set remaining cilantro aside.
8. Transfer chopped papaya to a blender; add oil and blend, adding 1A2 Tbsp. water to get it going if needed, until smooth.
9. Add chopped cilantro, onion, garlic, ginger, ground chile, tsp. salt, and reserved ground cubeb pepper.
10. Blend mixture to a smooth paste.
11. Coat fillets all over with marinade, rubbing into score marks.
12. Place fish, skin side down, in a single layer in a large baking dish.
13. Squeeze juice from half a lemon over.
14. Set remaining lemon half aside for serving.
15. Cover with plastic wrap or a lid and chill at least 2 hours and up to hours.
16. Fill a large bowl with water.
17. Peel yams and slice in half lengthwise, then slice in half crosswise.
18. Cut each quarter into "-thick slabs. (Length of slabs will depend on yamAs size, but youAre going for something that looks like steak fries.) Add yam slices to bowl as you work to prevent oxidation.
19. When all yams are cut, swish in water to remove excess starch, then drain.
20. Refill bowl and repeat process until water is clear, about 2 more times.
21. Add sugar and tsp. salt and stir until dissolved; add the drained chips. (Yams, like many foods that are good for you, can be quite bitter; the sugar will help counter this.) Let soak minutes.
22. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
23. Drain chips and pat dry with a clean kitchen towel.
24. Transfer to a rimmed baking sheet and drizzle with 2 Tbsp. oil.
25. Season with black pepper, then sprinkle with chili powder and remaining 1 tsp. salt.
26. Toss to coat, then spread out into an even layer.
27. Roast chips, turning halfway through, until golden brown and crisp, about minutes.
28. Heat broiler.
29. Scrape off excess marinade from fish and place fillets, skin side up, on another rimmed baking sheet.
30. Broil until browned and crisp on top and cooked through, 5A7 minutes.
31. Using a vegetable peeler, shave reserved papaya into ribbons, pick leaves from reserved cilantro stems, and cut reserved lemon half into wedges.
32. Transfer fish to plates and top with papaya and cilantro.
33. Mound yam chips next to fillets.
34. Serve with shito and lemon wedges alongside.

Recipe name: Drunk Apricot Shito Ghanaian Hot Pepper Sauce
Ingredients: '4 oz. dried apricots', ' cup dark rum or arrack', '1 Tbsp. unsalted butter', ' tsp. ground cinnamon', '3 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. coconut sugar or dark brown sugar', '2 grains of selim pods', ' tsp. cubeb pepper or Ashanti pepper (uziza)', ' cup peanut oil or vegetable oil', '1 medium white onion, finely chopped', '1 small red onion, thinly sliced', '2 garlic cloves, coarsely chopped', '1 1" piece ginger, peeled, finely grated (unpeeled if organic)', '1 Tbsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '1 tsp. chopped rosemary', '1 tsp. chopped thyme', ' tsp. dried prawn or shrimp powder', '1 tsp. okra salt or kosher salt, plus more', '2 Tbsp. tomato purAe', '3A4 Tbsp. dried chile powder', 'A cups low-sodium chicken broth', '1 Scotch bonnet or habanero chile, pierced a few times with a knife', 'A spice mill or mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Place apricots in a small bowl and pour in hot water to cover.
2. Let sit minutes, then drain and coarsely chop.
3. Return apricots to same bowl and add rum; stir to coat.
4. Chill at least minutes and up to 1 day.
5. Drain into a mesh sieve set over another small bowl or container.
6. Reserve apricots and rum separately.
7. Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
8. Add cinnamon, 3 Tbsp. coconut sugar, and reserved apricots.
9. Cook, stirring occasionally, until apricots are slightly softened and coated in caramel, 6A8 minutes.
10. Add reserved rum and cook, stirring often, until thickened and syrupy, about 5 minutes.
11. Let cool slightly. (Just FYI: You can eat these drunk apricots right away over vanilla ice cream and be very happy.) Toast grains of selim pods and cubeb peppers in a small skillet over medium-low heat, shaking occasionally, until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
12. Transfer to a spice mill or mortar and pestle and let cool.
13. Grind as well as you can.
14. The grains of selim wonAt completely break up, so cracked open but mostly whole is okayAyou just want to release some of their flavor.
15. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium.
16. Cook white and red onions, stirring occasionally, until softened and just starting brown, minutes.
17. Stir in ground grains of selim and cubeb pepper, garlic, ginger, and remaining 1 tsp. coconut sugar.
18. Cook, stirring occasionally, 5 minutes.
19. Add red pepper flakes, rosemary, thyme, prawn powder, and 1 tsp. salt to pot and cook, stirring occasionally, about 2 minutes.
20. Add drunk apricots along with any syrup and cook, stirring often, about 2 minutes.
21. Add tomato purAe to pot and cook, stirring occasionally, 4 minutes, then stir in chile powder.
22. Add broth and fresh chile.
23. Cover pot and cook, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking, until a loose dark brown paste forms, minutes.
24. The oil will rise to the surface when the sauce is ready.
25. Remove from heat; taste and season with more salt if needed.
26. If you like texture, leave as is, or blend with an immersion blender (or let it cool slightly and blend it in a blender) to make it smooth.
27. Let cool completely.
28. Spoon shito into jars or an airtight container with at least a " layer of oil on top.
29. Do ahead: Shito can made 1 week ahead.
30. Cover and chill.
31. Note: The amount of chile described in this recipe is what I would classify as pretty hotAsome West Africans might call this medium hot, some very hot.
32. Folks familiar with heat will enjoy this, but if you donAt like super-jacked heat you should probably err on the lower end of the range.
33. West African pantry staples can be found at Zoe's Ghana Kitchen.

Recipe name: Summer Bean Soup With Tomato Brown Butter
Ingredients: '8 oz. ( g) dried beans, such as flageolet or navy, or fresh shelling beans', '2 in. square dried kombu', '2 large tomatoes', '2 celery ribs', '1 carrot', '1 leek, white and light green parts only', '6 shallots, skins intact', '6 garlic cloves, minced', '4 oil-packed anchovy fillets', '6 Tbsp. ( ml) extra-virgin olive oil', '2 Tbsp. maple syrup', '1 Tbsp. kosher salt', '1 Tbsp. red pepper flakes', '1 tsp. whole-grain mustard', '8 oz. ( g) bacon', '8 oz. ( g) Romano beans or green beans, chopped into 1 in. pieces', '2 shallots, thinly sliced', '1 tsp. red pepper flakes', 'Zest and juice of 1 lemon', '1 garlic clove, grated', ' pickled green beans (see Basic Wet Salt Method, below)', '9 cherry tomatoes, quartered', '2 tsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 tsp. sherry vinegar', 'Tomato Brown Butter', 'Crumbled feta, parsley leaves, and marjoram leaves for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Combine the dried beans and kombu in a large container and cover with plenty of water to soak for at least 4 hours or overnight; skip this step if using fresh beans.
2. Drain the beans and kombu and add them to a large pot with enough water to cover by about 1 in. (if using fresh beans, add the kombu now).
3. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat; meanwhile, halve the tomatoes and cut the celery, carrot, and leek into about 3 in. pieces.
4. As soon as the water boils, decrease the heat to medium-low and add the vegetables to the pot, along with the whole shallots and minced garlic.
5. Continue to cook at a gentle simmer until the beans are very creamy, to minutes for dried beans and about half that for fresh.
6. Remove the pot from the heat and use a slotted spoon to reserve the tomatoes; remove and discard the other vegetables.
7. Set up a sieve over a heat proof container and strain the beans (there should be about 3 cups or g), reserving all the cooking liquid.
8. In a blender, combine 1 cup ( g) of the cooked beans with 2 cups ( ml) of the cooking liquid, plus the tomatoes, anchovies, oil, maple syrup, salt, red pepper flakes, and mustard.
9. PurAe until extremely smooth, pour into a large bowl, and fold in the reserved cooked beans by hand.
10. At this point, you can serve it right away or transfer to an airtight container and refrigerate for up to 3 days, gently rewarming over medium-low heat when youAre ready to serve.
11. While the bean soup is cooking, preheat the oven to .
12. Cut the bacon into in. lardons; reserve 3 whole beans for garnish and cut the rest into 2 to 3 in. pieces.
13. In a medium saucepan or Dutch oven, spread the bacon in an even layer and cook over medium-low heat until it starts to turn golden and crisp, about minutes.
14. Use a slotted spoon to remove the pieces but leave the fat behind.
15. Increase the heat slightly, and sautA the shallots until translucent, 5 to minutes.
16. Stir in the chopped beans, bacon, and red pepper flakes.
17. Cover with a lid or a tight layer of foil and bake until the beans are very soft, to minutes.
18. Stir in the lemon zest, juice, and garlic as soon as the beans come out of the oven and cover again; the residual steam will cook the garlic.
19. Thinly slice the pickled green beans on the bias.
20. Combine with the cherry tomatoes, oil, and vinegar.
21. To serve, ladle the bean soup into bowls with a big spoonful of the baked beans on top.
22. Garnish with the pickled green bean mix, a swirl of tomato brown butter, crumbled feta, and torn fresh herbs.
23. Leftovers can all be refrigerated in separate airtight containers, though the green bean salad is best on the day itAs made, when the beans are most crisp and the tomatoes are fresh.
24. This works for any amount of dense vegetables or dense, underripe fruit, either whole or cut up, such as carrots, turnips, beets, winter squash, onions, green beans, Brussels sprouts, green tomatoes, small apples, underripe peaches, and whole baby cabbages.
25. Peel off any tough skins, such as on beets, winter squash, and onions, and trim the tops.
26. Leave the vegetables whole or cut them into the desired finished shape.
27. Place the vegetables in a nonreactive container and pour water to completely submerge them; as you do so, keep track of how much water youAre adding since the amount of water will dictate the amount of salt (generally 3.5 percent salt by weight of the water for most vegetables, although cucumbers can handle 4 to 5 percent and the increased salt assists with keeping their texture crispy).
28. With a long wooden spoon, stir kosher salt into the brine (or add the salt, cap the jar, and give it a hearty shake) until the salt is dissolved.
29. Place a weight on top of the vegetables to keep them submerged in the brine.
30. Seal the container, using a lid with an airlock if you have one.
31. If you donAt have an airlock, youAll just need to open the container every few days or so to release carbon dioxide buildup and check for mold.
32. Place the container in a clean, low-light area with an ambient temperature of to until the pickles taste sour, about 3 weeks.
33. Refrigerate indefinitely.

Ingredients: '1 large tomato (about 8 oz. or g)', ' cup ( g) unsalted butter', 'Kosher salt', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Place the tomato in a blender and purAe, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve; you should get about cup ( ml).
2. Add the butter to a large skillet and gently cook over medium heat until it starts to brown and smell nutty.
3. Stir in the smooth tomato purAe (be careful; it might spatter) and simmer for another minute or so.
4. YouAre looking to slightly cook the purAe but keep its brightness.
5. The mix wonAt emulsify, which is fine.
6. Season with salt and pepper and set aside.

Ingredients: '3 acorn squash, unpeeled, halved through the stem, and seeded', '3 tablespoons unsalted butter, diced', '3 tablespoons pure maple syrup, plus extra for serving', 'Good olive oil', 'Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper', 'Flaked sea salt, such as Maldon, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. Place the squash, cut sides up, on a sheet pan.
3. Place tablespoon butter and tablespoon maple syrup in the cavity of each squash.
4. Brush the cut sides with olive oil and sprinkle the squash with 3 teaspoons kosher salt and 1 teaspoon pepper.
5. Roast for to minutes, depending on the size of the squash, until tender when pierced with a small knife.
6. Place the squash on a serving platter.
7. If the halves are too large for one serving, cut each piece in half through the stem.
8. Drizzle lightly with extra maple syrup, sprinkle with sea salt, and serve hot.
9. Acorn squash is hard to cut.
10. To halve a whole squash, plunge the blade of a large chefAs knife into the side of the squash as far as it will go.
11. Holding the handle of the knife, bang the squash (with the knife in it) on the board until the blade cuts all the way through.

Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 serrano chile or jalapeAo, thinly sliced', '1 (3") piece fresh ginger, peeled, cut into matchsticks', '4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '2 scallions, thinly sliced, plus more for serving', ' tsp. ground turmeric', '4 ears of corn, shucked, kernels removed', '1 cup cooked grains (such as freekeh, farro, or quinoa)', '1 cup unsweetened coconut milk, plus more for serving', 'Kosher salt', '4 Tbsp. store-bought crispy onions or shallots (such as Lars Own, FrenchAs, or Maesri)', 'Lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium.
2. Cook chile, ginger, garlic, and 2 sliced scallions, tossing, until softened and fragrant, about 3 minutes.
3. Add turmeric and cook, stirring frequently, just until darkened and fragrant, about seconds.
4. Add corn and increase heat to medium-high.
5. Cook, tossing occasionally, until corn is beginning to lightly brown, about 5 minutes.
6. Add grains to skillet and cook, tossing often, until heated through and beginning to crisp around the edges, about 2 minutes.
7. Add cup coconut milk; season with salt.
8. Bring to a simmer and cook, adding 1A2 Tbsp. water if needed to loosen, until flavors have come together, about 3 minutes.
9. Divide corn mixture between plates or shallow bowls and drizzle with more coconut milk.
10. Top with crispy onions and more scallion.
11. Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

Ingredients: ' pounds mixed heirloom tomatoes, cored, sliced (") thick', '3 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil', ' teaspoon kosher salt, divided, plus more', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, plus more', '6 garlic cloves, peeled', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter', '9 ounces Stoned Wheat Thins or other whole grain crackers', '2 large eggs', ' ounces Taleggio cheese, rind removed, coarsely grated (about cups)', ' ounces finely crumbled Parmesan (about cup)', ' cup mayonnaise', '1 tablespoon chopped thyme, plus teaspoon leaves', '1 small shallot, thinly sliced', 'Special Equipment: A (9"-diameter) springform pan'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Arrange tomatoes on a rimmed baking sheet, covering entire surface (itAs okay if they overlap).
3. Drizzle with oil; season with salt and pepper.
4. Roast until tomatoes begin to look dry on top, minutes.
5. Let cool.
6. Meanwhile, cook garlic and butter in a small saucepan over medium-low heat, swirling often, until butter foams and milk solids turn golden, 5A7 minutes.
7. Strain garlic butter through a fine-mesh sieve into a heatproof measuring cup; transfer garlic and toasty bits to a cutting board.
8. Finely chop and set aside.
9. Reduce oven temperature to .
10. Pulse crackers and tsp. salt in a food processor until fine crumbs form (you should have about 2 cups).
11. Add eggs and 6 Tbsp. garlic butter and pulse until mixture is the consistency of wet sand; set remaining garlic butter aside.
12. Transfer to springform pan.
13. Using a flat-bottomed measuring cup, press crumbs firmly onto bottom and " up sides of pan.
14. Bake crust until edge is just starting to take on color, minutes.
15. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let crust cool.
16. Mix Taleggio, Parmesan, mayonnaise, chopped thyme, remaining tsp. salt, remaining tsp. pepper, and reserved chopped garlic in a medium bowl.
17. Gently dollop half of cheese mixture over bottom of crust, then spread into an even layer with an offset spatula (donAt press too hard or you will break the crust).
18. Layer half of tomato slices over and press down to even out layers.
19. Repeat with remaining cheese mixture and tomatoes.
20. Brush remaining garlic butter over tomatoes.
21. Top with shallot and thyme leaves.
22. Set pan on a rimmed baking sheet and bake pie until filling is bubbling vigorously and crust is browned, minutes.
23. Transfer to rack and let cool 1 hour before serving.
24. Do Ahead: Pie can be made 1 day ahead.
25. Cover loosely and chill.

Recipe name: Paneer With Burst Cherry Tomato Sauce
Ingredients: '6 scallions, thinly sliced', '1 (1") piece ginger, scrubbed, cut into matchsticks', '2 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '1 tsp. coriander seeds', '1 tsp. cumin seeds', ' tsp. black mustard seeds', ' tsp. Kashmiri chile powder or tsp. cayenne powder', ' tsp. ground turmeric', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '8 oz. paneer, sliced into ("-thick) planks, patted dry', 'Kosher salt', ' oz. cherry tomatoes (about 2 cups)', '4 oz. sugar snap peas (about 1 cup), strings removed, halved on a diagonal if large', 'Pinch of sugar (optional)', ' cup (lightly packed) mint leaves', 'Cooked rice (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Toss scallions, ginger, and garlic in a medium bowl to combine.
2. Using a mortar and pestle or the side of a chefAs knife, coarsely crush coriander seeds and add to vegetables along with cumin seeds, black mustard seeds, chile powder and turmeric; set aside.
3. Heat 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
4. Arrange paneer in a single layer in pan and cook until golden brown underneath, about 1 minute. (If paneer is sticking, just give cheese a little more time to brown.
5. It will release from pan once it is sufficiently crisped.) Using a slotted spatula or spoon, transfer paneer to a plate, leaving as much oil behind in pan as possible.
6. Turn browned side up and season generously with salt.
7. Reduce heat to medium and add remaining 1 Tbsp. oil to same skillet.
8. Cook reserved scallion mixture, stirring often, until scallions are wilted and mustard seeds begin to pop, about 2 minutes.
9. Add tomatoes, sugar snap peas, and 1 cup water to skillet and season with salt.
10. Increase heat to medium-high and bring to a simmer.
11. Gently smash each tomato open with a spatula (you just want to break them open a little) and simmer until tomato juices thicken slightly, about 3 minutes (it should still be pretty brothy and light).
12. Taste sauce and add more salt if needed.
13. If your tomatoes arenAt super sweet, you may need to add a pinch of sugar at this point to balance their acidity.
14. Add paneer back to pan and simmer until warmed through.
15. Remove pan from heat and toss in mint.
16. Serve with cooked rice.

Ingredients: '2 skinless, boneless chicken breasts ( lb. total)', '3 Tbsp. kosher salt, plus more', '3 scallions, thinly sliced on a deep diagonal', '3 Persian cucumbers', ' cup toasted sesame seeds, divided', '2 Tbsp. fresh lime juice', '2 Tbsp. tahini', '1 Tbsp. soy sauce', '1 tsp. honey', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', ' head of romaine or green or red leaf lettuce or 1 head of Little Gem lettuce', 'Big handful of cilantro leaves with tender stems', ' lime (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Bring chicken, 3 Tbsp. kosher salt, and 5 cups water to a bare simmer in a medium pot over medium heat (chicken should be submerged).
2. Be patientAthis will take a little while.
3. Once liquid begins to simmer, reduce heat to low and cook (water should not be simmering at all now) until juices run clear when thickest part of chicken is pierced, minutes.
4. Transfer chicken to a medium bowl; discard poaching liquid.
5. Let chicken sit until cool enough to handle, then shred meat into big pieces; set aside.
6. Meanwhile, place scallions in a small bowl and pour in very cold water to cover; let soak minutes.
7. Drain scallions in a mesh sieve or colander, then rinse under cold running water, swishing vigorously to rinse and to encourage them to curl up.
8. Shake off as much water as possible and place in a large bowl; set aside.
9. Working one at a time, slice cucumbers in half crosswise, then slice each piece in half lengthwise.
10. Place quarters cut side down and slice in half lengthwise again to create 8 pieces total per cucumber.
11. Add to bowl with reserved chicken.
12. Set 1 Tbsp. sesame seeds aside in a small bowl for serving.
13. Crush remaining sesame seeds in a mortar and pestle until some of the seeds have turned to powder, but with plenty of whole seeds left. (Alternatively, you can pulse seeds a few times in a food processor or spice mill, then transfer to a medium bowl before proceeding).
14. Add lime juice, tahini, soy sauce, honey, and red pepper flakes and mash to create a paste.
15. Add cup water and whisk to combine (if you donAt have a small enough whisk, work in with the pestle).
16. Taste and season with salt.
17. Add lettuce and cilantro to bowl with reserved scallions.
18. Pour half of dressing over and toss to coat.
19. Arrange on a platter.
20. Pour remaining dressing over chicken and cucumbers, toss to coat, and arrange over greens.
21. Top salad with reserved 1 Tbsp. sesame seeds and squeeze juice from lime half over.

Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '3 garlic cloves, crushed', '6 Tbsp. double-concentrated tomato paste', ' cup pure maple syrup', ' cup soy sauce', ' cup Worcestershire sauce', '2 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '4 tsp. Sriracha or other hot sauce', 'Vegetable oil (for grill)', '1 (-lb.) whole chicken, cut into 6A8 pieces, or whatever bone-in cut of chicken you feel like cooking', 'Kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a small saucepan over medium.
2. Cook garlic, turning occasionally, until golden brown, about 4 minutes.
3. Add tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly and scraping bottom of pan, until slightly darkened, about 3 minutes.
4. Add maple syrup, soy sauce, Worcestershire sauce, vinegar, and Sriracha.
5. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until flavors have come together, about 1 minute.
6. Remove from heat.
7. Transfer about half of barbecue sauce to a heatproof bowl and set aside for serving.
8. Prepare a grill for medium-high indirect heat (for a charcoal grill, bank coals on one side of grill; for a gas grill, leave one or two burners off); oil grate.
9. Season chicken generously with salt.
10. Grill chicken over direct heat, turning every minute, until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
11. Move chicken over indirect heat.
12. Cover, positioning vent (if your grill has one) over chicken, and continue to grill, keeping grill covered as much as possible and turning every 5 minutes or so, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part registers , minutes depending on the size of the pieces.
13. Baste chicken with barbecue sauce and grill, turning and basting occasionally, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part registers for breast and for dark meat, about minutes longer.
14. Arrange chicken on a platter.
15. Serve with reserved barbecue sauce alongside.
16. Do Ahead: Barbecue sauce can be made 2 weeks ahead.
17. Transfer to an airtight container; cover and chill.

Recipe name: Spiced Lamb And Dill Yogurt Pasta
Ingredients: '3 large egg yolks', '2 cups kefir (cultured milk) or plain whole-milk yogurt', '3 cups (lightly packed) dill fronds with tender stems (about 1 large bunch), divided', '3 garlic cloves, divided', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter', ' cup pine nuts or slivered almonds', ' cup dried currants or raisins', '1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '1 Tbsp. ground cumin', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper', '1 lb. ground lamb', '1 lb. orecchiette or other short pasta', ' lemon'
Instructions:
1. Combine egg yolks, kefir, and cups dill in a blender; finely grate in 1 garlic clove and blend until smooth.
2. Set purAe aside.
3. Finely chop remaining cups dill and set aside separately.
4. Melt butter over medium heat in a large skillet.
5. Add pine nuts and cook, stirring often, until golden brown, about 2 minutes.
6. Add dried currants and cook, stirring often, until plump, about 1 minute.
7. Scrape nut mixture into a small bowl; season with salt.
8. Wipe out skillet and heat over medium-high.
9. Stir together cumin, pepper, and 1 tsp. salt in a small bowl.
10. Place lamb in pan and use a sturdy spatula to aggressively flatten (like youAre making smash burgers); sprinkle spice mixture over.
11. Cook, undisturbed, until lamb is well browned and crisp underneath, about 4 minutes.
12. Hold back meat and drain off all of the fat.
13. Break up meat into small pieces and mix in reserved nut mixture.
14. Finely grate in remaining 2 garlic cloves and add reserved chopped dill.
15. Cook, stirring, until herbs are wilted, about 1 minute.
16. Set aside until pasta is ready.
17. Meanwhile, cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until 1 minute shy of al dente (pasta will finish cooking in the sauce).
18. Drain pasta and return to pot.
19. Pour reserved purAe over pasta and set over medium heat.
20. Cook, stirring constantly, until sauce thickens enough to cling to pasta and just comes to a simmer, about 3 minutes.
21. Remove from heat; finely grate zest from lemon half over pasta, then squeeze in juice.
22. Season with salt.
23. Divide pasta among bowls and top with lamb mixture.

Ingredients: '1 cup short-grain sushi rice', '8 scallions', '1 (1") piece ginger, peeled, finely grated', '3 Tbsp. vegetable oil, divided', ' lb. skinless, boneless cod, cut into 2A pieces', 'Kosher salt', '2 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', '1 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 tsp. sugar', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces'
Instructions:
1. Rinse rice in several changes of cold water in a medium bowl until water runs clear.
2. Drain well, then transfer to a small saucepan.
3. Pour in cups cold water and bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
4. Give rice a stir so grains donAt stick to the bottom.
5. Cover pot and reduce heat to lowest setting.
6. Cook, undisturbed, until water is evaporated and rice is tender, minutes.
7. Remove from heat and let sit, still covered, minutes.
8. Meanwhile, trim dark green tops from scallions and thinly slice; set aside.
9. Thinly slice white and pale green parts of scallions of a deep diagonal.
10. Set aside separately.
11. Mix ginger and 2 Tbsp. oil in a medium bowl.
12. Add cod and season with salt and sprinkle all over with pepper.
13. Toss to coat.
14. Heat remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
15. Cook cod, undisturbed, until golden underneath, about 2 minutes.
16. Turn over and scatter reserved white and pale green scallion parts on top.
17. Cook, shaking the pan a few times, until fish is cooked through and some scallions are caramelized and some are just slightly softened, about 2 minutes.
18. Remove from heat.
19. Stir vinegar and sugar in a small bowl until sugar dissolves.
20. Mix into rice.
21. Add butter and reserved dark green scallion tops and gently stir until butter is melted.
22. Season with salt.
23. Transfer rice to a platter and top with fish; pour any pan juices over.

Recipe name: Salmon Burgers With Ginger And Quick-Pickled Cucumbers
Ingredients: ' lb. boneless, skinless center-cut salmon, patted dry, cut into 2" pieces', '5 scallions', '1 (1") piece ginger, peeled, finely grated', '1 garlic clove, finely grated', '2 tablespoons plus A cup mayonnaise', '1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '1 tsp. toasted sesame oil', '4 tsp. unseasoned rice vinegar, divided', '3 medium Persian cucumbers, shaved lengthwise', ' serrano chile, very thinly sliced', '1 tsp. sugar', ' cup (or more) vegetable oil', ' cup rice flour', '2 cups tender herbs (such as mint andor cilantro)', ' cup trimmed watercress', '2 tsp. toasted sesame seeds (optional)', '4 brioche buns, lightly toasted'
Instructions:
1. Process one third of salmon (about 8 oz.) in a food processor, scraping down sides as needed, until very smooth and paste-like.
2. Add remaining salmon and pulse until pieces are no bigger than ," 4A5 times (be careful not to make it too smooth).
3. Transfer to a large bowl.
4. Remove dark green tops from scallions and finely chop.
5. Thinly slice white and pale green parts and set aside.
6. Add chopped scallion greens, ginger, garlic, 2 Tbsp. mayonnaise, and 1 tsp. salt to salmon and gently mix to combine.
7. Form mixture into 4 patties about " thick.
8. Transfer to a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet and cover with plastic wrap.
9. Chill at least 1 hour or up to 3 hours.
10. Meanwhile, mix sesame oil, 1 tsp. vinegar, remaining A cup mayonnaise, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl; set aside.
11. Toss cucumbers with a pinch of salt in another small bowl.
12. Massage with your hands for a few minutes to soften and expel liquid; discard liquid.
13. Add chile, sugar, and 2 tsp. vinegar and toss to coat.
14. Cover and chill pickles until ready to assemble burgers.
15. Heat vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
16. Remove salmon patties from refrigerator and sprinkle with flour just to coat the outside (you wonAt need all of it).
17. The patties will be a little loose but you can always pat them back together with your hands before they hit the pan.
18. Working in batches and adding more oil in between batches if needed, cook patties until golden brown, about 3 minutes per side (watch carefully; you donAt want to overcook).
19. Toss herbs, watercress, sesame seeds (if using), reserved white and pale green parts of scallions, remaining 1 tsp. vinegar, and a pinch of salt in a medium bowl.
20. Build burgers with buns, patties, herb mixture, pickles, and reserved sauce.

Ingredients: ' guajillo chiles, seeds removed', '3 morita chiles, seeds removed', '3 lb. skinless striped bass or halibut fillets, cut crosswise into ("-wide) pieces', ' cup fresh orange juice', ' cup store-bought or homemade achiote paste', '5 garlic cloves, 4 whole, 1 finely chopped', ' cup plus 3 Tbsp. fresh lime juice', '1 Tbsp. plus tsp. kosher salt', ' red onion, finely chopped', '1 habanero chile, finely chopped', '2 cups finely chopped pineapple (from about medium pineapple)', '3 Tbsp. chopped cilantro, plus leaves for serving', 'Vegetable oil (for grill)', ' corn tortillas', 'Lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Bring guajillo and morita chiles and cups water to a boil in a medium saucepan.
2. Cover, remove from heat, and let sit minutes to allow chiles to soften.
3. Place fish in a large bowl or baking dish.
4. Transfer chile mixture and soaking liquid to a blender.
5. Add orange juice, achiote paste, 4 garlic cloves, cup lime juice, and 1 Tbsp. salt and purAe until smooth, about 2 minutes.
6. Set aside 1 cup chile sauce, uncovered, at room temperature for serving.
7. Pour remaining sauce over fish and toss to coat.
8. Cover and chill at least 1 hour and up to 3 hours.
9. Meanwhile, mix onion, habanero chile, pineapple, 3 Tbsp. cilantro, remaining finely chopped garlic clove, 3 Tbsp. lime juice, and tsp. salt in a medium bowl.
10. Cover salsa and chill until ready to use.
11. Prepare a grill for medium-high heat; thoroughly clean grate and brush with oil.
12. Working in batches if necessary, grill fish, leaving a few inches of space between each piece, until marinade is dried and beginning to char, about 2 minutes.
13. Using a fish spatula, release fish from grate, turn, and continue to grill until charred on second side and flesh flakes easily with a fork, about 3 minutes more.
14. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest minutes.
15. Grill tortillas until just beginning to brown in spots, about seconds per side.
16. Top each tortilla with a piece of fish, then a schmear of reserved chile sauce, a spoonful of pineapple salsa, and some cilantro leaves.
17. Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.
18. Do Ahead: Pineapple salsa can be made 4 hours ahead.
19. Keep chilled.

Ingredients: '2 cups raw nuts (such as almonds, walnuts, pistachios, pecans, andor blanched hazelnuts)', ' cup raw pumpkin seeds', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for pan', '2 cups old-fashioned oats, divided', ' cups dried fruit (such as tart cherries, raisins, currants, dates, apricots, andor blueberries)', ' cup unsalted or low-salt natural peanut butter', ' cup plus 2 Tbsp. honey', '2 tsp. kosher salt', '2 tsp. vanilla extract', '1 large egg white'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Toast nuts pumpkin seeds on a rimmed baking sheet until golden brown or slightly darkened in color, minutes.
3. Transfer to a food processor and let sit until cool enough to handle.
4. Reduce oven temperature to .
5. Lightly oil 13x9" pan and line with parchment paper, leaving overhang on both long sides.
6. Lightly oil parchment.
7. Add 1 cup oats to nut mixture in food processor and pulse until nuts are a sandy consistency and no whole oats remain.
8. Transfer to a large bowl.
9. Pulse any dried fruit larger than a raisin in food processor (no need to wipe out) until about the size of chocolate chips.
10. Transfer to bowl with nut mixture.
11. Add remaining 1 cup oats and stir to combine, breaking up any fruit that is stuck together.
12. Add remaining 3 Tbsp. oil and stir to coat.
13. Add peanut butter, but donAt stir it in; set aside.
14. Bring honey, salt, and vanilla to a boil in a medium saucepan over medium heat (it will bubble up).
15. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, stirring occasionally, until it is foaming vigorously and smells like caramel, about 3 minutes.
16. Immediately pour honey mixture over peanut butter in reserved bowl (it will melt the peanut butter).
17. Stir to combine, making sure to aggressively break up any clumps.
18. Using a fork or small whisk, lightly beat egg white in a small bowl to thin out.
19. Add to oat mixture and mix until egg white is no longer visible.
20. Scrape oat mixture into prepared pan and press firmly into an even layer. (Be sure to really compact the mixture into the panAit helps to use the bottom of a heavy glass or metal measuring cup.) Bake granola bars until deep golden brown and no longer sticky or wet, - minutes.
21. Let cool 5 minutes, then lightly score into long rectangles (1 cut lengthwise and 7 cuts crosswise will make bars that are about 6A12x1").
22. Let cool completely in pan, then use parchment to hoist bars out onto a cutting board.
23. Using a sharp chefAs knife, cut along marks to separate bars.
24. Do Ahead: Bars can be made 1 week ahead.
25. Store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: ' cup whole-milk yogurt', '2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', ' tsp. sugar', 'Kosher salt', '1 cup red lentils (masoor dal)', '1 medium zucchini (about 5 oz.)', ' medium onion, thinly sliced', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided, plus more', ' tsp. Kashmiri chile powder or tsp. cayenne pepper', ' tsp. ground turmeric', '1 cup parsley leaves with tender stems', '1 Tbsp. finely grated lemon zest', '1 cup ghee or neutral vegetable oil'
Instructions:
1. Whisk yogurt, lemon juice, and sugar in a small bowl to combine.
2. Season with salt.
3. Do Ahead: Yogurt can be made 1 day ahead.
4. Cover and chill.
5. Rinse lentils, then soak in 2 cups water at room temperature at least 1 hour and up to hours. (Soaking the lentils makes them tender and a lot easier to blend.) Meanwhile, trim ends of zucchini and cut crosswise into 3 pieces about 2" long.
6. Cut each piece lengthwise into "-thick planks.
7. Stack a few planks and slice lengthwise into "-thick matchsticks (about the size of a skinny french fry).
8. Transfer zucchini to a colander set in a medium bowl.
9. Add onion and 1 tsp. salt and toss to combine.
10. Let sit until vegetables look wilted and softened and about 1 Tbsp. liquid has released into bowl, minutes to 2 hours.
11. Gently pat dry with paper towels to remove any excess moisture.
12. Drain lentils and transfer to a food processor.
13. Add chile powder, turmeric, and tsp. salt.
14. Pulse, scraping down sides, until a purAe forms.
15. Transfer to a medium bowl and add zucchini and onion, parsley, and lemon zest.
16. Toss with a rubber spatula to combine.
17. Line a rimmed baking sheet with paper towels; set a wire rack inside.
18. Heat ghee in a medium cast-iron skillet over medium-high until a pinch of batter added to the pan sizzles and sputters.
19. Using a large spoon, scoop out about cup batter, then use a second large spoon dipped in the hot ghee to carefully scrape into pan; flatten with ghee-coated spoon.
20. Repeat to make 3 more fritters.
21. Fry until deep golden brown underneath, about 3 minutes.
22. Turn over with a wide slotted spatula and fry until deep golden brown on the second side, about 3 minutes.
23. Transfer fritters to prepared rack to drain; season immediately with salt.
24. Repeat with remaining batter (you end up with fritters total).
25. Transfer fritters to a platter.
26. Serve with lemony yogurt alongside.

Ingredients: '1 cup vegetable oil', '2 cooking onions, finely sliced'
Instructions:
1. Heat oil in a wok or frying pan over medium heat until hot enough to fry.
2. Add one piece of onion to test the oil (it should begin to sizzle right away).
3. SautA onions for minutes, until golden brown.
4. Using a slotted spoon, transfer onions to a baking sheet lined with paper towels.
5. Spread onions out to drain and cool.
6. Store in an airtight container for up to 7 days.

Recipe name: Sour Cream And Onion Potato Salad
Ingredients: '2 lb. baby Yukon Gold potatoes', '1 cup kosher salt, plus more', ' cup sour cream', ' cup mayonnaise', '1 Tbsp. onion powder', '2 tsp. Dijon mustard', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', '1 garlic clove, finely grated', '1 bunch chives, thinly sliced, divided', ' small red onion, very thinly sliced through root end', ' cup sour cream and onion potato chips'
Instructions:
1. Place potatoes in a large pot and pour in 3 qt. water to cover.
2. Add 1 cup salt and bring water to a boil over medium-high heat. (Yes, this is a lot of salt, but itAs the right amount to ensure potatoes are seasoned through and through.) Reduce heat and simmer, adjusting heat as needed, until potatoes until fork-tender, minutes.
3. Scoop out cup potato cooking liquid and set aside.
4. Drain potatoes in a colander and let sit until cool enough to handle, about minutes.
5. Meanwhile, combine sour cream, mayonnaise, onion powder, mustard, and pepper in a large bowl and mix well to combine.
6. Add garlic and half of chives and mix just to incorporate.
7. Place red onion in a fine-mesh sieve and rinse for several seconds to take away some of its raw bite.
8. Pat dry with paper towels.
9. Mix half of onion into dressing; set aside remaining onion for serving.
10. Working one at a time, set potatoes on a flat surface and crush slightly with the palm of your hand.
11. Add to bowl with dressing along with cup potato cooking liquid.
12. Do this while the potatoes are still warm so that they can absorb all of the seasoning.
13. Don't worry if the dressing looks a little loose and watery at the beginningAit will all get absorbed and become creamier as it sits.
14. Taste and season with more salt or, if your dressing looks too thick, a splash of potato cooking liquid.
15. Top potatoes with remaining chives and reserved onion and crumble potato chips over.

Ingredients: '1 tsp. ground coriander', '1 tsp. ground cumin', ' tsp. ground turmeric', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '7 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '6 garlic cloves, lightly crushed', '4 skinless, boneless chicken thighs, cut into (") pieces', 'Kosher salt', '2 Tbsp. fresh lime juice', '2 ripe avocados, sliced', '2 small ripe mangoes, sliced', ' red onion, thinly sliced', '6 cups torn Little Gem or green lettuce leaves', '1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved', 'Flaky sea salt, mild red pepper flakes, and basil andor mint leaves (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Mix coriander, cumin, termeric, and red pepper flakes in a small bowl; set aside.
2. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium.
3. Add garlic and cook, tossing occasionally, until golden brown all over, about 5 minutes.
4. Increase heat to medium-high.
5. Season chicken with kosher salt and add to skillet.
6. Cook, tossing occasionally, until chicken is golden brown and nearly cooked through, about 3 minutes.
7. Remove from heat and sprinkle reserved spice mix over.
8. Toss several times to coat and let chicken sit in skillet until cooked through, about 1 minute longer.
9. Transfer to a plate and let cool slightly.
10. Whisk lime juice and remaining 5 Tbsp. oil in a small bowl; season dressing with kosher salt.
11. Arrange lettuce, chicken, avocado, mango, onion, and tomatoes on a platter.
12. Drizzle with dressing.
13. Sprinkle with sea salt and red pepper flakes and scatter herbs over.

Ingredients: ' cup golden raisins', '2 lb. thin-skinned cucumbers (such as Persian, English hothouse, andor kirby) cut into bite-size pieces', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided, plus more', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '3 Tbsp. pine nuts', ' cup Castelvetrano olives, pitted, divided', '1 large egg', '2 garlic cloves, finely grated', ' cup panko (Japanese breadcrumbs)', '3 Tbsp. finely chopped parsley', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes, plus more for serving', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '1 lb. ground pork', ' small red onion, very thinly sliced (use a mandoline if you have one)', '2 cups tender herb leaves (such as mint andor parsley)', '1 Tbsp. red wine vinegar', 'Plain whole-milk Greek yogurt (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Place racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat to .
2. Place raisins in a small bowl and pour in hot water to cover; let sit until ready to use.
3. Place cucumbers in a colander or sieve set in or over a large bowl; sprinkle with 1 tsp. salt and toss to combine.
4. Let sit while you make the meatballs. (It will seem like a lot of cucumbers at first, but they will shrink down as they drain.) Meanwhile, drizzle 2 Tbsp. oil over a large rimmed baking sheet.
5. Arrange pine nuts in an even layer on another rimmed baking sheet (use a quarter-sheet if you have one!).
6. Finely chop cup olives.
7. Transfer to a large bowl and add egg, garlic, panko, parsley, tsp. salt, and tsp. red pepper flakes; season with black pepper.
8. Drain raisins and add half to bowl; mix well to combine.
9. Add pork and gently mix with your hands to incorporate.
10. Working one at a time, scoop out heaping tablespoonfuls of pork mixture and form into balls (think about the size of a golf ball; you should get about ).
11. Arrange on oiled baking sheet, spacing 1" apart.
12. Turn to coat.
13. Bake meatballs, rotating baking sheet front to back halfway through, until browned on bottom and cooked through, minutes.
14. Toast pine nuts on bottom rack until golden brown, about 3 minutes.
15. Coarsely chop remaining cup olives.
16. Gather cucumbers into a clean kitchen towel and squeeze out any excess liquid into bowl (donAt worry if they get a little smashed); discard liquid.
17. Wipe out bowl and combine cucumbers, olives, pine nuts, onion, herbs, and remaining raisins in bowl; toss to combine.
18. Drizzle in vinegar and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil, season with salt and black pepper, and toss again to coat.
19. Swoosh a generous scoop of yogurt over each plate, then top with salad and meatballs.
20. Season with more black pepper and red pepper flakes if desired.

Ingredients: 'Softened butter (for the pan)', '8 whole graham crackers ( g)', ' digestive biscuits or 4 additional graham crackers ( g)', '2 ( oz397 g) cans sweetened condensed milk', '3 Hass avocados', '1 pound ( g) cream cheese, at room temperature', 'Pinch fine sea salt', '2 lemons', '2 limes'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to ().
2. Lightly butter the inside of a 9-inch (-cm) springform pan.
3. Make the crust: Crumble the crackers and cookies into a medium bowl.
4. Use a pestle or the bottom of a heavy bottle to crush them into fine crumbs.
5. Add A cup ( ml) of the condensed milk and stir until very well combined. (You can grind the crumbled graham crackers and mix them with the condensed milk in a food processor if you prefer.) Press the crumb mixture firmly and evenly into the bottom of the pan.
6. Bake until the crust looks a little darker and smells sweet and toasty, to minutes.
7. Cool the crust in the pan on a wire rack.
8. One at a time, cut an avocado in half lengthwise.
9. Twist the halves to separate them.
10. Hold the half with the pit in one hand.
11. Holding the knife in your other hand, rap the knife blade into the pit to lodge it there.
12. Twist the knife to loosen and remove the pit.
13. Use a large spoon to scoop the avocado flesh onto a chopping board, discarding the skins.
14. Coarsely chop the avocados.
15. Add the chopped avocados, cream cheese, remaining condensed milk, and salt to a food processor. (You can combine the filling in a blender instead but you may need to work in batches.) Grate the zest of 1 lemon and set aside.
16. Squeeze the zested lemon and the limes (you should have about A cup75 ml lemon), reserving the remaining lemon for garnish.
17. Add the juice to the avocados and process the mixture until smooth, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed.
18. Spread the filling in the cooled crust and sprinkle with the grated lemon zest.
19. Cover the pan and freeze until the filling is firm, at least 6 hours or preferably overnight.
20. Let the frozen cake stand at room temperature for to minutes before slicing.
21. Dipping a thin knife into hot water between each cut, slice the cake into wedges and serve, topping each serving with freshly grated lemon zest.

Ingredients: '2 cups sifted all purpose flour', ' cup unsweetened cocoa powder', '1 teaspoon baking soda', '1 teaspoon salt', '1 cups sugar', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, room temperature', ' cup vegetable oil', '2 large eggs', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract', ' cup buttermilk', '2 cups grated unpeeled zucchini (about 2 medium)', '1 6-ounce package (about 1 cup) semisweet chocolate chips', ' cup chopped walnuts'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Butter and flour x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
3. Sift flour, cocoa powder, baking soda and salt into medium bowl.
4. Beat sugar, butter and oil in large bowl until well blended.
5. Add eggs 1 at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. Beat in vanilla extract.
7. Mix in dry ingredients alternately with buttermilk in 3 additions each.
8. Mix in grated zucchini.
9. Pour batter into prepared pan.
10. Sprinkle chocolate chips and nuts over.
11. Bake cake until tester inserted into center comes out clean, about minutes.
12. Cool cake completely in pan.

Recipe name: Swiss Chard Pasta With Toasted Hazelnuts And Parmesan
Ingredients: ' cup hazelnuts', '1 pound bow tie pasta (farfalle)', '8 tablespoons unsalted butter, plus more if needed', '4 cloves of garlic, minced', 'Hefty pinch each of salt and freshly ground black pepper', 'Small pinch of crushed red pepper flakes', '1 bunch Swiss chard, stems finely chopped and greens thinly sliced', '4 ounces Parmesan cheese, shaved', '2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Add the hazelnuts to a small skillet over medium heat.
2. Toast them slowly, shaking the pan often, until lightly browned, 8 to minutes.
3. Remove them from the skillet, and when they are cool enough to handle, roughly chop the nuts.
4. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta until al dente.
5. Drain it, reserving cup of the cooking liquid and add it to a large bowl.
6. In a large skillet, heat the butter over medium-low heat.
7. Once the butter begins to foam, add the garlic and use a wooden spoon to stir the mixture constantly until the butter begins to brown and have a slight nutty aroma, about 5 minutes.
8. Add the salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.
9. Give the mixture a good stir, and then set it aside to infuse for about 5 minutes longer away from the heat.
10. Pour the butter mixture (scraping the garlic, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes) all over the warm pasta.
11. If the pasta feels a bit dry, add a touch of the reserved cooking liquid.
12. Toss to combine and set aside.
13. Set the same skillet (without cleaning it) over medium-high heat.
14. Add the chard stems and cook for 5 minutes.
15. Add the chard leaves and continue to cook, tossing the mixture every so often, until the greens begin to wilt and turn bright green, 3 to 5 minutes longer.
16. Add a touch more butter or oil to the pan if it dries out too much.
17. Add the Swiss chard and hazelnuts to the pasta and toss it all together.
18. Add the Parmesan shavings and the balsamic vinegar (if youAre using it); toss.
19. Taste for seasonings and add more salt and pepper if needed.
20. Serve warm or at room temperature.
21. Localize it: You can use kale instead of chard, and any nut will work in place of the hazelnuts.
22. Fresh out of balsamic vinegar Red wine vinegar or freshly squeezed lemon juice will work.

Recipe name: Sweet Corn Frittata With Cherry Tomato Compote
Ingredients: '2 tablespoons olive oil', '2 cloves of garlic, minced', '2 cups cherry tomatoes (any variety will work)', ' cup dry white wine', '8 to fresh basil leaves, thinly sliced', 'Healthy pinch of fine sea salt', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '2 tablespoons cooking fat', '1 bunch of scallions, white parts and green parts diced separately', '2 cloves of garlic, minced', '1 medium potato, scrubbed and cut into -inch chunks', '2 cups fresh or thawed frozen corn kernels', '4 large eggs', ' teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes', '4 ounces feta cheese, crumbled'
Instructions:
1. Heat the oil in a medium-sized skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the garlic and cook, stirring often, for about 1 minute.
3. Add the tomatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, for about 2 minutes longer.
4. Pour in the wine and cook until the wine is reduced by about half.
5. Add the basil, salt, and pepper and continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes burst and become fragrant, 5 to 8 minutes.
6. Remove from the heat and set aside the tomatoes in a bowl.
7. Position an oven rack about 3 inches from the top and preheat the broiler.
8. In a -inch cast-iron skillet over medium, heat 1 tablespoon of the cooking fat.
9. Add the diced white part of the scallions and the minced garlic and cook, stirring often, until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
10. Add the potato and continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until the potato softens, about minutes.
11. Add the corn and continue to cook for about 2 minutes longer.
12. In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, red pepper flakes, and half the feta cheese.
13. Pour the egg mixture over the veggie mixture in the skillet and top with the remainder of the cheese.
14. Stir in the potato mixture and the scallion greens.
15. Wipe out the skillet and, over medium-high, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of cooking fat.
16. Cook the frittata, lifting up the cooked egg around the edges with a rubber spatula to let the uncooked eggs flow underneath, 2 to 3 minutes.
17. Reduce the heat to medium and cook, giving the pan a shake now and again, until the eggs are mostly set but the center is still slightly jiggly, 3 to 5 minutes more.
18. Transfer the frittata to the oven and broil until the top is golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes (keep a close eye on the frittata, as it can easily burn).
19. Serve topped with the tomato compote.

Recipe name: Tomato Pie With Sour Cream Crust
Ingredients: '2 cups all-purpose flour, plus a little extra for dusting', ' teaspoon salt', '2 teaspoons dried parsley', ' tablespoons (1 stick plus 5 tablespoons) cold unsalted butter, diced', '6 tablespoons sour cream or plain full-fat yogurt', '1 tablespoon white wine vinegar', ' cup ice water', '2 tablespoons cooking fat', '1 large onion, thinly sliced', ' pounds mixed heirloom tomatoes (mix of full size and cherries)', ' cup hard salty cheese, such as Manchego or Parmesan', ' cup shredded mozzarella cheese', ' cup mayonnaise', '3 tablespoons chopped basil leaves', '3 tablespoons chopped parsley', '1 teaspoon chopped thyme', 'Salt and freshly ground black pepper', '1 egg, beaten', '1 tablespoon milk, cream, or water'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. In a large, wide bowl, combine the flour, salt, and parsley.
3. Add the butter pieces and, using a pastry blender or your fingertips, cut them up and into the flour mixture until it resembles little pebbles.
4. Keep breaking up the bits of butter until the texture is very coarse.
5. In a small dish, whisk together the sour cream, vinegar, and water, and combine it with the butter-flour mixture.
6. Using a spatula or wooden spoon, stir the wet and the dry together until a shaggy dough forms.
7. If needed, get your hands into the bowl to knead it a few times into one big ball.
8. Pat the dough into a disk, wrap it in plastic, and chill it in the fridge for 1 hour or up to 2 days.
9. In a large skillet over medium, heat 1 tablespoon of the cooking fat.
10. Add the onion and cook, stirring, until golden, about minutes.
11. Let cool.
12. Meanwhile, thinly slice the tomatoes; toss them with 1 teaspoon salt and let them drain in a colander for minutes, tossing them occasionally.
13. In a bowl, combine the cheeses, mayo, 2 tablespoons each of the chopped basil and parsley, the thyme, a hefty pinch each of salt and pepper, and the sautAed onion.
14. Sprinkle a little flour in the bottom of a -inch well-seasoned cast-iron skillet.
15. Set it aside.
16. On a lightly floured surface, roll the dough into a -inch circle.
17. Fit it into the prepared skillet, allowing the edges to extend over the sides of the pan.
18. Spread the cheese mixture evenly over the bottom of the dough.
19. Arrange the tomatoes on top.
20. Gently fold the overhanging pie crust over the tomatoes.
21. It doesnAt have to be perfect.
22. Drizzle the pie with the remaining 1 tablespoon cooking fat and season with pepper.
23. In a small bowl, whisk together the beaten egg and milk.
24. Brush the crust with this egg wash, place the cast-iron pan on a rimmed baking sheet (to protect the bottom of your oven from any overflowing filling), and bake the pie until the tomatoes are browned, to minutes.
25. Top the pie with the remaining 1 tablespoon each basil and parsley.
26. Localize it: Swap out any of the herbs or cheeses.
27. The Manchego in this recipe is salty, and the mozzarella melts really well, so just keep that in mind when youAre doing your swaps.

Recipe name: Pork Chops And Padraa3N Chiles En Escabaa Che
Ingredients: '2 " bone-in pork chops (about 1 lb. total)', '6 tsp. kosher salt, divided', '1 tsp. coriander seeds', '2 cups small PadrA3n chiles or shishito peppers, stems trimmed of dry ends but not completely cut off', '1 small red onion, halved through root end, sliced " thick', '2 small garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '8 oz. small carrots (about 6), tops trimmed, scrubbed, sliced " thick on a diagonal, or 8 oz. large carrots (about 2), tops trimmed, peeled, halved, sliced " thick on a diagonal', '3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar', '1 Tbsp. sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar', '1 Tbsp. honey', '1 tsp. paprika', '5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '3 Tbsp. coarsely chopped oregano', 'A spice mill or a mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Season each pork chop all over with 1 tsp. salt and let sit at least 1 hour or chill on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet, uncovered, up to 2 days.
2. Preheat oven to .
3. If pork chops have been chilling in the fridge, let sit at room temperature while you make the escabA che.
4. Finely grind coriander seeds in a spice mill or with a mortar and pestle; set aside.
5. Heat a dry large skillet over high.
6. Add chiles in a single layer and cook, turning with tongs or a large spoon, until evenly blistered on all sides and deeply browned in spots, 5A7 minutes.
7. Transfer chiles to a small pot; reserve skillet.
8. Add red onion, garlic, carrots, red wine vinegar, sherry vinegar, honey, paprika, reserved ground coriander, 4 Tbsp. oil, remaining 4 tsp. salt, and 2 Tbsp. water to pot.
9. Set over low heat, cover, and bring liquid to a gentle simmer.
10. Cook, stirring vegetables often to keep submerged, until just-cooked through and still al dente, about minutes.
11. Remove escabA che from heat and stir in oregano.
12. Heat remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in reserved skillet over high.
13. Arrange pork chops in pan so bone ends are in the center (this part cooks slower and this will help with even browning) and press down on pork chops with tongs to ensure surface is making good contact with the pan.
14. Cook until golden underneath, about 3 minutes.
15. Turn pork chops over and press down on them again.
16. Transfer to oven and roast until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part registers for medium (you should still have a little pink flesh; temperature should rise to about when the pork chops rest), 5A7 minutes.
17. Transfer pork chops to a cutting board and let rest minutes.
18. To serve, transfer pork chops to plates and spoon escabA che over.
19. Do ahead: EscabA che can be made 2 weeks ahead.
20. Transfer to an airtight container and let cool.
21. Cover and chill.

Ingredients: '4 cups Dashi', ' cup doenjang (Korean fermented soybean paste)', '1 tablespoon gochujang (Korean red chili paste)', '1 tablespoon gochugaru (Korean red chili flakes)', '6 garlic cloves, minced', '2 tablespoons soy sauce, or more to taste', '2 medium onions, cut into 1-inch dice', '2 medium green zucchini, cut into 1-inch chunks', '1 red Korean chili pepper or other long hot chili, thinly sliced on the bias', '1 green Korean chili pepper or other long hot chili, thinly sliced on the bias', ' pound brisket, sliced A inch thick (you can ask your butcher to slice it) and cut into 2- to 3-inch strips', '1 pound soft or silken tofu, homemade (page ) or store-bought, cut into 1-inch cubes', ' bunch scallions, cut into 1-inch batons'
Instructions:
1. Put the dashi in a stockpot or other large pot set over medium-high heat and bring to a simmer.
2. Whisk in the doenjang and cook for minutes to bring out its natural sweetness.
3. Add the gochujang and simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Add the gochugaru, garlic, and soy sauce and simmer for another minutes.
5. Add the onions and simmer for 5 minutes.
6. Add the zucchini and simmer for 5 minutes. (Adding the vegetables at different times will ensure that they will all be fully cooked at the end.) Add the chili peppers, beef, and tofu and simmer for 5 minutes.
7. Taste and adjust the seasoning with more soy sauce if necessary.
8. Remove the pot from the heat, add the scallions, put on the lid, and let the stew rest for at least minutes, and up to 2 hours, before serving.
9. Serve with steamed rice and your favorite banchan, as any and all pair well with this stew.

Recipe name: Dakgangjeong Aa A Korean Sweet Crunchy Fried Chicken
Ingredients: '2 pounds chicken wingettes or dumettes or small pieces of chicken (see headnote)', ' teaspoon kosher salt', ' teaspoon ground black pepper', ' cup potato starch', 'Vegetable oil', 'A cup toasted peanuts (optional)', ' cup rice syrup or honey', '3 tablespoons soy sauce', '2 tablespoons brown or white sugar', '2 teaspoons white vinegar', '2 teaspoons yellow mustard', '2 tablespoons vegetable oil', '3 garlic cloves, mined', '1 teaspoon minced peeled ginger', '8 small dried red chili peppers', '2 teaspoons toasted sesame seeds', '2 to 3 teaspoons crushed red pepper flakes (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Mix the chicken pieces, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl.
2. Transfer to a large zipper-lock bag, add the potato starch, close the bag, and mix well by flipping the bag over and back again until the chicken is well coated.
3. Place a large mesh strainer over a bowl.
4. Heat 2 inches vegetable oil in a large, deep pan or wok over medium-high heat until it reaches about , 8 to minutes.
5. If you donAt have a thermometer, test it by dipping a tip of a chicken piece into the oil.
6. If it bubbles, itAs ready.
7. Carefully add the chicken to the oil one piece at a time, working in batches to avoid overcrowding.
8. Deep-fry, turning the chicken with tongs, until all sides are light golden brown and crunchy, to minutes.
9. As each piece is done, transfer it to the strainer.
10. Once the chicken has drained, transfer it to a large bowl.
11. Repeat with the rest of the chicken, making sure to bring the oil back up to between batches.
12. Return the oil to over high heat and carefully add all the chickenAthereAs no need to work in batches this time.
13. The chicken will look a little soggy at first.
14. Deep-fry, turning occasionally, until all the chicken pieces are dark golden brown and very crunchy, another to minutes.
15. Transfer the chicken pieces to the strainer or a rack to drain, then place in a large bowl.
16. If using the peanuts, place them in a slotted spoon or a small mesh strainer, carefully dip them into the hot oil, and fry for to seconds, just until light golden brown.
17. Transfer to a small bowl.
18. Mix the rice syrup, soy sauce, sugar, vinegar, and mustard in a small bowl.
19. Heat a large pan or wok over medium-high heat.
20. Add the oil, garlic, ginger, and chili peppers and stir for seconds to 1 minute, until the garlic is a little crispy and fragrant.
21. Add the soy sauce mixture and stir.
22. Let it bubble for 2 to 3 minutes, until the mixture is shiny and a little sticky.
23. Remove from the heat if not using right away and reheat until bubbling when ready to finish the chicken.
24. Add the chicken and peanuts (if using) to the bubbling sauce and toss with a wooden spoon to coat nicely.
25. Sprinkle with the toasted sesame seeds and a few teaspoons crushed red pepper flakes (if using).
26. Transfer to a large plate or platter and serve.
27. The chicken will remain crunchy for several hours if left at room temperature, or you can cover and refrigerate it for up to 3 days.

Ingredients: '1 medium onion, thinly sliced', '1 cup gochujang (Korean red chili paste)', ' cup soy sauce', ' cup minced garlic', ' cup sugar', '3 tablespoons sake', '2 tablespoons mirin', '2 tablespoons toasted sesame oil', '2 pounds skinless pork belly, sliced inch thick (ask the butcher to slice it) and cut into 2-inch squares', 'Unsalted butter', ' slider buns', 'Mayonnaise', '2 English cucumbers, peeled, halved lengthwise, seeded, and sliced into matchsticks'
Instructions:
1. To make the marinade, put the onion, gochujang, soy sauce, garlic, sugar, sake, mirin, and sesame oil in a bowl and mix well to combine.
2. Add the pork and turn to coat the meat.
3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
4. The next day, set a large sautA pan over medium heat.
5. Once itAs hot, add the pork, working in batches so as not to overcrowd the pan and leaving any liquid in the bowl.
6. Cook, stirring occasionally and flipping the meat, until lightly caramelized and golden brown on both sides, about minutes. (If you overcrowd the pan, the meat will steam instead of sear.) Transfer to a platter.
7. When the meat is cooked, open the buns, butter the interiors, and toast them until they are a light golden brown.
8. To serve, spread both cut sides of each bun with a healthy smear of mayo and pile the spicy pork generously on the bottoms of the buns.
9. Top with the sliced cucumbers.
10. Serve immediately.
11. The pork belly must be marinated overnight.
12. You can also grill the pork on an outdoor grill over a high flame.
13. The sugars in the marinade will char and caramelize, giving you a more interesting texture and flavor, but be careful not to burn the pork.

Ingredients: '1 -lb. whole chicken', '3 tsp. white or black peppercorns', '6 garlic cloves', '2 cilantro roots, chopped, or 2 Tbsp. (heaping) finely chopped cilantro stems', '3 Tbsp. (packed) light brown sugar', '2 Tbsp. fish sauce', '4 tsp. ground turmeric', '1 Tbsp. kosher salt', '2 tsp. ground coriander', '2 tsp. ground cumin', '1 tsp. ground cardamom', ' cup (packed) chopped fresh ripe pineapple', ' cup distilled white vinegar or apple cider vinegar', '6 large garlic cloves, finely chopped', '3 red jalapeAos or serrano chiles, seeds removed, finely chopped', ' cup granulated sugar', '1 tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. freshly ground white pepper', '1 tsp. (lightly packed) saffron threads or 1 tsp. ground turmeric', '2 tsp. granulated sugar', '1 tsp. kosher salt', 'A cup virgin coconut oil', 'Vegetable oil (for grill; optional)', '1 Tbsp. coarsely chopped cilantro', 'Cooked jasmine rice, coconut rice, or sticky rice (for serving)', 'A spice mill or a mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Spatchcock chicken the Thai way by placing it breast side up on a large cutting board.
2. Using a sharp, heavy knife and working just to the side of the center of the breast, cut down through to the breastbone (do not cut all the way through to the backbone).
3. Now that the path has been created, use the same knifeAor, easier, a sturdy pair of kitchen scissorsAto make a clean cut all the way through the breastbone and anything else that is still connecting the 2 sides of the breast together.
4. Open up chicken and turn skin side up.
5. Press down on chicken to flatten.
6. Pat thoroughly dry.
7. Grind peppercorns in a spice mill or a mortar and pestle.
8. Transfer to a food processor or leave in mortar and pestle and add garlic, cilantro roots, brown sugar, fish sauce, turmeric, salt, coriander, cumin, and cardamom and process or pound until smooth.
9. Coat chicken completely in marinade, pressing to adhere and working into nooks and crannies.
10. Place in a large bowl and cover with a plate.
11. Let chill at least hours and up to hours.
12. Let chicken sit at room temperature 1 hour before grilling or baking.
13. PurAe pineapple and vinegar in a food processor or blender until smooth.
14. Transfer to a small saucepan and add garlic, jalapeAos, granulated sugar, salt, and pepper.
15. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce heat so sauce is at a simmer and cook until thick and smooth (consistency should be similar to cocktail sauce), about 5 minutes.
16. Grind saffron to a fine powder in a spice mill or a mortar and pestle.
17. Transfer to a small saucepan and add granulated sugar, salt, and cup water and cook over medium heat just until heated through and small bubbles appear around the edges of the pan.
18. Whisk in coconut oil and remove from heat.
19. If using a grill, prepare a grill for medium-high indirect heat (for a charcoal grill, bank coals on one side of grill; for a gas grill, leave one or two burners off); oil grate.
20. Arrange chicken, skin side up, on cooler part of grill.
21. Close lid and grill, rotating chicken but keeping skin side up every minutes, until skin looks dry and golden brown and an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part of breast and thighs registers , minutes.
22. If using charcoal and your coals are dying, start a new chimney of coals minutes before this point so you are ready for the next step.
