23. Uncover grill and increase heat to high (add more coals for a charcoal grill).
24. Brush one fourth of basting liquid over skin and turn chicken over, positioning over direct heat on the hot side of the grill.
25. Grill, brushing half of remaining basting liquid over bone side of chicken, until skin is browned and lightly charred in spots, about 3 minutes. (Watch carefully as this can happen quickly.) Turn chicken over and return to cooler side of grill.
26. Brush skin with remaining basting liquid.
27. Cover grill and continue to grill chicken until thermometer inserted into thickest part of breast registers and thickest part of thighs registers , minutes longer.
28. If using an oven, place a rack in bottom third of oven and preheat to .
29. Place chicken, skin side up, on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet.
30. Brush one third of basting liquid over skin.
31. Bake, rotating baking sheet every minutes, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part of breast and thighs registers , minutes.
32. Increase oven temperature to .
33. Brush remaining basting liquid over skin and continue to bake, rotating baking sheet halfway through, until skin is crisp and charred and thermometer inserted into thickest part of breast registers and thickest part of thighs registers , minutes longer.
34. Transfer chicken to a cutting board and let rest at least minutes.
35. Carve up further as desired.
36. Top dipping sauce with cilantro.
37. Serve chicken with rice and dipping sauce alongside.
38. If you cook the chicken in the oven, using the dhungar method to perfume the finished chicken with the scent of smoky ghee is highly recommended.

Recipe name: Fresh Fruit Tart With Almond Press-In Crust
Ingredients: '2 large egg yolks', '1 cup all-purpose flour', ' cup almond flour (such as BobAs Red Mill Super-Fine Natural Almond Flour) or cup all-purpose flour', '3 Tbsp. sugar', '1 tsp. kosher salt', ' cup (1 stick) chilled unsalted butter, cut into pieces', '2 cups whole milk', '1 tsp. vanilla paste or tsp. vanilla extract', '4 large egg yolks', 'A cup sugar', ' cup cornstarch', ' tsp. kosher salt', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces', ' oz. fresh blueberries', '6 oz. fresh blackberries, halved if very large', 'Special Equipment: A (9"-diameter) springform pan or tart pan with removable bottom'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in the middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Whisk egg yolks and 1 Tbsp. cold water in a small bowl to combine.
3. Whisk all-purpose flour, almond flour, sugar, and salt in a medium bowl.
4. Add butter and rub and smash into dry ingredients with your fingers to create large, shaggy pieces (you want some pieces that are flat and thin and some that are large and irregular).
5. Drizzle egg yolk mixture over flour and, using a fork or your fingers, toss into dough to incorporate.
6. Knead dough in bowl until it starts to come together (it will still look a little dry; although, if it looks too dry and doesn't come together, add 1A2 Tbsp. more water, but only if needed).
7. Turn dough out onto a clean work surface and knead 1 or 2 more times.
8. Divide dough in half, stack pieces, and press down firmly to smash together.
9. Repeat process until dough is well combined and becomes slightly sticky, 2A3 more times.
10. Press dough into bottom and 1" up sides of pan and prick all over with a fork.
11. Freeze until very firm, minutes.
12. Bake crust until deep golden brown all over, minutes.
13. Let cool.
14. Do Ahead: Crust can be baked 2 days ahead.
15. Store tightly wrapped at room temperature.
16. Bring milk and vanilla paste to a simmer in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat.
17. Remove from heat.
18. Meanwhile, whisk egg yolks, sugar, cornstarch, and salt in a medium bowl, starting slowlyAit wonAt want to combine easily at firstAthen vigorously whisk until mixture is very pale yellow and very thick, about 1 minute (the sugar should be nearly dissolved at this point).
19. Whisking constantly, gradually add hot milk mixture.
20. Return custard base to saucepan and bring to a simmer over medium heat, whisking constantly.
21. Simmer, still whisking constantly, until custard is thickened and has no cornstarch taste, about 3 minutes.
22. Remove custard from heat and add butter a couple of pieces at a time, whisking until fully melted before adding more, until all of the butter is incorporated.
23. Transfer to a medium bowl and cover with plastic wrap, pressing directly against surface.
24. Let cool.
25. Vigorously whisk custard until smooth and slightly loosened.
26. Scrape into crust and smooth surface.
27. Top with berries.
28. Do Ahead: Custard can be made 4 days ahead.
29. Keep chilled.

Ingredients: ' cups raw Thai jasmine rice', ' cup coconut milk, preferably freshly extracted', '1 cup water', '1 tablespoon plus 2 teaspoons granulated sugar', ' teaspoon salt', ' cup coconut cream, preferably freshly extracted'
Instructions:
1. To make the rice, which is best done in a rice cooker, put the rice in a fine-mesh sieve or colander and rinse under cold running water until the water runs clear.
2. Transfer the rice to a rice cooker, add the coconut milk, water, sugar, and salt, and stir to combine.
3. Cover the rice cooker and turn it on.
4. As soon as the rice cooker turns itself off, uncover, pour the coconut cream evenly over the top of the rice (do not stir it in), and replace the lid.
5. Keep the rice warm until serving.

Ingredients: 'A cup raw sesame seeds', '1 lemon', ' lb. peaches (6A7), thinly sliced', 'A cup plus cups sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', '1 cup all-purpose flour', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter', ' tsp. ground cinnamon', 'A cup tahini', '2 tsp. toasted sesame oil', '1A2 tsp. cornstarch', 'Vanilla ice cream (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in top third of oven; preheat to .
2. Toast sesame seeds on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing halfway through, until golden brown, minutes.
3. Immediately transfer to a large bowl.
4. Meanwhile, finely grate zest from lemon into a medium bowl; set aside.
5. Place peaches in a 13x9" baking dish.
6. Halve lemon and squeeze 1 Tbsp. juice over peaches, then sprinkle A cup sugar and tsp. salt over.
7. Toss to gently combine, then let sit while you prep the topping, at least minutes.
8. Add flour and remaining cups sugar and 1 tsp. salt to bowl with sesame seeds.
9. Whisk to combine.
10. Add butter and cinnamon to bowl with lemon zest.
11. Microwave until butter is melted, about 1 minute. (Or, if you don't have a microwave, melt butter with cinnamon and lemon zest in a small saucepan over medium.) Whisk in tahini and oil.
12. Scrape tahini mixture into dry ingredients and mix until incorporated (mixture should hold together when squeezed in your hand).
13. The peaches should look juicy at this point.
14. Add 1 tsp. cornstarch.
15. If theyAre really juicy, add another 1 tsp.
16. Gently toss to combine. (If your peaches happen to be not-so-ripe and aren't juicy, don't add any cornstarch.) Squeeze topping into big clumps, then break off uneven pieces and scatter over peaches.
17. Bake crumble until topping is well browned and firm, peaches are tender, and juices are bubbling, about 1 hour.
18. Let cool slightly.
19. Divide crumble among shallow bowls and add a scoop of ice cream if desired.

Ingredients: '1 cup hazelnuts, toasted, loose skins rubbed off in a kitchen towel, and cooled', ' cup sugar', ' cup all-purpose flour', ' stick (6 tablespoons) cold unsalted butter, softened', '2 large eggs', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract', ' teaspoon almond extract', '1 tart shell', '3 firm-ripe Bosc or Anjou pears', ' cup apricot preserves, heated and strained'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Pulse hazelnuts with cup sugar in a food processor until finely ground, then add flour and pulse to combine.
3. Beat together butter and remaining cup sugar with an electric mixer at moderately high speed until pale and fluffy.
4. Add eggs 1 at a time, beating well after each addition, then beat in extracts.
5. Reduce speed to low and mix in nut mixture until just combined.
6. Spread frangipane filling evenly in tart shell.
7. Peel, halve, and core pears, then cut lengthwise into -inch-thick slices, holding slices together to keep pear shape intact.
8. Arrange pears decoratively on filling, fanning slices slightly.
9. Bake until pears are golden and frangipane is puffed and golden brown, to minutes.
10. Brush pears (not filling) with preserves and cool tart completely in pan on rack, then remove side of pan.

Recipe name: Melted Broccoli Pasta With Capers And Anchovies
Ingredients: 'Kosher salt', '2 heads (about 1 pound, or 454g, total) broccoli, cut into bitesize florets', ' ounces ( g) whole-wheat penne pasta, or other short tubular pasta', '3 tablespoons ( ml) extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '1 cup ( g) panko or Freezer Bread Crumbs (see Note)', '4 oil-packed anchovy fillets', ' cup ( g) capers, chopped if large (rinsed well if salt-packed)', '2 garlic cloves, minced', ' teaspoon red pepper flakes'
Instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil over high heat.
2. Add the broccoli florets and cook until bright green and crisp-tender, 2 to 3 minutes.
3. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the broccoli to a large bowl.
4. Add the pasta to the boiling water and cook for 1 minute less than the package instructions for al dente, about 9 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, toast the bread crumbs.
6. Heat 1 tablespoon ( ml) of olive oil in a large, high-sided sautA pan or skillet over medium heat.
7. Add the breadcrumbs and sautA until the crumbs are golden brown and crisp, 4 to 5 minutes.
8. Transfer to a small bowl and set aside.
9. Pour the remaining 2 tablespoons ( ml) of olive oil into the pan.
10. Add the anchovies and sautA until they disintegrate, about 1 minute.
11. Add the capers, garlic, and red pepper flakes.
12. SautA until fragrant, about 1 minute, and remove from the heat.
13. When the pasta is ready, reserve cups ( ml) of pasta water with a measuring cup, then drain the pasta.
14. Add the broccoli and reserved pasta water to the pan and bring to a simmer.
15. Continue to simmer, using a wooden spoon to break the florets into small pieces as they become more tender, until the water is reduced by about half and you've been able to break apart enough florets that you're left with a very chunky mixture, 5 to 7 minutes.
16. Add the pasta to the pan.
17. Cook, tossing and stirring, until the pasta is al dente and the sauce thickens and coats the pasta, 1 to 2 minutes.
18. Remove from the heat, add half the toasted bread crumbs, and toss again to combine.
19. Serve garnished with the remaining toasted bread crumbs.

Ingredients: '1 gallon cold water', '1 (6-inch) square dashima (kombu)', '3 medium dried shiitake mushrooms', ' large dried anchovies (gutted and heads removed)'
Instructions:
1. Put the water, dashima, shiitake mushrooms, and anchovies in a covered stockpot or other large pot and let stand at room temperature overnight, or for 8 to hours.
2. Remove the lid, set the pot over high heat, and heat until the surface of the water begins to ripple; pay attention, and do not let it come to a boil.
3. Lower the heat to maintain a very gentle simmer (the ideal temperature is a few degrees below a light boil) and simmer for to minutes.
4. Taste the dashi every minutes to monitor the changes in flavor and texture.
5. You will notice the texture becoming softer.
6. I describe it as having a slippery, slightly viscous texture compared to tap water.
7. The sea flavor will become stronger, and the dashi will become darker, like pale Earl Grey tea.
8. The sweetness will take a while to appear, but when it does, the dashi is almost ready.
9. When you can taste the sweetness and deep sea flavors, and the dashi has a very soft texture, it is done.
10. Be vigilant, because dashi will become bitter if cooked too long.
11. Strain the dashi into a covered container and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
12. After hours, the dashi will begin to lose some of its flavor and it will turn slightly flat.
13. ItAs best to use dashi the same day you make it, though if you keep it refrigerated, you can use it for another day or two.
14. By the third day, it will no longer taste fresh.
15. Vegetarians can make a dashi from just dashima and dried shiitakes.

Ingredients: ' cups ( ml) water', ' oz ( g) sour tamarind pulp or paste', '8 oz ( g) fresh ginger, preferably young', '1 cup ( g) sugar', 'One 1 L bottle chilled club soda or 4 cups ( ml) chilled water'
Instructions:
1. Bring cups ( ml) of the water to a boil.
2. Put the tamarind pulp in a heat-proof nonreactive bowl, and cover with the boiling water.
3. Cover and let sit for about 1 hour, until the pulp has become soft and mushy.
4. Using a small potato masher or clean hands, massage and squeeze the block to soften the pulp.
5. Strain through a fine-mesh strainer suspended over a medium bowl, pressing the pulp through the strainer with a large spoon.
6. Discard the seeds and other solids left behind.
7. Refrigerate the tamarind extract in an airtight container in the refrigerator until ready to use, for up to 4 days.
8. Rinse the ginger and gently scrub to remove any traces of dirt.
9. If the ginger is young, with a thin skin, leave the skin on.
10. Otherwise, peel.
11. Cut into thin slices.
12. In a medium saucepan, combine the ginger, sugar, and remaining 1 cup ( ml) water.
13. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and immediately remove the saucepan from the heat.
14. Cover and steep for minutes.
15. Strain the ginger simple syrup through a fine-mesh strainer and discard the ginger.
16. In a large pitcher, mix the tamarind extract and the ginger simple syrup together.
17. Fill eight tall glasses with ice.
18. Pour 4 oz ( ml) of the tamarind-ginger syrup into each glass, top with 4 oz ( ml) of the club soda or chilled water, and stir.
19. Store any remaining tamarind-ginger syrup in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
20. This rejuvenating drink combines the mild heat of young ginger and the sourness of tamarind.
21. ItAs wonderful during the warmer months.
22. Young ginger requires no peeling before use because its outer skin is so thin.
23. I recommend using South Asian tamarind, which is a lot more sour than the Mexican variety.
24. Look for it in Asian and Indian markets.

Ingredients: ' to saffron strands, plus a few extra strands for garnish', ' cup ( g) sugar', '3 green cardamom pods, smashed', ' cup ( g) ground sumac', '3 cups ( ml) chilled club soda or water'
Instructions:
1. Grind the saffron with 2 Tbsp. of sugar to a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
2. In a medium saucepan over medium-high heat, combine 1 cup ( ml) water, sugar, cardamom, and powdered saffron mixture and bring to simmer, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
3. Remove from the heat and stir in the sumac.
4. Cover the saucepan with a lid and let it steep for minutes, no more.
5. Strain the liquid through a fine mesh strainer over a medium bowl and chill before ready to serve.
6. To serve, fill four tall glasses with ice.
7. In a large pitcher, stir the syrup with the chilled club soda or water.
8. Pour 1 cup ( ml) of the drink into each glass.
9. Garnish each glass with 1 or 2 saffron strands.
10. Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
11. Sumac is rich in citric, malic, and tartaric acids but also bitter tannins.
12. Steeping it in water dissolves the water-soluble acids.
13. Gallotannin, one of the main tannins present in sumac, is bitter to taste and dissolves in water easily.
14. Avoid steeping the sumac for too long or its tannins will make the syrup bitter.
15. Saffron is ground to a fine powder using a little sugar as an abrasive.
16. Grinding saffron increases the amount of color and flavor extracted than would be achieved if the strands were used directly.
17. Club soda if used, adds a second note of acidity from the carbonic acid as well as the crackling sound of bubbles that create a sensation via chemesthesis.

Ingredients: '160g ( oz cup 2 tablespoons) smooth peanut butter', '220g (8 oz 1 cup) light brown sugar', '1 large egg, plus 2 large egg yolks', '100g ( oz cup) all-purpose flour', '1 teaspoon bicarbonate of soda (baking soda)', ' teaspoon fine sea salt', '100g (3 oz) salted peanuts, roughly chopped', '150g ( oz 1A sticks) unsalted butter, diced, plus extra for greasing', '85g (3 oz 1 cup) cocoa powder', '100g ( oz cup) caster (superfine) sugar', '100g ( oz A cup 2 tablespoons) light brown sugar', ' teaspoon fine sea salt', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract', '2 large eggs', '70g ( oz cup 1 tablespoon) all-purpose flour'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to (), Gas Mark 4.
2. Lightly grease a 13x9" baking tin and line with a piece of parchment paper that overhangs the two long sides.
3. Secure in place with metal clips.
4. For the peanut butter cookie layer, mix the peanut butter and brown sugar together in a large bowl for a couple of minutes until combined and slightly lightened.
5. Add the egg and egg yolks and beat together for a further 2A3 minutes.
6. Add all the remaining ingredients (reserving 3 tablespoons of the peanuts for sprinkling over later) and gently mix together to form a uniform cookie dough.
7. Spread the cookie dough in an even layer in the prepared tin, then set aside.
8. For the brownie layer, add the butter and cocoa to a small saucepan over a medium heat, stirring constantly, until melted and smooth.
9. Remove from the heat and set aside.
10. Place the sugars, salt, vanilla and eggs in a large bowl and whisk together with an electric mixer for 2A3 minutes or until slightly thickened and pale.
11. Pour in the melted butter mixture, mixing briefly until combined, then fold in the flour.
12. Pour the batter over the cookie layer and spread into an even layer, then sprinkle over the reserved peanuts.
13. Bake for about minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the middle comes out with a few moist crumbs.
14. Leave the brookies to cool completely in the tin, before using the parchment paper to lift them from the tin and cutting into squares.Store in a sealed container for 3A4 days.

Recipe name: Tacos De Gobernador Shrimp Poblano And Cheese Tacos
Ingredients: '4 teaspoons neutral cooking oil, such as canola oil', '1 onion, peeled and diced', '1 garlic clove, peeled and minced', '2 poblano chiles, stems removed, cut into 1A4-inch-wide strips', '8 corn tortillas', 'Juice of 3 limes', '1 slice bacon, diced small', '8 ounces peeled and deveined medium shrimp (preferably sizes or ; if you have bigger shrimp, slice them in half lengthwise)', '1 teaspoon kosher salt', '1 cup Queso Asadero cheese (if not available, substitute Teleme or Monterey Jack)', '2 limes, cut into wedges', 'Homemade or store-bought tangy red salsa'
Instructions:
1. Warm 3 teaspoons of the oil in a frying pan over medium-low heat.
2. Add the onion and cook for 5 minutes, stirring gently.
3. Add the garlic and continue to cook for 3 minutes. (If, at any time during the cooking process, the items in the pan dry out at all, deglaze the pan with some lime juice, scraping up the browned bits with a wooden spoon, and continue.) Turn the heat to medium and add the chiles.
4. Cook for minutes, or until the chiles are thoroughly cooked.
5. At this point, warm a separate frying pan over medium heat and add the remaining 1 teaspoon oil.
6. Interleaved with finishing the rest of the recipe, heat each tortilla in the oil-coated pan for about seconds per side, just long enough to make them the slightest bit golden but still pliable, and put between two towels to stay warm and wick away any excess oil.
7. Once the chiles have cooked for minutes, pour the lime juice on the ingredients in the pan.
8. Add the diced bacon to the pan and turn the heat to medium-high.
9. When the pan has heated up and the bacon has started to turn color, add the shrimp and use the salt to season everything in the pan.
10. Cook for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring and flipping the shrimp as necessary to cook them evenly.
11. The shrimp should be bright pink and white and opaque.
12. If they are still translucent, cook them a little longer.
13. Sprinkle the cheese over everything in the pan and turn the heat to low.
14. Continue stirring until the cheese is melted.
15. Remove the pan from the heat.
16. Distribute the cheesy shrimp mixture equally among the tortillas, making a plate full of tacos.
17. Serve along with the lime wedges and red salsa, if desired.

Ingredients: '4 oz. cream cheese, room temperature', ' cup finely chopped kimchi; plus more for serving (optional)', '2 scallions, thinly sliced on a diagonal', '1 cup cilantro leaves with tender stems', ' lime', 'Kosher salt', '4 ("-thick) slices country-style bread, grilled or toasted', 'Chili oil and toasted white sesame seeds (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Mix cream cheese and cup kimchi in a medium bowl.
2. Toss scallions and cilantro in a small bowl to combine.
3. Squeeze in juice from lime, season with salt, and toss again.
4. Smear kimchi cream cheese over each slice of bread.
5. Top with scallion salad and more kimchi (if using).
6. Drizzle with chili oil; sprinkle with sesame seeds.

Ingredients: '4 scallions', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '1 (1") piece ginger, peeled, cut into matchsticks', ' pounds tomatoes (about 3 medium), cut into (1") pieces', '2 Tbsp. plus tsp. white or regular soy sauce', 'Kosher salt (optional)', 'Toasted country-style bread or cooked rice or pasta (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Trim dark green tops from scallions and thinly slice; set aside for serving.
2. Finely chop remaining white and pale green parts.
3. Heat butter in a large skillet over medium.
4. Add white and pale green parts of scallions and ginger and cook, stirring often, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
5. Add tomatoes and cook, turning gently with a spoon, until juicy and just warmed through, about 2 minutes.
6. Add soy sauce and toss to combine.
7. Taste and season with kosher salt if needed.
8. Top with reserved scallion greens.
9. Spoon tomatoes over toast and sprinkle with more salt if desired.

Ingredients: ' cup extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling', '6 garlic cloves, smashed', ' lb. cherry tomatoes (about 4 pints)', '2 large sprigs basil, plus 1 cup basil leaves, torn if large', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', 'Pinch of sugar (optional)', ' oz. casarecce or other medium-size pasta', '1 oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about cup), plus more for serving'
Instructions:
1. Heat cup oil in a large heavy pot over low.
2. Add garlic and cook, stirring often with a wooden spoon, until softened but not browned, about 2 minutes.
3. Increase heat to medium and add tomatoes, basil sprigs, red pepper flakes, and tsp. salt.
4. Cook, stirring to coat, until some of the tomatoes begin to burst and release their juices, about 4 minutes.
5. Smash some but not all of the tomatoes with the spoon to help release their liquid, then continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until a chunky, thickened sauce forms (about half the tomatoes should still be intact), minutes.
6. Taste and add sugar if sauce is too tart and add more salt if needed.
7. Pluck out and discard basil sprigs.
8. Meanwhile, cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until al dente.
9. Drain pasta, add to pot with sauce, and cook, stirring, until coated, about 1 minute.
10. Remove from heat and stir in 1 oz.
11. Parmesan.
12. Divide pasta among bowls; drizzle with oil.
13. Top with more Parmesan and 1 cup basil leaves.

Ingredients: '1 sleeve ( g) Ritz crackers or other cracker of your choice', 'A cup ( g) raw peanuts or other nut or seed, finely chopped', 'A cup ( g; packed) light or dark brown sugar', ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', '6 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted', '3 large eggs', ' cup ( g; packed) light or dark brown sugar', ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', '1 tsp. vanilla extract', ' cup ( g) natural peanut butter or other natural nut or seed butter', ' cups heavy cream', 'A cup ( g) grape jelly (preferably Bonne Maman) or other jelly, jam, or compote'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Place crackers in a deep pie dish (a shallower one will work but you may have some filling left over) and gently crush with your hands until you have a mix of dime-size pieces, small crumbs, and everything in between.
3. Add peanuts, brown sugar, and salt and toss to combine.
4. Drizzle in butter and toss to evenly coat.
5. Using your hands and the bottom of a measuring cup, press firmly and evenly into bottom and up sides of dish. (Mixture will look and feel crumbly, but it will hold together when baked.) Bake crust until a shade darker, minutes.
6. Let cool.
7. Do Ahead: Crust can be baked 3 days ahead.
8. Store tightly wrapped at room temperature, or freeze up to 1 month.
9. Pour water into a medium saucepan to come " up the sides and bring to a bare simmer over medium-low heat.
10. Whisk eggs, brown sugar, and salt in a large heatproof bowl just to combine (or use the bowl of a stand mixer if youAve got one).
11. Set over saucepan (bowl should not touch water) and heat egg mixture, stirring constantly with a heatproof rubber spatula, until sugar dissolves, about 4 minutes (mixture will no longer feel gritty when rubbed between your fingers).
12. If you are concerned about undercooked eggs, heat mixture longer, still stirring constantly, until an instant-read thermometer registers , about 3 minutes more.
13. Remove bowl from heat and add vanilla to egg mixture.
14. Using an electric mixer, beat on high speed until thick and pale and doubled in volume, about minutes. (Or, if using a stand mixer, beat with the whisk attachment, 6A8 minutes.) Using spatula, mix peanut butter in another large bowl to loosen up and smooth out.
15. Stir one third of egg mixture into peanut butter to lighten, then gently fold in half of remaining egg mixture, running spatula down sides and along bottom of bowl, then lifting up through center and over top while rotating bowl, until just combined.
16. Repeat with remaining egg mixture (it will lose a lot of volume, and thatAs okay).
17. Starting on low speed and gradually increasing to medium-high, beat cream in the same bowl you used for egg mixture (no need to wash) until soft peaks form, about 2 minutes.
18. Gently fold into peanut butter mixture and scrape into crust.
19. Dollop jelly all over; swirl with a butter knife. (For more defined swirls, freeze pie minutes before adding jelly.) Freeze at least 8 hours before serving.
20. To slice, run a chefAs knife under hot water to warm up the blade, then wipe to clean and dry between slices.
21. Use an offset spatula or fork to loosen slices from dish.
22. If pie is frozen very hard, you may need to let it temper in the fridge minutes before serving.
23. Do Ahead: Pie can be made 2 weeks ahead.
24. Wrap tightly and freeze.

Ingredients: '1 cup semi-pearled farro', '1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '4 large eggs', '1 large watermelon radish or 6A8 red radishes, trimmed, thinly sliced on a mandoline if youAve got one', ' cup unseasoned rice vinegar', '2 Tbsp. sugar, divided', '1 (-oz.) block extra-firm tofu, drained', '3 Tbsp. plus cup grapeseed or vegetable oil, divided', '2 Tbsp. fresh lime juice', '2 Tbsp. gochujang (Korean hot pepper paste)', '1 Tbsp. mayonnaise', '1 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. soy sauce', '1 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. toasted sesame oil', '1 medium zucchini or summer squash, halved lengthwise, cut crosswise into ("-thick) half-moons', '2A3 cups thinly sliced or shredded raw vegetables (such as carrots, scallions, blanched sugar snap peas, baby bok choy, cucumbers, pea shoots, or alfalfa sprouts)'
Instructions:
1. Pour farro into a medium saucepan of boiling salted water, reduce heat, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until tender, minutes.
2. Drain farro and spread out on a rimmed baking sheet; let cool.
3. Meanwhile, using a slotted spoon, carefully lower eggs into a small saucepan of boiling water and cook minutes for an oozy, jammy yolk or 7 minutes for a slightly firmer middle.
4. Transfer eggs to a bowl of ice water with slotted spoon and let sit until cool enough to handle, about 2 minutes.
5. Remove eggs from ice water; peel and slice in half lengthwise.
6. Pack radish slices into a pint jar.
7. Whisk vinegar, 1 Tbsp. sugar, 1 tsp. salt, and cup hot water in a small bowl until sugar and salt are dissolved.
8. Pour brine over radish slices; let cool.
9. Cover and chill at least 1 hour before using.
10. Cut tofu crosswise into "-thick slabs, then cut each slab on a diagonal to create 2 triangles.
11. Pat dry with a clean kitchen towel.
12. Heat 2 Tbsp. grapeseed oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
13. Add tofu in a single layer, season with salt, and cook, undisturbed, until golden brown underneath, about 4 minutes.
14. Turn over and cook until golden brown on second side, about 4 minutes.
15. Transfer tofu to a plate; reserve skillet (no need to wipe out).
16. Whisk lime juice, gochujang, mayonnaise, cup grapeseed oil, 1 Tbsp. soy sauce, 1 Tbsp. sesame oil, and remaining 1 Tbsp. sugar in a small bowl.
17. Taste; season with salt if desired.
18. Heat remaining 1 Tbsp. grapeseed oil in reserved skillet over medium-high.
19. Add zucchini, season with salt, and cook, tossing just once or twice, until tender and blistered in spots, about 4 minutes.
20. Add remaining 1 tsp. soy sauce and 1 tsp. sesame oil and toss to coat.
21. Transfer to a plate.
22. To serve, divide farro among bowls.
23. Arrange eggs, drained pickled radish, tofu, zucchini, and raw vegetables of choice on top as desired.
24. Drizzle each bowl with a few spoonfuls of dressing to your liking.

Ingredients: ' lb. heirloom tomatoes (about 3 medium), sliced into wedges, depending on size', ' oz. mixed cherry tomatoes (about 2 cups), halved, quartered if large', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided, plus more', '1 lemon', ' garlic clove', '6 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '2 Tbsp. zaAatar', '2 cups pita chips', ' tsp. (or more) honey', '3.5 oz. feta, thinly sliced into planks', ' cup basil leaves, torn if large', ' cup mint leaves, torn if large'
Instructions:
1. Gently toss heirloom and cherry tomatoes and tsp. salt in a medium bowl to combine and set aside.
2. Finely grate lemon zest into a small bowl, then grate in garlic; set lemon aside.
3. Heat oil in a small saucepan or skillet over medium until just starting to shimmer.
4. Add zaAatar and cook, stirring occasionally, until fragrant and slightly darkened in color, about 1 minute.
5. Add lemon zest and garlic, cook seconds, then remove from heat. (You're looking to cook off the raw flavor of the garlic without losing the brightness of the lemon.) Transfer zaAatar oil to a heatproof measuring cup.
6. Place pita chips in a large bowl and drizzle 2 Tbsp. zaAatar oil over; season with salt.
7. Mix well with a rubber spatula, aiming to coat chips without breaking them into too many pieces.
8. Cut reserved lemon in half and squeeze juice from 1 lemon half into a small bowl.
9. Stir in honey and remaining tsp. salt.
10. Gradually add remaining za'atar oil, stirring constantly until emulsified.
11. Taste dressing and add more lemon juice, honey, or salt if needed.
12. Add feta, basil, mint, and reserved tomatoes to bowl with chips and drizzle dressing over; toss gently to combine.
13. Spoon salad onto platter, making sure to leave no juices behind.
14. Eat fast for crunchy chips or let sit for a crispy-gone-soggy experience.

Recipe name: Chicken Thighs With Tomatoes And Feta
Ingredients: '6 skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs (about lb. total)', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', ' lb. cherry tomatoes (about 2 pints)', ' cup harissa paste', '3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar', '3 sprigs oregano, divided', '4 oz. feta, cut into ("-thick) planks', 'Crusty bread (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Pat chicken thighs dry with paper towels; season all over with salt.
2. Arrange skin side down in a cold large cast-iron skillet.
3. Set over medium heat and cook chicken, undisturbed, rotating pan as needed for even browning, until skin is very deep golden brown and crisp and chicken releases easily from pan, minutes.
4. Using tongs, transfer chicken to a plate, arranging skin side up.
5. Combine tomatoes, harissa paste, vinegar, 1 oregano sprig, and tsp. salt in same skillet.
6. Increase heat to medium-high and cook, stirring occasionally, until tomatoes burst and their juices start to thicken, minutes.
7. Nestle chicken thighs into tomatoes, skin side up.
8. Reduce heat to medium-low, bring to a simmer, and cook until chicken is just cooked through and flesh is no longer pink (an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part near the bone will register ), and tomato sauce is thickened so that a wooden spoon dragged through it leaves a trail, 6A8 minutes.
9. Remove from heat and let sit 5 minutes.
10. Break feta into large pieces; scatter over chicken.
11. Some pieces will stay intact while others will soften into the sauce a littleAand that's exactly what you want.
12. Pick leaves off remaining 2 oregano sprigs and scatter on top.
13. Serve with bread for sopping up any extra tomato sauce.

Recipe name: Corn And Chickpea Bowls With Miso Tahini
Ingredients: '2 small jalapeAos, seeded, chopped', '1 (-inch) piece ginger, peeled, finely grated', '2 small garlic cloves, finely grated', '1 cup (packed) cilantro leaves with tender stems', 'A cup fresh lime juice', 'A cup tahini', '1 Tbsp. plus tsp. miso', 'Kosher salt', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided, plus more for drizzling', '2 garlic cloves, lightly crushed', '1 (-oz.) can chickpeas rinsed, drained', '3 ears of corn, shucked, kernels removed', '1 Tbsp. spice blend (such as zaAatar, vadouvan, hawaij, or berbere)', 'Kosher salt', '4 cups tender greens (such as Little Gem lettuce andor watercress), torn if large', '2 avocados, cut into wedges', '4 small tomatoes, cut into wedges', 'Crumbled feta, cilantro sprigs, and lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. PurAe jalapeAos, ginger, garlic, cilantro, lime juice, tahini, miso, and 3 Tbsp. water in a blender until smooth.
2. Season with salt.
3. Do Ahead: Dressing can be made 1 day ahead.
4. Transfer to an airtight container and chill.
5. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium.
6. Add garlic and cook, turning occasionally, until golden brown, about 4 minutes.
7. Mash garlic with a spoon and stir into oil.
8. Add chickpeas and increase heat to medium-high.
9. Cook, undisturbed, until golden brown and beginning to crisp underneath, about 3 minutes.
10. Toss and continue to cook, tossing occasionally, until golden brown all over, about 2 minutes more.
11. Add corn and 1 Tbsp. oil and cook, tossing, until corn kernels are golden around the edges and tender, 5A7 minutes.
12. Sprinkle spice blend over and toss to coat.
13. Season with salt.
14. Combine lettuce, avocados, and tomatoes in a large bowl.
15. Drizzle lightly with oil and toss to combine.
16. Season with salt.
17. Divide dressing among bowls and top with salad and chickpea mixture, then feta and some cilantro.
18. Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

Ingredients: ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter', ' cup ( g) raw pistachios, divided', '1 large egg', ' cup (packed; g) light brown sugar', '2 cups ( g) all-purpose flour', '1 tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. baking powder', '3 large eggs', 'A cup (packed; g) light brown sugar', ' cup sour cream', '1 Tbsp. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract', '1 tsp. ground cardamom', ' tsp. ground ginger', ' tsp.(packed) finely grated orange zest', ' tsp. kosher salt', '3 Tbsp ( g) all-purpose flour', ' lb. stone fruit (such as peaches, nectarines, apricots, and plums; all about the same size), cut into sixths or eighths, depending on their size', '1 Tbsp. coarse sugar', 'Powdered sugar, whipped cream, or crA me fraAche (for serving; optional)', 'A 9"-diameter springform pan'
Instructions:
1. Preheat an oven to .
2. Cook butter in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring often, until it foams, then browns, 5A8 minutes.
3. Set aside.
4. Pulse pistachios in a food processor until finely ground.
5. Transfer 4 Tbsp. to a small bowl; set aside.
6. Scrape reserved browned butter over pistachios in food processor; add egg, brown sugar, flour, salt, and baking powder.
7. Pulse until incorporated, then process, scraping down sides as needed, until mixture looks like wet sand and holds together when squeezed in your hand, about 2 minutes.
8. Press dough evenly into bottom and about 2" up sides of pan with a straight-sided glass or measuring cup.
9. Chill while you make the filling.
10. Process eggs and brown sugar in clean food processor until eggs are well combined, about 1 minute.
11. Scrape down sides; add sour cream, vanilla bean paste, cardamom, ginger, orange zest, and salt.
12. Sprinkle flour and 2 Tbsp. reserved pistachios over; process until smooth, about 1 minute.
13. Starting at the edges of the chilled tart shell and working inward, arrange stone fruit in snug concentric circles, standing each wedge up so one tip is raised and rounded skin side is pressed against crust.
14. Carefully pour custard around fruit (fruit should not be completely covered).
15. Sprinkle coarse sugar over.
16. Bake pie until crust is golden brown and filling is puffed and set in the center (it shouldnAt wobble when gently shaken), minutes.
17. Transfer pan to a wire rack and scatter remaining reserved 2 Tbsp. pistachios over.
18. Let cool, then chill, uncovered, until fully set.
19. Unmold tart.
20. Dust with powdered sugar andor top with dollops of whipped cream or crA me fraAche if desired.
21. Serve chilled or room temperature.

Recipe name: Platanos Horneados Con Crema Y Queso Baked Banana With Crema And Cheese
Ingredients: '6 heirloom bananas or small plantains, ripe but not black, unpeeled', '1 cup ( g) crema or creAme fraiAche', '1 cup ( g) crumbled queso fresco'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. Place the whole bananas or plantains on a baking sheet and bake, turning them every minutes, until the peel starts to split and the inside is tender when tested with a skewer, about minutes.
3. Remove from the oven but keep on the baking sheet.
4. Carefully slice the peel lengthwise to just reveal the flesh and drizzle with some crema and sprinkle with cheese.
5. Transfer to plates to serve individually, on a platter, or directly on the baking sheet on the table.
6. Either way, people should carve out their own banana.
7. Serve the remaining crema and cheese in bowls on the side, in case anyone wants more.

Recipe name: Tamale Pie With Fresh Tomato And Corn
Ingredients: ' pound fresh chorizo, casings removed (about 2 links), or vegetarian substitute such as Lightlife Plant-Based Italian Sausage', ' pound ground beef, or vegetarian substitute such as Lightlife Plant-Based Ground', '1 tablespoon tomato paste', '2 teaspoons cumin', '1 large onion, grated on a box grater, divided', '1A2 medium jalapeAos, thinly sliced, seeded if desired, divided', '1 teaspoons kosher salt, divided', '2 large beefsteak tomatoes (about 2 pounds), cut into " cubes', '2 cups corn kernels (preferably fresh; from about 3 ears of corn), divided', '8 ounces cheddar cheese, grated (about 2 cups)', '2 large eggs', '6 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted', ' cup sour cream, plus more for serving', '1 cups cornmeal', '1 teaspoon baking powder'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Heat an 8" cast-iron skillet over high.
3. Pat chorizo and beef dry with paper towels, then press into an even layer in bottom of hot skillet with a spatula.
4. Cook, undisturbed, until a brown crust begins to form on the bottom, about 6 minutes.
5. Turn, break into pieces, and continue to cook until cooked through, about 4 minutes.
6. Add tomato paste, cumin, half of grated onion, half of jalapeAo, and 1 tsp. salt and cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant and well combined, about 1 minute.
7. Remove from heat and stir in tomatoes, 1 cups corn, and 1 cups cheese.
8. Whisk eggs, butter, cup sour cream, and remaining onion in a medium bowl.
9. Whisk cornmeal, baking powder, and remaining tsp. salt in a small bowl.
10. Add dry ingredients to butter mixture and stir to combine, then stir in cup cheese and remaining cup corn.
11. Dollop cornbread batter over meat mixture, then smooth into an even layer.
12. Top with remaining jalapeAos and cup cheese.
13. Bake cornbread until golden brown, minutes.
14. Serve with sour cream alongside.

Recipe name: Charred-Peach Panzanella With Pickled Pepper Vinaigrette
Ingredients: '2 garlic cloves, finely grated', ' cup sliced hot pickled peppers (such as banana peppers), plus 1 Tbsp. pickle brine', '1 Tbsp. stone-ground or whole grain mustard', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' lb. plum tomatoes (6A7 medium), sliced " thick', '8 oz. thick-cut bacon (about 6 slices), cut into " pieces', '1 (-oz.) loaf crusty multigrain bread, sliced " thick', '3 large slightly firm peaches (about lb.), preferably freestone, halved, pitted'
Instructions:
1. Whisk garlic, pickle brine, mustard, and salt in a large bowl to combine.
2. Add tomatoes and pickled peppers and gently toss to combine.
3. Let sit, tossing occasionally, while you cook the bacon, bread, and peaches.
4. Arrange bacon in a cold large cast-iron skillet and set over medium heat.
5. Cook, stirring occasionally, until mostly crisp (there can still be a few soft, chewy spots if youAre into that sort of thing), about minutes.
6. Transfer bacon to paper towels to drain.
7. Pour bacon fat into a small bowl; set aside.
8. Wipe out skillet.
9. Heat 2 Tbsp. reserved bacon fat in same skillet.
10. Working in batches and adding more bacon fat as needed, toast bread until golden (a few char marks are fine), about 2 minutes per side.
11. Transfer to paper towels to drain.
12. Wipe out skillet.
13. Heat another 1 Tbsp. bacon fat (if youAve run out, use vegetable or olive oil) in same skillet and cook peach halves, cut side down, until lightly charred, about 3 minutes.
14. Transfer to a cutting board; let sit until cool enough to handle.
15. Slice each half in half again or into thirds and add to bowl with tomatoes.
16. Tear toasted bread into 1"" pieces and add to bowl.
17. Add bacon and toss gently to combine and saturate bread with juices collected in bowl.
18. Transfer panzanella to a platter or individual bowls to serve.

Recipe name: Stuffed Eggplants And Zucchini In A Rich Tomato Sauce Baatingan W Kusaa Bil Banadoura
Ingredients: ' tbsp olive oil', '2 onions, finely diced ( cups350g)', '4 cups1kg tomato puree', ' oz350g ripe tomatoes (2 large), coarsely grated', ' cups750ml chicken stock or water', '1 tbsp sugar', 'Salt and black pepper', ' cup plus 2 tbsp175g Egyptian rice or arborio rice (see note below)', '9 oz250g ground lamb', ' cup60ml olive oil', ' tsp ground cinnamon', ' tsp ground allspice', ' tsp ground cumin', 'About 3 green onions, finely sliced (A cup35g)', ' cup15g mint leaves, roughly chopped', ' cup15g parsley leaves, roughly chopped', '1 cup20g dill, roughly chopped', '1 red chile, seeded and finely diced (1 tbsp10g)', '1 lemon: finely grate the zest to get 1 tsp', '3 tbsp water', ' lb700g eggplants (between 3 and 6, depending on size)', ' lb700g zucchini (between 3 and 6, depending on size)', '7 tbsp100ml olive oil', '6 garlic cloves, finely chopped', '1 tbsp coriander seeds, lightly crushed in a mortar and pestle', '1 red chile, seeded and finely diced (1 tbsp10g)', ' cup5g parsley leaves, roughly chopped', ' cup5g mint leaves, roughly torn', ' cup5g dill leaves', '1 green onion, thinly sliced (2 tbsp10g)', '1 cup250g Greek yogurt'
Instructions:
1. To make the sauce, put the oil into a saucepan or casserole pan with a lidAabout inches25cm wideAand place over medium heat.
2. Add the onions and cook for about minutes, stirring frequently, until soft and caramelized.
3. Add the rest of the sauce ingredients, along with tsp of salt and a good grind of black pepper.
4. Simmer over medium heat for about minutes, stirring from time to time, then remove from the heat and set aside.
5. To make the stuffing, while the sauce is cooking, place all the ingredients in a large bowl with tsp of salt and a good grind of black pepper.
6. Mix well, using your hands to make sure that everything is well incorporated.
7. If making in advance, keep in the fridge until ready to use.
8. Trim the stalks from the eggplants, then insert a manakra (or peeler or corer) into the eggplant; you want it to be very close to the skinAabout A inch3mm awayAbut not so close that it tears and wonAt hold its shape when itAs stuffed.
9. Scoop out the flesh to create a generous cavity.
10. You donAt need the flesh anymore, but keep it for another recipe; it can be cut into cubes and steamed or added to your next omelette.
11. If your eggplants are particularly large, slice them in half, crosswise, and scoop out the flesh using a regular small serrated knifeAbe sure to keep one end of each half intact, so that the stuffing does not fall out!
12. Use the manakra or a swivel peeler to scoop out the zucchini flesh.
13. Keep about A inch3mm of flesh attached to the skin inside the zucchini and about inch1cm from the end intactAthey need to be robust enough to keep the stuffing inside.
14. Again, keep the scooped-out flesh to use elsewhere.
15. Using your hands, so that you can push in a bit of stuffing at a time, fill all the eggplant and zucchini cavities.
16. Stop filling them about inch1cm from the top of each vegetable; the stuffing needs some space to expand inside the vegetables when they are cooking.
17. Gently lower the stuffed vegetables into the sauce.
18. They wonAt fit in a single layer, but try to avoid too much overlap and submerge them in the sauce as much as you can.
19. Return the sauce to medium heat and, once simmering, decrease the heat to low.
20. Cover the pan and simmer very gently for minutes, or until the rice is completely cooked through and softAtest if it is ready by sticking a knife into the middle of one of the vegetables; it should go in very easily.
21. DonAt worry if some of the ricestuffing spills into the tomato sauce, this can happen and it will be fine when served.
22. To make the adha, meanwhile, put the oil into a small frying pan and place over medium heat.
23. After about 1 minute, add the garlic and decrease the heat to medium-low.
24. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring very often, until the garlic is golden and crispy.
25. Keep a close eye on the pan here; you donAt want the oil to get too hot and for the garlic to burn.
26. Reserving the oil as you pour, strain the garlic through a sieve.
27. Set the garlic asideAit will crisp up as it cools downAand return the oil to the pan.
28. Add the coriander seeds and chile and cook for about 1 minute, stirring a few times, until fragrant.
29. Remove from the heat, transfer to a separate bowl, and set aside until needed.
30. When the vegetables are cooked and the sauce is thick and rich (but still pourable), use a slotted spoon to carefully lift the vegetables out of the pan.
31. Pour the sauce onto a large serving platter (or individual serving plates) with a rim and top with the stuffed vegetables.
32. Spoon on the adhaAthe coriander-chile oil first, followed by the fried garlicAthen sprinkle with the fresh herbs and green onion.
33. Serve warm or at room temperature, with the yogurt spooned alongside.

Recipe name: Chicken Meatballs With Molokhieh Garlic And Cilantro
Ingredients: ' oz50g crustless sourdough bread, finely blitzed into crumbs, or 1 large zucchini ( lb220g), coarsely grated', '1 lb 2 oz500g ground chicken or turkey thighs', '2 garlic cloves, crushed', ' tsp ground cumin', '1 tsp ground cinnamon', ' tsp ground allspice', ' tsp chile flakes', ' cup10g cilantro leaves, roughly chopped', ' cup10g parsley leaves, roughly chopped', ' cup10g mint leaves, roughly chopped', '1 lemon: grate finely to get tsp zest, then cut into wedges', 'Salt and black pepper', '2 tbsp olive oil', '2 garlic cloves, crushed', '1 lemon: shave the rind to get 4 strips, then juice to get tbsp', ' cups450ml chicken stock', ' lb800g frozen molokhieh (or lb800g frozen chopped spinach plus 7 oz200g okra, thinly slicedAsee note below)', ' tsp ground cinnamon', 'Salt and black pepper', ' cup10g parsley leaves, roughly chopped', '1 cup20g cilantro leaves, roughly chopped', ' cup olive oil', '6 garlic cloves, crushed', ' tsp chile flakes', ' cup cilantro, roughly chopped'
Instructions:
1. To make the meatballs, place the breadcrumbs in a small bowl and cover with water.
2. Stir, then drain through a colander, squeezing out most of the moisture from the bread. (If using grated zucchini, toss it with tsp of salt and place in a sieve set over a bowl.
3. Let to sit for minutes, then use your hands to squeeze out as much liquid as possible.) Transfer the crumbs to a large bowl with the chicken, garlic, spices, herbs, lemon zest, tsp salt, and plenty of black pepper.
4. Using well-oiled hands, shape into about small balls; roughly a scant 1 oz25g each.
5. Put 1 tbsp of oil into a large nonstick sautA pan with a lid, and place over high heat.
6. Once hot, add half the meatballs and cook for 2A3 minutes, turning throughout, until golden brown all over.
7. Transfer the meatballs to a baking sheet and set aside.
8. Keep the pan on the heat and continue with the remaining 1 tbsp of oil and remaining meatballs in the same way.
9. Return the same pan to medium-high heat.
10. Add the 2 garlic cloves, lemon rind, and stock and bring to a boilAthis should take about 3 minutes.
11. Add the molokhieh (or the spinach and okra combination), cinnamon, 2 tsp of salt, and plenty of black pepper, then decrease the heat to medium and cook, covered, for about minutes, or until the molokhieh is bubbling and completely defrosted.
12. Add the meatballs, parsley, and cup10g of cilantro and cook, covered, for another minutes, or until completely cooked through.
13. Stir in the lemon juice.
14. To make the adha, meanwhile, put the olive oil into a small frying pan over medium-high heat.
15. Add the garlic and cook for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally, or until golden and crispy.
16. Add the chile flakes, then remove from the heat and pour into a bowl.
17. Stir in the cilantro and set aside.
18. Divide the soup among four bowls and top with the fried garlic and remaining cup10g cilantro.
19. Serve at once, with lemon wedges alongside.

Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. ( mL) vegetable oil, divided', '2 Tbsp. ( mL) unsweetened coconut A Aakes', '1 Tbsp. ( mL) coriander seeds', '1 Tbsp. ( mL) dried chana dal', '5 black peppercorns', 'Pinch fenugreek seeds', '1 (2-inch or 5-cm) cinnamon stick', '3 whole cloves', 'Pinch asafoetida (optional)', '1 dried red chile, crushed', '1 onion, chopped', '1 Tbsp. ( mL) kosher salt', '2 green chiles, minced', '2 tsp. ( mL) ground turmeric', '1 tsp. (5 mL) yellow mustard seeds', '1 cup ( mL) white basmati rice, rinsed', '1 cup ( mL) dried lentils (toor or masoor dal)', '2 cups ( mL) chopped trimmed acorn or butternut squash', "2 cups ( mL) chopped mustard greens (see Cooks' Note)", '2 cups ( mL) no-added-salt vegetable broth or water', 'Special Equipment: CoA Aeespice grinder'
Instructions:
1. Using the SautA function on High, heat 1 teaspoon (5 mL) oil in the inner pot for 1 minute, until shimmering.
2. Add the coconut, coriander seeds, chana dal, peppercorns, fenugreek seeds, cinnamon stick, cloves, asafoetida (if using) and dried chile; cook for about 2 minutes, until fragrant.
3. Transfer the mixture to a clean coA Aeespice grinder and grind to a powder.
4. Using the SautA function on High, heat the remaining 5 teaspoons ( mL) oil in the inner pot for 1 minute, until shimmering.
5. Add the onion, salt, green chiles, turmeric and mustard seeds; cook for about 2 minutes, until the mustard seeds begin to pop.
6. Add the rice, lentils, squash, and mustard greens; stir to combine.
7. Stir in the toasted spices, then the broth.
8. Secure the lid and cook on high pressure for 3 minutes.
9. Once the cooking is complete, let the pressure release naturally for minutes, then quick-release the remaining pressure.
10. Remove the lid and stir.
11. Transfer to a bowl; serve.
12. If you canAt find mustard greens, use turnip greens, kale or any other sturdy greens.

Ingredients: ' cup raw walnuts', '3 oz. white chocolate, chopped', 'A cup refined coconut oil', ' cup tahini', '2 tsp. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract', 'Pinch of kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Spread out walnuts on a small rimmed baking sheet; toast, tossing halfway through, until golden brown, minutes.
3. Let cool.
4. Meanwhile, microwave white chocolate, oil, and tahini in a small microwave-proof bowl on low heat in second bursts, stirring after each burst, until melted and combined.
5. Process walnuts in a food processor until a paste forms.
6. Scrape down sides of bowl and add vanilla bean paste and salt and pulse to combine.
7. With motor running, gradually pour in white chocolate mixture and process until smooth.
8. Use immediately.

Ingredients: '3 oz. dry white vermouth', ' oz. fresh lemon juice', ' oz. simple syrup (see note)', '3 orange slices'
Instructions:
1. Combine the vermouth, lemon juice, simple syrup, and 2 of the orange slices in a cocktail shaker and muddle to break down the orange.
2. Add ice cubes to fill the shaker three-quarters full, cover, and shake to chill, about seconds.
3. Fill a rocks glass with ice cubes and strain the cocktail over the ice.
4. Garnish with the remaining orange slice.

Ingredients: '2 strawberries, hulled', '4 oz. rosA wine, chilled', '1 oz. Aperol', ' oz. fresh lemon juice', 'Soda water, chilled'
Instructions:
1. Muddle the strawberries in a mixing glass or cocktail shaker.
2. Add the rosA, Aperol, and lemon juice, and stir to incorporate.
3. Pour into a wineglass, add a handful of ice cubes, and top with a splash of soda water.

Ingredients: '1 cup French green or brown lentils', ' cup whole-milk Greek yogurt', ' tsp. finely grated lemon zest', '1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', '2 garlic cloves, finely grated, divided', 'Kosher salt', '6 oz. crimini mushrooms, stems trimmed, finely chopped', '2 Tbsp. white miso', ' tsp. smoked paprika', '4 Tbsp. (or more) extra-virgin olive oil, divided', ' cup (or more) oat flour, gluten-free 1:1 blend, or all-purpose flour', 'Whole wheat or gluten-free buns, sprouts andor lettuce, and pickle slices (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Place lentils in a large saucepan and pour in water to cover by 4".
2. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender but not mushy, about minutes from the time the water reaches a boil.
3. Drain well, then let cool at least minutes.
4. Meanwhile, mix together yogurt, lemon zest, lemon juice, and half of garlic in a small bowl.
5. Season with salt.
6. Mash mushrooms, miso, paprika, 2 Tbsp. oil, and remaining garlic in a medium bowl with a spoon to combine.
7. Add lentils and mix and smash until partly mashed but with lots of whole lentils still remaining.
8. Add flour and vigorously stir until mixture holds together when squeezed in your hand; if it doesnAt, continue to mash until it does, adding another 1A2 Tbsp. flour if needed.
9. Form into 6 patties about " thick.
10. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet (patties will stick to a regular skillet) over medium.
11. Cook 3 patties until deeply browned and crisp underneath, about 3 minutes.
12. Carefully turn over; cook, adding more oil as needed to maintain a light coating around patties, until deeply browned and crisp on the other side, about 3 minutes.
13. Transfer to a plate.
14. Repeat with remaining patties and 1 Tbsp. oil.
15. Spread cut side of buns with reserved yogurt mixture.
16. Build burgers with buns, patties, sprouts, and pickles.

Ingredients: '3 oz. Noilly Prat dry vermouth', '1 oz. club soda', 'Garnish: 1 cucumber wheel'
Instructions:
1. Build in a chilled wine glass filled with ice.
2. Garnish with the cucumber wheel.

Ingredients: ' cup seasoned rice vinegar', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', ' cup fresh lime juice', ' cup soy sauce', '2 Tbsp. toasted sesame seeds', '1 tsp. mild red pepper flakes (such as Aleppo-style or Maras)', '1 tsp. toasted sesame oil', ' oz. fresh or oz. dried ramen noodles', 'Kosher salt', '5-6 cups shredded or shaved vegetables (such as radishes, carrots, scallions, cabbage, lettuce, zucchini, andor cucumbers)'
Instructions:
1. Whisk vinegar, olive oil, lime juice, soy sauce, sesame seeds, mild red pepper flakes, and sesame oil in a small bowl to combine.
2. Set dressing aside.
3. Cook noodles in a large pot of boiling salted water according to package directions.
4. Drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking.
5. Transfer to a large bowl, add half of reserved dressing, and toss to coat.
6. Divide noodles among bowls.
7. Top with vegetables and drizzle with remaining dressing.

Ingredients: '2 cups short-grain white sushi rice, rinsed until water runs clear', '2 tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt, plus more', '3 tsp. sugar', ' cup unseasoned rice vinegar, divided', ' cup pure maple syrup', ' cup red or white miso', ' cup tamari or regular soy sauce', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes, plus more for serving', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '4 (5A6-oz.) skin-on salmon fillets', '6 scallions, thinly sliced', '1 Tbsp. toasted sesame seeds', 'Lime wedges (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Bring rice, a pinch of salt and cups cold water to a boil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat.
2. Stir once, cover, and reduce heat to low.
3. Cook until water evaporates and rice is tender, minutes.
4. Remove from heat and let sit, covered, minutes.
5. Whisk sugar, cup vinegar, and 2 tsp.
6. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
7. Morton kosher salt in a small bowl until sugar dissolves.
8. Stir into rice; re-cover.
9. Let sit until ready to use.
10. Whisk maple syrup, miso, tamari, tsp. red pepper flakes, and remaining cup vinegar in a small bowl.
11. Set glaze aside.
12. Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium.
13. Season salmon with salt; cook, skin side down, shaking pan occasionally, until skin is very crisp and deep golden brown, 7A9 minutes.
14. Turn over and cook just until flesh is very lightly browned ( it should be nearly cooked through), about 2 minutes.
15. Using a spatula to hold back fish, pour off fat from skillet.
16. Reduce heat to low and add reserved glaze to skillet; cook, stirring occasionally, until it begins to thicken, about 1 minute.
17. Baste salmon with glaze and cook, basting occasionally, until glaze evenly coats fish, about 1 minute.
18. Divide rice among plates and top with salmon.
19. Drizzle with any leftover glaze over; sprinkle with scallions, sesame seeds, and red pepper flakes.
20. Serve with lime wedges if desired.

Recipe name: Grilled Carrots With Avocado And Mint
Ingredients: '1 tsp. cumin seeds', '3 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', '2 tsp. honey', ' cup plus 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 serrano chile, seeds removed if desired, thinly sliced', '1 (1") piece ginger, peeled, finely grated', 'Kosher salt', ' lb. medium carrots with tops (about 5), tops trimmed to about ", scrubbed, halved lengthwise', '2 avocados, cut into large pieces', ' cup mint leaves'
Instructions:
1. Prepare a grill for medium heat.
2. Toast cumin seeds in a dry small skillet over medium heat, tossing often, until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
3. Let cool.
4. Coarsely crush cumin seeds with a mortar and pestle (or on a cutting board with the flat side of a chefAs knife).
5. Transfer to a large bowl.
6. Add lemon juice and honey.
7. Whisk in cup oil, then add chile and ginger.
8. Season dressing with salt.
9. Let sit while you grill carrots (this will give the chile and ginger time to infuse into dressing).
10. Toss carrots and remaining 2 Tbsp. oil on a rimmed baking sheet to coat; season with salt.
11. Grill carrots, turning occasionally, until lightly charred in spots and tender, minutes.
12. Immediately transfer carrots to bowl with dressing and toss to coat.
13. Taste and season with more salt if needed.
14. Arrange carrots and avocados on a platter and spoon any dressing left in bowl over.
15. Top with mint.

Ingredients: '8 oz. red or black plums (2A ), pitted, chopped', '3 Tbsp. light brown sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' cup ( g) walnuts', ' cup ( g) black andor white sesame seeds', '1 cup pure maple syrup, preferably grade B', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, plus more for pan', ' cup (packed; g) light brown sugar', '2 Tbsp. tahini', '2 tsp. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract', ' tsp. kosher salt', '1 chilled large egg', '1 cup ( g) all-purpose flour', ' tsp. baking powder', '5 pints high-quality vanilla ice cream, divided', 'A 9" springform pan'
Instructions:
1. Bring plums, brown sugar, and salt to a boil in a medium heavy saucepan over medium heat, stirring constantly.
2. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally, until fruit is softened and juices deepen in color, about 8 minutes.
3. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve set over a medium bowl, pushing plum flesh through; discard skins.
4. Return sauce to pan and bring to a simmer, stirring occasionally.
5. Cook, still stirring, until reduced to about A cup, about 3 minutes.
6. Pour ripple into a jar and let cool, then cover and chill 1 hour before using.
7. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
8. Place walnuts on 1 side of a rimmed baking sheet; season with salt.
9. Toast, tossing halfway through, 8 minutes (nuts should not be quiet golden brown yet).
10. Remove from oven and add sesame seeds on empty side of baking sheet.
11. Toast until nuts and seeds are golden brown, about 2 minutes.
12. Let cool; chop nuts.
13. Combine nuts, sesame seeds, maple syrup, and salt in a small jar or bowl and stir well.
14. Cover and set aside.
15. Lightly butter pan and line with a parchment paper round.
16. Heat brown sugar, tahini, and remaining cup (1 stick) unsalted butter until bubbling in a medium heavy saucepan over medium heat, stirring until butter is melted and sugar is dissolved.
17. Cook, without stirring, 1 minute.
18. Let cool slightly.
19. Stir vanilla bean paste and salt followed by egg into brown sugar mixture.
20. Sift flour and baking powder through a fine-mesh sieve into mixture and fold to barely combine.
21. Scrape batter into the prepared pan.
22. Bake until golden around the edges, evenly tan across the top, and puffed in the center, about minutes.
23. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let blondie base cool.
24. Run a knife around the sides to help base release but keep springform pan intact, then cover and chill in freezer at least minutes or until ready to use.
25. Cut 3 pints of ice cream out of their containers and place in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment. (Alternatively, you can beat in a large bowl with a wooden spoon or stiff rubber spatula until the consistency of cake batter.) Mix on low speed 2 minutes, then working up to medium speed, beat until barely pliable.
26. It will soften further as it sits, but you want it to be sticky and slightly challenging to spread when you assemble cake.
27. Chill in the freezer at any point if it becomes too soft and melted.
28. Remove blondie base and remaining 2 still-frozen pints of ice cream from freezer.
29. Using an offset spatula, spread a third of softened ice cream over base.
30. Using a spring-loaded ice cream scoop, scoop out and place mounds of 1-pint still-frozen ice cream on top in a random manner.
31. Dollop one third of maple ripple over ice cream, avoiding edges of pan.
32. Next, drizzle one third of plum ripple over ice cream, creating stripes.
33. Repeat layering process, using half of remaining softened ice cream, all of remaining frozen ice cream, half of remaining maple ripple, and half of remaining plum ripple.
34. Top with a final layer of remaining softened ice cream, smooth surface, then drizzle with remaining maple ripple and plum ripple. (If at any point the ice cream gets so soft that you canAt achieve distinct layers, chill in freezer for a few minutes.) Freeze, uncovered, 2 hours, then cover and freeze at least 8 hours before serving.
35. To serve, run a knife or offset spatula around edge of pan to release.
36. Unmold and remove parchment paper; discard.
37. Transfer cake to a platter, smooth sides with an offset spatula, and freeze minutes.
38. Slice with a warm knife, wiping off between cuts.

Ingredients: '1 lb. underripe stone fruit (such as plums, nectarines, or peaches), pears, or mangoes, thinly sliced', '1 serrano chile, seeds removed if desired, thinly sliced', '2 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 Tbsp. sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '1 bunch cilantro', '4 scallions, thinly sliced on a diagonal', '2 celery stalks, thinly sliced on a diagonal', '2 tsp. toasted sesame oil', '1 tsp. low-sodium soy sauce', 'Zest of 1 lime', '2 Tbsp. toasted sesame seeds', 'Lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Gently toss fruit, chile, vinegar, sugar, and tsp. salt in a medium bowl to combine.
2. Let sit until fruit is spicy and flavorful, about minutes.
3. Meanwhile, gather cilantro into a bundle and cut into 2"-long pieces.
4. Transfer to a medium bowl, add scallions and celery, and toss to combine.
5. Drain fruit in a fine-mesh sieve set over a small bowl; set fruit aside.
6. Add oil, soy sauce, and lime zest to marinating liquid in bowl and whisk to combine.
7. Pour dressing over cilantro mixture and toss to coat.
8. Add reserved fruit and sesame seeds and toss gently to combine.
9. Taste and season with more salt if needed.
10. Transfer salad to a platter and serve with lime wedges.

Recipe name: Grilled Pork Shoulder With Butter Vinegar Sauce
Ingredients: '1 Tbsp. fennel seeds', '2 Tbsp. dried oregano', '-lb. boneless pork shoulder (Boston butt)', 'Grapeseed or other neutral oil (for pork)', '1 Tbsp. kosher salt, plus more', '3 chiles de Arbol, torn in half, seeds removed if desired', ' cup apple cider vinegar', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter', '2 Tbsp. sugar', 'Special Equipment: A spice mill or mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Toast fennel seeds in a dry small skillet over medium heat, tossing often, until golden brown and starting to pop, about 3 minutes.
2. Transfer to a spice mill or mortar and pestle and let cool; coarsely grind.
3. Transfer to a small bowl and mix in oregano.
4. Place pork on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet and rub with oil.
5. Season all over with 1 Tbsp. salt, then rub with spice mixture, packing it on.
6. Let sit at room temperature 2 hours or chill, uncovered, up to 2 days.
7. Bring chiles, vinegar, butter, and sugar to a simmer in a small saucepan over medium-high heat; cook, stirring occasionally, until sugar is dissolved and sauce is reduced by about a third, about 5 minutes.
8. Season with salt; cover and keep warm over low heat until ready to use.
9. Prepare a grill for medium heat.
10. Drizzle pork all over with oil, then grill, turning every 5 minutes or so, until extremely, gorgeously browned and crisp and an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part registers , minutes.
11. Continue to grill, brushing with sauce and turning not quite every minute (youAre going to see flare-ups, but donAt worry), until pork is glazed and shiny and temperature on thermometer has risen to , minutes more.
12. Transfer pork to a platter and let rest minutes; reserve remaining sauce.
13. Transfer pork to a cutting board and pour any accumulated juices on platter into reserved sauce.
14. Return sauce to a simmer.
15. Slice pork against the grain " thick.
16. Arrange on platter, spoon some sauce over, and season with salt.
17. Serve remaining sauce alongside.
18. If using a charcoal grill, a bed of medium-hot coals will gradually lose heat.
19. Get a chimney started when the meat first hits the grill.
20. About halfway through, throw some new glowing coals on the pile to keep things going.

Recipe name: No-Churn Almond And Raspberry Swirl Ice Cream
Ingredients: '3 cups ( g) fresh raspberries', '2 Tbsp. sugar', '1 tsp. kosher salt, divided', '1 (-oz.) can sweetened condensed milk', '1 tsp. almond extract', '2 cups heavy cream'
Instructions:
1. Bring raspberries, sugar, and tsp. salt to a simmer in a medium saucepan over medium heat and cook, stirring often, until raspberries begin to burst and lose their shape, about 4 minutes.
2. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a large measuring glass, pressing on solids; discard solids.
3. Pour back into saucepan and return to medium heat.
4. Cook, stirring occasionally, until sauce is thick enough to coat a spoon, minutes. (You should have about 6 Tbsp.) Transfer back to measuring glass and chill until cold, about minutes.
5. Mix condensed milk, almond extract, and remaining tsp. salt in a medium bowl to combine.
6. Using an electric mixer, beat cream in a medium bowl, starting on low speed and gradually increasing to medium-high as it thickens, until billowy and stiff peaks form, about 4 minutes.
7. Add a dollop of whipped cream to condensed milk mixture and mix in with a rubber spatula (this is just to lighten it so that the next stageAfoldingAis easier).
8. Add remaining whipped cream; fold in, running spatula down sides and along bottom of bowl, then lifting up through center and over top while rotating bowl to integrate without deflating, until very few streaks of condensed milk mixture remain.
9. Scrape about one third of ice cream base into a loaf pan thatAs at least 8A12x4A12".
10. Drizzle over one third of raspberry syrup.
11. Repeat layers 2 more times, ending with raspberry syrup (no need to be precise).
12. Using a small offset spatula or butter knife, make figure-eight motions through ice cream base to swirl raspberry syrup throughout.
13. Cover with plastic wrap or an airtight silicone lid and freeze until solid, at least 8 hours.
14. To serve, transfer loaf pan to refrigerator and let ice cream soften minutes before scooping into bowls.
15. Do Ahead: Ice cream can be made 1 week ahead.
16. Keep frozen.

Recipe name: No-Churn Fresh Mint And Chocolate Ice Cream
Ingredients: ' cup ( g) coarsely chopped mint', '2 cups (or more) heavy cream, divided', '4 oz. bittersweet chocolate (%% cacao), coarsely chopped', '1 (-oz.) can sweetened condensed milk', '1 tsp. kosher salt', ' cup ( g) Dutch-process cocoa powder'
Instructions:
1. Bring mint and 1 cup cream to a bare simmer in a small saucepan over medium heat.
2. Remove from heat, cover, and let sit minutes.
3. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a measuring glass, pressing on solids; discard solids.
4. Chill cream until very cold, about minutes.
5. Just before cream is ready, heat chocolate in a heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water (do not let bowl touch water), stirring occasionally, until melted and smooth. (Alternatively, heat in -second increments in a microwave, stirring between bursts.) Mix together sweetened condensed milk and salt in a medium bowl.
6. Pour remaining 1 cup cream into infused cream in measuring glass. (You should have 2 cups; add more if needed.) Transfer to a medium bowl.
7. Using an electric mixer, beat cream, starting on low speed and gradually increasing to medium-high as it thickens, until billowy and stiff peaks form, about 4 minutes.
8. Add a dollop of whipped cream to condensed milk mixture and mix in with a rubber spatula (this is just to lighten it so that the next stageAfoldingAis easier).
9. Add remaining whipped cream, then sift in cocoa powder.
10. Fold in, running spatula down sides and along bottom of bowl, then lifting up through center and over top while rotating bowl to integrate without deflating, until very few streaks of condensed milk mixture remain and cocoa powder is combined.
11. Scrape about one quarter of ice cream base into a loaf pan thatAs at least 8A12x4A12".
12. Using a fork, drizzle about one third of melted chocolate over.
13. Repeat layering process, finishing with a layer of ice cream base, to create 4 layers of ice cream base total and 3 layers of drizzled chocolate.
14. Cover with plastic wrap or an airtight silicone lid and freeze until solid, at least 8 hours.
15. To serve, transfer loaf pan to refrigerator and let ice cream soften minutes before scooping into bowls.
16. Do Ahead: Ice cream can be made 1 week ahead.
17. Keep frozen.

Ingredients: '4 egg yolks, beaten', 'One 1-lb ( g) package frozen grated cassava, thawed', '1 cup ( g) jarred macapuno coconut strings, or sweetened shredded coconut', ' cups ( ml) canned unsweetened coconut milk', ' cup ( ml) sweetened condensed milk', '4 egg whites', ' cup ( ml) sweetened condensed milk'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. To make the cake, combine the egg yolks, cassava, macapuno (or shredded coconut, if using), coconut milk and the cup sweetened condensed milk in a large bowl and mix well.
3. Pour the cake batter into a greased 8 x 8-in ( x -cm) cake pan.
4. The cake batter will appear to be very loose and wet at this point, but donAt worry, the cassava will absorb most of the liquid during baking.
5. Place the cake pan into the oven and bake until the top of the cake appears dry and no liquid is floating on the surface, minutes.
6. Meanwhile, to make the topping, whisk together the egg whites and the cup ( ml) sweetened condensed milk in a medium bowl until very well incorporated.
7. Although you are using egg whites, donAt be concerned with making a foam or a meringue, just mix until combined.
8. Remove the cake from the oven and evenly pour the topping onto the cake.
9. Return the cake to the oven and bake until the topping sets, about minutes more.
10. Remove the cake from the oven and rest the cake in its pan on a cooling rack.
11. Cool the cake completely before cutting it into squares.
12. The cassava cake can be served at room temperature, or cold out of the refrigerator.

Ingredients: ' large peaches (about pounds), unpeeled, cut into 1-inch chunks', 'Zest and juice of 1 large lemon', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, softened', '2 cups sugar, divided', ' cups (about grams) all-purpose flour', '2 teaspoons baking powder', '1 teaspoon kosher salt', ' cup whole milk', ' cup hot tap water', 'Heavy cream, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to degrees F.
2. Put the peaches in a 9-by--inch (or similar) baking pan or gratin dish.
3. Pat the peaches into a roughly even layer, then, using a zester or a Microplane, zest the lemon evenly over the fruit and squeeze the lemon juice evenly over the top.
4. In the work bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, cream the butter and cups of the sugar on medium speed until sandy, about 1 minute.
5. Add the flour, baking powder, and salt, and beat again for another seconds, until all the flour is incorporated and the mixture is evenly crumbly.
6. With the mixer on low speed, slowly add in the milk.
7. Increase the speed to medium and beat until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes.
8. Plop the batter in 6 large blobs over the top of the peaches.
9. With an offset spatula or small knife, carefully spread the batter evenly over the fruit, so itAs no more than about inch thick in any one place.
10. Sprinkle the remaining cup sugar directly over the batter.
11. Drizzle the hot water evenly over the sugar, using it to melt the sugar into the topping. (Use it all.
12. ItAs a strange method, but it works.) Bake the cobbler for to minutes, or until the top is browned and cracked. (A toothpick inserted into the topping should come out dryAbe sure to check in a few places.) Let the cobbler sit for about a half an hour to firm up before serving warm in big bowls, with heavy cream poured on top.

Ingredients: '2 cups all-purpose flour', ' cup cornstarch', '2 teaspoons baking powder', '2 teaspoons sugar', '1 teaspoon doenjang (Korean fermentedsoybean paste)', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', '2 cups ice-cold club soda', '1 medium egg yolk', '2 teaspoons minced garlic', '3 bunches scallions, cut into 2-inch batons (if the scallions are thick, cut the white parts lengthwise in half before cutting into batons)', 'About 5 tablespoons grape seed or canola oil, for frying', 'Pajeon Sauce'
Instructions:
1. To make the batter, combine the flour, cornstarch, baking powder, sugar, doenjang, and pepper in a medium bowl and mix well to blend.
2. Add the club soda, egg yolk, and garlic and mix gently using a whisk.
3. Do not whisk too much, or extra gluten will form in the batter, making it too thick and doughy.
4. Whisk about times, then let the batter rest for minutes in the freezer so any remaining small clumps of flour can dissolve and blend into the mixture by themselves.
5. When youAre ready to make the pancakes, line a sheet pan with paper towels and set aside.
6. Fold the scallions into the cold batter.
7. Set a -inch nonstick sautA pan over high heat and add about 1 tablespoon of the oil.
8. When the oil begins to shimmer and just barely smoke, add 1 cup of the scallion batter to the center of the pan.
9. Using a spatula, spread it out to form a 7-inch pancake. (You donAt want the pancake to touch the sides of the pan, or the edges may burn before the center is cooked through.) Reduce the heat to medium and cook for 4 to 5 minutes, until the bottom of the pancake has set.
10. Once it has set, gently slide your spatula under the edges of the pancake and lift them, tilting the pan, so some of the hot oil runs underneath the pancake.
11. Then cook for 2 to 3 minutes more, until the bottom is a deep golden brown and crisp.
12. You can check the color by gently lifting an edge of the pancake with your spatula.
13. Flip the pancake and cook on the other side for 3 to 4 minutes, until it is golden brown on the second side and cooked through.
14. Transfer to the prepared sheet pan to drain.
15. Wipe out the pan, set it back over high heat, add another tablespoon or so of oil, and repeat until all of the pancakes are cooked.
16. The finished pancakes can be kept in a low () oven on a clean sheet pan while you cook the remaining batches, but itAs best to eat them right away.
17. Cut each pancake into quarters and serve with small bowls of the Pajeon Sauce and ice-cold makgeolli (Korean rice wine).

Ingredients: '2 tablespoons water', '2 tablespoons rice vinegar', '6 tablespoons soy sauce', '1 tablespoon gochugaru (Korean red chili flakes)', '2 garlic cloves, minced', ' teaspoon minced ginger', '1 teaspoon toasted sesame oil', 'Sliced fresh red chile, optional'
Instructions:
1. Combine all the ingredients in a bowl and mix well.
2. Use immediately, or store for up to 2 weeks in a tightly sealed container in the refrigerator.
3. Shake or stir well before using.

Ingredients: 'A cup vegetable oil', '4 Tbsp. whole black mustard seeds (or 4 tbsp. store-bought coarsely crushed mustard seeds)', '3 Tbsp. whole fenugreek seeds (or 3 Tbsp. store-bought coarsely crushed fenugreek)', '1 tsp. asafetida powder', '1 Tbsp. ground cayenne (to taste) or 1 large serrano pepper, deseeded, and sliced thin', 'A cup julienned ginger (optional, see Pre-Prep below)', '2-4 Tbsp. white vinegar', '2 tsp. kosher salt (optional)', '2 cups mixed vegetables, prepped as follows'
Instructions:
1. Pre-Prep: Trim fresh vegetables like cauliflower, carrots, radish, and others into bite sized pieces, and no larger than the container they will be stored in.
2. Flash steam firm vegetables like cauliflower and carrots in a colander over boiling water for just a minute or two: the pieces should still be firm.
3. Lay them on a kitchen towel to dry.
4. If using fresh turmeric, cover the chopping board in plastic wrap, and wear gloves.
