5. Scrape off the skin with a spoon as you would for ginger and rinse it clean to wash away any tough bits.
6. Pat each fingerling down and slice into thin rounds or to match the ginger, and place on a paper towel to air dry.
7. All vegetables must be dry to touch before pickling.
8. To prepare ginger, peel and slice ginger into eighth-inch-thick slices, and chop them to look like carrot sticks.
9. Spread on a paper towel and set aside to air dry for - minutes.
10. If using whole mustard seeds or whole fenugreek seeds, in two separate sets, crush each in a spice mill in short bursts until they each are broken down but not powdered.
11. They should be the consistency of coffee grounds.
12. Method: Heat the oil in a large, wide mouth saucepan until it is hot but not smoking.
13. Reduce the heat to low.
14. Slowly add all the spices: mustard halves, fenugreek halves, asafetida powder, cayenne pepper if using, along with the serrano pepper if using, and ginger sticks.
15. Add vinegar and salt.
16. Allow this to sizzle for a minute on low.
17. Stir to allow the heat to distribute evenly.
18. When the oil is aromatic, slowly add the trimmed vegetables and stir in well.
19. Turn up the heat and let this cook for 4-5 minutes until vegetables appear to have absorbed the spices a little.
20. Turn off the heat and cover with a paper towel to capture any steam.
21. Allow this to come to room temperature before storing.
22. To Store: Use smaller (4 A 6 oz.) sanitized and dried glass jars to store the pickle.
23. Using a clean and dry spoon that is smaller than the mouth of the jar, ladle out portions of the pickle into each jar.
24. DonAt worry if you donAt get the oils or juices when first filling the jars.
25. Fill jars at least a half inch below the rim of the jar.
26. Shake them once to allow the pieces to settle.
27. Divide up any of the liquid over each of these jars, taking care that each jar has some of the spicy sauce.
28. Lightly shake each jar to ensure the liquid finds its way into the nooks and crannies of vegetable pieces.
29. Close the lid and store in the fridge.
30. The pickle is ready to eat immediately after it is made, but the flavors are pronounced after a day or two.
31. Serve using a clean and dry fork or spoon for each serving to avoid contamination.

Ingredients: ' cup finely diced red or white onions', ' tsp. fine sea salt, plus additional to taste', '1 mango (Australian preferred), skin removed, diced', ' tsp. ground cumin', ' tsp. ground coriander', '1 tsp. ground cayenne', '4 Tbsp. finely grated jaggery', 'Additional salt, to taste'
Instructions:
1. Dust the onions with the salt and set aside for minutes to let the onions sweat.
2. Using a muslin cloth or strainer, extract and remove as much of the onion juice as possible.
3. Rinse once after done and squeeze any remaining water away from the onions.
4. Spread the onions onto a paper towel for 5 minutes.
5. Mix all remaining ingredients in a large bowl until well combined.
6. Stir carefully to keep the mango pieces intact.
7. Add onion and mix again.
8. Taste for salt, and adjust if needed.
9. This salsa is best consumed cold, within 2 days of preparation.

Ingredients: '3 cups all-purpose flour, plus more if needed', ' cup pork lard (or substitute vegetable shortening), chilled', '1 tablespoon kosher salt', 'About cup hot water, plus more if needed'
Instructions:
1. Mix the flour and pork lard with your fingers until the mixture is the texture of coarse sand.
2. Then add the salt with the hot (but not scalding) water and knead well.
3. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour; if itAs a little dry, add more hot water.
4. The kneaded dough should be slightly tacky but not sticking to the bowl.
5. Cover with plastic wrap and let sit at room temperature for 1 hour.
6. Divide the dough into 8 pieces and roll each one into a round ball.
7. Roll each ball between sheets of parchment paper or plastic wrap into as thin a round as possible (the rounds will contract a bit when you stop rolling).
8. As you roll each one, place it on a hot nonstick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
9. Cook for about seconds, or until the tortilla starts to puff.
10. Then flip and cook another seconds.
11. It should be cooked through and just speckled with golden brown spots.
12. As the tortillas cook, place them in your serving dish covered with a dish towel, which will help keep the tortillas soft and pliable.

Recipe name: Grilling Cheese With Sweet Peppers And Black Lentils
Ingredients: 'Kosher salt', ' cup black beluga lentils', '3 Tbsp. sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar', '1 Tbsp. honey', '5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '8 oz. grilling cheese (such as bread, Halloumi, or paneer), torn into 2" pieces', '1 lb. sweet mini peppers, ribs and seeds removed, halved lengthwise, cut into thirds if large', ' tsp. dried oregano', ' cup (packed) basil leaves, torn if large', 'Flaky sea salt'
Instructions:
1. Bring a medium pot of salted water to a boil.
2. Add lentils and cook until just tender but not falling apart (they should hold their shape), about minutes.
3. Drain well.
4. Meanwhile, whisk vinegar, honey, and 3 Tbsp. oil in a small bowl to combine; season with kosher salt and pepper.
5. Set dressing aside.
6. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
7. Add cheese and cook, turning occasionally, until heated through and brown and crispy on all sides, about 5 minutes total. (Some varieties of paneer are made without salt; if using one of these, season your pieces of cheese while cooking.) Transfer to a plate.
8. Add remaining 1 Tbsp. oil to same pan, then add sweet peppers and oregano.
9. Season with kosher salt and black pepper and cook over medium-high, stirring and pressing down occasionally on sweet peppers with a wooden spoon so they make good contact with the pan, until softened and blistered in spots, minutes.
10. Remove from heat.
11. Add warm lentils and reserved dressing to pan and toss to combine, then toss in cheese.
12. Transfer sweet pepper mixture to a platter or plates.
13. Top with basil and season with sea salt and more black pepper.

Ingredients: ' cup raw pistachios', ' prunes, pits removed, coarsely chopped', '1 garlic clove, finely grated', '2 cups finely chopped parsley', '1 cup finely chopped mint', ' cup finely chopped tarragon', ' cup kimchi or sauerkraut brine', '2 Tbsp. Champagne vinegar or white wine vinegar', 'Kosher salt', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', '1 (2A4-lb.) flatfish (such as sole, flounder, or turbot)', 'Extra-virgin olive oil', 'Kosher salt', 'Aleppo-style pepper'
Instructions:
1. Toast pistachios in a dry small skillet over medium-low heat, tossing occasionally, until browned in spots, about 5 minutes.
2. Let cool, then finely chop.
3. Gently mix pistachios, prunes, garlic, parsley, mint, tarragon, kimchi brine, and vinegar in a medium bowl to combine; season with salt.
4. Whisk in oil, then taste and season spoon sauce with more salt if needed.
5. Prepare a grill for medium heat.
6. Using a chefAs knife, cleaver, or serrated knife, cut fish crosswise into 2""-wide planks (the larger the fish, the wider the planks); halve planks if fish is large.
7. Pat fish dry, rub all over with oil, and season with salt and Aleppo-style pepper.
8. Grill fish, undisturbed, until about percent cooked through (use a knife to slide into flesh to check), about 5 minutes.
9. The skin should release easily from the grate when the fish is ready to turn.
10. If itAs sticking, keep cooking.
11. Using a large fish spatula, turn fish over and grill until cooked through (fish is done when a paring knife easily pierces the flesh along the side), about 2 minutes.
12. Transfer fish to a platter and spoon sauce over.

Ingredients: '2 lb. assorted mushrooms (such as crimini, shiitake, andor maitake), wiped clean, trimmed, torn into big pieces if large', '7 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', 'Kosher salt', '2 Tbsp. Champagne vinegar or white wine vinegar', '1 tsp. Aleppo-style pepper', '1 tsp. dried oregano', '1 garlic clove, finely grated', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '2 oz. Parmesan, shaved', ' cup Castelvetrano olives, coarsely chopped', ' cup drained Peppadew peppers in brine, coarsely chopped'
Instructions:
1. Prepare a grill for high heat.
2. Toss mushrooms and 3 Tbsp. oil in a large bowl to coat.
3. Grill, turning occasionally with tongs, until lightly charred, 2A6 minutes (depending on size and type).
4. Return to bowl; season with salt.
5. Whisk vinegar, Aleppo-style pepper, oregano, garlic, and remaining 4 Tbsp. oil in a small bowl to combine; season vinaigrette with salt and black pepper.
6. Pour over mushrooms and toss to coat.
7. Add Parmesan, olives, and Peppadew peppers; gently toss to combine.

Ingredients: ' cup raw pistachios', ' prunes, pits removed, coarsely chopped', '1 garlic clove, finely grated', '2 cups finely chopped parsley', '1 cup finely chopped mint', ' cup finely chopped tarragon', ' cup kimchi or sauerkraut brine', '2 Tbsp. Champagne vinegar or white wine vinegar', 'Kosher salt', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil'
Instructions:
1. Toast pistachios in a dry small skillet over medium-low heat, tossing occasionally, until browned in spots, about 5 minutes.
2. Let cool, then finely chop.
3. Gently mix pistachios, prunes, garlic, parsley, mint, tarragon, kimchi brine, and vinegar in a medium bowl to combine; season with salt.
4. Whisk in oil, then taste and season spoon sauce with more salt if needed.

Recipe name: No-Churn Labneh And Lime Ice Cream With Granola
Ingredients: ' cup labneh (Lebanese strained yogurt) or 1 cup plain whole-milk Greek yogurt', ' cup ( g) granola', '1 (-oz.) can sweetened condensed milk', 'Zest and juice of 2 limes', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' cups heavy cream', 'Special Equipment: 2 layers of cheesecloth'
Instructions:
1. If using Greek yogurt, set a fine-mesh sieve over a bowl; line with cheesecloth.
2. Place yogurt in sieve and let drain in refrigerator 4 hours (you should have cup); discard drained liquid in bowl.
3. Pulse granola in a food processor to break up a bit. (Or roll up in a kitchen towel and gently crush with a rolling pin.) Whisk labneh (or strained yogurt), condensed milk, lime zest and juice, and salt in a medium bowl until smooth.
4. Using an electric mixer, beat cream in a medium bowl, starting on low speed and gradually increasing to medium-high as it thickens, until billowy and stiff peaks form, about 4 minutes.
5. Add a dollop of whipped cream to labneh mixture and mix in with a rubber spatula (this is just to lighten it so that the next stageAfoldingAis easier).
6. Add remaining whipped cream and fold in, running spatula down sides and along bottom of bowl, then lifting up through center and over top while rotating bowl to integrate without deflating, until very few streaks of labneh mixture remain.
7. Fold in granola; scrape ice cream base into a loaf pan thatAs at least 8A12x4A12".
8. Cover with plastic wrap or an airtight silicone lid and freeze until solid, at least 8 hours.
9. To serve, transfer loaf pan to refrigerator and let ice cream soften minutes before scooping into bowls.
10. Do Ahead: Ice cream can be made 1 week ahead.
11. Keep frozen.

Recipe name: Grilled Eggplant With Garlicky Tahini-Yogurt Sauce
Ingredients: '2 lb. assorted small eggplant (such as fairy tale andor Graffiti)', '2 heads of garlic', '1 cup plain whole-milk Greek yogurt', '1 Tbsp. tahini', 'Kosher salt', '1 lemon, halved', 'Sumac (for serving)', "Brad's Spoon Sauce", 'Ingredient Info: Sumac, a tart, citrusy spice generally sold in ground form, can be found in the spice aisle and online.'
Instructions:
1. Prepare a grill for medium-high indirect heat (for a charcoal grill, bank coals on one side of grill; for a gas grill, leave one or two burners off).
2. Grill eggplant over indirect heat, turning occasionally, until completely tender, hours (depending on size and type).
3. Meanwhile, grill whole garlic heads over indirect heat until cloves are tender and jammy, minutes.
4. Mix together yogurt and tahini in a small bowl; season with salt.
5. Slice heads of garlic in half crosswise and squeeze cloves into yogurt mixture; stir well to incorporate.
6. Cut a slit lengthwise down each eggplant and carefully pull open with your hands.
7. Arrange on a platter and squeeze juice from lemon over; season with salt.
8. Dollop yogurt sauce around and sprinkle with sumac.
9. Serve with spoon sauce.

Recipe name: Grilled Potatoes With Red Miso Butter
Ingredients: ' lb. baby Yukon Gold potatoes', ' cup Diamond Crystal or cup Morton kosher salt', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '2 Tbsp. red miso', '1 garlic clove, finely chopped', '1 Tbsp. seasoned rice vinegar', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '2 Tbsp. chopped parsley'
Instructions:
1. Prepare a grill for medium-high heat.
2. Bring potatoes, salt, and 1 quart water to a boil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat.
3. Reduce heat and simmer until tender, minutes; drain.
4. Grill potatoes, turning often with tongs, until lightly charred, 6A8 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, set a medium skillet, preferably cast iron, on grill.
6. Place butter and miso in skillet and cook, stirring, until butter is melted and mixture is smooth, about 2 minutes.
7. Remove from heat; stir in garlic and vinegar.
8. Season with pepper.
9. Transfer potatoes to skillet, breaking some in half with tongs.
10. Toss to coat in miso butter.
11. Add parsley; toss again.

Ingredients: '6 Tbsp. Diamond Crystal or 3 Tbsp. plus tsp. Morton kosher salt', '6 ears of corn, in husk'
Instructions:
1. Combine salt and 3 quarts water in a large pot; stir until salt is dissolved.
2. Add corn; let sit at room temperature at least 2 hours, or cover and chill up to hours.
3. Prepare a grill for high heat.
4. Drain corn and grill until husks are lightly charred all over, about minutes.
5. Wrap in a clean towel and place in a cooler.
6. Let steam at least minutes and up to 3 hours.

Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. black peppercorns', '4 Tbsp. pink peppercorns, divided', '6 tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt, divided', '1 (-lb.) whole chicken', '1 Tbsp. vegetable oil, plus more for grill', '2 medium Fresno chiles, thinly sliced', ' cup fermented garlic honey, honey, or pure maple syrup', '3 Tbsp. Champagne vinegar or white wine vinegar', 'Special Equipment: A spice mill or mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Finely grind black peppercorns and 2 Tbsp. pink peppercorns in a spice mill or mortar and pestle.
2. Transfer to a small bowl and mix in 5 tsp.
3. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
4. Morton kosher salt; set seasoning aside.
5. Place chicken, breast side down, on a cutting board and use kitchen shears to cut along both sides of the backbone to remove (freeze and save it for stock!).
6. Open chicken and turn skin side up.
7. Press down on center of breast to flatten chickenAyou should hear the breastbone crack.
8. Using a chefAs knife or cleaver, split chicken in half through breastbone.
9. Pat dry; rub all over with 1 Tbsp. oil.
10. Sprinkle reserved seasoning all over, making sure to get into all the nooks and crannies.
11. Place on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet.
12. Chill, uncovered, at least 8 hours and up to 2 days.
13. Coarsely grind remaining 2 Tbsp. pink peppercorns in spice mill or mortar and pestle.
14. Cook chiles, honey, vinegar, and remaining 1 tsp.
15. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
16. Morton kosher salt over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until mixture just turns amber in color and is reduced by about half (it should coat a spoon), minutes.
17. Stir in pink pepper and set aside.
18. Prepare a grill for medium heat.
19. Lightly oil grate.
20. Set chicken on grate, cover, positioning vent over chicken if your grill has one, and grill, turning every 5 minutes, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of thighs registers , minutes.
21. Continue to grill, basting with honey mixture and turning chicken every 2A3 minutes, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of thighs registers and in the thickest part of breast, minutes longer.
22. Transfer to a cutting board and place skin side up.
23. Let rest minutes before carving.

Recipe name: Pork Shoulder Steaks With Horseradish-Mustard Sauce
Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. finely grated peeled horseradish or 1 Tbsp. (packed) drained prepared horseradish', '2 Tbsp. Dijon mustard', '1 Tbsp. whole grain mustard', '1 tsp. honey', 'Kosher salt', '1 toasted nori sheet (optional)', '1 Tbsp. ground coriander', '1 Tbsp. mustard powder', '2 tsp. light brown sugar', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', '1 tsp. garlic powder', 'Vegetable oil (for grill)', '4 ("-thick) pork shoulder steaks ( lb.)', 'Kosher salt', '2 Tbsp. finely grated peeled horseradish or 1 Tbsp. (packed) drained prepared horseradish', 'Special Equipment: A spice mill or mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Mix horseradish, both mustards, and honey in a small bowl to combine; season with salt.
2. If using nori, finely grind in a spice mill or mortar and pestle.
3. Mix coriander, mustard powder, brown sugar, pepper, garlic powder, and half of nori powder (if using) in a small bowl.
4. This is your dry rub.
5. Set remaining nori powder aside for serving.
6. Prepare a grill for medium-high heat; oil grate.
7. Season steaks generously with salt.
8. Sprinkle generously all over with dry rub.
9. Grill, turning every 1A2 minutes, until lightly charred and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of pork registers , 7A9 minutes.
10. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest minutes before thinly slicing.
11. Spread mustard sauce onto plates and arrange pork over.
12. Top with horseradish and sprinkle with reserved nori powder.

Ingredients: '1 cucumber, roughly chopped', '6 medium tomatoes (heirloom variety if possible)', ' oz basil leaves, stems removed', '1 celery stalk, roughly chopped', ' red pepper, deseeded and roughly chopped', '2 garlic cloves, grated', 'Juice of 1 lemon', '1 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil, plus extra for drizzling', '1 tbsp red wine vinegar', 'Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Put all the ingredients in a food processor and blitz until smooth, then season to taste with salt and pepper.
2. Add some water to loosen the soup if needed, then divide it between bowls and drizzle some more oil on top to serve.

Recipe name: Black-Eyed Pea Burgers With Creamy Barbecue Sauce And Chowchow
Ingredients: '1 cup amaranth', '2 scallions, coarsely chopped', '2 large crimini or button mushrooms, sliced', '1 small Scotch bonnet or habanero chile, seeds removed, finely chopped', '2 (-oz.) cans black-eyed peas (not seasoned), rinsed', ' cup parsley leaves with tender stems', '3 Tbsp. tamari soy sauce', '2 Tbsp. flaxseed meal', '2 Tbsp. miso', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', 'Freshly ground black pepper', 'Extra-virgin olive oil (for frying)', ' cup store-bought barbecue sauce', ' cup mayonnaise', '1 Tbsp. chili-garlic sauce (such as Huy Fong)', '1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard', '1 tsp. fresh lemon juice', '1 tsp. apple cider vinegar', '6 hamburger buns or brioche buns, toasted', 'Chowchow (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Cook amaranth, according to package directions.
2. Spread out on a rimmed baking sheet and let cool.
3. Combine scallions, mushrooms, chile, black-eyed peas, parsley, tamari, flaxseed meal, miso, and tsp. salt in a food processor.
4. Sprinkle 1A2 Tbsp. cooled amaranth over mixture and pulse until just combined.
5. Repeat process until all of amaranth has been incorporated.
6. Taste and season purAe with pepper and more salt if needed.
7. Scoop onto a parchment-lined baking sheet to make 6 portions (a cookie scoop works great if youAve got one) and form into patties that match the size of your buns.
8. Freeze minutes.
9. While the patties are chilling, make the sauce.
10. Whisk barbecue sauce, mayonnaise, chili-garlic sauce, mustard, lemon juice, and vinegar in a small bowl to combine.
11. Sauce should taste spicy, tart, and sweet.
12. Heat a large skillet, preferably cast-iron, over medium-low.
13. Pour in oil just to thinly coat bottom of pan.
14. Working in 3 batches to avoid crowding, cook patties, adjusting heat and adding more oil as needed, until browned (a crisp crust should form), about 5 minutes per side. (You can keep patties warm on a fresh parchment-lined baking sheet in a oven if needed.) Build burgers with patties, buns, sauce, and chowchow.
15. Do ahead: Sauce can be made 1 month ahead.
16. Cover and chill.
17. Bring to room temperature before serving.

Ingredients: '1 small green cabbage (about 2 lb.), chopped into " pieces (about cups)2 large green tomatoes, cut into " pieces', '1 large sweet onion, cut into " pieces', '1 large green bell pepper, seeds and ribs removed, cut into " pieces', '1 large red bell pepper, seeds and ribs removed, cut into " pieces', '2 Tbsp. pickling salt or 3 Tbsp. kosher salt', '1 Tbsp. yellow mustard seeds', ' cups apple cider vinegar', ' cups distilled white vinegar', '2 cups cane sugar (such as Wholesome) or raw sugar', '1 Tbsp. yellow mustard', '1 tsp. ground turmeric', '2 garlic cloves, finely chopped'
Instructions:
1. Toss cabbage, green tomatoes, onion, bell peppers, and salt in a large bowl until all of the vegetables are coated in salt (this will help them release some water).
2. Cover and chill at least 8 hours and up to hours.
3. Drain vegetables.
4. Toast mustard seeds in a large pot over medium-high heat, shaking pan, until fragrant and slightly darkened in color, about 2 minutes.
5. Add both vinegars, sugar, mustard, and turmeric.
6. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer minutes.
7. Add garlic and drained vegetables to pickling liquid and mix well.
8. Increase heat to medium-high and bring to a boil; cook 5 minutes.
9. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until flavors have melded and liquid is slightly reduced, about minutes.
10. The cabbage and green tomatoes should still have a bit of a crunch.
11. Let cool (this will take about 1 hour).
12. Using a slotted spoon, divide chowchow among sterilized glass jars of choice and spoon pickling liquid over (chowchow should be submerged and there should be about " space at the top of each jar.
13. Cover and chill.
14. Do ahead: Chowchow can be made 1 month ahead.
15. Keep chilled.
16. If youAd like to keep it longer, you can use the water bath boiling technique to make the jarred chowchow shelf stable.

Recipe name: Creole Caesar Salad With Corn Bread Croutons
Ingredients: '2 cups Fried Okra', '2 heads romaine lettuce, leaves separated', '1 cup cooked fresh corn kernels, chilled', ' cups cubed corn bread, toasted', ' cup sliced grape tomatoes', ' cup sliced seedless cucumber', ' cup sliced red onion', 'Creole Caesar Dressing (recipe follows)', 'A cup extra-virgin olive oil', '3 garlic cloves, peeled', '2 tablespoons chopped onion', '2 tablespoons red wine vinegar', ' roasted red bell pepper', '1 tablespoon finely chopped celery', '1 teaspoon light brown sugar', ' teaspoon cayenne pepper', '2 tablespoons mayonnaise', ' cup grated Parmesan cheese', 'Salt and pepper'
Instructions:
1. Place half the fried okra in a large bowl and add the romaine, corn, corn bread, tomatoes, cucumber, and onion.
2. Add the dressing and toss until well coated.
3. Divide the salad among six serving plates and top evenly with the remaining okra.
4. Serve immediately.
5. In a small saucepan, combine the oil, garlic, and onion.
6. Cook over low heat until the garlic and onion are golden and tender, about minutes.
7. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the garlic and onion to a food processor; reserve the oil.
8. Add the vinegar, bell pepper, celery, sugar, cayenne, mayonnaise, Parmesan, and a pinch each of salt and black pepper to the food processor.
9. Pulse until smooth.
10. With the machine running, add the oil in a thin stream and process until emulsified.
11. Taste and season with salt and pepper.
12. Use immediately or store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

Ingredients: 'Zest and juice of 2 limes', '2 habanero chiles (with seeds), finely chopped', '1 2"-piece ginger, scrubbed, finely grated', '5 garlic cloves, finely chopped', 'A cup chopped scallions', '2 Tbsp. finely chopped thyme', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 Tbsp. dark or light brown sugar', '4 tsp. ground allspice', '1 Tbsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', '2 tsp. dried thyme', '1 tsp. coarsely ground black pepper', '1 tsp. granulated garlic', '1 tsp. ground cinnamon', '1 tsp. ground ginger', '1 tsp. ground nutmeg', ' tsp. cayenne pepper', ' tsp. ground cumin', '5 large Yukon Gold potatoes (about 2 lb. total), scrubbed, cut into 1" pieces', '1 large sweet potato (about oz.), peeled, cut into 1" pieces', '1 tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt; plus more (optional)', ' cup mayonnaise', ' large red onion, finely chopped', '2 scallions, chopped', ' tsp. coarsely ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Using a fork, mix lime zest and juice, chiles, ginger, chopped garlic, scallions, chopped thyme, oil, brown sugar, allspice, salt, dried thyme, black pepper, granulated garlic, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, cayenne, and cumin in a medium bowl to combine (adding the chiles to the lime juice and zest at the beginning is important: it allows the juice to slightly blunt some of the sting of the chile).
2. Do ahead: Rub can be made 1 week ahead.
3. Cover and chill.
4. Combine Yukon Gold and sweet potatoes in a large pot, pour in water to cover, and season with salt if desired.
5. Bring to a boil over medium heat and cook until fork-tender, about minutes.
6. Drain and transfer to a large bowl (potatoes will be crushed at this point, especially the sweet potato, and thatAs okay).
7. Meanwhile, mix mayonnaise and 2 Tbsp. jerk rub in a small bowl to combine (you will have leftover rub, see headnote for other uses).
8. Set dressing aside.
9. Add red onion, scallions, 1 tsp.
10. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
11. Morton kosher salt, and tsp. black pepper to potatoes and toss to combine.
12. Add dressing and fold into potatoes with a rubber spatula until well coated.
13. Serve potato salad warm, room temperature, or chilled.
14. Do ahead: Potato salad can be made 2 days ahead (it will get more spicy).
15. Cover and chill.

Recipe name: Grilled Peach Toast With Pimiento Cheese
Ingredients: '2 firm-ripe peaches, halved and pitted', '2 tablespoons plus 1 teaspoon blended oil', '1 medium-size ripe avocado', '4 (1A2-inch-thick) slices multigrain boule-style bread', 'Smoky Pimiento Cheese With Bacon', '2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil', '1A4 cup thinly sliced red radishes (about 3 ounces)', '1 bunch watercress', '1 teaspoon gray sea salt', '1A2 teaspoon coarsely ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Preheat a grill to medium-high ().
2. Brush the peach halves with 1 tablespoon of the blended oil, and place on the grill grates, cut sides down.
3. Grill, uncovered, until grill marks appear and the juices begin to release, about 3 minutes.
4. Remove from the grill, and slice.
5. Set aside.
6. Cut the avocado in half lengthwise, pit, and brush cut sides with 1 teaspoon of the blended oil.
7. Place halves, cut sides down, on the grill grates.
8. Grill, uncovered, just until the avocado is charred and begins to soften, about 5 minutes.
9. Remove from the grill.
10. Brush the bread slices with the remaining 1 tablespoon oil, and place on the grill grates.
11. Grill until the bread is toasted, about 2 minutes per side.
12. Remove from the grill.
13. Spread Pimiento Cheese onto 1 side of each piece of toast.
14. Cut each piece into 4 equal rectangles, keeping the pieces together.
15. Scoop the avocado from the peel into a medium bowl and mash with 1 tablespoon of the extra-virgin olive oil until it is chunky and spreadable.
16. Spoon onto the toast, and top with sliced peaches.
17. Top the toast slices with radish slices and watercress leaves.
18. Drizzle the remaining 1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil over the toast slices, and sprinkle with gray sea salt and black pepper.

Ingredients: '2 cups long-grain white rice', ' cup whole or split urad dal (black gram)', ' tsp. fenugreek seeds', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '2 tsp. plus 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '1 small onion, finely chopped', '2A3 small green chiles (such as serrano), seeds removed if desired, finely chopped', '4 small tomatoes or canned whole peeled tomatoes, chopped', 'Kosher salt', '2 tsp. black mustard seeds', ' fresh or frozen curry leaves', '1 dried red chile (such as bird or chile de Arbol)', '1 tsp. split or whole urad dal (black gram)', 'A pinch of asafoetida (optional)', '1 tsp. ghee or clarified butter, melted, divided, plus more for griddle'
Instructions:
1. The morning before you make your batter, rinse rice in a fine-mesh sieve under cold running water, until water runs almost clear.
2. Transfer to a large bowl and cover with 4 cups cold water.
3. If your tap water is heavily chlorinated, use filtered water for soaking as chlorine will inhibit fermentation later on.
4. Pick over dal for debris, then rinse in sieve under cold running water.
5. Transfer to a medium bowl and add fenugreek.
6. Cover with 2 cups cold water (again using filtered if needed).
7. Let rice and dal soak at least 8 hours and up to hours.
8. Drain rice, holding back grains with your hand and pouring off most of the soaking liquid into a pitcher or large measuring glass.
9. Transfer rice to a blender.
10. Pulse to get things going, then purAe, sparingly adding soaking liquid as needed, until a paste forms (mixture should be foamy and still slightly gritty when rubbed between your fingers).
11. Pour into a clean large bowl.
12. Repeat process with dal mixture, then vigorously stir blended dal into rice paste.
13. Add reserved soaking liquid as needed to create a pourable batter that falls off the spoon in a steady stream and gradually dissolves into itself.
14. Stir in salt. (If you are in a warm climate, add salt after fermentation to keep batter from becoming overly sour.) Cover bowl with a kitchen towel, then a silicone lid (this will keep the surface of the batter from drying out).
15. Set bowl in a warm spot (a proofing box or an oven with the light on and a bowl of hot water placed inside work well).
16. Let batter sit until airy and pleasantly sour (when you drizzle it from a spoon, it should fall on itself in slowly dissolving ribbons), 4A6 hours in a very warm environment and as long as 2 days in a very cool environment.
17. In general, hours is a safe starting point.
18. Do ahead: Batter can be made 1 week ahead.
19. Once fermented, cover and chill.
20. Bring to room temperature before cooking.
21. Thin with water if needed to reach a thick but pourable consistency.
22. Heat 2 tsp. oil in a medium skillet over medium.
23. Cook onion, stirring occasionally, until translucent, about 3 minutes.
24. Add green chiles and cook, stirring, 1 minute.
25. Reduce heat to low, add tomatoes and any juices and a generous pinch of salt.
26. Cook, stirring often, until tomatoes are tender, 7A9 minutes.
27. Carefully transfer to clean blender or a food processor and blend until smooth; reserve skillet.
28. Taste chutney and season with more salt if needed.
29. Transfer to a small bowl.
30. Wipe out reserved skillet and heat remaining 2 Tbsp. oil in skillet over medium.
31. Cook mustard seeds, stirring constantly, until they start to pop, about seconds.
32. Add curry leaves, dried red chile, and dal and cook, stirring occasionally, until curry leaves wilt and crisp and dal is starting to brown, about 2 minutes.
33. Mix in asafoetida, if using, and remove spice oil from heat.
34. Mix into chutney.
35. To cook dosas, heat a griddle or large skillet (the larger the better; you want the batter to have room to spread) over medium.
36. Sprinkle a little water on surface; if it sizzles youAre good to go. (ItAs important though not to let griddle get hotter than medium.
37. If itAs too hot, the batter will start to cook before itAs fully spread out, yielding a thick dosa, or worse, a thin dosa that tears.
38. Adjust heat as needed).
39. Brush griddle with a thin layer of ghee, wiping off excess with a folded paper towel.
40. Ladle a generous A-cupful of batter in the center of griddle and use ladle to spread batter, working outward in continuous circles and never lifting ladle off surface, to create an 8"-diameter round.
41. Ridges of thick and thin batter will form naturally.
42. Cook dosa seconds, then drizzle A tsp. ghee over batter.
43. For a spongier dosa, cover with a large bowl and steam until cooked through, about 2 minutes (cooked underside should show through thinner spots, and thicker spots should be opaque and bouncy).
44. For a drier dosa, cook until surface is mostly set, about 2 minutes, then, using a fish spatula or other wide spatula, flip dosa over (it should easily lift from the griddle at this point) and cook until second side is lightly golden, about seconds.
45. Fold dosa in half so toasted side is facing out and transfer to a plate.
46. Repeat process with remaining batter and ghee.
47. Serve dosas as they are ready with chutney alongside for dipping.

Recipe name: Shimbra Wat Chickpeas With Spicy Flaxseed Paste
Ingredients: '3 tablespoons brown or golden flaxseed', '3 tablespoons awaze dipping sauce', ' oz ( g) cooked or canned chickpeas, drained and rinsed', 'Salt'
Instructions:
1. Heat a small dry frying pan over medium-low heat.
2. Add the flaxseed and lightly dry-toast for about 2 minutes, stirring and shaking the pan, until aromatic.
3. Transfer to a dish and leave to cool.
4. Once cool, grind using an electric spice or coffee grinder.
5. In a Dutch oven or sautA pan, heat 2 tablespoons of water over low heat and stir in 1 tablespoon of the awaze.
6. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring continuously, then gradually work in the remaining 2 tablespoons of awaze, along with the ground flaxseed and 1 cup ( ml) of water.
7. Reduce the heat to its lowest possible level, cover, and cook, stirring frequently, for minutes.
8. Add a touch more water if it looks like the mixture is drying outAit should still be a bit runny at the end.
9. Meanwhile, place the chickpeas in a saucepan and cover with 2 cups ( ml) of water.
10. Bring to a boil, remove from the heat, and soak in the liquid until ready to use.
11. Drain the chickpeas, reserving about 1 cup ( ml) of the liquid.
12. Add the chickpeas to the awaze-flaxseed mixture, generously season with salt, and mix well.
13. Tip in some of the reserved cooking water if needed so that the sauce is a touch runny and evenly coats the chickpeas.
14. Cook for a couple of minutes to combine the flavors before serving.

Ingredients: '1A cups chilled mezcal', '1A cups chilled Lillet blanc', 'A cup chilled 1:1 simple syrup (see Note)', ' teaspoons celery bitters', ' cup plus 2 tablespoons water', '1A cups fresh celery juice (from about celery stalks)', '1 cup fresh lemon juice', 'Garnish: Smoked sea salt and thinly sliced lemon wheels'
Instructions:
1. Up to 1 day before serving, make the batch.
2. Pour chilled mezcal, chilled Lillet blanc, chilled simple syrup, celery bitters, and water into a 2-quart pitcher and stir to mix.
3. Seal well, covering with plastic wrap if needed, and refrigerate.
4. Up to 2 hours before serving, prepare celery and lemon juices and stir into pitcher mix.
5. Reseal and return to refrigerator if not serving immediately.
6. To serve, stir mixture well.
7. Place a large ice cube in each rocks glass, then pour in cocktail and garnish with a small pinch of smoked salt and a lemon wheel, if desired.

Ingredients: ' oz. mezcal, such as Del Maguey Vida', ' oz. Aperol', ' oz. maraschino liqueur', ' oz. fresh lime juice', '1 grapefruit twist'
Instructions:
1. Shake mezcal, Aperol, maraschino liqueur, and lime juice together with ice.
2. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
3. Express the oils from a grapefruit peel over the top of the drink and discard.

Ingredients: '2 oz. mezcal, such as Vago EspadiAn', ' oz. fresh lime juice', ' oz. fresh ginger juice', '1 oz. agave nectar', ' oz. sparkling water'
Instructions:
1. Add all ingredients to a cocktail shaker and dry shake.
2. CarefulAthe bubbles in the soda water will give a little pushback, but donAt sweat it!
3. Strain into a copper mule mug and add fresh rocks.
4. Garnish with a sprig of mint and a quartered lime (optional).

Ingredients: ' oz. mezcal, such as Don Amado RAostico', ' oz. cinnamon syrup (see Note)', ' oz. fresh lime juice', ' oz. fresh fresh carrot juice', '2 dashes ReganAs Orange Bitters', 'Pink peppercorn, for rim', 'Salt, for rim'
Instructions:
1. Rim coupe glass with ground pink peppercorn and salt.
2. Combine all other ingredients in a shaker tin with ice.
3. Shake until the tin feels cold.
4. Strain into prepared glass.

Ingredients: '1 1A2 ounces honey', '2 ounces mezcal, such as Del Maguey Vida brand', '1 1A2 ounces freshly squeezed lemon juice (from 1 lemon)'
Instructions:
1. To make a honey syrup, in a small bowl or jar, combine the honey with 1 1A2 ounces water.
2. In a cocktail shaker, combine the mezcal, lemon juice, and 3A4 ounce of the honey syrup (save the remaining honey syrup for another cocktail).
3. Fill the shaker three-quarters of the way with ice and shake vigorously for seconds if serving on the rocks or seconds if serving Aup.A Strain into an ice-filled double old-fashioned glass or bucket glass, or serve up with no ice.

Ingredients: '1 large ice cube, plus more ice cubes for the cocktail shaker', '1 slice serrano chile, seeded', '5 or 6 fresh cilantro leaves', '2 oz. ml mezcal', '1 oz. ml fresh pineapple juice', ' oz. .5 ml rich simple syrup (see note)', ' oz. ml fresh lime juice', '1 pineapple leaf for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Put the large ice cube in an old-fashioned glass.
2. Muddle the serrano chile with the cilantro leaves in a cocktail shaker.
3. Add the mezcal, pineapple juice, simple syrup, lime juice, and ice cubes.
4. Shake vigorously and strain over the large ice cube in the glass.
5. Garnish with the pineapple leaf.

Ingredients: ' oz. mezcal, such as Ilegal Mezcal Joven', ' oz. Lillet Blanc', ' oz. Dolin Dry Vermouth', ' oz. Yellow Chartreuse', ' tsp. Luxardo Maraschino Liqueur', '2 dashes PeychaudAs Bitters', 'Grapefruit twist for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Add all the ingredients, except the grapefruit twist, to a mixing glass with ice and stir.
2. Strain over a large ice cube in a rocks glass.
3. Express the grapefruit twist over the drink and then rest the twist on the cube in the glass.

Ingredients: '1 lime', '1 bar spoon cane or simple syrup', '2 ounces rhum agricole blanc, preferably percent ABV or above, such as Damoiseau Proof Rhum Agricole Blanc'
Instructions:
1. With a sharp paring knife, cut a disc of skin from the lime about the size of a silver dollar, taking as little of the pith and actual flesh of the lime as possible.
2. In a rocks glass, muddle the lime disk with cane or simple syrup, then top with rhum.
3. Stir well to mix.

Ingredients: ' milliliters ( ounces) pot still Jamaican rum, such as Hamilton Jamaican Pot Still Black Rum', ' milliliters ( ounces) pineapple syrup (see Note)', ' milliliters ( ounces) grapefruit juice', ' milliliters ( ounces) brewed Earl Grey tea', 'Garnish: Lime wheels'
Instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients and stir to mix.
2. Keep at least 4 hours or up to overnight in a covered container in the refrigerator.
3. Stir before serving.
4. If utilizing a punch bowl, pour over a large block of ice to slow dilution.
5. Garnish with fresh lime wheels.

Ingredients: ' oz. blackstrap rum', '1 oz. freshly squeezed pineapple juice', ' oz. Campari', ' oz. freshly squeezed lime juice', ' oz. simple syrup (1:1)', 'Garnish: pineapple chunk andor pineapple leaf'
Instructions:
1. Combine all the ingredients except the garnish in a cocktail shaker filled with ice.
2. Shake until chilled and strain into a double old-fashioned glass over a large ice cube.
3. Garnish with the pineapple chunk or leaf (or both).

Recipe name: Savory Gruyaa Re Bread With Ham
Ingredients: '4 tablespoons ( stick56 grams) unsalted butter, melted and cooled, plus more for the pan', ' cups ( grams) all-purpose flour, plus more for the pan', ' teaspoons baking powder', '1 teaspoon fine sea salt', ' teaspoon baking soda', '1 cup ( grams) coarsely chopped ham', '3 tablespoons thinly sliced scallions, white and green parts (optional)', '1 tablespoon chopped fresh thyme leaves', '2 cups (8 ounces198 grams) grated GruyA re or sharp cheddar cheese', '2 large eggs', '1 cup ( milliliters) buttermilk'
Instructions:
1. Heat the oven to 350AoF.
2. Butter and flour a metal 9 A 5-inch loaf pan and set it aside.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda.
4. Stir in the chopped ham, scallions if using, thyme, and cups (7 ounces) of the GruyA re (reserving the rest for topping).
5. In a separate bowl, whisk together the melted butter, eggs, and buttermilk.
6. Add the wet mixture to the flour mixture and stir to combine.
7. The batter will be thick.
8. Transfer the batter to the prepared loaf pan and spread it out evenly, smoothing the top.
9. Sprinkle the remaining cup (1 ounce) cheese over the top.
10. Bake until the top springs back when lightly pressed and a skewer inserted in the center comes out with some moist crumbs attached, to minutes.
11. Transfer the pan to a wire rack and let it cool for minutes.
12. Then remove the bread from the pan and let it cool completely on the wire rack.

Recipe name: Grilled Cauliflower Wedges With Herb Tarator
Ingredients: ' cup sliced or slivered almonds', '2 garlic cloves, finely grated', '1 cup chopped dill', ' cup mint leaves', ' cup tahini', 'A cup fresh lemon juice', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', '1 small cauliflower (about lb.)', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil (for drizzling)', ' tsp. ground coriander', ' tsp. ground cumin', ' tsp. turmeric', 'Handful of tender herbs (such as dill, mint, parsley, cilantro, basil, andor chives)'
Instructions:
1. Toast almonds in a dry small skillet over medium heat, tossing occasionally, until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
2. Transfer to a small bowl and let cool.
3. Set aside for serving.
4. Blend garlic, dill, mint, tahini, and A cup water in a blender or food processor until a thick paste forms.
5. Add lemon juice and blend again, adding an additional 1A2 Tbsp. water if sauce is too thick.
6. It should be easily spreadable but not runny.
7. Season with salt and lots of pepper.
8. Remove outer leaves and trim stem from cauliflower so it sits flat.
9. Cut cauliflower into 4 wedges.
10. Whisk oil, coriander, cumin, turmeric, and a big pinch of salt in a large bowl to combine.
11. Add cauliflower and coat in oil.
12. The cauliflower can fall apart easily, so handle with care and use your fingers to work oil into the uneven surface.
13. Prepare a grill for medium-high heat (if using a charcoal grill, bank most of the coals on one side of the grill).
14. Place cauliflower, cut side down, on grate over hottest part of grill and grill, undisturbed, until char marks form, about 3 minutes (you can carefully lift to check, but try not to move too much).
15. Turn over onto other cut side and grill, undisturbed, until grill marks form, about 3 minutes.
16. Turn onto uneven floret side and slide over to cooler part of grill or reduce heat to low and cover with lid.
17. Grill until tender (if a bamboo skewer easily slides through the thickest part, itAs done), minutes. (And if you donAt have a grill, you can do this in a grill pan over high.
18. Once pan is very hot, add cauliflower, reduce heat to medium-high, and cook both cut sides as above.
19. When you turn cauliflower over onto uneven floret side, reduce heat to low and cover pan with foil to finish cooking.) To serve, spread some tarator onto individual plates or a platter.
20. Arrange cauliflower wedges on top and dollop remaining tarator over.
21. Scatter mixed tender herbs and almonds on top.

Recipe name: Open-Face Mushroom Sandwiches With Pecorino Salsa Verde
Ingredients: '3 medium shallots, thinly sliced crosswise, rings separated', '1 Tbsp. plus tsp. red wine vinegar', '1 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', 'Kosher salt', '3 oz. Pecorino Romano, Grana Padano, or Parmigiano-Reggiano', '2 cups (loosely packed) mixed tender herb leaves or greens (such as parsley and dill or dill and arugula)', '5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 lb. king trumpet or crimini mushrooms, trimmed', '6 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '6 1"-thick slices country-style bread'
Instructions:
1. SixAeight " metal skewers or bamboo skewers, soaked (optional) Toss shallots, vinegar, red pepper flakes, and black pepper in a small bowl to combine; lightly season with salt (the Pecorino will add some saltiness, so bear that in mind).
2. Let sit 3 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, very thinly slice Pecorino into tiles with a knife (irregular pieces are okay).
4. Add Pecorino, herbs, and oil to pickled shallots and toss gently to combine.
5. Taste and season with more salt if needed; set aside.
6. Prepare a grill for medium-high heat; clean grate.
7. Working one at a time, set mushrooms on their sides and slice lengthwise " thick to make 3A4 planks per mushroom, depending their size.
8. If your mushrooms are particularly small or you are using crimini, slice " thick and thread onto skewers to keep them from falling through grate.
9. Using some of the oil, brush cut sides of mushrooms and transfer to a large bowl or arrange on a rimmed baking sheet if skewered.
10. Lightly brush both sides of each slice of bread with remaining oil and transfer to a platter.
11. Press each slice of bread down across grate for even contact and grill until browned with some lightly charred spots, about 2 minutes per side.
12. The goal is a crunchy exterior with a still chewy center.
13. Transfer bread back to platter.
14. Brush grate to clean.
15. If using a gas grill, turn heat down to medium.
16. A charcoal grill should be at the tail end of medium-high and headed toward medium at this point, but wait a few minutes if needed.
17. Grill mushrooms, turning halfway through and moving around as needed to avoid flare-ups, until golden all over with lightly charred edges, 6A8 minutes for small pieces and minutes for large ones.
18. Return to bowl (slide off of skewers if needed).
19. Add Pecorino salsa verde and toss to combine.
20. Spoon mushroom mixture over grilled bread.

Ingredients: '5 Tbsp ( mL) packed brown sugar', '3 Tbsp ( mL) kosher salt', '2 Tbsp ( mL) sweet smoked paprika 4 tsp ( mL) ancho chili powder', '1 Tbsp ( mL) paprika', '1 Tbsp ( mL) onion powder', '1 Tbsp ( mL) freshly cracked black pepper 2 tsp ( mL) dried thyme leaves', '6 racks baby back ribs, about 6 lb (2.7 kg)', '4 medium chunks hickory wood, soaked in water for at least 2 hours (if using a charcoal grill) or 4 cups (1 L) hickory wood chips, soaked in water for at least 2 hours (if using a gas grill)', '3A4 cup ( mL) apple cider', '1A2 cup ( mL) ketchup', '3 Tbsp ( mL) red wine vinegar', '1 Tbsp ( mL) Dijon mustard', '2 tsp ( mL) light soy sauce', '1 tsp (5 mL) Worcestershire sauce', '1 tsp (5 mL) molasses', '1A2 tsp (2 mL) ancho chili powder', '1A2 tsp (2 mL) ground cumin', '1A2 tsp (2 mL) minced garlic', '1A4 tsp (1 mL) freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. For the ribs, stir together the sugar, salt, smoked paprika, chili powder, regular paprika, onion powder, black pepper and thyme.
2. Set aside.
3. With a small, sharp knife, release the edge of the membrane on the back of each rack of ribs.
4. Grasp the edge with needle-nose pliers or a piece of paper towel and peel the membrane off the rack. (If you leave the membrane on, the ribs will be chewy.) Spread the dry rub all over each rack of ribs until completely coated.
5. Make sure you press the dry rub into the meat.
6. This is where your flavor is going to come from.
7. Arrange the ribs in a rib rack, with all the racks facing the same direction.
8. A rib rack has 8 slab compartments, looks like a rack of coat hangers and can be purchased at most barbecue stores.
9. Fire up your charcoal grill and prep the grill for cooking over indirect heat.
10. You need a low temperature of around to ( to ) to grill the ribs.
11. For gas grills, preheat the grill to low then turn off one side of the grill to achieve indirect heat.
12. Place a drip tray on the cooler side of the grate and half fill the pan with warm water or the beer of your choice.
13. Drain 2 chunks of hickory, place them on top of the charcoal and set the grate in place.
14. For gas barbecues, place half of the hickory chips in a foil pouch and place the pouch directly on the heated side of the grill.
15. Wait for the hickory to start to smoke.
16. Place the rib racks over indirect heat as far from the heat as possible, with the bone sides facing toward the heat.
17. Close the lid and close the top vent on a charcoal grill about halfway.
18. This will maintain a temperature between and ( and ).
19. Cook the ribs for 2 hours.
20. Once the ribs are on, itAs time to start working on the barbecue sauce.
21. If you have a side burner this can be done outside.
22. If you donAt, go inside and use your stove top to simmer your sauce.
23. Combine all the ingredients in a small saucepan set over medium heat.
24. Simmer for to minutes or until the sauce reaches a glaze consistency.
25. Remove the saucepan from the heat and set aside to cool.
26. The first batch of charcoal should be cooling down after 2 hours.
27. Now is the time to use your chimney starter and get another batch of charcoal lit.
28. Add the remaining wood chunks to the coals to get a little more smoke.
29. If using a gas grill, wrap the remaining chips in foil and place directly on the hotter side of the grate.
30. Close the lid and grill for another 2 hours.
31. Make sure to come back every hour on the hour to check the temperature and to add more charcoal, if necessary.
32. In the last hour of grilling, your ribs will start to show signs of cooking all the way through.
33. When the meat pulls back from the bone, the ribs are almost ready.
34. Baste the ribs with the barbecue sauce during the last 5 to minutes of cooking.
35. I like to tent my ribs before serving, so take them off the grill and let them rest, loosely covered with foil, for minutes.

Recipe name: Grilled Watermelon Salad With Lime Mango Dressing And Cornbread Croutons
Ingredients: ' cup coarsely chopped ripe mango', ' cup fresh lime juice', ' cup dry sake', '2 tablespoons champagne vinegar', '1 teaspoon minced seeded birdAs-eye chile', ' teaspoon kosher salt', ' cup canola oil', '3 pounds seedless watermelon, peel left on, cut into 1-inch-thick slabs', 'Olive oil', 'Kosher salt', ' cup hulled pumpkin seeds', ' teaspoon ground cayenne', '1 teaspoon sugar', '4 cups 1-inch cubes homemade or store-bought cornbread', '2 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted', '8 fresh shiso leaves, shredded (or Thai basil or mint)', ' cup dates, pitted and chopped', '1 serrano chile, sliced into rings', 'Freshly ground black pepper', ' cup ricotta or mascarpone'
Instructions:
1. Put all of the ingredients except the oil in a blender.
2. Blend on high.
3. After the ingredients are blended, slowly add the oil and continue to blend until the mixture is thickened and emulsified. (Makes 1 cup.) Heat a grill pan to medium-high heat.
4. Drizzle the watermelon slices with just enough oil to thinly coat and place them on the hot grill.
5. Grill each side of the watermelon slices for about 2 minutes, until charred.
6. Transfer to a plate and season with salt.
7. Remove the rind and dice the grilled watermelon into large cubes.
8. Preheat the oven to .
9. Toss the pumpkin seeds with the cayenne, sugar, and 2 teaspoons oil and place on a small baking sheet.
10. Roast for 6 to minutes, until golden.
11. Brush the cornbread cubes with the melted butter and place in a single layer on a baking sheet.
12. Put in the oven along with the pumpkin seeds and bake for minutes, turning halfway with a flexible spatula.
13. Remove from the oven when the croutons are toasted and golden.
14. Toss the watermelon, shiso, dates, and chile in the bowl with cup Lime Mango Dressing (reserve remaining dressing for another use).
15. Taste and adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper.
16. Add the cornbread croutons and toss very gently to combine without breaking the croutons.
17. Spoon the salad onto a large platter and garnish with the pumpkin seeds and cheese.

Recipe name: Fried Plantain Chips With Lime Sour Cream And Mango Hot Sauce
Ingredients: '2 tablespoons sunflower oil or other neutral oil', '4 medium shallots, finely chopped', '3 Scotch Bonnet chiles, or to taste, cored, seeded, and finely chopped (see Note)', '3 garlic cloves, chopped', '1 (-inch) piece fresh turmeric, peeled and finely chopped, or 1 teaspoon ground turmeric', ' cup chopped fresh mango', ' teaspoon sea salt, plus more to taste', ' cup sugarcane vinegar or apple cider vinegar', ' cup orange juice', '1 cup sour cream', ' teaspoon lime zest', '1 tablespoon fresh lime juice', '1 teaspoon sea salt', 'Vegetable oil, for frying', '6 green plantains', 'Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Make the mango hot sauce: Heat the oil in a medium pan over low heat.
2. Add the shallots, chiles, garlic, turmeric, and mango and sautA for 5 to 7 minutes, until the aromatics start to soften.
3. Add the salt, vinegar, and cup water.
4. Stir to combine, then cover and simmer until the aromatics are completely softened and the liquid thickens slightly, about minutes.
5. Transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until smooth.
6. Stir in the orange juice and season with more salt to taste.
7. Transfer the hot sauce to a resealable glass jar and refrigerate until ready to use. (The hot sauce will keep for up to 2 weeks.) Make the lime sour cream: In a medium bowl, stir together the sour cream, lime zest and juice, and salt.
8. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
9. Make the plantains: Fill a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot about two-thirds of the way with vegetable oil.
10. Heat over medium heat until the oil reaches .
11. With a very sharp knife, peel the plantains, then slice lengthwise about A inch thick.
12. Working in batches, fry the plantains in the hot oil, flipping occasionally, until golden brown on both sides, 4 to 6 minutes.
13. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to a paper towelAlined plate to drain.
14. Season with salt and pepper.
15. Serve the fried plantains with the mango hot sauce and lime sour cream for dipping.

Ingredients: '5 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted and cooled slightly, plus more for greasing', '2 large eggs, beaten', 'A cup sugar', ' cup whole milk', ' cup heavy cream', ' cup sour cream', ' cup all-purpose flour', ' cup coarse yellow cornmeal', '1 teaspoon kosher salt', 'A teaspoon white pepper', 'A teaspoon paprika', 'A teaspoon baking soda', ' cup chopped yellow onion', ' cup chopped celery', '1 pound fresh corn kernels, cut from 4 cobs', '1 cup shredded cheddar cheese', '1 cup butter cracker crumbs, such as Ritz'
Instructions:
1. Set a rack in the lower third of the oven.
2. Preheat the oven to .
3. Grease a 9 by -inch casserole dish with butter, making sure to get in all the corners and sides of the dish.
4. In a medium bowl, whisk together the eggs and sugar until well combined.
5. Add the melted butter, milk, heavy cream, and sour cream and whisk to incorporate.
6. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cornmeal, salt, pepper, paprika, and baking soda.
7. Using a spatula, fold the wet ingredients, 1 cup at a time, into the dry ingredients until well combined.
8. Fold in the onion, celery, corn, and cheese.
9. Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish and sprinkle the cracker crumbs on top.
10. Bake the pudding for to minutes, rotating halfway through, until lightly browned on top and the tip of a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.

Recipe name: Black-Eyed Pea Salad With Hot Sauce Vinaigrette
Ingredients: '2 garlic cloves, grated on a Microplane', '2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar', '1 tablespoon yellow mustard', '1 tablespoon hot sauce', '1 teaspoon honey', 'Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper', '6 tablespoons vegetable oil', '1 (-ounce) can black-eyed peas, rinsed and drained', '2 mini cucumbers, cut into -inch dice', ' sweet onion, finely chopped', '1 pint cherry tomatoes or grape tomatoes, halved', ' cup picked fresh dill'
Instructions:
1. Whisk the garlic, vinegar, mustard, hot sauce, honey, teaspoon salt, and teaspoon pepper in a large bowl until smooth.
2. While whisking, add the oil in a slow, steady stream.
3. Whisk until emulsified.
4. Add the peas, cucumbers, onion, tomatoes, dill, and teaspoon salt.
5. Toss until well mixed.
6. You can serve this right away or let it sit at room temperature for up to 1 hour.
7. DO AHEAD: The salad can be refrigerated for up to 1 day.

Recipe name: Caribbean Smothered Chicken With Coconut Lime And Chiles
Ingredients: '1 teaspoon vegetable oil', '4 large bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs ( pounds)', 'Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper', '6 large sprigs thyme', '2 large onions, thinly sliced', '2 garlic cloves, chopped', '1 habanero chile, slit', '1 cup light coconut milk', 'Zest and juice of 1 large lime, plus wedges for serving', ' teaspoon mild yellow curry powder'
Instructions:
1. Heat the oil in a shallow Dutch oven or casserole with a lid over high heat.
2. Season the chicken generously with salt and pepper and add to the hot oil, skin side down.
3. Sear, flipping once, until browned on both sides and the fat renders, about 5 minutes.
4. Push the chicken to one side of the pan, add the thyme and onions to the other side, and reduce the heat to medium-low.
5. Cook, stirring, until the onions get some color, about 4 minutes.
6. Add the garlic, chile, teaspoon salt, and teaspoon pepper.
7. Cook, stirring, for 1 minute, then add the coconut milk and cup water.
8. Bring to a boil, stirring.
9. Arrange the chicken in a single layer skin side up and surround with the onion mixture.
10. The skin should be just above the liquid line.
11. Cover and simmer until the chicken is cooked through, about minutes.
12. Uncover and stir in the lime juice.
13. Simmer uncovered until the sauce thickens, about 5 minutes.
14. Stir in the curry powder and lime zest.
15. Serve immediately with lime wedges.
16. DO AHEAD: The cooked chicken can be refrigerated for up to 3 days.

Recipe name: Watermelon Salad With Radishes And Mint
Ingredients: 'Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper', '1 bunch red radishes, cut into A-inch slices', '2 tablespoons red wine vinegar', '4 teaspoons extra-virgin olive oil', ' red onion, cut into thin slivers', ' pounds watermelon ( of a large melon), rind removed, flesh cut into 1-inch cubes', '2 tablespoons torn mint leaves'
Instructions:
1. Dissolve a spoonful of salt in a large bowl of ice and water.
2. Add the radishes and let sit for a few minutes to crisp.
3. Whisk the vinegar, oil, and 1 teaspoon salt in a large bowl.
4. Drain the radishes and add to the bowl, along with the onion and watermelon.
5. Gently toss with your hands to coat evenly.
6. Spread on a serving platter, grind pepper all over, and top with the mint.

Ingredients: ' pound thick-cut bacon, diced', ' large onion, chopped', '1 red bell pepper, chopped', '3 -ounce cans kidney beans', ' cup brown sugar', '3 tablespoons ketchup', '3 tablespoons apple cider vinegar', '3 tablespoons maple syrup', '3 tablespoons molasses', '3 tablespoons yellow mustard', '1 tablespoon hot sauce', '2 teaspoons Creole seasoning'
Instructions:
1. Preheat grill to high.
2. Place a large Dutch oven or cast-iron pot over direct heat and cook bacon until fat has begun to render.
3. Add onion and red pepper and cook until they begin to soften, about 3-5 minutes.
4. Add the beans, including canning liquid, and bring to a simmer.
5. Add brown sugar, ketchup, apple cider vinegar, maple syrup, molasses, mustard, hot sauce, and Creole seasoning and stir to combine.
6. Close the lid and cook the beans until the liquid has thickened, about minutes.
7. Cover the pot and move to the cooler side of the grill, then cook for another - minutes or until desired tenderness.

Ingredients: ' cups graham cracker crumbs', ' tablespoons ( sticks) unsalted butter, melted', ' cup sugar', '3 large eggs, separated', 'Juice of 3 lemons', '1 (-ounce) can sweetened condensed milk', '1 teaspoon cream of tartar', '3 tablespoons sugar'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. In a medium bowl, combine the graham cracker crumbs, melted butter, and sugar until the crumbs are evenly moistened.
3. Press the mixture into the bottom and sides of a 9-inch pie tin.
4. Cover and chill the egg whites.
5. In a medium bowl, combine the egg yolks, lemon juice, and condensed milk.
6. Mix well and pour over the graham cracker crust.
7. Bake until stiff, about minutes.
8. Let cool to room temperature, then chill for at least 3 hours or up to overnight.
9. When ready to assemble the pie, preheat the oven to .
10. In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the whisk attachment, beat the egg whites and cream of tartar on high speed.
11. With the mixer running, gradually add the sugar and beat until stiff peaks form.
12. Spread the meringue over the pie filling.
13. Bake until the meringue is golden brown, about minutes.
14. Let cool for minutes, then chill for minutes before serving.

Ingredients: ' to cups water, fish stock, or seafood stock', '4 bay leaves', ' cup plus 2 tablespoons Old Bay seasoning', '1 cup chopped celery', '1 cup chopped onions', ' cup whole peeled garlic cloves', '3 whole birdAs-eye chiles', '1 pound small red potatoes, scrubbed, or 6 purple yams, cut into 2-inch chunks', '1 pound smoked hot sausage links or chicken sage sausage, cut into 2-inch pieces', '6 ears corn, shucked and quartered', '2 pounds shell-on large shrimp', '2 pounds shelled scallops', '1 cup dry white wine', 'Salt and pepper', 'Coarsely chopped fresh cilantro, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. In a large stockpot, combine cups of the water or stock, the bay leaves, Old Bay, celery, onions, garlic, and chiles and bring to a boil over high heat.
2. Add the potatoes and cook until tender, about minutes.
3. Add the sausage and cook until heated through, about 5 minutes.
4. Add the corn and cook until just tender, about 3 minutes.
5. Stir in the shrimp, scallops, and wine and cook until the shrimp are cooked through, about 4 minutes.
6. Season with salt and pepper and pour the stew into a large serving bowl.
7. For more casual gatherings, serve directly from the pot on the stove.
8. Garnish each serving with cilantro and serve immediately.

Recipe name: Very Red Velvet Cake With Cream Cheese Icing And Pecans
Ingredients: ' cups cake flour', ' teaspoon baking soda', '2 large eggs, at room temperature', ' cups sugar', ' cups vegetable oil', '2 tablespoons distilled white vinegar', '1 cup buttermilk', '1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract', '3 tablespoons red food coloring (from two 1-ounce bottles)', '1 (1-pound) box confectionersA sugar, sifted', '1 (8-ounce) package cream cheese, softened', '1 stick (8 tablespoons) butter, softened', '1 cup chopped pecans'
Instructions:
1. To make the cake: Set the oven to and adjust the rack positions to the middle and top third.
2. Spray three 8-inch round cake pans well with baking spray.
3. Sift the flour with the baking soda.
4. In a large bowl, with an electric mixer on medium speed, beat the eggs well, then beat in the sugar, oil, and vinegar.
5. On low speed, slowly add the flour and beat in well.
6. Add the buttermilk slowly, then the vanilla, and then the food coloring.
7. Pour the batter evenly into the pans and rap them on the countertop several times to eliminate any air bubbles.
8. Bake for minutes, then rotate the pans from shelf to shelf so they bake evenly and bake until the tops spring back when lightly touched, about minutes altogether.
9. Let the cakes cool in the pans on racks for minutes.
10. Run a knife around the sides of the pans and invert the cakes onto the racks to cool completely.
11. Once theyAre cool, refrigerate the cakes for to minutes to make icing them easier.
12. To make the icing: Combine everything in a medium bowl and mix well until very smooth.
13. ItAs best to use the icing right away, when itAs soft and spreadable.
14. Put one cake layer upside down on a cake plate or cake stand and cover the top evenly with frosting.
15. Add a second layer, right side up, and spread with more frosting.
16. Top with the third layer, right side up, and spread the frosting evenly around the sides of the cake and on top; check for any places where you can see through to the cake.
17. Finally, sprinkle the pecans evenly all over the top of the cake.
18. Once the frosting sets, cut into slices to serve.
19. This cake keeps for up to 2 weeks in a cake keeper, but once itAs ready, people want to dive right in.

Ingredients: '2 cups ( g) hulled and coined strawberries', '2 tbsp ( g) pure cane sugar', '3 cups ( g) whole wheat pastry flour, plus more for dusting', '4 tsp ( g) baking powder', '3 tbsp ( g) granulated sugar', ' tsp salt', ' cup ( ml) olive oil', ' cup ( ml) buttermilk, plus more for brushing', 'Raw sugar, for sprinkling (optional)', ' cup ( ml) balsamic vinegar', ' cup ( ml) agave or honey', '1 cup ( ml) cold heavy cream', '3 tbsp ( ml) honey or agave', 'Mint or basil for topping'
Instructions:
1. To make the strawberries, toss the coined berries and cane sugar together in a bowl.
2. Let them sit for about minutes, or until they start to break down and liquefy.
3. Preheat the oven to ( or gas mark 8) and have a sheet pan lined with parchment nearby.
4. To make the biscuits, in a bowl, stir together the flour, baking powder, granulated sugar and salt.
5. Drizzle over the olive oil, and gently toss with your hands until droplets of oil are dispersed evenly throughout the flour.
6. Add the buttermilk, stirring gently and compressing with your hands until a shaggy dough is formed.
7. Dump out onto a floured work surface and knead until it comes together into a solid mass.
8. Roll it out into a -inch (3-cm)-thick disk.
9. Using a 3-inch (7.5-cm) biscuit cutter, punch out 6 biscuits, folding and re-rolling the dough if needed.
10. Place the biscuits on the lined sheet tray, brush with more buttermilk, sprinkle with raw sugar if using, and bake for 8 to minutes, or until slightly puffed and golden brown.
11. Remove the biscuits and transfer to a wire rack to cool.
12. To make the balsamic glaze, combine the balsamic vinegar and honey in a saucepan and bring it to a simmer.
13. Cook over medium-low heat for to minutes, or until the liquid reduces by half.
14. Set aside to cool and thicken.
15. To make the whipped cream topping, when youAre ready to serve, beat together the cream and honey in a stand mixer with a whisk attachment, or in a bowl with an electric mixer, until stiff peaks form, about 3 minutes.
16. Split the cooled biscuits in half and spoon on some of the strawberries along with some of their juices so it soaks into the bottom biscuit.
17. Top with a dollop of whipped cream, followed by a drizzle of the balsamic glaze.
18. Top with the other half of the biscuit, and repeat with more strawberries, more cream, and another drizzle of glaze.
19. Repeat with the rest of the biscuits, and serve immediately.

Ingredients: '3 cup all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', '3 tablespoons sugar', '2 teaspoons kosher salt', ' cup ( sticks) unsalted butter, cut into cubes and chilled', '1 cup ice-cold water, plus more as needed', ' pounds fresh peaches, pitted and quartered or two (-ounce) cans peaches, drained', '1 cup sugar', ' cup all-purpose flour', '1 tablespoon ground cinnamon', ' teaspoon freshly grated nutmeg', 'Fine sea salt', '1 large egg, beaten', 'Ground cinnamon, for dusting', 'Sugar, for dusting', 'Ice cream, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Make the crust: In the bowl of a food processor, pulse together the flour, sugar, and 2 teaspoons salt until just combined.
2. Add the butter and pulse a few more times until the mixture is coarse but the butter pieces are still visible.
3. Slowly add the ice water and pulse to form a dough, six to eight pulses.
4. If the dough looks and feels very dry, add more ice water, 1 tablespoon at a time.
5. Divide the dough in half and wrap each half tightly in plastic wrap; flatten each piece into a disc.
6. Refrigerate the dough for 1 hour.
7. Make the filling: In a large bowl, mix together the peaches, sugar, flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, and a pinch of salt.
8. Set aside.
9. Preheat the oven to 375AoF.
10. Remove the dough from the refrigerator and cut in half.
11. On a lightly floured surface, roll it into a 9 x -inch rectangle, about inch thick.
12. Set aside and repeat with the second piece of dough.
13. Line a 9 x -inch baking dish with one of the dough rectangles, pressing any excess dough against the sides of the dish.
14. Pour in the peach filling and spread it evenly.
15. Place the second rectangle of dough on top of the peaches and tuck the excess dough into corners.
16. Brush the dough with the beaten egg and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar.
17. Pierce the top with the tip of a sharp knife in several places to allow steam to escape while baking.
18. Bake until the top crust is golden brown, about 1 hour minutes.
19. Let cool on a rack before serving.
20. Serve with ice cream.

Ingredients: '2 cups granulated sugar', ' cup ( sticks) unsalted butter, room temperature', '2 cups fresh strawberries, hulled', '3 large eggs, room temperature', '3 cups sifted all-purpose flour', '3 teaspoons baking powder', '1 teaspoon salt', ' cup sour cream, room temperature', 'A cup vegetable oil', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract', '1 teaspoon strawberry extract', '3A4 drops red food coloring (optional)', '2 (8-ounce) packages cream cheese, room temperature', ' cup confectionersA sugar', '1 cup heavy cream, cold', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract', ' teaspoon coconut extract (optional)', 'Pinch salt', ' cup sweetened coconut flakes, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to .
2. Liberally prepare 3 9-inch round pans with the nonstick method of your choice.
3. In the bowl of your stand mixer fitted with the whisk attachment, cream together the granulated sugar and butter on medium-high speed until nice and fluffy, about 6 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, place the strawberries in your food processor and purAe until smooth.
5. Set aside.
6. With your stand mixer running, add the eggs 1 at a time, combining well after each addition and scraping down the sides and bottom of the bowl as needed.
7. Change your mixer speed to medium-low and add the strawberry purAe slowly into the batter.
8. Continue mixing while you tend to the dry ingredients.
9. In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
10. Slowly add of the flour mixture to your stand mixer bowl.
11. Continue to mix on low speed to combine.
12. Meanwhile, in a small bowl, whisk together the sour cream and oil and add to your stand mixer bowl.
13. Pour in the remaining flour mixture and continue to mix on low until well incorporated.
14. Add the vanilla extract, strawberry extract, and food coloring, if using.
15. Scrape down the sides and bottom of the bowl and mix the batter until just combined.
16. Be careful not to overmix.
17. Evenly pour the batter into the prepared pans and bake for to minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a layer comes out clean.
18. Let the layers cool in the pans for minutes, then invert onto wire racks.
19. Let cool to room temperature.
20. Lightly cover the layers with foil or plastic wrap so they do not dry out.
21. Clean your stand mixer bowl and whisk attachment.
22. Beat the cream cheese on high speed until it begins to thicken and become fluffy.
23. Turn your mixer down to low speed and carefully add the confectionersA sugar.
24. Once the sugar is fully incorporated, turn your mixer speed back to high and continue whipping.
25. Add the heavy cream; vanilla extract; coconut extract, if using; and salt and continue to mix until a smooth, light, and fluffy frosting is achieved.
26. Once the layers are completely cooled, place 1 layer on a serving plate.
27. Spread just the top of the layer with A of the frosting.
28. Add the second layer and spread with another A of the frosting.
29. Add the final layer, bottom-side up, and spread with the remaining frosting.
30. Frost the top and the side of the cake.
31. Gently pat the side and the top of the cake with coconut flakes.
32. Serve at room temperature.

Ingredients: '2 cups dried hibiscus (sorrel) flowers', '1A3 cup peeled fresh ginger slices ( inch thick)', '2- to 3-inch cinnamon stick', '6 whole cloves', ' cup grated orange zest', 'Grated zest and juice of 1 lemon', 'Demerara sugar, honey, or agave nectar', 'Mint leaves, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. In a large saucepan, bring 2 quarts water, the hibiscus, ginger, cinnamon, cloves, and orange and lemon zests to a boil over medium heat.
2. Boil for 5 minutes.
3. Remove from the heat.
4. Add the lemon juice, cover tightly, and let stand for 1 to 2 days at room temperature.
5. Strain and discard the solids.
6. Sweeten to taste with demerara sugar, honey, or agave nectar.
7. Chill thoroughly.
8. Serve over ice and garnish with mint.

Ingredients: '3 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for the work surface', '1 teaspoon baking powder', ' teaspoon baking soda', ' teaspoon salt', ' teaspoon ground or freshly grated nutmeg', '1 stick (4 ounces) butter, at room temperature', '1 cup sugar', '2 large eggs', ' cup buttermilk', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract', 'Demerara sugar, for sprinkling'
Instructions:
1. In a bowl, whisk together the flour,baking powder, baking soda, salt, and nutmeg.
2. In a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, cream together the butter and sugar on medium speed until light and fluffy, 2 to 3 minutes.
3. Scrape down the sides of the bowl.
4. Add the eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Scrape down the sides of the bowl again, then beat in the buttermilk and vanilla.
6. Gradually add the flour mixture, beating just until smooth and well blended.
7. Divide the dough in half.
8. Flatten each half into a disc.
9. Wrap in plastic and refrigerate until chilled and slightly stiff, at least 1 hour, but overnight ideally.
10. Preheat the oven to .
11. Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
12. On a lightly floured surface, working with one portion at a time, roll the dough to a -inch thickness.
13. Cut with a floured -inch round biscuit cutter.
14. Gather the scraps, reroll, and cut again.
15. Sprinkle lightly with demerara sugar.
16. Transfer the tea cakes to the baking sheets and space them about 1 inch apart.
17. Bake until the tea cakes are lightly browned, 8 to minutes.
18. Cool on the pan for 1 minute, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
19. The tea cakes will keep for about 2 weeks in an airtight container.

Ingredients: '1 cup unbleached all-purpose flour', ' cup packed light brown sugar', ' cup old-fashioned rolled oats', '2 tablespoons yellow cornmeal', '2 teaspoons ground cinnamon', ' teaspoon fine sea salt', '8 tablespoons (1 stick) cold unsalted butter, cut into -inch cubes', '1 tablespoon canola oil', '2 cups strawberries', '4 cups 1-inch pieces peeled rhubarb', '1 cups turbinado sugar', '3 tablespoons unbleached all-purpose flour', '1 tablespoon cornstarch'
Instructions:
1. Position a rack in the lower third of the oven and preheat the oven to .
2. Butter a 9-inch deep-dish pie plate and line a baking sheet with parchment.
3. To make the topping: In a medium mixing bowl, stir together the flour, brown sugar, rolled oats, cornmeal, cinnamon, salt, butter, and oil with a fork until completely blended.
4. Set aside.
5. To make the filling: In another medium bowl, combine the strawberries and rhubarb with the turbinado sugar, flour, and cornstarch, stirring to coat the fruit.
6. Pour the fruit mixture into the prepared pie plate.
7. Sprinkle the topping evenly over the fruit mixture.
8. Place the pie plate on the prepared baking sheet.
9. Bake for to minutes, until the fruit is bubbling around the edges and the top is golden brown and crispy.
10. Let cool slightly.
11. Serve the crisp warm.
12. It is best served the same day, but it can be covered with plastic wrap and refrigerated for up to 3 days.

Ingredients: '4 tablespoons sweet paprika', '2 tablespoons chili powder', '2 tablespoons ground cumin', '2 tablespoons dark brown sugar', '2 tablespoons salt', '1 tablespoon dried oregano', '1 tablespoon sugar', '2 tablespoons ground black pepper', '2 teaspoons cayenne pepper', '1 bone-in pork roast, preferably Boston butt (6-8 pounds)', '2 tablespoons vegetable oil', '1 onion, finely chopped', '3 garlic cloves, minced', ' cups ketchup', ' cup cider vinegar', ' cup Worcestershire sauce', 'A cup sugar', '1 tablespoon chili powder', ' teaspoon cayenne pepper'
Instructions:
1. Add paprika, chili powder, cumin, brown sugar, salt, oregano, sugar, black pepper, and cayenne pepper to a small bowl and mix to combine.
2. Rub the mixture into the pork roast, then wrap meat tightly with two layers of plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 3 hours.
3. About 1 hour prior to cooking, unwrap the roast and let it come to room temperature.
4. At the same time, soak 3-4 cups of wood chunks in cold water and then drain.
5. Heat the vegetable oil in a saucepan over medium heat, then cook the onion and garlic for about 5 minutes.
6. Add the ketchup, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, sugar, chili powder, and cayenne pepper, then reduce the heat and simmer until the sauce has thickened, about minutes.
7. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
8. Preheat grill to high.
9. Place the wood chips in a smoker box or vented foil pouch and heat until they begin to smoke.
10. Turn the heat down to medium.
11. When your grill is about degrees, place the pork on the grill and cook until it is fork-tender and the internal temperature reaches degrees, about 5-6 hours.
12. Rotate the meat every minutes.
13. Transfer the pork to a rimmed baking sheet.
14. When cool enough to handle, use two large forks to shred the meat into bite-size pieces.
15. Mound the meat onto a platter and toss with 1 cup of the barbecue sauce.
16. Serve the remaining sauce with the buns of your choice.

Ingredients: 'One 2-pound whole pineapple', ' teaspoon coarse salt', ' teaspoon ground ginger', '1 teaspoon brown sugar', '8 cups boiling water', '2 cups hibiscus leaves', ' cups granulated sugar', '1 cup fresh lime juice (about 3 limes)', ' cups white rum (optional)', ' cups ginger beer, or ounces', '3 scoops lime sorbet (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Cut the top and bottom of pineapple.
3. Next, sit the fruit upright and cut down the sides.
4. Flip the pineapple on its side cut in half.
5. Cut into -inch round slices.
6. Sprinkle pineapple slices with salt, ginger, and brown sugar.
7. Place slices on parchment paper or Silpat (nonstick baking mat) on baking sheet or jelly roll pan.
8. Roast for minutes.
9. Transfer pineapple slices to Vitamix or food processor, pulse until liquid is loose and foamy.
10. The fragrance will be bright.
11. Set aside.
12. Place water in a large stockpot, over medium-high heat.
13. After coming to a boiling, drop in hibiscus leaves.
14. Cover and let brew for minutes.
15. Stir in sugar and turn off heat.
16. Let completely cool.
17. Add lime juice, 2 cups of the roasted pineapple juice, rum, and ginger beer.
18. Stir well.
19. Transfer to punch bowl.
20. Place scoops of lime sorbet in punch bowl, if desired.
