21. The mixture will slightly foam.
22. The sorbet will make the punch a tad sweeter.

Ingredients: '2 large green tomatoes (1 pound), cleaned and sliced into -inch slices', '1 teaspoon plus teaspoon coarse salt, divided', '1 teaspoon plus teaspoon ground black pepper, divided', ' cup buttermilk', ' cup fine cornmeal', ' cup all-purpose flour', ' teaspoon onion powder', ' teaspoon smoked paprika', ' teaspoon red pepper flakes', ' cups sunflower oil'
Instructions:
1. Sprinkle tomato slices with 1 teaspoon salt and 1 teaspoon pepper, pour buttermilk into a shallow bowl.
2. Place the tomatoes in and soak for minutes.
3. Combine teaspoon salt, teaspoon pepper, cornmeal, flour, onion powder, smoked paprika, and red pepper flakes in a large bowl.
4. Whisk well.
5. Dredge both sides of the tomatoes in the mixture.
6. Place oil in a large skillet over high heat.
7. Heat until an instant-read thermometer reads .
8. Carefully drop tomatoes in the oil (work in batches).
9. Cook about a minute on each side or until golden brown.
10. Remember to let the oil reach the mark before dropping more fritters, and donAt crowd the pan.
11. Transfer to jelly roll sheet with cooling rack on top.

Ingredients: ' oz. vodka', ' oz. KahlAoa', ' oz. heavy cream'
Instructions:
1. Combine all the ingredients in a cocktail shaker filled with ice and shake until chilled and well integrated, about seconds.
2. Strain into a chilled Old-Fashioned glass.

Ingredients: 'Scant 2 cups ( g) all-purpose flour', ' tsp ( g) baking powder', '3 tbsp ( ml) milk', '2 tbsp ( ml) elderflower cordial', '1 cup ( g) unsalted butter', '1 cup plus 2 tbsp ( g) superfinecaster sugar', 'Zest of 1 lemon', '4 eggs', 'Approximately 1 lb ( g) fresh strawberries', ' cup ( g) superfinecaster sugar', '2 tsp ( g) vanilla bean paste', ' cup plus 1 tbsp ( g) egg whites', '1 cup ( g) superfinecaster sugar', '1A cups ( g) unsalted butter, softened', '6 tbsp ( ml) elderflower cordial', 'Fresh strawberries, to decorate', 'Fresh elderflower, to decorate'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to ().
2. Grease the base and sides of three deep, round 6-inch (-cm) cake pans and line the bottoms with parchment paper.
3. To make the cake, sift together the flour and baking powder in a large bowl, and set aside.
4. Mix the milk and elderflower cordial in a small bowl and set aside.
5. Using a stand mixer or electric whisk, beat the butter, sugar and lemon zest for 5 minutes, or until pale and fluffy.
6. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
7. If the mixture looks like itAs starting to curdle, add 1 tablespoon (8 g) of the flour mixture.
8. Turn the mixer speed to low and add half of the flour mixture followed by the milk and elderflower.
9. Stir in the remaining flour and divide the batter equally into the prepared cake pans.
10. Bake the cakes for to minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Let the cakes cool in their pans for minutes before turning them out onto a wire rack to cool completely.
12. Once cool, wrap them tightly in plastic wrap and place in the fridge to firm up.
13. To make the purAe, preheat the oven to ().
14. Slice the stems off the strawberries, cut them in half and put them into a baking dish.
15. Toss the fruit with the sugar and vanilla.
16. Roast the strawberries in the oven for to minutes, stirring once halfway.
17. The strawberries are done once they are soft and fragrant and the juices have turned into a syrup.
18. Leave the strawberries to cool completely before transferring them to a food processor or blender and pulsing until smooth.
19. Store in an airtight container in the fridge until youAre ready to use it.
20. To make the buttercream, add the egg whites and sugar to the bowl of a stand mixer or a heatproof bowl.
21. Make sure there are no traces of grease in the bowl as this will stop the meringue from whipping up.
22. Place the bowl over a saucepan of simmering water, making sure that the base doesnAt touch the water.
23. Whisk the eggs and sugar constantly until the mixture reaches a temperature of about ().
24. If you donAt have a thermometer, rub a little of the mixture in between your fingers.
25. The eggs should be hot to the touch with all the sugar dissolved.
26. Remove the bowl from the heat and start whisking the egg whites on high speed in a stand mixer fitted with the whisk attachment or with an electric whisk.
27. Beat until the whites are thick and have doubled in volume.
28. Once the bowl is cool to the touch and with the mixer still running, add the butter a piece at a time.
29. The meringue will deflate and will start to look a little runny or curdled.
30. DonAt panicAthis is normal.
31. Continue beating until you have added in all of the butter and the mixture is smooth and glossy.
32. If after minutes, your meringue buttercream is still soupy, chill it in the fridge for minutes and then beat again.
33. Add about cup ( g) of the strawberry puree and beat the buttercream for 2 minutes, or until smooth.
34. Leave the buttercream at room temperature until you are ready to assemble.
35. Unwrap the cakes from the fridge and if any of them are domed, level them with a serrated knife or a cake leveler.
36. All the layers need to be completely flat to ensure you donAt end up with a wonky cake.
37. Brush each cake layer with 2 tablespoons ( ml) of elderflower cordial.
38. Place one layer of cake on a cake board and put this board on a turntable, if using.
39. If your buttercream has firmed up, whip it up again in the mixer until smooth.
40. Add a scoop of buttercream and spread it out evenly with an offset spatula, pushing the buttercream right to the edge (itAs fine if it spills over).
41. Place the next layer on top of the first, but this time add about 1 tablespoon (8 g) of strawberry purAe on top of the buttercream layer, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) clear around the edge.
42. Place the last layer topside down to give you a perfectly flat top.
43. Frost the top and sides of the cake with a thin layer of buttercream.
44. Smooth the edges as much as you can with a bench scraper or palette knife.
45. This is the crumb coat layer that will trap any stray crumbs and act as a base for the next layer.
46. Place the cake in the fridge for minutes to firm up before adding another layer of buttercream on the top and sides.
47. Fill a piping bag fitted with an open star nozzle (IAve used Wilton 8B) with any remaining buttercream and pipe swirls across the edge to make a wreath.
48. Top the cake with fresh strawberries, a drizzle of any leftover puree and fresh elderflower (if in season).

Recipe name: Jerk Baby Back Ribs With Pineapple Salsa
Ingredients: '1 large red onion, cut into -in6-mm slices', ' cup ( mL) soy sauce', ' cup ( mL) vegetable oil', ' cup ( mL) apple cider vinegar', ' cup ( mL) water', '1 jalapeAo chile, sliced', ' habanero chile, stemmed and seeded', 'A cup ( mL) white wine vinegar', 'Two 6 oz. (-mL) cans pineapple juice', ' cup ( g) firmly packed brown sugar', ' pineapple, diced', '1 small red onion, diced', '3 small jalapeAo chiles, diced', 'A cup ( g) diced red bell pepper', 'A cup (8 g) chopped fresh cilantro', '2 Tbsp. canola oil', 'Juice of 1 lime', 'Kosher salt', '2 Tbsp. garlic powder', '2 Tbsp. onion powder', '1 Tbsp. ground allspice', '1 Tbsp. kosher salt', '2 tsp. grated nutmeg', '2 tsp. cascabel or ancho chile powder', '2 tsp. ground cinnamon', '2 tsp. ground cayenne pepper', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', 'Two 2-lb ( g) racks baby back ribs'
Instructions:
1. To make the jerk marinade: In a medium bowl, combine the onion, soy sauce, oil, vinegar, water, and jalapeAo. (To make ahead, refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 3 days.) To make the habanero vinegar: In a food processor, comAbine the habanero chile and vinegar and purAe until smooth.
2. Pour into a small bowl. (To make ahead, refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 1 month.) To make the pineapple glaze: In a small saucepan, combine the pineapple juice and brown sugar.
3. Bring to a boil over medium heat and cook until sauce is thick and reduced to about one-third, about minutes. (To make ahead, refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 3 days.) To make the pineapple salsa: In a medium bowl, combine the pineapple, onion, jalapeAos, bell pepper, cilantro, oil, and lime juice.
4. Season with salt. (To make ahead, refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 8 hours.) To make the jerk spice rub: In a small bowl, combine the garlic powder, onion powder, allspice, salt, nutmeg, chile powder, cinnamon, cayenne, and black pepper in a small bowl.
5. Use a butter knife to pry the membrane off the bone side of the ribs.
6. Once you get a piece of the membrane loose, grab it with a paper towel and pull it toward the other end of the slab to remove it.
7. Rub the Jerk Spice Rub evenly over the ribs.
8. Arrange the ribs in a -by--in38-by--cm baking pan.
9. Pour the Jerk Marinade over the ribs, turning to coat both sides.
10. Cover baking pan with aluminum foil and refrigerate for at least 6 hours and up to hours.
11. Remove ribs from marinade, letting the excess drip off.
12. Transfer the ribs to a baking sheet and allow to come to room temperature, about minutes.
13. Brush with the Habanero Vinegar.
14. To cook ribs using a charcoal grill: Prepare grill for indirect cooking over medium heat.
15. Place a shallow metal pan filled with water in the center of the grill and arrange the hot coals around it.
16. Lightly oil the grill grates.
17. Place the ribs on the grate directly above the water-filled pan, cover the grill, and cook until tender, 1 to 2 hours.
18. Add coals as needed to maintain medium heat.
19. To cook ribs using a gas grill: Preheat the grill with all the burners on high.
20. Lightly oil the grill grates.
21. Place the ribs on the grate, turn off the burner directly under the ribs, and reduce the other burners to medium heat.
22. Cover the grill and cook until tender, 1 to 2 hours.
23. After minutes, brush the ribs with some of the Pineapple Glaze, and brush them two or three times before serving.
24. Do not flip the ribs during cooking.
25. When the ribs are done, they will be well browned and the meat will have shrunk back from the ends of the bones.
26. Transfer ribs to a cutting board and let stand 5 minutes before cutting rack into individual ribs.
27. Serve warm with the Pineapple Salsa.

Ingredients: '1 cup apricot preserves', 'Juice of 2 limes, plus wedges for serving', '1 habanero pepper, seeded and small diced', '2 tablespoons soy sauce', '2 small garlic cloves, finely grated', '1 teaspoon grated fresh ginger', '2 pounds extra-large shrimp, peeled and deveined', 'Vegetable oil, for brushing'
Instructions:
1. In a small bowl, mix together the apricot preserves, lime juice, habanero, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger.
2. Transfer half the sauce to a large zip-top bag and add the shrimp.
3. Seal the bag and refrigerate for minutes.
4. Heat a grill to high or heat a large grill pan over high heat.
5. Brush the grates or the pan with oil.
6. Soak bamboo skewers in water and a wedge lime.
7. Thread 5 shrimp onto a bamboo or metal skewer (discard the sauce in the bag) and grill until charred on both sides, 4 to 5 minutes.
8. Do not overcook, please.
9. Serve with the remaining sauce on the side for dipping and lime wedges.

Recipe name: Cornbread Muffins With Whipped Sweet Corn
Ingredients: '3 tablespoons coconut oil, plus more for greasing', '6 pitted Medjool dates', 'Boiling water', ' cup medium-grind yellow cornmeal', ' cup flour', '2 tablespoons coconut palm sugar', '1 tablespoon baking powder', ' teaspoon fine sea salt', '2 heaping tablespoons freshly ground golden flaxseeds', '1 cup unsweetened oat milk', '3 tablespoons vegan butter', ' cup finely diced white onion', '2 cups fresh corn kernels (from about 3 ears)', '2 teaspoons coconut palm sugar', ' teaspoon kosher salt, plus more as needed', ' cup Cashew Cream', 'Fresh lemon juice', 'Freshly ground white pepper', 'JalapeAo Pepper Jelly, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Make the muffins: Preheat the oven to .
2. Generously coat nine wells of a standard muffin tin with coconut oil.
3. Put the dates in a small heatproof bowl and cover with boiling water.
4. Let soak for about 5 minutes.
5. Drain the dates, reserving the soaking liquid, and transfer them to a food processor.
6. Add 1 tablespoon of the reserved soaking liquid and process, scraping down the sides and adding more of the reserved soaking liquid if necessary, until mostly creamy but with a few chunks remaining.
7. Place the prepared muffin tin in the oven to heat for 5 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, in a large bowl, sift together the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
9. Whisk until blended.
10. Put the flaxseeds and 6 tablespoons water in a medium bowl and whisk until well blended.
11. Whisk in the pureed dates and the coconut oil until smooth.
12. Whisk in the oat milk until well combined.
13. Add the oat milk mixture to the cornmeal mixture and stir until just combined.
14. When the muffin tin is hot, remove it from the oven and immediately scrape the batter into the prepared muffin cups, distributing it evenly among them (they should be a little over three-quarters full).
15. Bake until the muffins are golden brown, to minutes.
16. Let cool in the tin for about minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool a bit more.
17. Make the whipped sweet corn: While the muffins are cooling, in a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
18. Add the onion and sautA, stirring often, until soft, 3 to 5 minutes.
19. Add the corn, sugar, and salt and cook, stirring often, until the corn is just tender, about 5 minutes.
20. Scrape the contents of the skillet into a food processor.
21. Add the cashew cream and process until silky smooth, adding water as necessary to reach the desired consistency.
22. Taste and season with lemon juice, salt, and white pepper.
23. Transfer to a serving bowl.
24. Serve the warm muffins slathered with whipped sweet corn and topped with pepper jelly.

Ingredients: '1 cup shredded napa cabbage', '1 cup shredded red cabbage', '2 teaspoons kosher salt, plus more as needed', '2 cups kohlrabi matchsticks', '2 cups Granny Smith (or other green apple) matchsticks', 'A cup minced fresh parsley, plus cup whole leaves', ' cup fresh lime juice', 'A cup unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 tablespoon shoyu', '1 teaspoon coconut palm sugar', ' cup safflower oil', 'Freshly ground white pepper'
Instructions:
1. Combine the cabbages in a large bowl.
2. Sprinkle with 2 teaspoons of the kosher salt.
3. With clean hands, massage the cabbage until soft and wilted, about 3 minutes.
4. Transfer the cabbage to a colander set in the sink and rinse the bowl.
5. Put a plate on top of the cabbage and weight it (a -ounce can of tomatoes works well for this).
6. Let sit for 1 hour.
7. Rinse the cabbage in cold water and let drain for minutes.
8. Wipe the bowl with a clean kitchen towel.
9. Return the cabbage to the bowl and add the kohlrabi, apples, and minced parsley.
10. Toss well to combine.
11. Set aside.
12. In a blender, combine the lime juice, vinegar, shoyu, sugar, and teaspoon salt.
13. With the blender running, slowly pour in the safflower oil through the hole in the lid and blend until emulsified.
14. Season with salt and white pepper to taste.
15. Pour enough of the dressing over the salad to lightly coat, toss, and garnish with the parsley leaves, then serve.

Ingredients: ' tsp. baking powder', '2 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', '1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '2 large egg yolks', '1 cup whole-milk Greek yogurt', ' cup granulated sugar', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted, slightly cooled', '1 tsp. vanilla extract', 'Vegetable oil (for frying; 6A8 cups)', 'Zest of lemon', '1 cup powdered sugar', 'Special Equipment: A deep-fry thermometer'
Instructions:
1. Whisk baking powder, 2 cups flour, and 1 tsp. salt in a medium bowl to combine.
2. Whisk egg yolks, yogurt, granulated sugar, butter, and vanilla in a large bowl until smooth.
3. Add dry ingredients to egg mixture with a spoon or rubber spatula until dough comes together and is smooth with no spots of dry flour remaining.
4. Turn out dough onto a well-floured large sheet of parchment or wax paper (you can be generous with the flour since youAll brush off the excess later).
5. Dust dough with more flour, place another large sheet of parchment or waxed paper over, and roll to " thick.
6. Remove top sheet of parchment and brush off any excess flour with a dry pastry brush.
7. Punch out circles with a "-diameter biscuit or cookie cutter, glass, or mug.
8. Punch out centers of rounds with a 1"-diamter cutter or plastic twist-off bottle cap (or poke a hole with a chopstick, then gently pull hole open to widen the center).
9. Reroll any scraps and punch out additional doughnuts and holes.
10. Fit a large pot with thermometer; pour in oil to come " up the sides of the pot.
11. Heat over medium-high until thermometer registers .
12. Fry doughnuts, turning halfway through, until puffy and a color somewhere between golden brown and mahogany, about 4 minutes total (time will be slightly less for doughnut holes).
13. Using a spider or slotted spoon, transfer to a wire rack and let cool minutes.
14. Meanwhile, whisk lemon zest, powdered sugar, a tiny pinch of salt, and cup water in a small bowl until a smooth glaze forms.
15. Dip each side of doughnuts and holes into glaze, letting excess run off before returning to rack.
16. Eat as soon as you canAt stand the suspense anymore.

Ingredients: ' cups assorted raw nuts and seeds', ' cup ( g) old-fashioned oats', '1 large egg', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted', ' cup (packed; g) dark brown sugar', '2 Tbsp. ( g) granulated sugar', ' tsp. vanilla extract', '1 tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. baking soda', ' cup assorted dried fruit (cut into " pieces if large)', ' cup chopped bittersweetchocolate bars or chips or disks', ' cup ( g) all-purpose flour', 'Flaky sea salt'
Instructions:
1. Place racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat to .
2. Toast nuts and seeds and oats on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing once, until golden brown, minutes.
3. Transfer to a medium bowl; let cool.
4. Meanwhile, mix egg, butter, brown sugar, granulated sugar, vanilla, salt, and baking soda in a large bowl; let sit until nuts are cool (this will make for a chewier cookie).
5. Add dried fruit and chocolate to nut mixture; toss to combine.
6. Give egg mixture a good stir, then stir in flour.
7. Mix in nut mixture, smashing it against the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula, until combined and mix-ins are evenly coated in dough. (It will look like too many mix-ins, but dough will come together as it chills.) Cover and chill at least 2 hours and up to 3 days.
8. Reheat oven to .
9. Using a A-cup measure or cookie scoop, portion out dough, packing firmly, to make cookies.
10. Divide between 2 parchment-lined baking sheets as you go.
11. Using measuring cup or your hand, press cookies into "-diameter disks about " thick; sprinkle with sea salt.
12. Bake cookies, rotating baking sheets top to bottom and front to back once, until golden brown and no longer wet-looking, minutes.
13. Let cool on baking sheets.
14. Do Ahead: Cookies can be made 1 week ahead.
15. Store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: ' cup raw pistachios', '2 Tbsp. nutritional yeast', '2 tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', '2 tsp. Aleppo-style pepper', '2 tsp. dried dill', '1 tsp. sugar', '1 tsp. onion powder', ' tsp. garlic powder', 'Special Equipment: A spice mill or mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Toast pistachios in a medium skillet over medium heat, shaking and tossing often, until golden brown, about 4 minutes.
2. Transfer to spice mill or mortar and pestle and let cool.
3. Add nutritional yeast and salt to pistachios and grind or pound until the texture of coarse meal.
4. Transfer to a small bowl and mix in Aleppo-style pepper, dill, sugar, onion powder, and garlic powder.
5. Serve dip with cruditAs.
6. Do Ahead: Ranch Fun Dip can be made 2 weeks ahead.
7. Store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 lemon, zest removed in wide strips, halved', 'A cup granulated sugar', '1 (-oz.) bag frozen blueberries', '1 Tbsp. cornstarch', '1 cup ( g) whole wheat flour', ' cup plus 2 Tbsp. ( g) quick-cooking old-fashioned oats', '1 tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. baking soda', '1 tsp. Diamond crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', ' cups plain yogurt (not Greek)', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted, plus more room-temperature for serving', '1 large egg', 'Nonstick vegetable oil spray or melted unsalted butter (for iron)'
Instructions:
1. Bring lemon zest, sugar, and A cup water to a boil in a heavy medium saucepan, stirring until sugar dissolves.
2. Cook (do not stir) until a thin syrup forms, 6A8 minutes.
3. Add blueberries and bring to a simmer.
4. Reduce heat to medium; cook, stirring constantly, 2 minutes.
5. Transfer 2 Tbsp. syrup to a small bowl, add cornstarch, and stir until smooth.
6. Add back to syrup; simmer until thickened, about 1 minute.
7. Remove from heat.
8. Fish out lemon zest and discard.
9. Squeeze in lemon juice to taste and let cool slightly.
10. Whisk flour, oats, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl.
11. Make a well in the center and add yogurt, then melted butter, then egg to well.
12. Using a fork, stir together wet ingredients, then mix, incorporating dry ingredients a little at a time, until a lumpy batter forms.
13. Heat waffle iron on medium-low.
14. Lightly coat with nonstick spray and pour a heaping cup batter onto iron (or up to 2 cups if using a larger one).
15. Cook until waffles are golden brown and cooked through, about 5 minutes. (Keep warm on a baking sheet in a oven if desired.) Repeat with remaining batter, coating iron with more nonstick spray as needed.
16. Serve with butter and warm syrup.

Recipe name: Salted Pistachio Crumbles With Berries And Ice Cream
Ingredients: ' cup ( g) raw pistachios', '1 cup ( g) all-purpose flour', ' cup ( g) sugar', ' cup ( g) cornmeal', '1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, melted', ' lb. strawberries (about 1 quart), hulled, halved, quartered if large', '1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', ' tsp. ground cardamom', '3 Tbsp. honey', '2 pints ice cream of your choice'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in upper third of oven; preheat to .
2. Spread out pistachios on a rimmed baking sheet and toast, tossing halfway through, until slightly golden brown, 6A8 minutes.
3. Let cool; pulse in a food processor until mostly finely ground but with some larger pieces remaining.
4. Mix pistachios, flour, sugar, cornmeal, and 1 tsp. salt in a large bowl, then drizzle in butter and work in with your fingers or the handle of a wooden spoon until small pebble- to pea-size clumps form.
5. Scatter crumble over a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet, trying not to break it up too much, and bake, tossing halfway through, until golden brown, minutes.
6. Let cool.
7. Meanwhile, toss strawberries, lemon juice, cardamom, and a pinch of salt in a medium bowl to combine.
8. Heat honey in a small skillet, stirring, until it begins to bubble and darkens slightly, about 4 minutes.
9. Remove from heat and scrape into strawberries.
10. Toss, until honey is evenly distributed (itAll be sticky in the beginning but will eventually dissolve).
11. Let sit, tossing occasionally, until berries release their juices, about minutes.
12. Scoop ice cream into bowls.
13. Spoon strawberries and their juices over; scatter crumble on top.

Recipe name: Grilled Pork Shoulder Steaks With Herb Salad
Ingredients: '4 medium shallots, 2 chopped, 2 thinly sliced', '6 garlic cloves', 'A cup plus 3 Tbsp. fish sauce', 'A cup plus 3 Tbsp. fresh lime juice', '3 Tbsp. light brown sugar, divided', '8 ("-thick) pork shoulder steaks ( lb. total)', 'Kosher salt', '2 red or green Thai chiles, thinly sliced', '3 cups Thai or sweet basil leaves, cilantro leaves with tender stems, andor dill'
Instructions:
1. Blend chopped shallots, garlic, A cup fish sauce, A cup lime juice, and 2 Tbsp. brown sugar in a blender until smooth.
2. Season steaks lightly with salt (the fish sauce will also season the steaks).
3. Transfer to a large bowl or a 12x9" baking dish.
4. Pour marinade over and turn steaks with tongs to coat evenly.
5. Let sit at room temperature 1 hour, or cover and chill up to hours, turning once (halfway through if you can).
6. Preheat a grill to high heat.
7. Grill steaks, turning every minute or 2, until lightly charred and crisp and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part registers , 7A9 minutes.
8. Transfer steaks to a cutting board and let rest at least 5 minutes before thinly slicing.
9. Meanwhile, mix chiles, remaining 3 Tbsp. fish sauce, remaining 3 Tbsp. lime juice, remaining 1 Tbsp. brown sugar, and 1 Tbsp. water in a large bowl to combine.
10. Add sliced shallots and herbs and toss to coat; season lightly with salt.
11. Arrange sliced meat on a platter and scatter herb salad over.

Ingredients: '2 tsp. black peppercorns', '1 tsp. ground turmeric', '1 tsp. sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt', '4 lb. chicken wings, patted dry', '1 (3") piece ginger, peeled, finely grated', '4 garlic cloves, finely grated', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', 'Lemon wedges (for serving)', 'Special Equipment: A spice mill'
Instructions:
1. Finely grind peppercorns, turmeric, sugar, and salt in spice mill, then transfer spice mix to a small bowl.
2. Arrange chicken wings on a foil-lined rimmed baking sheet.
3. Sprinkle wings all over with spice mix.
4. Reserve bowl.
5. Mix ginger, garlic, and oil in reserved bowl, then drizzle over wings.
6. Using tongs or your hands, turn wings to coat and let sit at room temperature at least minutes, or cover and chill up to 1 day.
7. Place baking sheet with chicken wings in a cold oven; preheat oven to .
8. Bake wings until golden brown and foil is stained with turmeric, minutes.
9. Using tongs, turn wings over and continue to cook until skin is crisp and wings are starting to char around the edges, another minutes.
10. Transfer wings to a platter and serve with lemon wedges alongside for squeezing over.

Recipe name: Flatbread With Avocado And Scallion Salsa
Ingredients: '1 tsp. sugar', '1 (-oz.) envelope active dry yeast (about tsp.)', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour', ' cup whole-milk plain Greek yogurt', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '2 tsp. kosher salt', '2 tsp. coriander seeds', '2 tsp. cumin seeds', '8 scallions', '4 Tbsp. plus 1 cup extra-virgin olive oil, divided, plus more for brushing', 'Kosher salt', '1 red or green chile (such as serrano or jalapeAo), finely chopped', '1 cup finely chopped parsley', '1 lemon', '4 medium avocados, pits removed', 'Flaky sea salt (optional)', 'Special Equipment: A spice mill'
Instructions:
1. Stir sugar into cup warm water in a large bowl.
2. Sprinkle in yeast and let sit until foamy, about minutes.
3. Add flour, yogurt, oil, and kosher salt to sugar mixture and mix until a shaggy dough forms (donAt worry about any dry or unincorporated bits).
4. Cover bowl with a damp kitchen towel and let sit in a warm, dry spot until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
5. While the dough is rising, toast coriander seeds and cumin seeds in a dry medium skillet over medium heat, shaking often, until fragrant and slightly darkened in color, about 3 minutes.
6. Transfer to a spice mill.
7. Let cool, then coarsely grind.
8. Set spice mixture aside.
9. Place scallions and 1 Tbsp. oil in skillet, season with kosher salt, and cook, turning occasionally, until softened and deeply charred, about 4 minutes.
10. Transfer to a cutting board; trim roots and discard.
11. Finely chop scallions and place in a medium bowl.
12. Add reserved spice mixture, chile, and parsley.
13. Finely grate half of lemon zest into bowl, then cut lemon in half and squeeze in juice.
14. Add 1 cup oil and stir well to combine.
15. Season with kosher salt and let sit while you make the flatbreads.
16. Turn out dough onto a lightly dusted surface and divide into 8 equal pieces.
17. Form into balls and, working with 1 ball at a time, roll out into 6" rounds about " thick.
18. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
19. Working 1 at a time and adding remaining 2 Tbsp. oil as needed, cook flatbread until bubbles appear over the surface, about 1 minute.
20. Flip and cook until cooked through, about 1 minute.
21. Continue to cook, turning often, until browned in spots on both sides, about 1 minute longer.
22. Transfer to a plate and wrap up in a clean kitchen towel to keep warm.
23. To serve, brush each flatbread with oil.
24. Coarsely smash avocados onto flatbread and spoon salsa over.
25. Sprinkle with sea salt if desired.

Recipe name: Charred Peppers With Lemon Ricotta And Cucumbers
Ingredients: ' cup walnuts', '6 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided, plus more for drizzling', '4 sweet Italian frying peppers or Anaheim chiles', ' cups whole-milk ricotta', '1 lemon', 'Kosher salt', '6 medium Persian cucumbers (about 1 lb.), sliced on a deep diagonal', '1 cup mint leaves, torn if large', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in upper third of oven; preheat to .
2. Spread out walnuts on a rimmed baking sheet and toast, tossing halfway through, until golden brown, minutes.
3. Let cool, then crush into large pieces with a flat-bottomed measuring cup or glass.
4. Meanwhile, heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium-high.
5. Cook peppers, shaking pan and turning peppers occasionally, until skins are lightly charred and flesh is tender, 6A8 minutes.
6. Transfer peppers to a cutting board and let cool slightly.
7. If peppers are large, cut in half lengthwise.
8. Combine ricotta and 2 Tbsp. oil in a small bowl; finely grate zest from lemon over and mix well.
9. Season lemon ricotta with salt.
10. Set lemon aside.
11. Combine peppers, cucumbers, mint, and half of walnuts in a large bowl.
12. Cut reserved lemon in half and squeeze juice into bowl.
13. Drizzle in 2 Tbsp. oil, season with salt, and toss to coat.
14. Transfer pepper mixture to a platter along with any juices in the bottom of bowl.
15. Dollop lemon ricotta over and top with remaining walnuts.
16. Drizzle with more oil and season with black pepper.

Recipe name: Radishes With Craa Me Frache And Furikake
Ingredients: '1 toasted nori sheet, coarsely torn', '1 Tbsp. mild red pepper flakes (such as Aleppo-style, Maras, or gochugaru)', '1 Tbsp. toasted sesame seeds', '1 tsp. flaky sea salt, plus more', ' lemon', ' lb. mixed radishes (such as red, watermelon, andor black), trimmed', ' cup (or more) crA me fraAche', 'Special Equipment: A spice mill'
Instructions:
1. Finely grind nori in spice mill; transfer to a small bowl and mix in red pepper flakes, sesame seeds, and 1 tsp. sea salt.
2. Using a Microplane, finely grate zest of lemon half into bowl and use your fingers to work it into nori mixture (this will keep the zest from clumping).
3. Set lemon half aside.
4. Cut radishes into a mix of "-thick rounds and 1"-thick wedges, depending on their size (you just want pieces that are easy to pick up and eat).
5. To serve, spoon crA me fraAche onto one side of a platter or large plate and sprinkle furikake over.
6. Pile up radishes on the other side of the platter.
7. Squeeze juice from reserved lemon half over radishes and sprinkle with a bit of sea salt.

Recipe name: Summer Tomato And Ricotta Tart With Oat Pastry
Ingredients: '1 cup (90g3 oz) rolled oats', ' cup (50g1A34 oz) white chia seeds', ' teaspoon fine table salt', '1 cup (140g5 oz) wholemeal (whole-wheat) spelt flour', ' cup (60g2 oz) almond meal (ground almonds)', 'A cup (80ml2A34 fl oz) light-flavored extra virgin olive oil', ' cup (60ml2 fl oz) water', '1 cup (240g8A12 oz) fresh ricotta', '2 teaspoons finely grated lemon zest', ' cup (40g1A12 oz) finely grated Parmesan', 'Sea salt and cracked black pepper', '400g ( oz) heirloom tomatoes, sliced', ' cup (10gA14 oz) small basil leaves', 'Extra virgin olive oil, for drizzling'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to ().
2. Place the oats, chia seeds and salt in a food processor and process for 1 minute or until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs.
3. Add the flour and almond meal and pulse to combine.
4. Place the oil and water in a small jug and whisk to combine.
5. With the food processor running, gradually add the oil mixture to the oat mixture and process until well combined but still crumbly.
6. Spoon the pastry into a 24cm-based 3cm-high ( in x 1 in) round loose-based fluted tart tin and press firmly over the base and sides until smooth.
7. Prick the pastry base all over with a fork and bake for minutes or until light golden.
8. While the tart shell is baking, place the ricotta, lemon zest, Parmesan, salt and pepper in a large bowl and mix to combine.
9. Allow the tart shell to cool completely before carefully lifting it from the tin.
10. Place on a serving plate and fill with the ricotta mixture.
11. Top the tart with the tomato and basil.
12. Drizzle with oil, sprinkle with salt and pepper and slice into wedges to serve.

Ingredients: '1 cup all-purpose flour', '1 tablespoon sugar', ' teaspoon salt', '6 tablespoons cold butter, cut into cubes', '3 to 5 tablespoons ice water', '3 cups sliced fruit (plums, peaches, apples) andor fresh or frozen berries', '4 tablespoons sugar, plus more for dusting', 'Pinch salt', '1 tablespoon all-purpose flour'
Instructions:
1. Stir together the flour, sugar, and salt, then add the cold butter and toss to combine.
2. Use your fingers to rub the butter into the flour, pinching it between your thumbs and fingers until youAve got a ragged mixture with irregular pieces of butter still intact, the largest about the size of an almond.
3. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons of the ice water over the mixture and use a fork to quickly incorporate.
4. Keep adding water by the tablespoon until it appears just moistenedAa dough thatAs too wet will lead to a tough crust, so err on the side of it seeming a little dry.
5. Dump the mixture onto a piece of plastic wrap.
6. Working from the outside of the plastic, shape it into a flat disc.
7. Wrap, then refrigerate for at least minutes and up to overnight.
8. Preheat the oven to .
9. Using the same bowl as for the crust, combine the fruit, 3 tablespoons of the sugar, and the salt.
10. In a small bowl, stir together the flour and remaining 1 tablespoon sugar.
11. Place the chilled dough on a lightly floured work surface and roll into a circle about inches in diameter.
12. Roll from the center, rotating degrees after each roll, and adding a bit more flour as needed if the dough sticks.
13. Drape the rolled dough over a rolling pin and transfer to a parchment-lined baking sheet.
14. Spread the flour-sugar mixture evenly over the dough, leaving a clean rim of about an inch, and pile the fruit on top.
15. Fold the crust around over the fruit, pleating rustically as you go.
16. Brush the exposed pastry with water, then sprinkle generouslyAover the crust and the fruit, tooAwith sugar.
17. Transfer to the oven and bake until the fruit juices are bubbling in the center and the crust is golden, to minutes, or more depending on the fruit.
18. Serve warm, with fresh whipped cream or ice cream, or cold, perhaps for breakfast, with yogurt.

Ingredients: '2 medium zucchini or yellow squash (about ounces total), coarsely grated', '1 teaspoon salt, divided', ' cup plus 1 tablespoon olive oil, plus more for greasing', '3 cups all-purpose flour (substitute up to half whole-wheat flour)', '1 tablespoon baking powder', '1 tablespoon sugar', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', '2 scallions, green and white parts, thinly sliced', ' cup coarsely shredded Parmesan cheese', '2 tablespoons finely shredded Parmesan cheese', ' cups light and crisp beer, such as a lager, pilsner, or Kolsch'
Instructions:
1. Toss the zucchini with teaspoon of the salt in a colander and let stand for minutes.
2. Gather it up in your hands and squeeze out as much moisture as you can.
3. Preheat the oven .
4. Generously grease an 8 x 4-inch loaf pan.
5. Whisk together the flour, baking powder, remaining teaspoon salt, the sugar, and black pepper.
6. Add the zucchini, scallions, coarsely shredded cheese, and cup of the olive oil, folding to combine, then pour in the beer.
7. Let stand for a few minutes as the dry ingredients absorb the beer, then fold gently until everything is moistened.
8. ItAll be a dense and tacky batter.
9. Scrape into the prepared pan.
10. Drizzle with the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil and sprinkle with the finely shredded cheese.
11. Transfer to the oven and bake until set in the center and a tester comes out clean, to minutes.
12. Cool in the pan for minutes, then unmold.
13. This bread can be served warm (though itAll be a bit gooey inside) and should be eaten within a day or two, stored loosely covered at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 cup basmati rice, preferably Dehraduni, or jasmine rice', '1A3 cup moong dal or red lentils', '1A4 teaspoon turmeric powder', '4 tablespoons plus 1A2 teaspoon ghee or unsalted butter', '1 teaspoon peeled, grated fresh ginger', '2 tablespoons cashews, broken into large pieces', '1 teaspoon cumin seeds or ground cumin', '1A2 teaspoon whole black peppercorns or freshly ground black pepper', '1 1A4 teaspoons salt', '1A4 cup dried unsweetened shredded coconut', 'Big pinch of asafetida (hing) powder'
Instructions:
1. Wash the rice in several changes of water until the water runs clear.
2. Soak the rice in water, generously covered, for at least minutes. (This is optional but results in softer, more evenly cooked rice.) Drain thoroughly using a fine-mesh sieve.
3. In a soup pot, dry-roast and stir the lentils continuously over medium heat until they are golden brown and have a nutty aroma, 2 to 3 minutes. (This step is optional but reduces the stickiness of the dal.) Thoroughly wash the lentils using a fine mesh colander.
4. Return them to the pot, together with the rice, and add 3 1A2 cups of water.
5. Bring to a boil.
6. Skim the foam off the top.
7. Add the turmeric powder, 2 tablespoons of the ghee, and the ginger to the boiling mixture.
8. While the rice and lentils are cooking, put 1A2 teaspoon of the ghee in a tempering pot or small pan over medium heat.
9. Add the cashews, stirring them until they are fragrant and turn golden brown, a few minutes.
10. Set the cashews aside to cool in a bowl lined with a paper towel.
11. If using cumin seeds and peppercorns, roughly crush them in a mortar with a pestle.
12. Set aside.
13. When the rice and lentil mixture is cooked, mix in the salt, coconut, and fried cashews, reserving some cashews for garnish.
14. Put the remaining 2 tablespoons of ghee in the tempering pot or small pan over medium heat.
15. When melted, add the crushed black peppercorns and cumin seeds and the asafetida.
16. Fry for a few seconds until fragrant.
17. Turn off the heat.
18. Immediately pour the spiced ghee over the rice.
19. To get all of the spiced ghee out of the pot, put a spoonful of the rice mixture into the pot, stir, and spoon it back into the rest of the dish.
20. Taste for salt and adjust as needed.
21. Garnish with the reserved cashews.
22. Serve hot.

Ingredients: ' cup coarsely chopped seeded jalapeAos (about 3 medium)', ' cup coarsely chopped green bell pepper', '3 cups organic cane sugar', ' cup apple cider vinegar', '1 (3-ounce) package liquid pectin'
Instructions:
1. Sterilize three -pint jars and their lids or similarly sized lidded storage containers.
2. In a food processor, combine the jalapeAos and the bell pepper and process, scraping down the sides as needed, until the peppers are very finely chopped, to seconds. (They will let off quite a bit of liquid; this is okay.) Transfer the contents of the food processor to a 4-quart stainless-steel or enameled Dutch oven.
3. Add the sugar and vinegar and bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat, stirring continuously.
4. Boil, still stirring, for 3 minutes, then squeeze in the pectin.
5. Return the mixture to a rolling boil, stirring continuously, and cook for 1 minute.
6. Immediately remove from the heat and let rest until the foam has settled, about 1 minute.
7. Skim off and discard any remaining foam.
8. Spoon the jelly into the sterilized jars and let cool for 1 hour.
9. Seal the jars and refrigerate until chilled before using.
10. The jelly will keep in the refrigerator for up to 3 months and up to 2 years if processed in a hot water bath and kept in a cool, dry place.

Recipe name: Win Son Bakerys Red Date Cake
Ingredients: '6 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature, plus more for pan', '6 oz. pitted dried red dates, coarsely chopped (about 2 cups)', ' tsp. baking soda', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour', '2 tsp. baking powder', '1 tsp. kosher salt', ' cup ( g) granulated sugar', ' tsp. vanilla extract', '2 large eggs', '4 whole pitted dried red dates', ' cup heavy cream', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter', ' cup (packed; g) dark brown sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. A 9"-diameter springform pan Preheat oven to .
2. Butter pan; set aside.
3. Bring dates, baking soda, and cup water to a boil in a small saucepan, stirring constantly.
4. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally, until water is almost completely evaporated, about 3 minutes.
5. Let cool.
6. Sift flour into a medium bowl; mix in baking powder and salt.
7. Using an electric mixer on medium-high speed, beat granulated sugar, vanilla, and remaining 6 Tbsp. butter in a large bowl until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes.
8. Add eggs one at a time, beating to blend after each addition; beat until mixture is very light and airy, about 3 minutes.
9. Reduce mixer speed to low; add half of dry ingredients and beat just until incorporated.
10. Mix in cooled date mixture, then add remaining dry ingredients and beat until just combined.
11. Scrape batter into prepared pan and smooth surface to make an even layer.
12. Bake cake until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, minutes.
13. While the cake bakes, make the glaze.
14. Slice each date in half lengthwise, then thinly slice each half into strips.
15. Place in a small bowl and pour in cup boiling water to cover; let soak.
16. Cook cream, butter, brown sugar, and salt in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally and breaking up sugar as needed, until butter is melted, about 5 minutes.
17. Bring just to a simmer and cook, stirring, until glaze thickens slightly, about 5 minutes.
18. Let cool until glaze thickens further but is still pourable, about minutes.
19. Remove cake from springform pan and slide onto a platter or large plate.
20. Arrange drained date strips along edge of cake so they look like spokes on a wheel.
21. Carefully drizzle warm glaze over cake, so it drips over the edges.
22. Let cool completely before serving.

Recipe name: Kong Jaban Korean Sweet Black Beans
Ingredients: ' cups dried black beans, rinsed and picked over', '2 quarts dashi or water, plus more if needed', '1 cup soy sauce', ' cup sugar', '2 tablespoons toasted sesame oil', '1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds'
Instructions:
1. Soak the beans in enough water to cover them by 3 inches in a covered container in the refrigerator for 8 to hours.
2. Drain the beans, put them in a pot, and add the dashi or water and soy sauce.
3. Set over medium-high heat and bring the liquid to a simmer.
4. Adjust the heat to maintain a gentle simmer and cook for 1 hour, stirring the beans on a regular basis.
5. The level of liquid will reduce as the beans cook; once it gets to the point where it does not completely cover the beans, you will need to stir more often to ensure that the beans cook evenly.
6. After an hour, the beans should be tender but still retain a little bite.
7. If they are still hard, add more dashi or water and cook for to minutes longer, until the beans are tender with a little bite.
8. Add the sugar, sesame oil, and sesame seeds to the pot and mix well. (ItAs important to wait to add the sugar until the beans are tender, as adding it in the beginning will cause the beans to harden when refrigerated.) Let the beans cool slightly, then transfer to an airtight container and refrigerate until chilled. (The beans will keep for up to 2 weeks in the refrigerator.) Serve the beans cold with hot rice.

Recipe name: Agua De Limaa3N Con Cha Limeade With Chia Seeds
Ingredients: '3 large limes, quartered', '4 cups water', ' cup sugar, or more if needed', ' cup chia seeds'
Instructions:
1. Put the limes, water, and sugar in a blender and pulse 3 to 4 times, just enough to extract the juice from the limes but not crush the skins (which would make the drink too bitter).
2. Strain through a fi ne-mesh sieve into a pitcher.
3. Taste and add more sugar if you like.
4. Whisk in the chia seeds and refrigerate for at least minutes, until completely chilled.
5. Stir before serving, then serve over ice.

Recipe name: Laagrimas De La Virgen Beet Cooler With Fruits
Ingredients: '4 red beets', '8 cups water', '1 cup sugar', '1 small apple (Fuji or McIntosh), peeled and diced', '1 banana, diced', '1 small orange, peeled and diced', '4 leaves romaine lettuce, cut into thin strips', 'Juice of 1 lime'
Instructions:
1. Peel the beets.
2. Cut them into chunks and pass them through a juicer.
3. Combine the water and sugar in a pitcher and stir until the sugar has dissolved.
4. Add the beet juice.
5. Refrigerate until completely chilled.
6. Add the apple, banana, orange, and lettuce, then stir in the lime juice (which will help prevent the fruit from browning).
7. Serve chilled over ice, if desired.
8. Alternately, divide the mixed diced fruit among the glasses; place the beet water in a pitcher for guests to pour over.

Recipe name: Guava Grapefruit And Rosemary Agua Fresca Agua Fresca De Guayaba Toronja Y Romero
Ingredients: '3 cups ( ml24 fl oz) water', 'Juice of 3 grapefruits', '2 cups ( g) guava puree (preferably from fresh guavas)', 'Leaves from 1 sprig fresh rosemary', ' cup ( g) sugar, or to taste'
Instructions:
1. In a blender, combine all the ingredients, except the sugar, and blend well.
2. Standard blender jars probably wonAt fit all the ingredients so blend in batches.
3. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a pitcher.
4. Add sugar (or your preferred sweetener) to taste, adjusting for your own preference and based on how sweet the fruit is to begin with.
5. Add enough ice to your liking.
6. As an option, you can garnish with a piece of an ingredient in the agua.
7. For example, a sprig of rosemary, a stick of cinnamon, or slices of lemon.
8. Pour into ice-filled glasses.

Ingredients: ' cup sugar', '4 cups water', '4 cups chopped fruit (watermelon, cantaloupe, pineapple, papaya, strawberries, oranges, peaches, mangoes, or any combination)', 'Ice cubes', 'Lemon or lime wedges (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Stir the sugar and water together in a large pitcher until the sugar dissolves.
2. Measure out 1 cup.
3. Puree the fruit and the 1 cup sugar water in a blender.
4. Pour the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer back into the pitcher with the sugar water, mashing with a whisk or wooden spoon to eliminate any pulp.
5. Taste and add more sugar, if desired.
6. Serve over ice, with a lemon or lime wedge, if desired.

Ingredients: '1 -oz. envelope active dry yeast (about tsp.)', '1 tsp. sugar', 'A cup ( g) whole wheat flour', ' tsp. kosher salt', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for bowl', '3 cups ( g) bread flour, plus more for surface', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 pint cherry tomatoes', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', '3 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '1 tsp. dried oregano', '1 bunch Swiss chard', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '1 garlic clove', '1 lemon', ' oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about cup)', '1 (-oz.) can whole peeled tomatoes', 'A cup extra-virgin olive oil', '2 tsp. kosher salt', 'Bread flour (for dusting)', 'Extra-virgin olive oil (for brushing and drizzling)', '1 1-lb. ball mozzarella, torn into bite-size pieces', '8 oz. Manchego cheese, coarsely grated', '4 oz. sliced pepperoni andor thinly sliced soppressata (optional)', 'Rosemary Agrodolce (optional)', 'Basil leaves, torn if large, and crushed red pepper flakes (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Stir yeast, sugar, and cups warm (not hot) water in the bowl of a stand mixer until yeast dissolves.
2. Let sit until mixture is foamy, about 5 minutes.
3. Add whole wheat flour, salt, and 2 Tbsp. oil to yeast mixture and mix to combine.
4. Fit mixer with dough hook and, with mixer on low speed, gradually add 3 cups ( g) bread flour, mixing until a shaggy dough forms, about 4 minutes from when you start adding the flour.
5. Stop mixer and scrape down sides of bowl to incorporate any dry bits into dough.
6. Increase mixer speed to medium and knead until dough is smooth and shiny, about 5 minutes.
7. Transfer dough to a lightly floured surface and knead briefly to bring together.
8. Shape into a ball, place in a large lightly oiled bowl, and turn to coat.
9. Cover bowl and let dough sit in warm draft-free spot until doubled in size, hours.
10. Make your toppings while your dough is rising.
11. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
12. Add tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon, until skins start to blister and split, about 2 minutes.
13. Add garlic and toss to coat.
14. Cook, lightly pressing on tomatoes with spoon to release juices without smashing completely, until garlic is softened, about 2 minutes.
15. Mix oregano into sauce; taste and season with more salt and pepper if needed.
16. Do ahead: Sauce can be made 4 days ahead.
17. Let cool, then cover and chill.
18. Bring to room temperature and stir to combine before using.
19. Cut Swiss chard leaves away from ribs and stems, then cut crosswise into 3"-wide strips.
20. Trim away woody part from stems; discard.
21. Finely chop ribs and stems.
22. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
23. Add ribs and stems and season with salt and black pepper.
24. Cook, stirring occasionally, until they release some liquid, look slightly shriveled, and are a shade or two darker, about 5 minutes.
25. Add leaves and red pepper flakes and toss to coat.
26. Season with more salt and black pepper and reduce heat to medium.
27. Cook, tossing occasionally, until leaves are bright green, wilted, and tender, about 4 minutes.
28. Transfer chard mixture to a medium bowl and let cool slightly.
29. Finely grate garlic into bowl with chard mixture, then finely grate in zest from lemon; mix well.
30. Add Parmesan and mix again to combine.
31. Taste and season with more salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes, if needed.
32. Do ahead: Chard mixture can be made 2 days ahead.
33. Let cool, then cover and chill.
34. Bring to room temperature and stir to combine before using.
35. PurAe tomatoes in a blender on low speed (or use an immersion blender if you have one) until smooth (try not to aerate it too much).
36. Transfer sauce to a medium bowl and stir in oil and salt.
37. Do ahead: Passata can be made 3 days ahead.
38. Cover and chill, or freeze up to 6 months.
39. Prepare a grill for medium-high indirect heat (for a charcoal grill, bank coals on one side of grill; for a gas grill, leave one or two burners off).
40. Clean grate.
41. Turn out dough onto a lightly floured surface and divide into 4 pieces.
42. Working one at a time, gently shape into balls.
43. Transfer to a lightly floured small rimmed baking sheet and cover.
44. Let sit minutes.
45. Place a ball of dough on a lightly floured surface (keep remaining balls covered) and, using your fingers, press out to an 8" round.
46. Drape dough over the backs of your hands and gently pull and stretch, rotating dough as you go, until about " in diameter (itAs okay if itAs not a perfect circle).
47. Brush dough with oil.
48. Place, oiled side down, on grill over direct heat and cook until large bubbles appear across surface, dough stiffens, and underside is dark brown, about 2 minutes.
49. Brush top with oil, turn over, and cook just to lightly dry out second side, about seconds.
50. Using tongs and a metal spatula, move dough over to cooler side of grill.
51. Top with a spoonful of passata or burst cherry tomatoes and one quarter of cheese (either alone or in combination).
52. Add pepperoni or salami (if using), then some of the lemony Swiss chard, andor Rosemary Agrodolce as desired.
53. Carefully slide pizza back over direct heat.
54. Cook until cheese is melted and toppings are heated through, about 2 minutes.
55. If dough is in danger of burning on underside but toppings need more time, return pizza to cooler side, cover grill, and cook another 1A2 minutes.
56. Transfer pizza to a cutting board and drizzle with more oil.
57. Top with basil and red pepper flakes if desired.
58. Repeat with remaining ingredients to make 3 more pizzas.

Ingredients: ' cup honey', '1 Tbsp. finely chopped rosemary', 'Kosher salt', '1 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar', ' red onion, thinly sliced'
Instructions:
1. Combine honey, rosemary, and 2 Tbsp. water in a small saucepan over medium heat; season with salt.
2. Bring to a simmer, stirring to dissolve honey, then cook, swirling pan occasionally, until slightly thickened and fragrant, about 2 minutes.
3. Stir in vinegar and cook 1 minute to evaporate some of the vinegar.
4. Remove from heat and stir in onion.
5. Let cool in pan.
6. Do ahead: Agrodolce can be made 1 week ahead.
7. Let cool, then cover and chill.
8. Bring to room temperature and stir to combine before using.

Ingredients: '2 large skinless, boneless chicken breasts (about lb. total)', '3 Tbsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt, plus more', '1 lemon, halved', '2 Tbsp. Dijon mustard', '6 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '8 oz. green beans, trimmed', '1 large head of Bibb lettuce', '1 small bunch chives, sliced into (2A-long) pieces', '1 cup basil leaves', '1 bunch radishes, trimmed, halved, cut into wedges if large', '1 cup torn peperoncini', '1 avocado, thinly sliced'
Instructions:
1. Place chicken in a large saucepan and pour in 4 cups cold water to cover; add 3 Tbsp.
2. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
3. Morton kosher salt.
4. Bring to a gentle boil over medium heat, then immediately turn over chicken with tongs.
5. Cover pot, remove from heat, and let chicken sit until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part registers , minutes (depending on thickness of breast).
6. Transfer chicken to a cutting board and let rest minutes.
7. Meanwhile, squeeze lemon halves into a medium bowl.
8. Whisk in mustard, then gradually stream in oil, whisking constantly until dressing is thick and emulsified.
9. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Using a rolling pin, whack green beans on a cutting board until skins split and insides are softened but not pulverized.
11. Transfer beans to a medium bowl and drizzle in 2 Tbsp. dressing.
12. Massage beans with your hands to break down further and coat; season with salt.
13. Separate lettuce leaves; toss in another medium bowl with chives and basil.
14. Arrange on a platter.
15. Toss radishes, peperoncini, and 1 Tbsp. dressing in the same bowl; season with salt.
16. Slice chicken thick; season with pepper.
17. Arrange chicken, radish salad, green beans, and avocado on platter with lettuce as desired.
18. Drizzle with more dressing; serve remaining dressing alongside.

Ingredients: '3 tsp. peanut or vegetable oil, divided', '2 lb. asparagus, trimmed, cut on a diagonal into (1"") pieces', 'Kosher salt', '8 oz. ground pork', '6 scallions, white and pale green parts only, finely chopped', '5 garlic cloves, finely chopped', '1 (2") piece ginger, peeled, finely chopped', '2 Tbsp. Shaoxing wine (Chinese rice wine) or medium-dry sherry', '2 Tbsp. soy sauce', '1 tsp. toasted sesame oil', 'Cooked rice and store-bought chili crisp (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Heat 1 tsp. peanut oil in a large skillet over high.
2. Once pan is hot (oil will be lightly smoking), add half of asparagus and a couple pinches of salt and cook, tossing only once or twice so the pieces have a chance to blister, until crisp-tender and lightly browned, about 4 minutes.
3. Transfer asparagus to a plate.
4. Add 1 tsp. peanut oil to skillet (no need to wipe it out) and repeat process with remaining asparagus.
5. Reduce heat to medium-high; add remaining 1 tsp. peanut oil to skillet, then add pork, spreading out into an even layer.
6. Season with a couple pinches of salt and cook, undisturbed, until meat begins to brown underneath, about 2 minutes.
7. Break up meat with a wooden spoon and add scallions, garlic, and ginger.
8. Cook, stirring, until pork is crisp and mixture is very fragrant, about 2 minutes.
9. Add wine and soy sauce and return asparagus to skillet.
10. Cook, turning to coat with pork mixture, until heated through, about 1 minute.
11. Transfer stir-fry to a platter or large shallow bowl and drizzle with sesame oil.
12. Serve with rice and chili crisp.

Recipe name: Ramen Noodles With Spring Onions And Garlic Crisp
Ingredients: '1 bunch spring onions or scallions, very thinly sliced, divided', '1 (3") piece ginger, peeled, thinly sliced', '4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', ' cup vegetable or sunflower oil', ' serrano or other green chile, thinly sliced (with seeds)', ' cup finely chopped tender herbs (such as cilantro, mint, andor basil)', '2 Tbsp. low-sodium soy sauce', '1 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 Tbsp. toasted sesame seeds', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. sugar', '2 (5-oz.) packages fresh wavy ramen noodles', '1 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces'
Instructions:
1. Place half of spring onions in a large heatproof bowl and set a fine-mesh sieve on top; set aside.
2. Combine remaining spring onions with ginger, garlic, and oil in a small saucepan.
3. Set over medium-high and cook, stirring occasionally, until garlic is golden and scallions are beginning to crisp and turn golden brown, minutes.
4. Pour mixture through reserved sieve onto spring onions.
5. Turn out garlic crisp in sieve onto paper towels to drain.
6. Stir spring onions in bowl until just softened, about 1 minute.
7. Stir in chile, herbs, soy sauce, vinegar, sesame seeds, pepper, salt, and sugar.
8. Let dressing sit minutes.
9. Meanwhile, cook noodles according to package directions.
10. Drain and rinse under warm water to get rid of any excess starch.
11. Transfer noodles to bowl with dressing and add butter and half of garlic crisp; toss to coat noodles.
12. Divide among bowls; top with remaining garlic crisp.

Ingredients: '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling', '1 bunch ramps (about 6 oz.), bulbs and greens separated, bulbs halved lengthwise, greens torn into (2"") pieces', '3 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', ' tsp. mild red pepper flakes (such as Maras, Urfa, or Aleppo-style), plus more for serving', ' cup dry white wine', ' lb. large shrimp, peeled, deveined, preferably tails left on', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter', 'Kosher salt', ' lemon'
Instructions:
1. Combine 3 Tbsp. oil and ramp bulbs in a large skillet.
2. Set over medium heat and cook, tossing occasionally, until bulbs are softened but have not taken on any color, 6A8 minutes.
3. Add garlic and tsp. mild red pepper flakes and cook, tossing occasionally, until garlic is softened, about 3 minutes.
4. Add wine and increase heat to medium-high.
5. Let cook, stirring occasionally, until wine is reduced by half, about 2 minutes.
6. Add ramp greens, shrimp, and butter to pan.
7. Season with salt and cook, tossing occasionally, until ramp greens are wilted, shrimp turn pink, and butter is melted, about 3 minutes.
8. Remove skillet from heat.
9. Finely grate zest from lemon half over shrimp, then squeeze in juice.
10. Transfer shrimp to a platter.
11. Sprinkle with more mild red pepper flakes and drizzle with plenty of oil.

Ingredients: '1 (-lb.) whole chicken, patted dry', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', '8 green garlic stalks, dark green tops removed, divided', 'Zest of 1 lemon', '6 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil'
Instructions:
1. Season chicken all over inside and out with salt and pepper (make sure to get in and around every nook and cranny; about 4 tsp.
2. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
3. Morton kosher salt).
4. Place on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet and let sit at room temperature while you make the garlic butter, or chill, uncovered, up to 1 day.
5. If chilling, let sit at room temperature 1 hour before roasting.
6. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
7. Coarsely chop 4 green garlic stalks; transfer to a food processor.
8. Pulse until very finely chopped.
9. Add zest and butter and pulse until almost smooth.
10. Cut remaining 4 green garlic stalks in half lengthwise; set aside.
11. Pat chicken dry again with paper towels (this will help the butter adhere and get the skin nicely browned).
12. Smear garlic butter all over chicken.
13. Gently lift the skin on the breast away from the flesh and rub some butter inside.
14. Toss reserved green garlic and oil in a large cast-iron skillet or 13x9" baking dish to coat; season with salt.
15. Tie chicken legs together with kitchen twine and tuck wings underneath back.
16. Place chicken, breast side up, on top of garlic in skillet.
17. Roast chicken, rotating pan halfway through for even browning, until skin is golden and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of breast registers (donAt worry; temperature will climb to as the chicken rests), minutes.
18. Let chicken rest at least minutes and up to minutes.
19. Transfer chicken to a cutting board and carve as desired.
20. Serve with roasted green garlic alongside.

Recipe name: Scallion Pancakes With Chili-Ginger Dipping Sauce
Ingredients: '1 (") piece ginger, peeled, thinly sliced', '2 Tbsp. low-sodium soy sauce', '2 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 tsp. chili oil', '1 tsp. sugar', '1 cup all-purpose flour', ' cup cornstarch', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. sugar', '1 cup chilled club soda', '2 tsp. low-sodium soy sauce', '1 tsp. toasted sesame oil', ' scallions, thinly sliced on a diagonal (about 2 cups)', '4 Tbsp. vegetable oil'
Instructions:
1. Whisk ginger, soy sauce, vinegar, chili oil, and sugar in a small bowl until sugar is dissolved.
2. Set sauce aside.
3. Whisk flour, cornstarch, salt, and sugar in a large bowl.
4. Whisk club soda, soy sauce, and sesame oil in a medium bowl to combine, then pour into dry ingredients and whisk until smooth (be careful not to overmix; itAs okay if there are a few small lumps).
5. Fold in scallions.
6. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high.
7. Pour cup batter into skillet.
8. Cook, moving pan around on the burner for even cooking, until bottom of pancake is set and golden, about 2 minutes.
9. Flip and cook, pressing down on pancake to create direct contact with pan, until other side is golden, about 1 minute.
10. Continue cooking, turning often to keep scallions from burning, until golden brown and crisp and cooked through, about 5 minutes longer.
11. Transfer pancake to a wire rack.
12. Repeat process with remaining batter and remaining 3 Tbsp. oil 3 more times to make a total of 4 pancakes.
13. Cut each pancake into wedges if desired and serve with reserved sauce on the side for dipping.

Ingredients: 'Nonstick cooking oil spray or room-temperature unsalted butter (for pan)', '8 oz. cream cheese (not low-fat), cut into (1") pieces', '3 large eggs, chilled', '1A cups ( g) sugar, divided', '1 tsp. vanilla extract, divided', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', ' tsp. plus cup plus 2 Tbsp. cocoa powder, preferably Dutch-process', ' Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces', '1 tsp. instant espresso powder (optional)', ' cup ( g) all-purpose flour'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Lightly coat a 9x9" pan, preferably metal, with nonstick spray.
3. Line with parchment paper, leaving overhang on all sides.
4. Lightly coat parchment with nonstick spray.
5. Place cream cheese in a medium heatproof bowl set over a medium saucepan of barely simmering water (do not let bowl touch water).
6. Heat cream cheese, stirring occasionally, until very soft, about 5 minutes.
7. Remove bowl from heat (leave water simmering).
8. Using a heatproof rubber spatula or wooden spoon, smash and mix cream cheese until smooth.
9. Add 1 egg, A cup ( g) sugar, tsp. vanilla, and tsp. salt and whisk until very smooth.
10. Transfer about half of cream cheese mixture to a small bowl and whisk in tsp. cocoa powder.
11. Place butter in another medium heatproof bowl.
12. Add espresso powder (if using) and remaining 1 cup ( g) sugar, cup plus 2 Tbsp. cocoa powder, and tsp. salt.
13. Place bowl over saucepan of still simmering water and cook, stirring occasionally once the butter starts to melt, until mixture is homogeneous and too hot to leave your finger in, 7A9 minutes.
14. Let cool 5 minutes.
15. Add remaining 2 eggs and remaining tsp. vanilla to butter mixture one at a time, whisking vigorously after each addition until smooth and glossy.
16. Add flour and mix with spatula or spoon until no longer visible, then vigorously mix another strokes.
17. Scoop out cup brownie batter and set aside.
18. Scrape remaining batter into prepared pan and spread into an even layer.
19. Working quickly, alternate dollops of cocoaAcream cheese mixture and cream cheese mixture over batter.
20. Dollop reserved batter on top (it will be quite thick).
21. DonAt worry if your design looks random and spotted.
22. Bake brownies until center is set and no longer looks wet, minutes.
23. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let cool.
24. Using parchment paper overhang, lift brownies out of pan and transfer to a cutting board.
25. Remove parchment paper and cut into sixteen " squares, wiping knife clean between slices.

Ingredients: ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', ' tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. baking soda', ' tsp. sugar', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour, plus more for surface', ' Tbsp. chilled unsalted butter, cut into (") pieces, plus 2 Tbsp. melted', '8 scallions, thinly sliced crosswise (not on a diagonal)', ' cups sour cream, plus more for serving', 'Flaky sea salt'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Whisk salt, pepper, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, and cups flour in a large bowl to combine.
3. Add chilled butter and toss to coat.
4. Using your hands, work butter into dry ingredients until there are lots of flat pieces and pea-size bits.
5. Add scallions and toss to evenly distribute.
6. Create a well in the center of mixture and add cups sour cream into the center.
7. Using a fork and working in circles, mix until large shaggy clumps form.
8. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead once or twice until it comes together (flour your hands if needed).
9. If there are any loose bits, add them to center of dough and knead once more to incorporate.
10. Pat dough into an 8x4" rectangle about 1" thick.
11. Working from a short side, fold dough in thirds as you would a letter.
12. It doesnAt need to overlap perfectly.
13. Pat dough into another 8x4" rectangle, then fold dough in thirds like a letter one more time.
14. Pat dough back into an 8x4" rectangle and cut in half lengthwise.
15. Cut each half crosswise into 4 squares for a total of 8 biscuits.
16. Transfer biscuits to a parchment-lined baking sheet.
17. Brush tops gently with melted butter; sprinkle with sea salt.
18. Bake biscuits until golden brown, minutes.
19. Serve warm with sour cream (or more butter!) for slathering.

Ingredients: ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, cut into 8 equal pieces', '1 cup ( g) all-purpose flour', ' cup ( g) buckwheat flour', ' tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. baking soda', ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt; plus more (optional)', 'A cup (packed; g) light brown sugar', ' cup ( g) granulated sugar', '1 large egg', '2 large egg yolks', '1 tsp. vanilla extract', '6 oz. bittersweet chocolate (% - % cacao) bars or wafers (disks, pistoles, fA ves), coarsely chopped'
Instructions:
1. Heat butter in a small saucepan over the lowest heat possible until melted (you donAt want it to sputter or brown), about 5 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, whisk both flours, baking powder, baking soda, and tsp.
3. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
4. Morton kosher salt in a medium bowl.
5. Scrape butter into a large bowl and add brown sugar and granulated sugar.
6. Whisk vigorously until butter is absorbed into sugar, about seconds.
7. Add egg, then egg yolks, one at a time, whisking to combine after each addition; whisk in vanilla.
8. Mixture should look much lighter in color and be almost creamy.
9. Add dry ingredients and use a rubber spatula or wooden spoon to stir until just incorporated and almost no dry streaks remain.
10. Set aside a handful of chocolate, then add remaining chocolate to batter.
11. Gently mix just to evenly distribute.
12. Cover bowl with a kitchen towel (a silicone lid or plastic wrap also works) and chill 2 hours.
13. Place racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat to .
14. Line 2 baking sheets with parchment.
15. Using a tablespoon, scoop out 2-Tbsp. portions of dough (or, if you have one, this is a leveled-off cookie scoop or a heaping ) and place 5 on each baking sheet, spacing evenly.
16. Roll into balls and gently press 2A3 pieces of reserved chocolate onto the top of each one.
17. ItAs okay to jam the chocolate on thereAsome pieces can even be vertical.
18. Cover and chill any remaining dough.
19. Bake cookies, rotating baking sheets top to bottom and front to back halfway through, until edges are golden brown and centers are puffed, minutes.
20. Working one at a time, pull baking sheets out of the oven and tap lightly on the stove to deflate cookies.
21. Sprinkle with Diamond Crystal kosher salt (if youAre using Morton, skip it: the crystals are too large).
22. Let cookies cool on baking sheets 5 minutes, then transfer to wire racks and let cool completely.
23. Let baking sheets cool, then turn parchment paper over.
24. Repeat process with remaining dough, dividing evenly between baking sheets, to make 6A8 more cookies.
25. Do Ahead: Cookies can be baked 3 days ahead.
26. Let cool; store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: ' oz. (45ml) gin', ' oz. (45ml) sweet vermouth', '2 dashes Fernet-Branca', 'Strip of orange peel, to serve'
Instructions:
1. Stir gin, sweet vermouth, and Fernet-Branca with ice until chilled.
2. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
3. Twist a swathe of orange peel over the surface of the drink and add to glass as garnish.

Recipe name: Avocado And Lemon Water Agua De Aguacate Y Limaa3N Amarillo
Ingredients: '1 avocado, halved', '4 cups ( ml32 fl oz) water', ' cup ( ml4 fl oz) lemon juice or lime juice', ' cup ( g) sugar, plus more to taste'
Instructions:
1. Scoop the avocado into a blender.
2. Add the rest of the ingredients and blend until smooth.
3. Add more sugar to taste, if desired.
4. Serve over ice.

Ingredients: ' cup buttermilk or plain whole-milk yogurt', '7 Tbsp. vegetable oil, divided', '1 large egg', ' cup (packed; g) dark brown sugar', '1 -oz. envelope active dry yeast (about tsp.)', '3 cups ( g) all-purpose flour', ' tsp. baking soda', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '1 cup (packed; g) Medjool dates, halved, pitted', '2 tsp. ground cinnamon', ' tsp. kosher salt', '3 Tbsp. vegetable oil, divided', ' cup (packed; g) dark brown sugar', ' cup ( g) powdered sugar', '3 Tbsp. buttermilk or plain whole-milk yogurt', '1 tsp. vanilla paste or extract'
Instructions:
1. Combine buttermilk and 6 Tbsp. oil in a small microwave-safe bowl (it wonAt get smooth and thatAs okay) and heat in the microwave in three -second bursts until just about body temperature (it shouldnAt feel hot or cold to the touch); an instant-read thermometer will register about . (Alternatively, you can heat in a small saucepan over medium-low, stirring constantly, about 1 minute.) Whisk egg, brown sugar, and yeast in a liquid measuring cup to combine, then whisk into buttermilk mixture.
2. Pulse flour, baking soda, and salt in a food processor to combine.
3. With the motor running, stream in buttermilk mixture.
4. Process until about % of the dough comes together into a ball (mixture will look very wet at first, then begin to pull away from the sides of processor), about 2 minutes.
5. Using a bowl scraper or rubber spatula, scrape dough onto an unfloured surface. (It should still be pretty wet and sticky.) Knead, pushing it away from you, then pulling it back toward you, until a smooth ball forms, about 3 minutes.
6. The dough will grow silkier, tighter, and easier to work with as you knead.
7. Roll out dough into a rough 8" square.
8. Fold dough over onto itself to make an 8x4" rectangle, then flatten it slightly and fold over once more to make a 4" square.
9. Roll dough back out to an 8" square and repeat folding process (you should be back at a 4" square).
10. Pour remaining 1 Tbsp. oil into a medium bowl and add dough (still folded); turn to coat.
11. Cover bowl tightly and chill dough until doubled in volume, at least 8 hours and up to 1 day.
12. Place dates in a small bowl and pour in cups hot water to cover.
13. Let soak until softened, about minutes.
14. Drain dates and transfer to a food processor; discard soaking liquid.
15. Add cinnamon, salt, and 2 Tbsp. oil.
16. PurAe, scraping down sides as needed, until smooth, about 4 minutes. (You should have about cup purAe.) Grease a " cast-iron skillet with remaining 1 Tbsp. oil.
17. Transfer dough to a clean, unfloured surface and roll out to an 8" square.
18. Fold in half into an 8x4" rectangle, then fold rectangle over itself to form a 4" square (same folding process as you did before).
19. If dough feels tough and uncooperative, let it sit about 5 minutes to relax and try again.
20. Roll out dough to a " square about " thick.
21. Dollop date purAe all over.
22. Using a small offset spatula, spread evenly over dough, leaving a " border without purAe along edge farthest from you.
23. Sprinkle brown sugar over purAe.
24. Starting at edge closest to you, roll up dough into a tight log.
25. Using a sharp serrated knife and long sawing motions, trim about " of dough from both ends and discard.
26. Slice log crosswise into 3 sections, wiping knife clean between slices.
27. Slice each section crosswise into 3 buns (you should have 9 buns total that are each about 1" thick).
28. Transfer buns to prepared pan as you go.
29. Cover pan tightly with foil (or a domed lid if you have one) and place in a warm, dry spot.
30. Let buns rise until theyAre doubled in volume and spring back when poked, leaving only a small indentation, hours, depending on how warm and humid your kitchen is.
31. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
32. Bake buns, still covered, until puffed, pale, and mostly set, about minutes.
33. Remove foil and continue to bake until golden brown, about minutes if you prefer a soft and squishy bun and up to minutes for a more toasted bun.
34. Let cool slightly.
35. Meanwhile, whisk powdered sugar, buttermilk, and vanilla in a medium bowl to combine.
36. Brush glaze over warm buns and serve in skillet.
37. Do Ahead: PurAe can be made 3 days ahead.
38. Place in an airtight container; cover and chill.
39. Bring to room temperature before using.

Recipe name: Grilled Coconut Shrimp With Shishito Peppers
Ingredients: '6 garlic cloves, finely grated', '1 Tbsp. finely grated lime zest', ' cup low-sodium or tamari soy sauce', ' cup grapeseed or vegetable oil, plus more for grill', '1 lb. large shrimp, peeled, deveined, preferably with tails left on', ' cup toasted unsweetened shredded coconut, plus more for serving', '8 oz. shishito peppers', ' cup basil leaves', ' cup fresh lime juice', 'Flaky sea salt'
Instructions:
1. Stir together garlic, lime zest, soy sauce, and cup oil in a medium bowl.
2. Add shrimp and toss to coat.
3. Add cup coconut and toss again to coat.
4. Let sit while grill heats, at least 5 minutes and up to minutes.
5. Prepare a grill for high heat, lightly oil grate.
6. Carefully arrange shrimp in an even layer on grate.
7. Grill, carefully turning halfway through, until opaque and lightly charred, about 2 minutes.
8. Some of the coconut will fall off in the process, and thatAs okay.
9. Transfer to a serving platter.
10. Grill peppers, turning occasionally and being careful not to let them fall through grate, until lightly charred all over, about 6 minutes.
11. Transfer to platter with shrimp.
12. Top shrimp and peppers with basil, drizzle with lime juice, and sprinkle with sea salt and more coconut.

Recipe name: Mochi Cake Any Way You Want It
Ingredients: '6 Tbsp. unsalted butter or virgin coconut oil, melted, plus more for pan', ' cups ( g) sugar, plus more for pan', '1 (.5-oz.) can unsweetened coconut milk or 1A cups half-and-half', '2 large eggs', '2 tsp. vanilla extract', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '2 cups ( g) glutinous sweet rice flour, preferably Koda Farms Blue Star Mochiko', '3 Tbsp. powdered flavoring of choice (such as unsweetened cocoa powder, pandan leaf powder, or malted milk powder) or 1 Tbsp. matcha powder plus 2 Tbsp. glutinous sweet rice flour or 3 Tbsp. glutinous sweet rice flour', '1 tsp. ground spice of choice (such as cinnamon, cardamom, or turmeric)', '1 tsp. baking powder', ' cup unsweetened shredded coconut or sesame seeds'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Generously butter a 9"-diameter cake pan or an 8x8" baking pan or cups of two standard -cup muffin pans. (If you only have 1 muffin pan, you can bake the extra batter in a small loaf pan or mini muffin pan, or just discard it.) Generously coat pan with sugar, tapping out excess (about cup should do it).
3. Place cups ( g) sugar and 6 Tbsp. butter in a large bowl.
4. If using coconut milk, heat in a small saucepan over medium, whisking constantly, until no clumps remain, then whisk into sugar mixture.
5. If using half-and-half, whisk directly into sugar mixture.
6. Add eggs, vanilla, and salt and whisk to combine.
7. Whisk rice flour, powdered flavoring of choice (or 3 Tbsp. more rice flour), spice of choice, and baking powder in a medium bowl to combine, then whisk into egg mixture.
8. Scrape batter into prepared pan and sprinkle with coconut or sesame seeds.
9. Bake until top begins to crack and cake springs back when gently pressed, minutes for cake and minutes for cupcakes.
10. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let cake cool in pan minutes.
11. Run a knife around perimeter of cake, place a plate over pan, and invert cake onto plate.
12. Hold wire rack over cake and invert cake onto rack (simply turn cupcakes out onto rack and turn over).
13. Let cool completely.
14. Do ahead: Cake can be baked 5 days ahead.
15. Store tightly wrapped at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 Tbsp. sugar', ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1 tsp. Morton kosher salt', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', ' Tbsp. ( sticks) chilled unsalted butter, cut into (") pieces', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, divided', '1 large onion, thinly sliced', '8 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt, divided', ' scallions, thinly sliced on a deepdiagonal', '2 Tbsp. Dijon mustard', '2 oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about 2 cups)', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Whisk sugar, salt, and cups flour in a medium bowl to combine.
2. Add butter and toss to combine.
3.
