Using your hands and working quickly, rub and smash butter into flat, irregular pieces (be careful not to overwork; you donAt want to soften the butter too much).1 Drizzle A cup cold water over and mix with a rubber spatula, smashing in butter, until dough mostly comes togetherAit will be dry and shaggy.
4. Turn out dough onto a lightly floured surface.
5. Working quickly and using a rolling pin and your hands, press dough together to form a mass.
6. Roll dough into a rough square about " thick.
7. Fold in half into a rectangle, then once again to make a small square.
8. Press down on the dough with rolling pin to make it hold together.
9. Roll dough out once more to make a rough square about " thick.
10. Repeat folding process, taking your time to make it neat as this will be your final fold.
11. Using rolling pin, gently flatten dough, rotating as needed, to make a 6"-diameter disk.
12. Dust surface with more flour and roll disk into a "-diameter circle or oval about A" thick.
13. Fold dough in half, then in half again to make a rough triangle (this will make it easier to move).
14. Slide onto a parchment-lined baking sheet and unfold.
15. Chill while you make the filling.
16. Do Ahead: Dough can be made 3 days ahead.
17. Wrap 6" disk tightly and chill until ready to roll out.
18. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
19. Heat 3 Tbsp. butter in a medium skillet over medium until foaming, about 1 minute.
20. Reduce heat to medium-low; add onion, garlic, and 1 tsp.
21. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
22. Morton salt, and stir to combine.
23. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is tender and just beginning to brown, 6A8 minutes.
24. Uncover and continue to cook, stirring occasionally and adding a splash of water if onion begins to stick, until golden brown in spots, about 5 minutes more.
25. Transfer onion mixture to a plate; reserve skillet.
26. Melt remaining 1 Tbsp. butter in reserved skillet (no need to wipe out) over medium heat.
27. Remove from heat and add scallions and remaining tsp.
28. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
29. Morton kosher salt.
30. Toss scallions until coated in butter.
31. Remove dough from refrigerator and, using a small offset spatula or a spoon, spread mustard in a thin layer over dough, leaving a "" border around the edges.
32. Scatter onion mixture over mustard and sprinkle with Parmesan; season with pepper.
33. Cutting from edge of dough to the start of the mustard and filling, make slits in border of galette at 2"" intervals.
34. Fold flaps up and over filling, slightly overlapping.
35. Scatter scallions over.
36. Bake galette until crust is deep golden brown and scallions are lightly charred, minutes.
37. Using parchment paper, slide galette onto a cutting board and cut into wedges.
38. Do Ahead: Onion can be cooked 3 days ahead; transfer to an airtight container, then cover and chill.
39. Galette can be assembled 1 hour ahead; chill.

Ingredients: '1 (-oz.) envelope active dry yeast (about tsp.)', '2 tsp. honey', '5 cups ( g) all-purpose flour', '5 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1 Tbsp. Morton kosher salt', '6 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided, plus more for hands', 'Flaky sea salt', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, plus more for pan', '2-4 garlic cloves'
Instructions:
1. Whisk yeast, honey, and cups lukewarm water in a medium bowl and let sit 5 minutes.
2. Add flour and salt; mix with a rubber spatula until a shaggy dough forms and no dry streaks remain.
3. Pour 4 Tbsp. oil into the biggest bowl that will fit in your refrigerator.
4. Transfer dough to bowl and turn to coat in oil.
5. Cover with a silicone lid or plastic wrap and chill until dough is doubled in size (it should look very bubbly and alive), at least 8 hours and up to 1 day.
6. Generously butter a 13x9" baking pan (for thicker focaccia thatAs perfect for sandwiches) or an 18x13" rimmed baking sheet.
7. Pour 1 Tbsp. oil into center of pan.
8. Keeping the dough in the bowl, gather up edges of dough farthest from you and lift up and over into center of bowl.
9. Give the bowl a quarter turn and repeat process.
10. Do this 2 more times; you want to deflate dough while you form it into a rough ball. (Using a fork in each hand makes this process even easier and less messy!) Transfer dough to prepared pan.
11. Pour any oil left in bowl over the dough and coat it in oil.
12. Let rise, uncovered, in a dry, warm spot until doubled in size, at least hours and up to 4 hours.
13. To see if itAs ready, poke the dough with your finger.
14. The dough should spring back slowly, leaving a small visible indentation.
15. If it springs back quickly, the dough isnAt ready. (If at this point the dough is ready to bake but you arenAt, you can chill it up to 1 hour.) Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
16. Lightly oil your hands.
17. If using a rimmed baking sheet, gently stretch out dough to fill (you may not need to do this if using a baking pan).
18. Dimple focaccia all over with your fingers, as if youAre aggressively playing the piano, creating very deep depressions in the dough.
19. Drizzle with remaining 1 Tbsp. oil and sprinkle with sea salt.
20. Bake focaccia until puffed and golden brown all over, minutes.
21. Melt 4 Tbsp. butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
22. Remove from heat and grate in garlic (use 2 cloves if youAre garlic-shy or up to 4 if you love it).
23. Return to medium heat and cook, stirring often, until garlic is just lightly toasted, about seconds.
24. Brush garlic butter all over focaccia.
25. If you donAt want to serve the focaccia immediately, hold off on brushing so it remains crisp.

Ingredients: 'Nonstick cooking oil spray or vegetable oil (for pan)', '1 cup ( g) all-purpose flour', ' cup ( g) powdered sugar', 'A cup toasted sesame seeds', ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', ' cup (1 stick) chilled unsalted butter, cut into (") pieces', '2 large egg yolks', ' cups (packed; g) light brown sugar', ' cup (1 stick) chilled unsalted butter, cut into 8 equal pieces', ' cup heavy cream', '2 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1 tsp. Morton kosher salt', ' cup tahini', '1 tsp. vanilla extract', '6 oz. bittersweet chocolate (%% cacao), coarsely chopped', 'Toasted sesame seeds (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Lightly coat a 9x9" or 8x8" baking pan, preferably metal, with nonstick spray.
3. Line with parchment paper, leaving overhang on all sides.
4. Whisk flour, powdered sugar, sesame seeds, and salt in a medium bowl.
5. Add butter and toss to coat.
6. Using your hands, work in butter until pieces are about pea-size.
7. Using a rubber spatula, mix in yolks. (Dough should hold together when squeezed.) Scrape dough into prepared pan and gently press into an even layer (lightly flour your hands if needed).
8. Prick dough with a fork in several spots (this will help it stay flat as it bakes).
9. Bake shortbread until golden brown, minutes (or up to minutes if using a glass dish).
10. Let cool slightly.
11. While the shortbread is cooling, heat brown sugar and butter, whisking constantly, in a heavy medium saucepan over medium until butter is melted and sugar is dissolved, about 3 minutes.
12. Whisk in cream and salt.
13. Bring to a boil and cook, whisking constantly, until butterscotch is thick enough to coat a spoon (bubbling will become less frequent), about 5 minutes.
14. Remove from heat, add tahini and vanilla and whisk until incorporated and smooth.
15. Pour butterscotch over shortbread and tilt pan to distribute evenly.
16. Chill until set, minutes.
17. Melt chocolate in a microwave-safe bowl in the microwave in -second increments, stirring between bursts. (Alternatively, place chocolate in a heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of gently simmering water; bowl should not touch water.
18. Heat, stirring often, until melted and smooth.) Pour over butterscotch and spread evenly to edges of pan; sprinkle with sesame seeds.
19. Chill until chocolate is firm, 1A2 hours.
20. Using parchment paper overhang, pull bars out of pan and set on a cutting board; remove parchment.
21. Using a serrated knife, slice into pieces (5A6 cuts in each direction).
22. Bring to room temperature before serving.
23. Do Ahead: Bars can be made 4 days ahead.
24. Store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: ' cup gin', '2 Tbsp. tonic syrup, such as Jack Rudy', '1 cucumber slice (optional, for garnish)'
Instructions:
1. Combine gin, tonic syrup, and 1 cup ice in a blender and blend until smooth.
2. Pour into serving glass and garnish with cucumber if desired.

Ingredients: ' ounces Zucca Rabarbaro Amaro', ' ounce gin', ' ounces freshly squeezed grapefruit juice', ' ounce freshly squeezed lemon juice', ' ounce simple syrup', 'Soda water', 'Garnish: grapefruit twist'
Instructions:
1. Combine all the ingredients except the soda water and the garnish in a cocktail shaker filled with ice.
2. Shake until chilled and strain into a highball glass filled with ice.
3. Top off with soda water.
4. Garnish with the grapefruit twist.

Recipe name: Coconut Shrimp Tacos With Mango Salsa And Avocado Cilantro Sauce
Ingredients: 'Coconut or avocado oil cooking spray', '1 cup unsweetened shredded coconut', ' teaspoon paprika', 'Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper', '1 egg', '1 pound medium wild shrimp, peeled, deveined, and tails removed', '1 cup diced mango or pineapple', ' cup diced red bell pepper', ' cup diced red onion', 'Zest and juice of 1 medium lime', '2 tablespoons chopped fresh cilantro or mint', '1 cup loosely packed fresh cilantro', '1 medium avocado', 'Juice of 1 medium lime', '1 garlic clove', ' teaspoon ground cumin', 'Pinch of cayenne', 'Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper', 'Small (gluten-free) corn tortillas or lettuce cups, A Anely shredded purple cabbage, avocado slices'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. Place a wire rack on top of a large baking sheet, coat with cooking spray, and set aside.
3. In a medium bowl, mix together the coconut and paprika and season with salt and pepper.
4. In a small bowl, whisk the egg with 1 tablespoon water.
5. Make an assembly line with the shrimp, egg wash, coconut mixture, and prepared baking sheet.
6. Dip the shrimp in the egg wash, then dredge in the coconut, lightly shaking off any excess, then place the coated shrimp on the baking rack.
7. Repeat with the remaining shrimp, spacing them out evenly on the baking rack so they will get crispy instead of steaming.
8. Bake for to minutes, until the shrimp are cooked through and the coconut has started to brown.
9. While the shrimp are cooking, make the salsa and sauce.
10. In a medium bowl, toss together the salsa ingredients and set aside.
11. In a food processor or high-powered blender, add the sauce ingredients and A cup water.
12. Pulse until smooth, adjusting the seasonings to your taste.
13. If desired, spoon the sauce into a small zip-top bag and cut the tip off of one of the bottom corners (like you would to make a piping bag for icing), just enough to make a small hole.
14. This will make it easier to drizzle your sauce more evenly, but you could also just use a spoon.
15. To assemble the tacos, place 3 to 4 shrimp on a tortilla or lettuce cup, top with shredded cabbage, a spoonful of salsa, and an avocado slice.
16. Pipe or drizzle the sauce over the top.

Ingredients: '1 cup plain Greek yogurt', '2 teaspoons lemon zest', '2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice', '2 garlic cloves, minced', '1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil', ' teaspoon sea salt', '1 pound ground chicken or turkey breast', '1 medium zucchini, shredded, excess water squeezed out with a clean kitchen towel or cheesecloth', ' cup corn kernels', ' medium red bell pepper, cored, seeded, and A Anely chopped', '3 green onions (white and green parts), thinly sliced', '1 large egg, lightly beaten', ' cup whole wheat panko bread crumbs or almond A Aour', '2 tablespoons chopped fresh cilantro', '3 garlic cloves, A Anely chopped', '1 teaspoon ground cumin', '1 teaspoon kosher salt', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', '2 tablespoons avocado oil', '2 whole grain pitas, halved into pockets', ' cup hummus, homemade or store-bought', 'Romaine lettuce or spinach', 'Chopped cucumber', 'Sliced tomatoes'
Instructions:
1. Make the Greek yogurt sauce by mixing together all of the ingredients in a small bowl.
2. Place in the fridge until ready to serve.
3. Preheat the oven to .
4. In a large bowl, combine all of the burger ingredients except the oil.
5. Use your hands to mix well and form 4 patties.
6. Heat the oil in a large ovenproof skillet over medium heat, swirling to coat the bottom of the pan.
7. Add the patties and sear for about 2 minutes on each side until golden brown.
8. Carefully transfer the pan to the oven and bake for 7 to minutes, until cooked through.
9. While the burgers are baking, smear about a tablespoon of hummus inside each pita pocket.
10. Stuff with the veggies and top with the cooked burgers.
11. Either drizzle the Greek yogurt sauce inside or serve it as a dip.

Ingredients: '215g ( oz.) can chipotle chillies in adobo sauce, chillies finely chopped and sauce reserved', '1 tablespoon maple syrup', '2 cloves garlic, finely chopped', '2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil', '500g (1 lb. 2 oz.) chicken thigh fillets, trimmed and quartered', '500g (1 lb. 2 oz.) cauliflower florets (about 1 head)', 'Sea salt and cracked black pepper', ' small corn tortillas, lightly toasted', '3A cups (300g10 oz.) finely shredded purple cabbage', '1 cup (12gA12oz) cilantro sprigs', 'Pickled red onions, to serve', 'Lime wedges, to serve', ' cup (140g5 oz.) plain Greek-style yogurt', ' tablespoons lime juice', 'Sea salt and cracked black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Line 2 oven trays with non-stick baking paper.
3. Place the chopped chillies and reserved adobo sauce in a large bowl.
4. Add the maple syrup, garlic and oil and mix to combine.
5. Place the chicken in a separate large bowl and top with half the chipotle mixture.
6. Toss to coat.
7. Add the cauliflower to the remaining chipotle mixture and toss to coat.
8. Transfer the chicken and cauliflower to the trays and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
9. Roast for minutes or until the cauliflower is just charred on the edges, the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
10. To make the lime dressing, place the yogurt, lime juice, salt and pepper in a small bowl and mix to combine.
11. Fill the warm tortillas with the cabbage, chicken, cauliflower and coriander.
12. Drizzle with the lime dressing and serve with pickled onion and lime wedges.

Recipe name: Instant Pot Ginger-Lime Baby Back Ribs
Ingredients: ' cup chicken broth', '3 tablespoons fresh lime juice, divided', '2 to -pound rack baby back ribs', '2 tablespoons chili powder', '2 tablespoons minced fresh ginger', '3 cloves garlic, minced', ' teaspoons fine Himalayan pink salt', '1 teaspoon black pepper', 'Simple slaw, to serve (optional; recipe follows)', ' cup coconut aminos, tamari, or soy sauce', '4 tablespoons butter', '2 teaspoons toasted sesame oil', '1 teaspoon grated lime zest', ' cup sliced green onion', '2 teaspoons sesame seeds', ' pound green or red cabbage, shredded', 'Juice of lime', '1 to 2 tablespoons avocado oil mayonnaise', 'Pinch of fine Himalayan pink salt'
Instructions:
1. Place a trivet with handles in the Instant Pot.
2. Add the broth and 1 tablespoon of the lime juice.
3. Pat the ribs dry with paper towels.
4. Remove the thin membrane from the back of the ribs.
5. Cut the ribs into 4 sections.
6. In a small bowl, combine chili powder, ginger, garlic, pink salt, and pepper.
7. Rub the spice mixture evenly over the ribs.
8. Arrange the ribs on the trivet.
9. Secure the lid on the pot and close the pressure-release valve.
10. Set the pot to HIGH pressure for minutes.
11. At the end of the cooking time, use a natural release to depressurize.
12. Meanwhile, make the sauce: In a small saucepan, combine the coconut aminos, butter, sesame oil, lime zest, and remaining 2 tablespoons lime juice.
13. Cook the sauce over medium-low heat just until the butter melts, about 2 minutes.
14. Adjust an oven rack to 6 inches from the heat source.
15. Preheat the broiler.
16. Line a large baking sheet with foil.
17. Open the Instant Pot and transfer the ribs, meaty-side down, to the prepared baking sheet (discard the cooking liquid from the pot).
18. Brush the ribs with some of the sauce.
19. Broil until browned, 3 to 4 minutes.
20. Turn the ribs over.
21. Brush with more sauce.
22. Broil until browned and slightly charred, 3 to 4 minutes more.
23. In a small bowl, toss together all ingredients for the slaw.
24. To serve, brush the ribs with any remaining sauce and sprinkle with the green onion and sesame seeds.
25. Serve with slaw.

Recipe name: Cashew Horchata Horchata De Nuez De La India
Ingredients: '1 cup ( g) cashews', '1 cup ( g) brown rice', ' Mexican cinnamon stick', '4 tablespoons shaved piloncillo or brown sugar, plus more to taste', '4 cups ( ml32 fl oz) water'
Instructions:
1. In a deep container, combine the cashews, rice, cinnamon stick, 4 tablespoons sugar, and the water.
2. Cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours and up to overnight.
3. Transfer all of the ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth.
4. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve (discard the solids).
5. If desired, add more sugar to taste.
6. Serve over ice.

Recipe name: Orange Chocolate Loaf Cake From Florida
Ingredients: '1 cups sifted all-purpose flour', '2 teaspoons baking powder', ' teaspoon salt', ' cup unsweetened cocoa powder (preferably Dutch-process)', '1 cup sugar', '1 cup heavy cream', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract', '2 large eggs', 'Finely grated zest of 1 large, deep-colored orange', ' cup orange juice', '3 tablespoons sugar'
Instructions:
1. Adjust rack one-third up from the bottom of the oven and preheat oven to degrees.
2. You will need a loaf pan measuring about x x inches, or one with about a 6Acup capacity.
3. Butter the pan and dust it all over lightly with fine, dry bread crumbs, shake out excess crumbs, and set the pan aside.
4. Sift together the flour, baking powder, salt, cocoa, and sugar and set aside.
5. Beat the cream and vanilla in the small bowl of an electric mixer until the cream holds a definite shape.
6. On low speed, add the eggs, one at a time, scraping the bowl with a rubber spatula and beating only until the egg is incorporated after each addition. (The eggs will thin the cream slightly.) Transfer to the large bowl of the mixer and gradually, on low speed, add the sifted dry ingredients, scraping the bowl and beating only until smooth.
7. Remove from the mixer and stir in the grated zest.
8. ThatAs all there is to it.
9. Turn the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top.
10. Bake for about 1 hour and 5 minutes, until the top springs back when lightly pressed with a fingertip.
11. As soon as the cake goes into the oven, mix the orange juice with the sugar and let it stand while the cake is baking.
12. After you remove the cake from the oven, let it cool for 5 minutes.
13. Then, a little at a time, brush the orange juicesugar mixture all over the cake; encourage most of it to run down the sides between the cake and the pan, but thoroughly wet the top also.
14. The cake will absorb it all.
15. Let the cake stand in the pan until it is completely cool.
16. Then cover the pan loosely with a piece of wax paper.
17. Invert the cake into the palm of your handAeasy does itAremove the pan, cover the cake with a rack, and invert again, leaving the cake right side up.

Ingredients: '2 cups cake flour or 2 cups all purpose flour, sifted (measured by sifting into the cup and leveling it off)', '2 teaspoons baking powder', ' teaspoon salt', '1 cups heavy cream, cold', '3 large eggs, at room temperature', '1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract', '1 cup plus 2 tablespoons superfine sugar', 'Special Equipment: 1 (-cup) fluted metal tube pan, coated with baking spray with flour'
Instructions:
1. Twenty minutes or more before baking, set an oven rack in the lower third of the oven and preheat the oven to ( if using a dark pan).
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the cake flour, baking powder, and salt and then sift them together to make the mixture easier to incorporate.
3. In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the whisk beater, whip the cream, starting on low speed, gradually raising the speed to medium-high as it thickens, until stiff peaks form when the beater is raised.
4. In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs and vanilla just until lightly combined.
5. On medium-high speed, gradually beat the egg mixture into the whipped cream.
6. The mixture will thicken into mayonnaise consistency (unless high-butterfat cream is used).
7. Gradually beat in the sugar.
8. It should take about seconds to incorporate it.
9. Detach the bowl and whisk beater from the stand.
10. Add half the flour mixture to the cream mixture and, with the whisk attachment stir and fold in the flour until most of it disappears.
11. Add the rest of the flour mixture and continue folding and mixing until all traces of flour have disappeared.
12. Using a silicone spatula or spoon, scrape the batter into the prepared pan.
13. Run a small metal spatula or dull knife blade through the batter to prevent large air bubbles, avoiding the bottom of the pan.
14. Smooth the surface evenly with a small metal spatula.
15. Bake for to minutes, or until a wooden toothpick inserted between the tube and the side comes out completely clean and the cake springs back when pressed lightly in the center.
16. The cake should start to shrink from the sides of the pan only after removal from the oven.
17. Let the cake cool in the pan on a wire rack for minutes.
18. With a small metal spatula, loosen the top edges of the cake and invert the cake onto a wire rack that has been coated lightly with nonstick cooking spray.
19. Cool completely.
20. The cake requires no adornment, but I love to serve it with a light dusting of powdered sugar or a large dollop of lightly sweetened Whipped Cream.

Ingredients: '1 garlic clove, finely grated', '3 Tbsp. toasted sesame oil', '3 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 Tbsp. finely chopped mint or cilantro', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', '1 large or 2 small heads of broccoli (about 1 lb.), " trimmed from stem', '1 Tbsp. tamari or low-sodium soy sauce', '1 Tbsp. finely grated peeled ginger', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', ' tsp. ground cinnamon', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 shallots, thinly sliced crosswise, rings separated', 'Kosher salt', '1 (-oz.) can Spam classic, sliced into "-thick planks, planks halved lengthwise', ' cups cooked farro or brown rice'
Instructions:
1. Whisk garlic, oil, vinegar, and mint in a small bowl to combine; season with salt and pepper.
2. Set aside.
3. Hold broccoli so crown is resting against the cutting board and stem is pointing up and slice through stem all the way down through the crown to create " -thick planks.
4. Working one at a time, lay planks flat against the board and slice lengthwise through stems to create "-thick strips with florets attached on top.
5. Mix tamari, ginger, red pepper flakes, cinnamon, and 2 Tbsp. water in a small bowl to combine.
6. Set tamari sauce aside.
7. Heat 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
8. Cook shallots, stirring occasionally, until brown and crisp, about 5 minutes.
9. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to paper towels to drain and immediately season with salt.
10. Set skillet with oil back over medium-high and cook Spam in a single layer, turning halfway through, until golden brown and crisp, minutes.
11. Transfer to a plate.
12. Cook broccoli in same skillet, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned and crisp-tender, about 8 minutes.
13. Mix in farro and reserved tamari sauce and cook, stirring occasionally, until heated through, about 2 minutes.
14. Mix in Spam and transfer to a platter or plates.
15. Drizzle reserved vinaigrette over and top with crispy shallots.

Ingredients: '2 ounces gin', ' ounce lemon juice', '1 bar spoon raspberry preserves'
Instructions:
1. Combine all the ingredients in a cocktail shaker filled with ice and shake until chilled, about seconds.
2. Strain into a chilled coupe.

Ingredients: ' cup silken tofu', '1 teaspoon finely grated lemon zest', '2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice', '2 tablespoons unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 teaspoon Dijon mustard', '1 tablespoon coconut palm sugar', '2 tablespoons minced fresh ginger', ' teaspoon minced garlic', ' teaspoon kosher salt', '2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil', 'Freshly ground white pepper'
Instructions:
1. In a blender, combine the tofu, lemon zest, lemon juice, vinegar, mustard, sugar, ginger, garlic, and salt.
2. With the blender running, slowly pour in the oil through the hole in the lid and blend until emulsified.
3. Season with white pepper.

Recipe name: Syrniki Aa12Aa Aaoaa Farmeras Cheese Pancakes
Ingredients: '2 egg yolks', '2 cups (1 pound) tvorog or farmerAs cheese, homemade or store-bought', 'Scant 1A2 cup sugar', '3A4 cup flour', '1A2 teaspoon baking powder', 'Pinch of salt', 'A tablespoon or two of heavy cream (optional)', '2 tablespoons unsalted butter', '2 tablespoons vegetable oil', 'Sour cream, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, beat the egg yolks into the farmerAs cheese, then stir in the sugar.
2. Mix together 1A2 cup of the flour, the baking powder, and the salt and add to the cheese mixture.
3. If the mixture seems dry, add a little heavy cream.
4. Place the butter and oil over medium-low heat in a large nonstick skillet and melt the butter.
5. Sprinkle the remaining 1A4 cup flour onto a plate and dust your hands with some flour, too.
6. With your hands or a spoon, scoop out about 2 tablespoons of the batter and set the mound on the floured plate.
7. Use your hands to gently shape it into a round patty about 2 inches in diameter and coat it lightly on all sides with flour.
8. Repeat with the remaining batter.
9. Carefully transfer the patties to the pan and cook them slowly until the undersides are browned, about 3 minutes.
10. Turn with a spatula and cook until the other sides are browned, 3 to 4 minutes more.
11. Serve immediately, with sour cream.

Recipe name: Charred Leeks With Honey And Vinegar
Ingredients: '4 medium leeks (about lb.), white and pale green parts only, tough outer layers removed (root ends left intact)', '2 Tbsp. sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar', '2 tsp. honey', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper'
Instructions:
1. Prepare a grill for high heat.
2. Rinse off any sand and dirt from leeks and pat dry.
3. Arrange directly on grate (no need to oil) and grill, turning every few minutes with tongs, until outsides are completely blackened (leeks should start to soften and may begin to release some juices), minutes.
4. Transfer leeks to a cutting board and let rest minutes (the interiors will continue to steam and get even softer as they cool).
5. While the leeks are resting, whisk vinegar and honey in a small bowl until honey dissolves.
6. Set dressing aside.
7. Cut leeks on a diagonal into "" pieces.
8. Transfer to a medium bowl and toss with 2 Tbsp. oil; season with salt.
9. Transfer leeks to a platter and spoon reserved dressing over.
10. Drizzle with more oil and season with pepper.

Ingredients: 'Vegetable oil (for pan)', '3 medium carrots, peeled', '1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, cut into pieces', '1 cup ( g) raw pistachios', '3 large eggs, room temperature', '1 cup (packed; g) dark brown sugar', ' cup ( g) granulated sugar', ' tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. baking soda', ' tsp. ground cardamom', ' tsp. ground cinnamon', ' tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour', ' cup carrot juice', ' cup heavy cream', ' cup ( g) granulated sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Lightly coat bottom and sides of a 9"-diameter cake pan with oil.
3. Line bottom of pan with a round of parchment paper and smooth to eliminate any air bubbles.
4. Grate carrots on the large holes of a box grater.
5. Measure out cups (lightly packed; g); reserve remaining carrots for another use (like raita!).
6. Set a fine-mesh sieve over a measuring glass and place next to stove.
7. Heat butter in a medium saucepan over medium until foaming, about 2 minutes.
8. Add pistachios and cook, stirring often, until butter browns and nuts smell toasty, about 5 minutes. (Butter will get very foamy and it will be hard to see whatAs going on, so once the foam is golden brown, remove pan from heat every seconds to check how the nuts are browning.) Pour mixture through prepared sieve, scraping in all of the browned bits; set nuts and butter aside.
9. Crack eggs into a large bowl; add brown sugar, granulated sugar, baking powder, baking soda, cardamom, cinnamon, and salt.
10. Using an electric mixer on high speed, beat 3 minutes (mixture should be thick, pale, and increased in volume).
11. Decrease speed to medium-high and gradually stream in reserved brown butter (including browned bits); beat until combined.
12. Coarsely chop reserved pistachios.
13. Using a rubber spatula, fold in flour until almost completely combined, then add nuts and carrots and continue to fold until everything is incorporated.
14. Scrape batter into prepared pan; smooth surface.
15. Place pan on a clean smooth surface and give it a fast -degree spin (this pushes the batter up the sides of the pan and minimizes doming during baking).
16. Bake cake until deeply browned and a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, minutes.
17. Let cake cool in pan.
18. Run a small offset spatula or butter knife around perimeter of cake to loosen.
19. Place a plate upside down over cake and invert cake onto plate (leave flat side up).
20. Remove parchment paper.
21. Do Ahead: Cake can be baked 1 day ahead.
22. Store tightly wrapped at room temperature.
23. Place a small plate in the freezer.
24. Cook carrot juice, cream, granulated sugar, and salt in a small saucepan over high heat (do not stir) until thick like lava, minutes.
25. Mixture will initially bubble and foam vigorously and look like itAs going to boil over, but it wonAt!
26. DonAt be tempted to reduce the heat; the bubbling will subside and mixture will thicken.
27. You want it to be thick enough to stick to the cake, but do not let it brown.
28. Test glaze by putting a small dollop onto the cold plate: When you run your finger through the glaze, it should leave a defined trail.
29. Once the glaze is thick enough, remove pan from heat and stir in butter.
30. Let cool until you can comfortably hold your finger in it (if itAs too hot, it will run right off the cake), about minutes.
31. Stir glaze well and pour over center of cake, nudging to the edges with offset spatula or butter knife.
32. Pop any air bubbles with a toothpick.
33. Let glaze cool at least minutes before slicing cake.

Ingredients: '1 cup ( g) cacao beans', '5 cups (1.2 liters or pints) fresh coconut water or good-quality bottled coconut water'
Instructions:
1. In a dry small saucepan or frying pan, toast the cacao beans until browned on both sides and fragrant, 5A8 minutes.
2. In a large pitcher, combine the cacao beans and coconut water.
3. Cover and refrigerate for 2 days.
4. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve (discard the cacao beans).
5. Serve over ice.

Ingredients: '1 oz. reposado tequila (such as Partida)', ' oz. Martini Rosso vermouth', ' oz. Dolin dry vermouth', ' oz. Campari', '1 dash Angostura bitters', 'Garnish: 1 orange twist'
Instructions:
1. Stir with ice, then strain into a chilled coupe.
2. Garnish with the orange twist.

Ingredients: '4 jalapeAos', ' ml blanco tequila (I prefer tequila from the Tequila valley, such as Siembra Valles, Fortaleza, Arette, etc.)', '2 cups cubed seedless watermelon', ' cup superfine sugar', '1 oz jalapeAo-infused Siembra Valles Blanco Tequila (see below)', ' oz Novo Fogo Silver CachaAa', ' oz Aperol', '1 oz Watermelon Syrup (see below)', ' oz lime juice', '5 to 7 mint leaves', 'Lime wheel for garnish', 'Salt for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Remove the stems from the jalapeAos.
2. Cut the jalapeAos in half and scrape the core (seeds and membranes) of two into a food-safe container.
3. Dice the remaining jalapeAos and place into the food-safe container.
4. Add the tequila and allow to infuse for to minutes, depending on spice level desired.
5. Rebottle.
6. Note: The recipe for the jalapeAo tequila will vary because not all jalapeAos are equally spicy.
7. You need to taste it frequently to make sure itAs right.
8. Ensure your tequila is not cold, as cold tequila will not infuse properly.
9. Blend the watermelon in a blender to yield about ounces of juice.
10. Add the sugar and blend again until fully dissolved.
11. Transfer to an airtight container.
12. Store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
13. Add all the ingredients, except for 1 mint leaf, the lime wheel, and salt, to a cocktail shaker with ice.
14. Wet the outside of a coupe glass with the lime wheel and roll the glass in salt for a salted rim.
15. Shake and fine-strain into the glass.
16. Garnish with the mint leafAfirst pressed lightly to express its oilsAby floating it on top of the drink.

Ingredients: '2 ounces blanco tequila', '1 ounces pineapple juice (preferably freshly juiced and strained)', ' ounce freshly squeezed lemon juice', ' ounce simple syrup', '2 dashes of orange bitters', '1 dash of Angostura bitters (optional)', 'Pineapple slice, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Combine the first six ingredients in a chilled cocktail shaker.
2. Shake and strain over fresh ice into a glass.
3. Garnish with the pineapple slice.

Ingredients: '6 Tbsp. ( g) sourdough starter', '2 cups ( g) all-purpose flour, divided', '2 large eggs', ' cup plus 1 Tbsp. (or more) milk', '3 Tbsp. granulated sugar', ' tsp. baking soda', ' tsp. kosher salt', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted, plus more room temperature', 'Grapeseed or vegetable oil (for pan)', 'Jam, crA me fraAche, demerara sugar, andor lox, (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Mix starter, 1 cup plus 6 Tbsp. ( g total) flour, and cup plus 1 Tbsp. ( g) room-temperature water (about ) in a medium bowl until no lumps remain.
2. Cover and let sit at room temperature overnight.
3. Fold in remaining cup plus 2 Tbsp. ( g total) flour.
4. Then add eggs, milk, granulated sugar, baking soda, salt, and 3 Tbsp. melted butter and whisk, adding more milk if needed, until mixture is thick but pourable (you want it to be about the consistency of heavy cream).
5. It will take awhile to work out lumpsAa few small lumps are fine, but try to get batter as smooth as possible.
6. Pour in enough oil to lightly coat a crepe pan or " nonstick skillet and heat over medium-high (this is just to help get things going; you shouldnAt need to add more oil to the pan).
7. Pour cup batter into center of pan and tilt and rotate pan as needed to coat bottom evenly with batter.
8. Cook crepe, undisturbed, until surface looks dry and bottom is golden brown, about seconds.
9. Using tongs or an offset spatula, gently flip crepe and cook just until other side is golden, about seconds.
10. Transfer to a plate and slather a bit of room-temperature butter on top.
11. Repeat process with remaining batter, stacking and buttering crepes as you go.
12. Serve crepes with jam, crA me fraAche, demerara sugar, lox, andor more butter as desired.
13. Do ahead: Crepes can be made 4 days ahead.
14. Wrap tightly and chill, or freeze up to 2 weeks.
15. If frozen, thaw in fridge before reheating.
16. Reheatone at a time in crepe pan or skillet over medium until warmed through.

Ingredients: ' ounces tequila blanco', '1 ounce Aperol', '1 ounce lime juice', ' ounce orange juice', ' ounce agave nectar', 'Salt for glass rim', 'Orange twist to garnish'
Instructions:
1. Add the tequila, Aperol, juices, and syrup to a cocktail shaker with ice.
2. Shake for approximately seconds to chill and combine the ingredients.
3. Rim your glass with salt.
4. Double strain into a wine glass over fresh ice.
5. Garnish with an orange twist.

Ingredients: 'Kosher salt, optional', '4 thin slices cucumber, plus a -inch-thick slice for garnish', '2 sprigs fresh cilantro', ' cup ice cubes', '2 ounces ( cup) silver tequila', '1 ounce (2 tablespoons) fresh lime juice', ' ounce (1 tablespoon) agave syrup', ' ounce (1 tablespoon) Bols Triple Sec'
Instructions:
1. To salt the rim of your glass, fill a shallow bowl with water.
2. On a small plate, pour a circle of salt.
3. Dip the rim of a rocks glass or margarita glass in the water, then dip it into the salt and set it aside.
4. In the bottom of a cocktail shaker, muddle the 4 cucumber slices with 1 cilantro sprig until the cucumber releases most of its juices and the cilantro is smashed and fragrant.
5. Add the ice cubes, tequila, lime juice, agave syrup, and triple sec.
6. Cover and shake vigorously for 4 seconds.
7. Pour the drink, including the ice, into the glass.
8. Serve the margarita garnished with the remaining cucumber slice and cilantro sprig.

Ingredients: '2 or 3 slices fresh jalapeAo', ' oz. grade B maple syrup', ' oz. fresh lemon juice', '2 oz. reposado tequila', 'Kosher salt for rim'
Instructions:
1. Rim half of a rocks glass with kosher salt.
2. Muddle jalapeAo in base of cocktail shaker.
3. Add maple syrup, lemon, and tequila.
4. Shake vigorously with ice.
5. Fill rimmed glass with fresh ice.
6. Double strain cocktail (to remove any pepper bits) into prepared glass.
7. Garnish with lemon slice.

Ingredients: '2 teaspoons black peppercorns', '7 teaspoons cumin seeds', '2 teaspoons cardamom seeds (from green cardamom pods)', '2 teaspoons coriander seeds', '2 tablespoons ground turmeric', 'Special Equipment: an electric coffeespice grinder'
Instructions: 1. Finely grind peppercorns and seeds in grinder, then transfer to a bowl and stir in turmeric.

Ingredients: ' Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature', ' cup ( g) granulated sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt', '2 large egg yolks', '2 tsp. vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste', '2 cups ( g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', 'Special Equipment: Assorted (2") cookie cutters', '2 cups ( g) powdered sugar, divided', '6 Tbsp. (or more) whole milk, divided', '2 tsp. (or more) any color plant-based food-coloring powder', '2 tsp. (or more) second color plant-based food-coloring powder', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided'
Instructions:
1. Using an electric mixer on high speed, beat butter, sugar, and salt in a large bowl, scraping down sides and bottom of bowl as needed, until light and creamy, about 3 minutes.
2. Add egg yolks and vanilla and beat until incorporated, about 1 minute.
3. Reduce speed to low, add 2 cups flour, and mix until incorporated.
4. Dough will be semifirm but can be rolled out without chilling.
5. If your kitchen is very warm andor dough is sticky, pat it into a 1"-thick disk, wrap in plastic, and chill minutes to let firm up before rolling out.
6. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
7. Roll out dough on a lightly floured sheet of parchment paper, dusting with more flour as needed to keep dough from sticking, to about " thick.
8. Punch out shapes as desired with lightly floured cookie cutters and transfer to 2 parchment-lined baking sheets, spacing " apart.
9. Pat scraps into a 1"-thick disk, wrap with plastic, and chill minutes if soft.
10. Bake 1 sheet of cookies, rotating halfway through, until edges are golden brown, minutes.
11. Let cookies cool minutes on baking sheet, then transfer to a wire rack and let cool completely.
12. Bake remaining sheet of cookies, then repeat process with remaining dough.
13. Do Ahead: Dough can be made 1 month ahead; wrap tightly and freeze.
14. Cookies can be baked 2 days ahead; store airtight at room temperature.
15. Whisk 1 cup powdered sugar, 3 Tbsp. milk, 2 tsp. food-coloring powder, and half of salt in a small bowl until no lumps remain.
16. Glaze should be the consistency of heavy cream; add more milk if needed.
17. For a deeper color, whisk in more powder 1 tsp. at a time until desired color is achieved; adjust with milk if needed.
18. Repeat with second food-coloring powder, remaining salt, remaining 1 cup powdered sugar, and remaining 3 Tbsp. milk, adjusting intensity of color as desired.
19. Spoon 1 tsp. first glaze in the center of a small plate.
20. Drizzle 1 tsp. second glaze over the first and use a toothpick to slightly swirl colors together; donAt go overboard. (If you want to decorate larger cookies, add 1 tsp. glaze to plate for each additional inch.) Gently press the top of a cookie into glaze, then lift up and allow excess glaze to drip back onto the plate.
21. Using a toothpick, pop any air bubbles and swirl colors more if desired.
22. Transfer to a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet.
23. Repeat with 2 more cookies, adding tsp. of each color of glaze to plate before each cookie is dipped.
24. The glaze will lose its swirly effect at this point.
25. Clean plate and repeat process, decorating cookies in batches of 3 until all cookies have been glazed.
26. Let sit until glaze is set, at least 2 hours.
27. Do Ahead: Cookies can be glazed 1 day ahead.
28. Store tightly wrapped on a rimmed baking sheet at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 cup ( g) nuts (such as blanched hazelnuts, blanched almonds, or walnuts)', ' cups ( g; or more) raw sesame seeds, divided', 'A cup ( g) ancho powder (pure ground ancho chiles, not ancho chile powder, which has added salt, cumin, and other spices)', ' cup agave syrup, preferably dark', '2 cups ( g) all-purpose flour', ' tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. ground cinnamon', ' Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature', ' cup ( g) sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt', '3 large egg yolks', '1 Tbsp. vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste', ' cup ( g) dried fruit (such as papaya, mango, or pineapple), cut into (") pieces', ' cup ( g) bittersweet chocolate wafers (disks, pistoles, fA ves; preferably % cacao)', '1 large egg white, lightly beaten'
Instructions:
1. Place racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat to .
2. Toast nuts and cup sesame seeds on a rimmed baking sheet on upper rack, tossing halfway through, until golden brown, minutes.
3. Let cool.
4. Stir together ancho powder and agave syrup in a small bowl.
5. Whisk flour, baking powder, and cinnamon in a medium bowl to combine.
6. Using an electric mixer on medium-high speed, beat butter, sugar, and salt in a large bowl, scraping down sides and bottom of bowl as needed, until light and creamy, about 3 minutes.
7. Add egg yolks and vanilla and beat until incorporated, about 1 minute.
8. Scrape in ancho mixture and beat until smooth and creamy, about 1 minute.
9. Reduce speed to low, add dry ingredients, and beat to combine.
10. Add dried fruit, chocolate, and toasted nuts and sesame seeds; mix with a wooden spoon until evenly distributed.
11. Divide dough in half.
12. Working one at a time, roll each piece into a "-long log.
13. Wrap each log tightly in plastic and roll across work surface to make as smooth and cylindrical as possible.
14. Place on a rimmed baking sheet and freeze until surface is firm, about minutes.
15. Slap a log down onto counter to create a flat side; roll over and slap again to flatten another side (youAre aiming for a triangular shape).
16. Repeat to create third side of the triangle.
17. Tap against counter to smooth if needed, then tap ends to flatten.
18. Repeat with second log.
19. Return logs to baking sheet; freeze until firm, about minutes.
20. Working one at a time, unwrap logs and brush sides with egg white.
21. Working on baking sheet, sprinkle sides with remaining 1 cup sesame seeds and gently press to coat.
22. Surface should be covered; use more sesame if needed.
23. Freeze logs on baking sheet, uncovered, until surface is very firm but not frozen solid, about 1 hour.
24. Preheat oven to .
25. Slice a log crosswise into "-thick triangles and divide cookies between 2 parchment-lined baking sheets, spacing 1" apart. (If dough is soft or starts to lose its shape, freeze minutes before continuing.) Bake cookies, rotating baking sheets top to bottom and back to front halfway through, until edges are just set, about minutes.
26. Let cool on baking sheets.
27. Transfer cookies to a platter or airtight container and line baking sheets with fresh sheets of parchment paper.
28. Repeat process with remaining dough.
29. Do Ahead: Dough can be rolled into logs 3 days ahead; refrigerate.
30. Transfer to freezer minutes before slicing.
31. Cookies can be baked 1 day ahead; store airtight at room temperature.

Recipe name: Mashed Plantains With Fried Eggs Mangaao De Plaatanos
Ingredients: '2 large plantains, peeled and cut in half lengthwise', ' red onion, thinly sliced', ' cup ( mL) white vinegar 2 tablespoons ( mL)', 'Extra-virgin olive oil or unsalted butter, divided', ' teaspoon (1 mL) sea salt', ' teaspoon (1 mL) freshly cracked pepper', '2 tablespoons ( mL) vegetable oil', '2 eggs', '1 avocado, pitted, peeled, and sliced'
Instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
2. Add the plantains and boil until fork-tender, about minutes.
3. Drain the plantains, reserving cup ( mL) of the cooking water.
4. Meanwhile, place the red onion in a small bowl and cover with the vinegar.
5. Transfer the plantains to a medium bowl and add the reserved cooking water, 1 tablespoon ( mL) of the olive oil, salt, and pepper.
6. Mash the plantains to the consistency of mashed potatoes.
7. In a medium frying pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
8. Drain the red onions and discard the vinegar.
9. Add the onions to the pan and cook, stirring often, until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
10. Transfer to a plate.
11. In the same pan (no need to wipe clean), heat the remaining 1 tablespoon ( mL) olive oil over medium heat.
12. Carefully crack the eggs into the pan, leaving space between each egg.
13. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes, or until the whites are cooked and no longer translucent and the yolks are still runny. (I prefer the eggs over easy for this dish.) To serve, divide the mashed plantain between plates and top with a fried egg, cooked onions, and a few slices of avocado.
14. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

Ingredients: 'Extra-virgin olive oil', '4 shallots, thinly sliced into rings', ' cup unbleached all-purpose flour', '2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil', '2 bone-in, skin-on chicken breasts', 'Kosher salt and freshly ground pepper', 'One 2-inch piece of ginger, peeled and finely chopped, (1 tablespoon)', ' tsp. ground cumin', '1 tsp. ground tumeric', '4 cups Swanson Chicken Broth', '8 ounces vermicelli rice noodles or thin spaghetti', '1 cup leafy herbs, such as cilantro, dill and basil'
Instructions:
1. Make the fried shallots: In a small skillet, heat -inch of oil until shimmering.
2. Working in small batches, add a handful of shallots, and cook, turning frequently, until golden, about 1 minute.
3. Using a slotted spoon, transfer fried shallots to a paper towel lined plate.
4. Make the soup: In a medium pot, heat the oil over medium-high.
5. Add the chicken and brown on both sides, about 4 minutes per side.
6. Remove the chicken to a plate.
7. Reduce the heat to medium and add the ginger, turmeric, and cumin.
8. Cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
9. Pour the broth into the pot and return the chicken.
10. Bring to a boil and then reduce to medium-low.
11. Cover and gently simmer until the chicken breasts are just cooked through, about to minutes.
12. Turn off heat.
13. Using tongs or a slotted spoon remove the chicken breast to a plate and let cool slightly.
14. Discard skin and bones and shred chicken meat.
15. Keep warm.
16. Meanwhile, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
17. Add the rice noodles or thin spaghetti and cook until al dente per package instructions.
18. Drain.
19. Add noodles to broth and serve brothy noodles topped with pulled chicken, herbs, fried shallots, and black pepper.

Ingredients: '1A cups ( g) chopped pecans', '4 Tbsp. pure maple syrup', '2 tsp. kosher salt, divided', ' Tbsp. chilled unsalted butter, cut into pieces, plus more for pan', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour', '6 Tbsp. (packed; g) light brown sugar', '1 large egg', '1 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste', '1 cup ( g) powdered sugar', '3 Tbsp. (or more) whole milk', 'Coarse white sanding sugar and edible glitter (for decorating)'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Toss pecans, maple syrup, and tsp. salt in a medium bowl.
3. Scrape onto a rimmed baking sheet lined with a silicone baking mat or parchment paper and spread nuts into a single layer.
4. Bake until pecans are slightly darkened and syrup is dark and bubbling, minutes.
5. Remove from oven and carefully toss pecans in hot syrup to coat again.
6. Let cool on baking sheet until caramel hardens, about minutes.
7. Break up candied pecans into pieces; discard any burned bits.
8. Reduce oven temperature to .
9. Generously butter a " cast-iron or stainless-steel skillet; line bottom with a round of parchment paper and butter parchment.
10. Pulse flour, brown sugar, half of candied pecans, and tsp. salt in a food processor until nuts are finely chopped.
11. Add egg, 1 Tbsp. vanilla, and remaining Tbsp. butter and process until a shaggy dough forms.
12. Transfer dough to a large bowl and knead a couple of times just to bring it together.
13. Using damp hands, pat dough evenly into skillet, extending all the way to the sides of the pan.
14. Bake cookie until edges are golden brown and center is slightly puffed, minutes.
15. Let cool in skillet 1 hour.
16. Invert cookie onto a cutting board and cut into wedges.
17. Turn right side up and transfer to a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet.
18. Whisk powdered sugar, milk, remaining tsp. salt, and remaining 1 tsp. vanilla in a small wide bowl until smooth.
19. The glaze should be the consistency of heavy cream; thin with more milk if needed.
20. Transfer half of the glaze to another small wide bowl (youAll need to use 2 bowls as the glaze will start to collect crumbs and youAll want a fresh start).
21. Working one at a time, lightly dip half of the wedges, top side down, into first bowl of glaze so that only the surface is covered and no glaze drips down the sides.
22. Use a toothpick to pop any bubbles and smooth glaze; coating should be thin enough to see the cookie peeking through.
23. Repeat with remaining wedges and second bowl of glaze.
24. Top wedges with remaining candied pecans and let sit minutes.
25. Sprinkle with sanding sugar and glitter and let sit until glaze is set, about 1 hour.
26. Do Ahead: Cookies can be baked 1 day ahead.
27. Store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 cup ( g oz) flat-leaf parsley leaves', '1 cup ( g oz) mint leaves', ' cup ( ml 2 fl oz) lemon juice', '2 green onions (scallions), trimmed and chopped', ' cup ( g 7 oz) plain Greek-style (thick) yogurt', ' small clove garlic', 'Sea salt and cracked black pepper', '600g (1 lb. 5 oz) broccoli florets (about 2 heads)', '1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil', 'Sea salt and cracked black pepper', '150g ( oz) sugar snap peas, trimmed', '1 cup (140g or 5 oz) frozen shelled edamame beans', '2 tablespoons hemp seeds or sesame seeds, toasted', '2 small avocados, quartered lengthwise', ' cup (70g or oz) store-bought pickled ginger'
Instructions:
1. Place the parsley, mint, lemon juice, onion, yogurt, and garlic in a food processor or blender and process until smooth.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Set aside.
4. In 2 batches, place the broccoli in a food processor and process until it resembles rice.
5. Heat a large deep-sided frying pan over medium-high heat.
6. Add the oil, broccoli, salt and pepper and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes or until the broccoli is just tender.
7. Divide between serving bowls and allow to cool.
8. Place the peas and edamame in a large heatproof bowl.
9. Cover with boiling water and allow to stand for 5 minutes.
10. Drain and rinse under cold running water.
11. Slice the peas lengthways.
12. Place the hemp seeds on a small tray and press one edge of each avocado quarter into the seeds to coat.
13. Top the broccoli rice with the peas, edamame, avocado and ginger.
14. Drizzle with the dressing to serve.

Ingredients: '6 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '3 Tbsp. all-purpose flour', '3 cups whole milk', '1 oz. Parmesan, grated (about cup)', 'Pinch of cayenne pepper', 'Pinch of ground nutmeg', 'Kosher salt', '2 bunches green Swiss chard', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 lb. sweet Italian sausage, casings removed', '1 lb. whole-milk fresh ricotta', 'Kosher salt', '1 lb. fresh pasta sheets (about (6x4") pieces) or oz. dried jumbo shells (about )', '2 cups store-bought marinara sauce', 'Grated Parmesan (for sprinkling)'
Instructions:
1. Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
2. Whisk in flour and cook, whisking occasionally, until mixture smells slightly nutty, about 3 minutes.
3. Whisk in milk, then increase heat to medium-high, and still whisking constantly, bring to a simmer.
4. Reduce heat to maintain a simmer and cook just until thick and no longer floury tasting, minutes.
5. Add Parmesan and whisk until melted, then whisk in cayenne and nutmeg; season with salt.
6. Cover bAchamel and keep warm until ready to use.
7. Do Ahead: BAchamel can be made 3 days ahead.
8. Let cool; cover and chill.
9. Reheat over medium-low, whisking constantly and adding a splash of water if needed to loosen.
10. Remove ribs and stems from Swiss chard leaves and thinly slice.
11. Tear leaves into large pieces.
12. Combine oil and sausage with Swiss chard ribs and stems in a large skillet and set over medium-high heat.
13. Cook, breaking up sausage with a heatproof rubber spatula or wooden spoon into small pieces, until sausage is almost cooked through, 5A7 minutes.
14. Add Swiss chard leaves to skillet a handful at a time, tossing and letting them wilt slightly between additions, until all the leaves have been added.
15. Cook, reducing heat as needed (liquid should be bubbling slightly at this point), until liquid is almost completely evaporated (itAs important to dry out the Swiss chard here so the filling isnAt soggy), about 5 minutes.
16. Transfer to a large bowl.
17. Let cool 5 minutes, then stir in ricotta.
18. Season filling with salt.
19. If using pasta sheets, cook in a pot of boiling salted water 1 minute.
20. Transfer to a large bowl of cold water.
21. Lay out a row of pasta sheets on a rimmed baking sheet (donAt overlap), then top with a sheet of waxed or parchment paper.
22. Repeat with remaining pasta sheets, placing sheets of paper between each layer.
23. If using shells, cook in a pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, just until very al dente, about 3 minutes less than package instructions; rinse under cold water to cool quickly.
24. Preheat oven to .
25. Spread half of bAchamel into a 13x9" baking dish.
26. If using pasta sheets, lay out a sheet on a clean surface with a short end facing you.
27. Spoon a heaping -cupful of filling so it runs all the way down the center.
28. Roll pasta around filling to create a 1 "-diameter tube (the pasta will overlap quite a bit).
29. Repeat with remaining pasta sheets, arranging in baking dish as you go to create 2 rows.
30. If using shells, spoon a heaping tablespoonful of filling into each shell and nestle next to each other to create rows (youAll have about 5 rows of 6 shells each).
31. Spread remaining bAchamel over cannelloni, then marinara sauce.
32. Top with Parmesan.
33. Bake cannelloni until edges of pasta are lightly browned and sauce is bubbling around edges of pan, minutes.
34. Do Ahead: Filling can be made 2 days ahead.
35. Let cool completely, then cover and chill.

Ingredients: 'Vegetable oil for deep-frying', '1 pound okra, pods halved lengthwise', '1 medium egg, beaten', ' cup rice flour', ' cup cornstarch', '1 teaspoon smoked paprika', 'Kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. Pour a few inches of vegetable oil into a heavy-bottomed pot, preferably cast iron.
2. Heat the oil to .
3. Meanwhile, put the okra in a large bowl and toss with the beaten egg.
4. Let the okra stand for 5 minutes to absorb the egg.
5. In a separate medium bowl, whisk the rice flour, cornstarch, paprika, and teaspoon salt.
6. Remove the okra from the large bowl, allowing excess egg to drip back into the bowl, then transfer the okra to the flour mixture.
7. Toss the okra to dredge evenly.
8. Lift from the flour and shake off the excess.
9. Cook the okra in batches in the hot oil until golden and crunchy, 3 to 5 minutes, depending on size.
10. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towelAlined plate.
11. Season with salt immediately.
12. Repeat with the remaining okra.

Ingredients: '1 pound small red potatoes, scrubbed', 'Salt and pepper', ' cup finely chopped onion', ' cup sweet pickle relish', ' cup mayonnaise', '4 hard-boiled eggs, finely chopped'
Instructions:
1. Put the potatoes in a large pot and add enough cold water to cover them by 1 inch.
2. Add a pinch of salt.
3. Bring to a boil and cook until the potatoes are tender but not mushy, about minutes.
4. Drain.
5. Set aside until cool enough to handle.
6. While the potatoes are still warm, cut them into quarters and place them in a large bowl.
7. Add the onion, relish, mayonnaise, and eggs.
8. Toss until well combined.
9. Taste and season with salt and pepper.
10. Cover and chill until ready to serve, at least 1 hour or up to overnight.

Recipe name: Cheesy Chicken Melt With All Of The Onions Relish
Ingredients: '4 teaspoons vegetable oil', '2 boneless, skinless chicken breasts, about 1 pound (450g), sliced lengthwise into 4 flat 1A2-inch-thick (1.3cm-thick) pieces', '1 teaspoon kosher salt', '1A2 yellow onion, sliced', '1 teaspoon distilled white vinegar', '1A2 red onion, thinly sliced', '1A2 bunch scallions, white and green parts, thinly sliced', '2 tablespoons fried shallots (store-bought is totally great)', '3 tablespoons salted butter', '4 slices rye bread', '4 slices good melting cheese, such as Colby Jack or Muenster'
Instructions:
1. Heat a large sautA pan with 2 teaspoons of the vegetable oil over high heat until it is smoking.
2. Season the chicken with 3A4 teaspoon of the salt and gently place it in the pan.
3. Cook on one side until almost cooked all the way through, about 5 minutes.
4. Flip the chicken and cook on the other side for about seconds, or until it is no longer pink.
5. Transfer the chicken to a plate to rest.
6. Immediately add the yellow onion and the remaining 2 teaspoons vegetable oil to the pan.
7. Stir and cook for 5 minutes, or until the onion is dark and smells amazing but is not burnt.
8. If itAs getting too dark, lower the heat.
9. Add the vinegar, along with the liquid that has collected on the plate where the chicken is resting, and turn the heat to low.
10. Simmer the onion for 2 to 4 minutes, or until the liquid has evaporated.
11. Transfer the onion to a bowl, using a rubber spatula to scrape the pan out completely.
12. When the onion has cooled down a little, about 5 minutes, add the red onion, scallions, fried shallots, and remaining 1A4 teaspoon salt and stir to combine.
13. Melt 2 tablespoons of the butter in the same pan.
14. Lay out two slices of bread on your work surface and place a slice of cheese on each.
15. Top each piece of cheese with half of the onions, two pieces of chicken, and another slice of cheese, and cover with another slice of bread.
16. Place the sandwiches in the pan with the melted butter.
17. Cook over medium heat, about 3 minutes, until the cheese on the bottom slice is melted and the bread is nicely toasted.
18. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon butter to the pan and flip the sandwiches.
19. Cook them for another 1 to 2 minutes, until the cheese is melted and the bottom is toasty.
20. Remove the sandwiches from the pan, cut them in half, and insert a frilly toothpick or cocktail sword near the top in each half and serve.

Recipe name: Chicken Spiedies Marinated Chicken On A Bun
Ingredients: '1A3 cup (80ml) vegetable oil', '1A4 cup (60ml) freshly squeezed lemon juice', '1A4 cup (60ml) distilled white vinegar', '2 garlic cloves', '2 sprigs fresh mint', '2 sprigs fresh dill', '1A2 tablespoon kosher salt', '1 teaspoon low-sodium soy sauce', '1 teaspoon garlic powder', '1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', '1A2 teaspoon dried oregano', '1A4 teaspoon red pepper flakes', '1A4 teaspoon ground cardamom', '1 1A2 pounds (680g) boneless, skinless chicken breasts or thighs, cut into 1-inch (2.5cm) chunks', '4 super-soft hero rolls, split lengthwise', 'Shredded iceberg lettuce, chopped tomatoes, chopped white onion, sliced pepperoncini, and chopped olives'
Instructions:
1. In a blender, combine the oil, lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, mint, dill, salt, soy sauce, garlic powder, black pepper, oregano, red pepper flakes, and cardamom and puree until smooth and emulsified.
2. Put the chicken in a large mixing bowl and pour the marinade over it, reserving about 1A2 cup marinade in a small bowl, and covering and refrigerating it.
3. Mix the chicken well with the marinade, cover, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or up to .
4. The acidic marinade will start to break down the chicken, so if a more tender texture bothers you, marinate for 4 to 5 hours.
5. I, however, am a fan of that texture and think an aggressively long, -hour marinade gives the chicken that much more flavor.
6. Heat a grill until it is so hot you (almost) canAt look directly at it.
7. Skewer the chicken; you can make a giant skewer to save space or lots of little ones for easier turning.
8. Cook the skewered chicken, turning three times, until nicely charred, 5 to 7 minutes on each of the four sides.
9. Let the chicken rest for minutes, then take it off the skewers.
10. To serve on the rolls, top with whichever of the following makes you the happiest: lettuce, tomatoes, onion, reserved marinade, pepperoncini, olives.
11. Or donAt use any toppings, as itAs meant to be eaten, in my book (which this literally is).

Ingredients: ' cup (50g or oz) chia seeds', ' cup (20g or oz) psyllium husks', '2 cups (320g or oz) cooked quinoa', ' cups (375ml or fl oz) water', '1 cup (160g or oz) sunflower seeds', ' cup (80g or oz) flaxseeds', ' cup (120g or oz) roughly chopped almonds', ' cup (60ml or 2 fl oz) extra virgin olive oil', '2 tablespoons maple syrup', ' teaspoon sea salt flakes'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to ().
2. Line a 10cm x 21cm (4-inch x 8-inch) loaf tin with non-stick baking paper.
3. Place the chia seeds, psyllium husks, quinoa and water in a large bowl and mix to combine.
4. Allow to stand for minutes.
5. Add the sunflower seeds, flax seeds, almonds, oil, maple syrup and salt and mix well to combine.
6. Pour the mixture into the tin.
7. Bake for 1 hour minutes or until firm to the touch.
8. Allow to cool in the tin before turning out and slicing to serve.

Recipe name: Pasta With Broccoli And Lemon Cashew-Cream Sauce
Ingredients: '1 cup (150g or oz) raw cashews', '2 cups (500ml or fl oz) boiling water', '1 cup (250ml or fl oz) good-quality vegetable stock', ' cup (60ml or 2 fl oz) lemon juice', 'Sea salt and cracked black pepper', '400g ( oz) dried wholemeal (whole-wheat) spaghetti', '300g ( oz) broccoli florets (about 1 head)', ' cup (10g or oz) small basil leaves', '1 tablespoon finely shredded lemon rind'
Instructions:
1. To make the lemon cashew-cream sauce, place the cashews in a medium heatproof bowl and cover with the boiling water.
2. Allow to stand for minutes.
3. Drain the cashews well and place them in a blender.
4. Add the stock, lemon juice, salt and pepper and blend until very smooth.
5. Cook the pasta in a large saucepan of salted boiling water for 6 minutes.
6. Add the broccoli and cook for a further 4 minutes or until the pasta is al dente and the broccoli is just tender.
7. Drain the pasta and broccoli and immediately return to the warm saucepan.
8. Add the cashew-cream sauce and toss to combine.
9. Divide the pasta between serving bowls and top with the basil and lemon rind to serve.

Ingredients: '1 cup pitted dates', '1 cup rolled oats', '1 cup unsweetened shredded coconut or coconut flakes, plus extra for rolling', ' cup macadamia nuts or cashews', ' cup hemp seeds', '2 tablespoons white chia seeds (black chia are okay, too)', '2 tablespoons unsweetened cashew butter', 'Zest and juice of 2 limes', '1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract', ' teaspoon spirulina or chlorella powder (optional)', ' teaspoon maca powder (optional)', ' teaspoon kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. In a food processor, combine all of the ingredients and pulse until the mixture is evenly chopped and beginning to stick together and form a ball.
2. Transfer the AdoughA to a medium bowl and chill in the refrigerator for minutes.
3. Using a small ice cream scoop, or about tablespoon measure, portion the dough into balls.
4. Roll each ball with your hands to make it smooth.
5. Sprinkle shredded coconut or coconut flakes onto a plate and roll the balls in it to lightly coat.
6. Store the bites in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks or the freezer for up to 1 month.

Ingredients: 'A cup plus 1 tablespoon coconut aminos', '3 tablespoons natural almond butter', '1 tablespoon raw honey or pure maple syrup', '2 teaspoons toasted sesame oil', ' to teaspoon sriracha hot sauce, to taste', '1 pound chicken or turkey breast, ground or cut into bite-size pieces', '1 red bell pepper, cored, seeded, and chopped', '1 cup finely chopped broccoli', '1 cup chopped purple cabbage', '1 cup shredded carrots', '8 green onions (white and green parts), chopped', ' cup chopped jicama', ' cup chopped celery', '4 to 6 lettuce leaves (Bibb, Boston, or romaine), washed and dried well', ' cup unsalted cashew pieces', 'Toasted sesame seeds or hemp seeds'
Instructions:
1. In a small bowl or medium jar, whisk or shake together the sauce ingredients until well combined.
2. Set aside.
3. In a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, cook the meat until browned and no longer pink, 5 to minutes.
4. Add the vegetables and the sauce mixture and cook until the green onions and red peppers are softened and the meat has soaked up the mixture, about 5 minutes more.
5. Remove the pan from the heat.
6. Spoon about A cup of the filling into a lettuce leaf.
7. Sprinkle with the cashews and sesame or hemp seeds and enjoy!

Ingredients: '1 medium sweet potato, baked until tender, skin removed, and mashed (about cup)', '1 cup rolled oats', '1 cup unsweetened almond, coconut, or cashew milk', '2 eggs or egg replacer equivalent', '3 tablespoons unsweetened almond butter', '1 tablespoon flax meal', '2 teaspoons baking powder', '1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract', '1 teaspoon ground cinnamon', 'Zest of 1 medium clementine', 'Pinch of ground cardamom', 'Pinch of ground nutmeg', ' cup chopped pecans, plus more for topping', 'Olive oil cooking spray', 'Maple syrup, for serving (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Combine all of the ingredients except the pecans in a blender or food processor and blend until relatively smooth (there may still be some lumps; thatAs okay).
2. Fold in the pecans.
3. Preheat a waffle iron to medium heat and grease with cooking spray.
4. Pour about cup of the batter into the center of the waffle iron.
5. Press closed and cook until the waffle is golden brown and the edges are slightly crisp, 3 to 4 minutes.
6. Flip and repeat on the other side.
7. Serve warm topped with pecans and a drizzle of maple syrup, if desired.

Recipe name: Instant Pot Lemon Chicken With Garlic And Olives
Ingredients: '1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil, plus more as needed', '2 pounds bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs, patted dry (4 to 6, depending on the size)', ' teaspoon kosher salt, plus more if needed', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper cup chicken stock, preferably homemade', ' cup mixed pitted olives 4 thin slices lemon', '4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '2 anchovy fillets, chopped (optional) 1 tablespoon drained capers 1 teaspoon fresh rosemary needles, chopped', '1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves, chopped, plus more for garnish', ' teaspoon fennel seeds, lightly crushed with a mortar and pestle or with the flat side of a knife'
Instructions:
1. Using the sautA function of your pressure cooker (set on high if possible), heat the oil in the pressure cooker pot.
2. Season the chicken with the salt and pepper and arrange in a single layer, skinside down, on the bottom of the pot. (Work in batches if they donAt fit in a single layer, adding more oil for each batch.) Cook, without moving, until well browned, about 8 minutes. (You only have to brown it on one side).
3. Flip the chicken, pour in the stock, and scatter with the olives, lemon slices, garlic, anchovies (if using), capers, rosemary, thyme, and fennel seeds.
4. Lock the lid into place and cook on high pressure for minutes.
5. Let the pressure naturally release.
6. Discard the lemon slices and transfer the chicken to serving plates; keep loosely tented with foil.
7. Using the sautA function, bring the sauce to a simmer and cook until thickened slightly, 3 to 5 minutes.
8. Taste and adjust the salt and pepper if necessary.
9. Spoon the sauce over the chicken and serve garnished with thyme.
10. Cook it slow: After step 2, slow cook on high for 3 to 4 hours or low for 4 to 5 hours.

Recipe name: Instant Pot Lemon Vanilla Rice Pudding With Whipped Cream
Ingredients: ' cup Arborio or other short-grain white rice', '3 cups whole milk', ' cup sugar', '1 cinnamon stick', '1 teaspoon finely grated lemon zest', 'Pinch of kosher salt', ' vanilla bean, halved lengthwise', ' cup heavy cream', '2 egg yolks', ' cup raisins (optional)', 'Ground cinnamon, for serving (optional)', 'Whipped cream, for serving (optional)'
Instructions:
1. In the pressure cooker pot, stir together the rice, milk, sugar, cinnamon stick, lemon zest, and salt.
2. Use the tip of a paring knife to scrape the vanilla seeds out of the pod into the pot and add the vanilla bean, too.
3. Lock the lid into place and cook on high pressure for 4 minutes.
4. Let the pressure release naturally for minutes, then manually release the remaining pressure.
5. Remove and discard the cinnamon stick and vanilla bean.
6. In a small bowl, whisk together the cream and yolks.
7. Whisk into the rice, continuing to stir until thickened, 2 to 4 minutes.
8. The residual heat of the rice will cook the yolks.
9. Stir in the raisins (if using).
10. Spoon the pudding into serving bowls, then cover with plastic wrap and chill for at least 4 hours.
11. The pudding will thicken as it cools.
12. Serve with a sprinkle of ground cinnamon and whipped cream, if desired.

Recipe name: Instant Pot Middle Eastern Lentils With Rice Leeks And Spinach
Ingredients: '3 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for serving', '2 large or 3 small leeks (white and light green parts only), halved and thinly sliced', 'Kosher salt', '2 garlic cloves, finely grated or minced', '1 teaspoon ground cumin', ' teaspoon ground allspice', ' teaspoon cayenne pepper', '1 cup brown or green lentils', '1 bay leaf', '1 cinnamon stick', ' cup long-grain white rice', '5 ounces baby spinach', '1 cup plain yogurt'
Instructions:
1. Using the sautA function, heat the oil in the pressure cooker pot.
2. Stir in the leeks and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are dark golden at the edges, about minutes.Season with a pinch of salt.
3. Use a slotted spoon to scoop out half the leeks and transfer to a bowl; set aside for the garnish.
4. Stir the garlic into the pot and cook until fragrant, about seconds.
5. Stir in the cumin, allspice, and cayenne and cook for seconds.
6. Add the lentils, 2 cups water, 2 teaspoons salt, the bay leaf, and cinnamon stick to the pot.
7. Lock the lid into place and cook on high pressure for 1 minute.
8. Let the pressure release naturally for 5 minutes, then manually release the remaining pressure.
9. Uncover the pot and stir in the rice, working quickly so the lentil mixture stays hot.
10. Lock the lid back into place and cook on high pressure for 8 minutes.
11. Manually release the pressure.
12. Uncover and stir in the spinach.
13. Cover the pot with the lid (but donAt lock it on) and let it sit for 5 to minutes to wilt the greens and finish cooking the rice.
14. In a small bowl, season the yogurt with salt to taste.
15. Discard the bay leaf and cinnamon stick.
16. Serve the lentils warm, garnished with the reserved leeks and salted yogurt on top, and drizzled with more olive oil.

Ingredients: '3 cups still-warm Big-Batch Parmesan Polenta', '6 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling', '1 (.5-oz.) can white beans, rinsed', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes (optional)', '1 bunch curly or Tuscan kale or escarole, tough ribs and stems removed, leaves torn into bite-size pieces', '1 Tbsp. white or red wine vinegar', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' cup finely grated Parmesan, plus more for topping'
Instructions:
1. Pour warm polenta into a large ovenproof skillet or a ceramic baking dish or metal baking pan and spread into an even layer (ideally about 1A thick).
2. Let cool, then cover and chill at least 1 hour and up to 1 week.
3. Preheat oven to .
4. Heat garlic and cup oil in a large skillet over medium, stirring occasionally, until garlic is fragrant and starting to brown, about 3 minutes.
5. Add beans and red pepper flakes (if using), then, working in batches, add kale, letting it wilt slightly after each addition before adding more, and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender and evenly darkened in color, about 3 minutes.
6. Stir in vinegar and salt and cook, still stirring occasionally, until liquid evaporates, about 2 minutes.
7. Remove from heat and stir in cup Parmesan.
8. Scrape kale mixture over prepared polenta and spread into an even layer.
9. Drizzle with more oil and sprinkle with more Parmesan.
10. Transfer skillet to oven and bake until polenta is warmed through and topping is lightly crisped and browned, minutes.

Recipe name: Pantry Dinner Salad With Polenta Croutons
Ingredients: ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', ' cup red or white wine vinegar', ' tsp. Dijon or whole grain mustard', ' tsp. honey or agave nectar', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper', '3 cups still-warm Big-Batch Parmesan Polenta', 'A cup finely grated Parmesan', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', ' red onion or 1 shallot, thinly sliced', '1 (heaping) cup thinly sliced raw crunchy vegetables (such as fennel, radishes, andor celery)', ' tsp. kosher salt', '1 head of radicchio, escarole, or romaine, leaves separated, torn into bite-size pieces', '2 oz. thinly sliced salami, prosciutto, or other cured meat (optional)', '1 cup olives, pickles, peperoncini, or marinated artichoke hearts, sliced if desired', ' cup (loosely packed) tender herbs (such as basil, parsley, mint, andor dill)'
Instructions:
1. Whisk together oil, vinegar, mustard, honey, salt, and pepper in a small bowl (or shake together in a small jar).
2. Set aside.
3. Pour polenta into a square or rectangular airtight container and spread into an even layer.
4. Let cool, then cover and chill at least 1 hour and up to 1 week. (The polenta will set into a solid block.) Turn out polenta onto a cutting board and cut into 1A cubes.
5. Place Parmesan in a wide shallow bowl and, working in batches, toss cubes in cheese to coat.
6. Using your hands, press cheese against edges of polenta to adhere, then transfer to a plate.
7. Heat oil in a nonstick skillet over medium-high.
8. Working in batches as needed, place as many polenta cubes as will fit in an even layer with 1A space between each in skillet.
9. Let fry, undisturbed, until bottoms are crisp and browned, about 4 minutes.
10. Resist the urge to move the cubes before 3 minutes have passed to avoid pulling the Parmesan coating off the polenta.
11. Turn over one at a time, making sure cubes donAt stick together, and repeat cooking process with opposite sides of cubes.
12. Transfer to a plate.
13. Toss onion, crunchy vegetables, and salt in a large bowl.
14. Add radicchio, salami (if using), olives, and herbs and toss to combine.
15. Drizzle reserved dressing over and toss gently to coat.
16. Add warm croutons and gently toss again.

Ingredients: '1 Tbsp. kosher salt', '3 cups polenta (coarse cornmeal)', ' cups finely grated Parmesan', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Bring 9 cups water to a boil in a medium pot over high.
2. Add salt and reduce heat to medium-low.
3. Stirring constantly with a wooden spoon, gradually stream in polenta.
4. Cook, stirring often, until thick and creamy, minutes.
5. Remove from heat and stir in Parmesan and pepper.

Ingredients: '1 stick (113g) unsalted butter', ' cup (100g) plus 2 tablespoons sugar', '2 large unwaxed lemons, ends trimmed, then halved, seeded, and sliced A inch thick', ' cups (313g) all-purpose flour', '1 teaspoon baking powder', ' teaspoon baking soda', ' teaspoon kosher salt', '2 large eggs', '1 cup (236ml) olive oil', '1 cup (227g) full-fat plain Greek yogurt', '1 cup (200g) sugar'
Instructions:
1. Position a rack in the center of your oven and preheat the oven to .
2. Butter a 9-inch round cake pan and line it with parchment paper.
3. In a medium saucepan over low heat, melt the butter.
4. Remove the pan from the heat and add cup of the sugar and 2 tablespoons of water.
5. Whisk the mixture until the sugar dissolves and the mixture is glossy and thickened a little.
6. Pour the melted butter and sugar into the prepared cake pan and smooth into an even layer to cover the bottom.
7. Place the cake pan in the freezer until the butter and sugar layer freezes; leave the pan in the freezer until you are ready to transfer the batter to the pan.
8. In a small bowl, gently toss the lemon slices with the remaining 2 tablespoons of sugar.
9. Leave the lemon slices in the sugar to macerate for minutes.
10. Sift the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a large bowl.
11. Set aside.
12. Combine the eggs with the olive oil in another large bowl and beat vigorously with a whisk until well combined.
13. Add the yogurt and sugar.
14. Make a well in the flour mixture and add the wet mixture in three additions, folding in each addition until no dry bits of flour remain.
15. Set aside.
16. Fan the lemon slices out to cover the melted butter and sugar in the cake pan, overlapping the slices by as much as 1A8 inch.
17. Pour the cake batter directly on top of the lemon slices.
18. Bake for about 1 hour, until a cake tester inserted into the center comes out with only a few moist crumbs clinging to it.
19. Transfer to a wire cooling rack and let cool for minutes.
20. Invert the cake onto a serving plate and carefully remove the parchment-paper lining.
21. Allow the cake to cool for 1 hour more before serving.
22. Note: This cake can be made with nearly any citrus, my favorite being lemon because I like it to be bitter and tart.
23. The only citrus I would not use is raw grapefruit, oro blanco, or lime.
24. The rinds of grapefruit and oro blanco are much thicker than lemons and oranges and they will take longer to bake.
25. Lime and grapefruit also retain too much of the bitter quinine flavor and will make for an odd-tasting cake
