Ingredients: ' tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. baking soda', '1 tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. sugar', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', ' cup (1 stick) chilled unsalted butter or vegan butter (preferably MiyokoAs), cut into small pieces, plus 2 Tbsp. melted for brushing', '1 cup ( g) sourdough starter discard from a starter fed within the previous hours, room temperature', 'Flaky sea salt (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Stir together baking powder, baking soda, kosher salt, sugar, and cups flour with a fork in a large bowl.
3. Add cup chilled butter and toss with your hands or fork just to coat.
4. Using your fingers, smash butter into flat disks (if you miss a few, it will be fine).
5. Using a pastry cutter or fork, work butter into dry ingredients until shaggy crumbles form (you should have some large pieces, some small pieces, some flat pieces, and some sandy flour).
6. Add sourdough starter and mix gently with fork to incorporate, then fold with your hands just until dough comes together with just a few crumbly pieces in the bottom of the bowl.
7. Transfer dough to a well-floured surface and pat out with your hands until about thick (the shape doesnAt matter too much at this point).
8. Fold into thirds as you would a letter to create a rough rectangle.
9. Working from short sides, fold in thirds like a letter again.
10. Pat dough out to a -thick square.
11. Repeat folding process.
12. Pat out dough for a third time to a 1A-thick squareAit should feel airy, like a pillow at this point.
13. Using a floured -diameter biscuit cutter or glass, punch out as many biscuits as you can (do not twist cutter).
14. Transfer biscuits to a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet, spacing at least 1A apart.
15. Using your hands, gently press scraps into a rectangle, then fold in half.
16. Pat out to a 1A-thick rectangle and cut out more biscuits.
17. Gather remaining scraps together to form 1 final biscuit (you should have total).
18. Transfer to baking sheet.
19. Brush biscuit tops with 2 Tbsp. melted butter and sprinkle with sea salt if desired Bake biscuits until tall and golden and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the side of a biscuit registers ), minutes.

Recipe name: Carrot Ribbon Salad With Ginger Parsley And Dates
Ingredients: '1 pound medium carrots (about 6), preferably rainbow', '2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil', '2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice', '1 teaspoon grated peeled fresh ginger', ' teaspoon honey', 'Kosher salt', 'A cup finely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley', ' cup roasted salted almonds, coarsely chopped', '6 large Medjool dates, pitted and thinly sliced lengthwise'
Instructions:
1. Using a vegetable peeler, shave the carrots into long, thin slices, watching out for your fingers.
2. Soak, along with any remaining carrot nubs, in a bowl of ice water until the slices curl, about minutes.
3. Drain and pat dry.
4. In a large salad bowl, whisk together the oil, lemon juice, ginger, honey, and A teaspoon salt.
5. Add the carrots, parsley, half the almonds, and half the dates and toss to combine.
6. Adjust the salt to taste.
7. Top the salad with the remaining almonds and dates and serve.

Ingredients: '2 large Granny Smith or Bramley apples, peeled, cored and roughly chopped', '1 Tbsp ( ml) lemon juice', ' cup ( ml) water', '1 cups ( g) wholemeal spelt flour', 'Scant cup ( g) ground almonds', '1 Tbsp ( g) sumac', '1 tsp baking powder', '1 tsp baking soda', ' cup ( ml) sunflower oil', ' cup plus 2 tbsp ( g) superfinecaster sugar', '1 cups ( g) applesauce', '3 Braeburn apples, peeled, cored and diced into small chunks', ' cup ( g) confectionersA sugar', '1 Tbsp ( ml) lemon juice', 'Sumac, to dust'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to ().
2. Grease a 1-pound (-g) loaf pan and line with parchment paper.
3. To make the applesauce, add the apple pieces to a saucepan with the lemon juice and water.
4. Let them simmer for to minutes, or until they have completely softened.
5. Remove from the heat and mash the apples with a fork until smooth.
6. You will need roughly 1 cups ( g) for this recipe.
7. To make the cake, mix together the flour, almonds, sumac, baking powder and baking soda in a large bowl and set aside.
8. In a medium bowl, combine the oil, sugar and applesauce.
9. Add the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir gently, ensuring there are no lumps of flour.
10. The batter will be fairly thick.
11. Mix in the chopped apples and pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan.
12. Bake for to minutes, or until a toothpick inserted comes out clean.
13. Leave to cool completely in the pan before turning it out.
14. To make the drizzle, mix the sugar and lemon juice in a medium bowl until itAs thick enough to coat the back of a spoon.
15. If itAs too thin, add some more sugar or loosen it up by adding more lemon juice.
16. Once the cake is completely cool, use a teaspoon to drizzle the icing and sprinkle on a little more sumac.

Recipe name: Strawberry Honey Balsamic With Black Pepper Ice Cream
Ingredients: ' ounces (about 1 pint) ripe strawberries, hulled and halved', ' cup honey', 'Heat the oven to . Line a sheet pan with parchment paper.', ' cup granulated sugar', '2 tablespoons dry milk powder', ' teaspoon xanthan gum', '2 tablespoons light corn syrup', '1A cups whole milk', '1A cups heavy cream', ' cup Strawberry Puree (recipe follows), very cold', ' cup honey balsamic vinegar, like Honey Ridge Farms or O Olive Oil', 'A teaspoon freshly (and very finely) ground black pepper', '3 cups Ice Cream Base (recipe follows), very cold', ' cup your favorite strawberry jam'
Instructions:
1. Spread the strawberries out evenly on the lined sheet pan and drizzle them with the honey.
2. Bake for minutes.
3. Stir the berries and continue baking until they just barely begin to caramelize and get a little bit of roasted color to them, about minutes more.
4. Remove the sheet pan from the oven and let them sit until theyAre cool enough to handle.
5. Transfer the berries to a blender along with the yummy sticky stuff on the parchment paper and puree to make a smooth sauce.
6. Chill in the fridge until cold.
7. Store the puree in the fridge for up to 2 weeks or in the freezer for up to 3 months.
8. Combine the sugar, dry milk, and xanthan gum in a small bowl and stir well.
9. Pour the corn syrup into a medium pot and stir in the whole milk.
10. Add the sugar mixture and immediately whisk vigorously until smooth.
11. Set the pot over medium heat and cook, stirring often and adjusting the heat if necessary to prevent a simmer, until the sugar has fully dissolved, about 3 minutes.
12. Remove the pot from the heat.
13. Add the cream and whisk until fully combined.
14. Transfer the mixture to an airtight container and refrigerate until well chilled, at least 6 hours, or for even better texture and flavor, hours.
15. Stir the base back together if it separates during the resting time.
16. The base can be further stored in the fridge for up to 1 week or in the freezer for up to 3 months. (Just be sure to fully thaw the frozen base before using it.) In a bowl, combine the strawberry puree, vinegar, and black pepper with the ice cream base.
17. Whisk as needed to get everything completely incorporated into the base.
18. Pour the mixture into an ice cream maker and turn on the machine.
19. Churn just until it has the texture of soft-serve.
20. Stir the jam with a fork to loosen it.
21. Quickly alternate spooning layers of the mixture and dollops of the jam in freezer-friendly containers.
22. Cover with parchment paper, pressing it to the surface of the ice cream so it adheres, then cover with a lid.
23. ItAs okay if the parchment hangs over the rim.
24. Store it in the coldest part of your freezer (farthest from the door) until firm, at least 6 hours.
25. It will keep for up to 3 months.

Ingredients: '2 cups gingersnap cookie crumbs', ' tablespoons sugar', '5 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted', '4 ounces fresh goat cheese, at room temperature', '6 ounces cream cheese, at room temperature', ' cup buttermilk', ' cup plus 2 tablespoons sugar', '4 large eggs', 'Grated zest and juice of 1 lemon', '1 teaspoon togarashi (see note)', 'About 1 tablespoon sorghum for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. Stir the cookie crumbs, sugar, and melted butter together in a medium bowl with a fork until the crumbs are evenly moistened.
3. Press the mixture evenly onto the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
4. Bake until golden brown and crispy, about minutes.
5. Cool completely.
6. Reduce the oven temperature to .
7. In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment (or using a handheld mixer), beat together the goat cheese, cream cheese, and buttermilk until smooth and fluffy, 4 to 5 minutes.
8. Gradually beat in the sugar until smooth.
9. Beat in the eggs one at a time.
10. Beat in the lemon zest and juice.
11. Beat in teaspoon of the togarashi.
12. Pour the filling into the springform pan.
13. Sprinkle the top with the remaining teaspoon togarashi.
14. Wrap the pan in aluminum foil to prevent leaks and place the pan in a large roasting pan.
15. Pour enough hot water into the roasting pan to come one-third of the way up the sides of the cake pan.
16. Bake the cheesecake for 1 hour and minutes, or until slightly puffed.
17. Remove the cake pan from the water bath and allow it to cool to room temperature, then chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours. (The cheesecake can be refrigerated, tightly wrapped in plastic wrap, for up to 5 days.) To serve, run a thin knife around the sides of the cake pan and release the sides of the pan.
18. Slide the cheesecake onto a serving platter.
19. Drizzle the top with a little sorghum, slice, and serve.

Recipe name: Spicy Tomatoatuna Noodle Skillet Casserole With Aioli
Ingredients: '1 large egg yolk', '1 garlic clove, finely grated', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', ' cup (or more) extra-virgin olive oil', '1 medium onion, cut into large pieces', '2 garlic cloves', ' cup coarsely chopped roasted red peppers from a jar', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling', ' oz. rigatoni, mezzi rigatoni, ditali, or other short or medium-length straight tube pasta', '2 (5A6-oz.) cans or jars oil-packed tuna', '1 oil-packed anchovy fillet, coarsely chopped', '1 tsp. kosher salt, divided', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '2 Tbsp. double-concentrated or regular tomato paste', '1 (.5-oz.) can crushed tomatoes or whole peeled tomatoes with their juices, crushed', '1 tsp. ground turmeric', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', 'Coarsely chopped parsley (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Whisk egg yolk, garlic, tsp. salt, and 2 tsp. water in a small bowl to combine.
2. Place bowl on a kitchen towel so it wonAt slide around as you whisk and gradually stream in oil, whisking constantly until thick and emulsified.
3. Aioli should be thick but pourable; gradually whisk in more oil if needed.
4. Taste and season with more salt if desired.
5. Place rack in upper third of oven; preheat to .
6. Pulse onion, garlic, and roasted red peppers in a food processor until finely chopped; set aside.
7. Heat 3 Tbsp. olive oil in a ovenproof skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium-low.
8. Add pasta and cook, stirring often, until evenly coated in oil and lightly browned, about 5 minutes (depending on the size and shape of your pasta, you may need to work in batches).
9. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to a large bowl.
10. Reserve pan.
11. Drain tuna, reserving 2 Tbsp. tuna oil. place oil in reserved pan and increase heat to medium.
12. Add anchovy, tsp. salt, and reserved chopped vegetable mixture and season with pepper.
13. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables soften, 5A7 minutes.
14. Add tomato paste and cook, stirring, until slightly darkened in color, about 3 minutes.
15. Mix in toasted pasta, tomatoes, turmeric, red pepper flakes, remaining tsp. salt, and cups water and bring to a boil.
16. Cook, stirring occasionally, until pasta is slightly softened, minutes.
17. Remove from heat and stir in drained tuna and cup water.
18. Transfer skillet to oven and bake casserole until pasta is tender and juices at the edges are bubbling, about minutes.
19. Remove from oven.
20. Heat broiler.
21. Drizzle casserole with a little olive oil and broil until browned and crisped in spots, about 4 minutes.
22. Spoon some aioli over casserole and top with parsley if desired.
23. Serve remaining aioli alongside.

Recipe name: Braised Celery With Lentils And Garlic
Ingredients: ' cup plus 1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 bunch celery, leaves reserved, stalks sliced thick on a diagonal', '6A8 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more', ' cup dry white wine or dry vermouth', 'A small handful hardy herb sprigs (such as rosemary, thyme, andor sage; optional)', ' cups low-sodium beef, chicken, or vegetable broth', '2 cups Big-Batch Marinated Lentils', '4 large eggs', 'Flaky sea salt', 'Freshly ground black pepper', 'Crusty bread (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Heat cup oil in a large high-sided skillet over medium-high.
2. Add celery and toss to coat.
3. Cook, stirring occasionally, until celery is very lightly browned, about 5 minutes, then add garlic and toss to coat.
4. Cook, stirring occasionally, until garlic is deep golden brown and celery is tender and golden brown, 5A7 minutes.
5. Mix in 1 tsp. kosher salt, then add wine and herbs (if using) and stir to combine.
6. Cook until wine is reduced by half, about 2 minutes.
7. Add broth and bring to a boil; stir in lentils.
8. Cook until lentils are warmed through and flavors have come together, about 5 minutes.
9. Taste and season with more kosher salt if needed.
10. Fish out herb sprigs if using and discard.
11. Heat remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium.
12. Crack eggs into skillet and cover with a lid (or a baking sheet if you donAt have one), reduce heat to low, and cook just until egg whites are set but yolks are still runny, about 3 minutes.
13. Divide braised celery mixture among bowls.
14. Top each with an egg and some celery leaves; sprinkle with sea salt and pepper.
15. Serve with crusty bread, if desired.

Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '2 oz. bacon or pancetta (Italian bacon), finely chopped (optional)', '1 large onion, finely chopped', '4 garlic cloves, finely chopped', '2 Tbsp. harissa paste or tomato paste', ' cup red wine or dry white wine', '1 (-oz.) can whole peeled or diced tomatoes', ' cups Big-Batch Marinated Lentils, divided', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', '1 lb. dried pasta (any kind)', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into small pieces', 'Finely grated Parmesan, (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Heat oil and bacon (if using) in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium, stirring, until bacon starts to sizzle, about 1 minute.
2. Add onion and garlic and cook until onion is translucent, 5A8 minutes.
3. Add harissa paste and cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly darkened in color, about 5 minutes.
4. Add wine and cook, scraping up browned bits and stirring occasionally, until wine is reduced by half, about 3 minutes.
5. If using whole tomatoes, use kitchen shears or a pairing knife to burst each in the can (this will prevent juice from squirting on your shirt when you crush them).
6. Add to pot, crushing with your hands as you go; add any juices remaining in can.
7. If using diced tomatoes, add directly to pot along with juices.
8. Stir to combine, then fill can almost to the top with water and pour into pot, swirling to get all the extra tomato juices out of the can.
9. Bring sauce to a boil over medium heat.
10. Set aside cup lentils for serving.
11. Add remaining lentils to sauce.
12. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally, until sauce thickens slightly and flavors come together, minutes.
13. Remove from heat and purAe sauce with an immersion blender until almost smooth in some places but chunky in others. (Alternatively, purAe half of sauce in a blender until almost smooth, then stir back into sauce in pot.) Season with salt and pepper.
14. Keep warm until ready to serve.
15. Cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water until not quite al dente, about 1 minute less than package directions.
16. Using a spider or tongs, transfer pasta to sauce and add butter and cup pasta cooking liquid.
17. Cook, stirring constantly and adding more pasta cooking liquid if needed, until sauce thickens enough to coat pasta and pasta looks glossy, about 1 minute.
18. Divide pasta among shallow bowls and top with reserved lentils and Parmesan (if using).
19. Do ahead: Sauce can be made 5 days ahead.
20. Let cool, then transfer to an airtight container.
21. Cover and chill, or freeze up to 3 months.
22. Reheat, adding a little water to thin if needed, before serving.

Ingredients: ' cups French green or black beluga lentils, rinsed, picked through', '3 tsp. kosher salt, divided', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', ' cup sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar', ' tsp. honey', ' tsp. hot smoked Spanish paprika or Hungarian sweet paprika'
Instructions:
1. Combine lentils and 2 tsp. salt in a large saucepan and pour in cold water to cover by at least 1A.
2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer gently until lentils are tender but still have some bite (like al dente pasta), about minutes.
3. Drain and return to pot.
4. Meanwhile, whisk oil, vinegar, honey, paprika, and remaining 1 tsp. salt in a small bowl or measuring glass.
5. Pour vinaigrette over hot lentils and toss to coat.
6. Let sit at least minutes before serving to give lentils time to absorb flavors from vinaigrette.
7. Do ahead: Lentils can be prepared 5 days ahead.
8. Transfer to an airtight container; cover and chill.

Ingredients: '8 tbsp ( g) unsalted butter, softened', '1 cup ( g) sugar', '1 large egg', '1A2 cup ( g) leftover starter', '1 cup ( g) whole milk ricotta', 'Zest of 1 lime or several key limes', '1 tsp (5 ml) pure vanilla extract', '3 cups ( g) all-purpose flour', '1 tbsp ( g) baking powder', '1A2 tsp fine sea salt', '3A4 cup ( g) powdered sugar, sifted', 'Juice of 1 to 2 limes, zest reserved for decoration'
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to ().
2. Line two sheet pans with parchment paper and set aside.
3. In the bowl of a stand mixer, cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy, about 3 to 4 minutes.
4. Scrape down the sides of the bowl.
5. Add the egg and mix to combine.
6. With the machine running, add the leftover starter, ricotta, lime zest, and vanilla.
7. Depending on the temperature of your ingredients, the mixture may become lumpyAthis is okay.
8. Meanwhile, whisk the flour, baking powder, and salt together in a large bowl.
9. On low speed, gradually add the dry ingredients and mix until just incorporated.
10. Scrape down the sides of the bowl once more, making sure that there is no flour hiding at the bottom.
11. It will smell wonderful.
12. Cover the bowl in plastic wrap and chill the dough for minutes to 1 hour.
13. Then portion the dough onto your sheet pans using a tablespoon or mini ice cream scoop.
14. Place both sheet pans into the oven and bake for about to minutes.
15. Rotate the pans and swap racks at the halfway mark for even coloring.
16. When finished, the cookies will be pale yellow with golden bottoms and will feel soft to the touch.
17. Transfer to a wire rack.
18. For the glaze, sift the powdered sugar into a small bowl.
19. Add the juice of 1 lime and whisk until a smooth glaze forms.
20. If the texture is too thick, add more lime juice as needed until you achieve a pourable consistency.
21. Once the cookies are completely cool, drizzle some of the glaze over the top.
22. Finish with grated lime zest to decorate.
23. These cookies will stay fresh for up to 3 to 5 days stored in an airtight container.
24. Alternatively, freeze for up to 2 months by placing unglazed cookies between sheets of parchment paper to prevent sticking.

Ingredients: '1A4 cup ( g) sugar', '1 tsp (3 g) cinnamon', '1A2 cup ( g) leftover starter', '1 cup ( ml) milk, whole or 2%', '3 tbsp ( g) unsalted butter, melted, divided', '1 large egg', '1 cup ( g) all-purpose flour', '1 tbsp ( g) sugar', '2 tsp ( g) baking powder', '1A2 tsp fine sea salt', 'Cooking spray, for coating', '1 cup ( g) cubed pineapple', 'Handful of mixed seasonal berries', '1A4 cup ( g) coconut flakes', 'Maple syrup, to serve'
Instructions:
1. Combine the cinnamon and sugar in a shallow bowl.
2. Preheat your waffle iron according to the manufacturerAs instructions.
3. Add the leftover starter, milk, 2 tablespoons ( g) of melted butter, and egg into a large bowl.
4. Whisk well to combine.
5. Add the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt and continue to whisk until smooth.
6. If the batter seems too thick, add more milk to thin out the texture.
7. This will all depend on the consistency of your sourdough starter.
8. Lightly coat the waffle iron with cooking spray.
9. Ladle some of the batter into the waffle iron to fill the pan.
10. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes, or until golden and crisp.
11. Transfer to a cutting board and brush lightly with some of the remaining melted butter.
12. Press the waffle into the cinnamon sugar to coat on both sides.
13. Repeat to cook the rest of the waffles.
14. To serve, top your waffles with the pineapple, mixed berries, and coconut flakes.
15. Enjoy with sweet maple syrup on the side.
16. TIP: Once completely cool, these waffles can be frozen for up to 2 months.
17. Cover in plastic wrap and a layer of foil before freezing.
18. Bake frozen at () until warmed through.

Ingredients: 'paper muffin cup liners', ' stick ( cup) unsalted butter', ' cup all-purpose flour', '1 teaspoons baking powder', ' cup yellow cornmeal', ' cup plus 1 tablespoon sugar', '1 tablespoon freshly grated lemon zest', ' cup whole milk', '2 large egg yolks', ' cup blueberries'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to . and line six -cup muffin cups with paper liners.
2. Melt butter and cool.
3. Into a large bowl sift together flour and baking powder and whisk in cornmeal, cup sugar, lemon zest, and a generous pinch salt.
4. In a bowl whisk together melted butter, milk, and yolks and add to flour mixture with half of blueberries, gently stirring until just combined.
5. Divide batter evenly among cups (batter will fill cups) and press remaining blueberries into tops of muffins.
6. Sprinkle tops of muffins evenly with remaining tablespoon sugar.
7. Bake muffins in middle of oven about minutes, or until tops are golden and a tester comes out clean.
8. Remove muffins from cups and cool on a rack.
9. Muffins keep in an airtight container at room temperature 2 days.

Ingredients: '2 to 2 ounces Cognac', ' ounce Champagne vinegar', ' ounce maple syrup', ' teaspoon pure vanilla extract', 'Garnish: lemon twist'
Instructions:
1. Combine Cognac, vinegar, maple syrup, and vanilla in a mixing glass and fill with ice.
2. Stir until well chilled, about revolutions.
3. Strain into a chilled coupe glass, gently squeeze the lemon twist over the top to express oils, then use as a garnish.

Ingredients: '2 ounces rye ( proof preferred)', '1 teaspoon maple syrup', ' teaspoon sherry vinegar', 'Garnish: dried apricot on a cocktail pick (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Combine rye, maple syrup, and sherry vinegar in a rocks glass and fill with ice.
2. Stir until chilled, about revolutions.
3. Garnish with a speared apricot.

Recipe name: Spicy Baked Pasta With Cheddar And Broccoli Rabe
Ingredients: 'Kosher salt', '1 lb. rigatoni, ziti, or fusilli', '8 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '3 large leeks, white and pale green parts only, halved lengthwise, thinly sliced into half-moons', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '1 tsp. (or more) crushed red pepper flakes', '2 bunches broccoli rabe or 3 bunches baby broccolini, trimmed, coarsely chopped', '1 cup heavy cream', ' oz. sharp white cheddar, coarsely grated (about 3 cups), divided', '1 cup chopped chives, divided', '1 cup coarse fresh breadcrumbs or panko (Japanese breadcrumbs)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil, then add a generous amount of salt.
3. Cook pasta, stirring occasionally to keep it from sticking together, until just barely al dente, about 2 minutes less than package directions.
4. Drain pasta, reserving 1 cup pasta cooking liquid.
5. While you are cooking the pasta, get the broccoli rabe going.
6. Heat 5 Tbsp. oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over mediumAhigh.
7. Add leeks and season with salt and black pepper.
8. Cook, stirring occasionally, until leeks are softened (but not so much that they donAt have any texture left) and starting to brown, minutes.
9. Add red pepper flakes to leeks and stir to incorporate.
10. Add broccoli rabe by the handful, stirring to combine and allowing each addition to wilt before adding more.
11. Season with salt and black pepper.
12. Once all of the broccoli rabe has been added, cook, stirring occasionally, until bright green and wilted, about 2 minutes.
13. Remove pot from heat and set aside.
14. Add pasta to reserved broccoli rabe mixture along with cream, three-quarters of the cheese, cup chives, and reserved pasta cooking liquid; mix well.
15. Season with salt and black pepper and add more red pepper flakes if you prefer more heat (keep in mind that the saltiness and spiciness will increase as the pasta bakes).
16. Transfer pasta to a 3-qt. baking dish (or, if your Dutch oven is ovenproof, just leave it in there).
17. Toss breadcrumbs and remaining 3 Tbsp. oil in a medium bowl until coated; season with salt and black pepper.
18. Scatter over pasta, then sprinkle evenly with remaining cheese.
19. Bake until pasta is bubbling across the entire surface and breadcrumbs are deep golden brown, minutes.
20. Let cool slightly.
21. Scatter remaining chives over pasta just before serving.

Ingredients: ' cup walnuts', '4 whole pickles (half- or full-sour), halved lengthwise, sliced crosswise into (1") pieces, plus 3 Tbsp. pickle brine', '3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar', '2 tsp. Dijon mustard', '1 tsp. honey', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', ' small white onion, thinly sliced, rinsed', 'Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper', '1 medium fennel bulb, tough outer layers removed, thinly sliced crosswise', ' cup chopped dill', '2 oz. Parmesan, shaved', 'Extra-virgin olive oil (for drizzling)', 'Toasted country-style bread (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Toast walnuts on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing once, until golden brown, minutes.
3. Let cool; coarsely chop.
4. Meanwhile, whisk pickle brine, vinegar, mustard, honey, and red pepper flakes in a large bowl.
5. Add onion and season with salt and pepper.
6. Let sit to allow onion to soften slightly, at least 5 minutes and up to minutes.
7. Add walnuts, pickles, fennel, dill, and Parmesan to dressing and toss to coat.
8. Transfer salad to a large shallow bowl or platter and drizzle with oil.
9. Serve with toast to sop up the dressing if desired.

Ingredients: ' lb. red beets (about 2 large), peeled, cut into (") pieces', '2 garlic cloves, finely grated', '2 Tbsp. ancho chile powder', '1 tsp. crushed dried oregano, preferably Mexican', '1 tsp. paprika', ' tsp. ground cumin', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper', '1 Tbsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt, plus more', '2A cups plus 3 Tbsp. vegetable oil, divided', '1 large white onion, halved through root end, sliced (" thick)', '6 medium serrano chiles, quartered lengthwise, leaving stems and seeds intact', 'A cup sage leaves', '6 (6"-diameter) corn tortillas', '6 large eggs', 'Salsa de Arbol (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Toss beets, garlic, chile powder, oregano, paprika, cumin, pepper, 1 Tbsp.
3. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
4. Morton kosher salt, and A cup oil in a medium bowl until beets are coated.
5. Scrape onto a foil-lined rimmed baking sheet; bake, tossing every minutes, until beets are tender and spices are very fragrant and smell slightly toasted, minutes.
6. Meanwhile, heat 2 cups oil in a large saucepan over high until oil bubbles immediately when the edge of a tortilla touches the surface.
7. Carefully fry onion, adding a little bit at time to avoid too much spattering and reducing heat as needed, until tender and beginning to brown, about 5 minutes.
8. Transfer to a paper-towel-lined baking sheet.
9. Repeat process with chiles, about 5 minutes.
10. Transfer to same baking sheet.
11. Fry sage leaves until crisp, about seconds.
12. Transfer to baking sheet.
13. Working one at a time, fry tortillas, turning once, until crisp, puffed in places, and deep golden brown, about 1 minute per side.
14. Transfer tostadas to baking sheet.
15. Heat remaining 3 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
16. Crack eggs into skillet, leaving space around each one, and cook until whites are set and edges are crisp, about 4 minutes.
17. Season with salt.
18. Spoon beet mixture onto tostadas, dividing evenly, and top each with an egg, then fried onion, chiles, sage, and Salsa de Arbol.

Ingredients: '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '3 dried chiles de Arbol, seeds removed, chopped', '2 garlic cloves, finely grated', '1 (.5-oz.) can diced fire-roasted tomatoes', 'Kosher salt', '2 Tbsp. fresh lime juice'
Instructions:
1. Heat oil in a medium saucepan over medium.
2. Add chiles and garlic and cook, stirring constantly, until lightly toasted and very fragrant, about seconds.
3. Add tomatoes and 1 cup water, increase heat to medium-high, and cook, stirring occasionally, until most of the liquid has evaporated and tomatoes are very tender, about minutes.
4. Season tomato mixture with salt.
5. Using a potato masher, smash until the consistency of a chunky salsa.
6. Let cool, then stir in lime juice.
7. Do Ahead: Salsa (without lime juice) can be made 3 days ahead.
8. Cover and chill.
9. Add lime juice just before serving.

Ingredients: '1 cup mixed unhulled grains (such as spelt, whole barley, andor whole farro)', '1 small onion, unpeeled, halved', '3 garlic cloves, crushed', '2 (3x1") strips lemon zest', 'Kosher salt', ' cups parsley leaves with tender stems', '1 cup dill fronds', '1 cup buttermilk, preferably room temperature', ' cup plain whole-milk Greek yogurt, preferably room temperature', ' lemon', 'Extra-virgin olive oil (for drizzling)', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Toast grains in a dry medium heavy pot over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until just slightly darkened and beginning to smell nutty, about 4 minutes.
2. Pour in 4 cups water, then add onion, garlic, lemon zest, and a couple of big pinches of salt; stir to combine.
3. Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, stirring occasionally and skimming any foam from surface as needed, until grains are tender (pull out a few grains and take a bite to test), minutes; pluck out and discard onion, garlic, and lemon zest.
4. Meanwhile, purAe parsley, dill, buttermilk, and a big pinch of salt in a blender until bright green and smooth.
5. Pour into a medium bowl, add yogurt, and whisk until your arm gets a bit tiredA3 minutes should do the trick (whisking the mixture well will ensure that the yogurt doesnAt split and get grainy when you add it to the grains).
6. Pour buttermilk mixture over grains.
7. Increase heat to medium and bring to a bare simmer.
8. Cook just until heated through, about 1 minute.
9. Remove from heat and squeeze in juice from lemon half.
10. Stir well, then taste grains and season with more salt if needed.
11. Skim any foam from surface.
12. Ladle grains into bowls.
13. Drizzle some oil into each bowl and top with coarsely ground pepper.

Recipe name: Pasta With Brown Butter Whole Lemon And Parmesan
Ingredients: '1 lb. short tube pasta (such as paccheri or rigatoni)', 'Kosher salt', '8 Tbsp. (1 stick) unsalted butter, cut into pieces, divided', '1 small regular lemon or Meyer lemon, very thinly sliced into rounds, seeds removed', '1 oz. Parmesan, finely grated, plus more for serving', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Cook pasta in a large pot of boiling generously salted water, stirring occasionally, until very al dente, about 2 minutes less than package directions (pasta will finish cooking in the sauce).
2. Meanwhile, heat half of the butter in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium until melted.
3. Add lemon slices and cook, stirring often, until softened and bottom of pot is browned in spots, 5A7 minutes.
4. Using tongs, transfer one-third of lemon slices to a plate; set aside.
5. Just before pasta is al dente, scoop out 2 cups pasta cooking liquid.
6. Add cups pasta cooking liquid to butter sauce. (This may seem like a lot of liquid, but it will thicken once the remaining ingredients are added.) Add remaining butter a piece at a time, whisking until each piece is incorporated before adding more, until the sauce is emulsified and creamy.
7. Drain pasta and add to sauce.
8. Cook, stirring often and adding 1 oz.
9. Parmesan a little at a time.
10. Once all of the cheese is added, continue to cook, still stirring, until cheese is melted and sauce is creamy and clings to pasta, about 3 minutes.
11. If sauce looks very thick, add more pasta cooking liquid 1A2 Tbsp. at a time to thin (saucier is ideal as it will thicken as it cools).
12. Remove from heat and sprinkle with an almost ridiculous amount of pepper (about 2 tsp.); toss once more.
13. Serve pasta topped with reserved lemon rounds and more Parmesan.

Recipe name: One-Pot Gingery Chicken And Rice With Peanut Sauce
Ingredients: '6 small skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs (about 3 lb.)', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '2 large shallots, finely chopped', '8 garlic cloves, finely chopped', '1 (2") piece ginger, peeled, finely chopped', '2 cups high-quality basmati rice, rinsed', '2 whole star anise', '1 Tbsp. soy sauce', ' cup creamy peanut butter', '2 Tbsp. soy sauce', '2 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '5 tsp. Sriracha', '1 (1") piece ginger, peeled, finely chopped', ' tsp. honey', '1 English hothouse cucumber, halved lengthwise, thinly sliced on a diagonal', '1 cup cilantro leaves with tender stems'
Instructions:
1. Pat chicken dry and season all over with salt.
2. Arrange, skin side down, in a cold, dry medium Dutch oven and set over medium heat.
3. Cook, undisturbed, until skin is golden brown and crisp and easily releases from pot, minutes.
4. Slide thighs around to different spots (this will ensure even browning) and continue to cook until deeply browned, 3A5 minutes longer.
5. Transfer chicken to a plate.
6. Let pot cool 2 minutes.
7. Set pot over medium-low heat; add shallots, garlic, and ginger and cook, stirring constantly, until very fragrant but not browned, about 2 minutes.
8. Stir in rice and star anise and cook, stirring constantly, 1 minute.
9. Stir in soy sauce, tsp. salt, and 3 cups water.
10. Nestle chicken back into rice mixture in pot, arranging skin side up.
11. Increase heat to medium and bring liquid to a simmer.
12. Immediately cover pot, reduce heat to low, and cook minutes.
13. Remove from heat and let chicken and rice sit minutes.
14. While the chicken and rice are cooking, make the sauce.
15. Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, vinegar, Sriracha, ginger, honey, and cup warm water in a small bowl until smooth and pourable.
16. If sauce still looks a little thick, continuing adding water until it reaches a drizzle-able consistency.
17. Remove lid from pot and fluff rice with a fork.
18. Pluck out and discard star anise.
19. Serve chicken and rice with peanut sauce, cucumber, and cilantro alongside.
20. Do Ahead: Sauce can be made 3 days ahead.
21. Cover and chill.

Ingredients: '5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '4 oz. crimini mushrooms, quartered', 'Kosher salt', '1 large onion, thinly sliced', '4 large shallots, halved through root ends, peeled', '6 garlic cloves, 5 thinly sliced, 1 finely chopped', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter', ' cups dry white wine', ' cup whole farro, barley, or freekeh', '3 Tbsp. red or white miso', '1 small kabocha squash (about 3 lb.), halved, seeds removed, cut into (2"-thick) wedges', '1 medium delicata squash (about oz.), halved, seeds removed, cut into (2"-thick) wedges', '1 cup parsley leaves with tender stems', 'Zest of lemon'
Instructions:
1. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a medium Dutch oven over medium-high.
2. Cook mushrooms, tossing occasionally, until browned and crisp, 6A8 minutes.
3. Season with salt; transfer to a plate.
4. Let pot cool 2 minutes.
5. Set pot over medium heat and pour in remaining 3 Tbsp. oil.
6. Cook onion and shallots, stirring occasionally, until softened and starting to brown, 6A8 minutes.
7. Add sliced garlic and butter; cook, stirring often, until garlic is softened and starting to brown, about 2 minutes.
8. Add wine; simmer, stirring once, until reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
9. Add farro, miso, and 6 cups water.
10. Bring to a boil, stirring to dissolve miso.
11. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, stirring once or twice, until farro is halfway cooked, about minutes.
12. Taste broth and season with salt.
13. Add squash (submerge the best you can), cover, and cook until tender, about minutes.
14. Add mushrooms; cook just until heated through, about 2 minutes.
15. Ladle stew into bowls; top with parsley, lemon zest, and chopped garlic.

Ingredients: ' cup plus 3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '1 lb. wild mushrooms (such as maitake or oyster), torn into 2" pieces', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', '1 lb. kielbasa sausage, cut 1" thick on a diagonal', '1 large fennel bulb, fronds finely chopped, bulb finely chopped', '1 large onion, finely chopped', '2 medium carrots, peeled, finely chopped', '2 celery stalks, finely chopped', ' garlic cloves, 8 thinly sliced, 3 left whole', '1 Tbsp. thyme leaves', ' tsp. hot smoked Spanish paprika', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '3 Tbsp. double-concentrated tomato paste', '2 cups black beluga or French green lentils', '1 baguette, torn into 2" pieces'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Heat cup oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high until smoking.
3. Add mushrooms and cook, undisturbed, until golden brown underneath, about 5 minutes.
4. Turn mushrooms over and cook, undisturbed, until browned on the other side, about 5 minutes.
5. Season mushrooms with tsp. salt; transfer to a plate.
6. Add 3 Tbsp. oil to same pot and add sausage, arranging in a single layer.
7. Reduce heat to medium and cook, undisturbed, until browned underneath, about 3 minutes.
8. Turn sausage over and cook on second side until browned, about 3 minutes.
9. Add chopped fennel bulb, onion, carrots, celery, sliced garlic, and 1 tsp. salt and cook, stirring often until vegetables are softened but have not taken on any color, minutes.
10. Add thyme, paprika, and red pepper flakes and cook, stirring, 1 minute.
11. Add tomato paste and cook, stirring occasionally, until paste darkens in color and begins to stick to the bottom of pot, about 1 minute.
12. Stir mushrooms, lentils, and 1 tsp. salt into sausage mixture and add 8 cups water.
13. Increase heat to medium-high and bring to a boil.
14. Reduce heat to low, cover pot, leaving lid slightly askew, and continue to cook, stirring a few times, until most of the liquid has been absorbed and stew is thick and lentils are barely al dente, minutes.
15. Meanwhile, pulse bread in a food processor until coarse crumbs form (the largest ones should be about ").
16. Transfer breadcrumbs to a large rimmed baking sheet and arrange in an even layer.
17. Drizzle with remaining cup oil and toss to evenly coat.
18. Bake until deep golden brown, minutes.
19. Remove from oven and finely grate remaining 3 garlic cloves over breadcrumbs; toss to incorporate.
20. Add fennel fronds (you want to have about cup) and toss again to combine.
21. Scatter half of breadcrumbs evenly over stew.
22. Transfer pot to oven and cook stew, uncovered, until some of the breadcrumbs have cooked into the stew and stew is bubbling around the edges, minutes.
23. Scatter remaining breadcrumbs over stew and cook until stew is very thick, minutes.
24. Do Ahead: Kielbassoulet can be made 3 days ahead.
25. Let cool; cover and chill.
26. Reheat, uncovered, in a oven.

Ingredients: '2 oz. dried wood ear mushrooms (about 2 cups)', '1 Tbsp. dried goji berries', '4 garlic cloves, finely chopped', '3 Tbsp. toasted sesame oil', '3 Tbsp. (or more) unseasoned rice vinegar', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '2 red finger or Fresno chiles, thinly sliced, seeds removed if desired', '1 cup (packed) coarsely chopped cilantro'
Instructions:
1. Place mushrooms in a large bowl and pour in boiling water to cover.
2. Let soak, adding more hot water if needed, until floppy but still sturdy, about hours.
3. Meanwhile, place goji berries in a small bowl and pour in cold water to cover.
4. Let soak until just softened, about minutes, then drain.
5. Drain mushrooms, wrap in a clean dish towel, and squeeze out any excess liquid.
6. Remove any woody parts from mushrooms and tear into smaller pieces if large.
7. Wipe out bowl and place mushrooms back into bowl.
8. Add goji berries and toss to combine.
9. Whisk garlic, oil, vinegar, and tsp. salt in a small bowl.
10. Drizzle dressing over mushroom mixture and toss to coat.
11. Let sit minutes, then toss again.
12. Taste and season with more salt andor vinegar if needed.
13. Let sit at room temperature at least 1 hour and up to 6 hours (cover and chill if holding longer than 1 hour).
14. Just before serving, add chiles and cilantro and toss to combine.

Ingredients: '4 medium russet potatoes (about lb.), peeled, cut into 2" pieces', '1 large or 2 medium carrots (about 8 oz.), peeled, cut into 1" pieces', '3 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '3 Tbsp. Pickle Brine Spice Rub', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', ' small red onion, thinly sliced', '4 celery stalks, thinly sliced on a diagonal', '2 tsp. white wine vinegar', '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '3 Tbsp. mayonnaise', '1 tsp. Dijon mustard', '3 cups shredded rotisserie or leftover cooked chicken', '4 kosher dill pickle spears, sliced " thick', ' cup parsley leaves with tender stems'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Toss potatoes and carrots with vegetable oil on a rimmed baking sheet to coat.
3. Sprinkle evenly with spice rub and tsp. salt.
4. Roast until vegetables are deeply browned, tender, and crisp on the exterior, about minutes.
5. Meanwhile, toss onion, celery, vinegar, and remaining 1 tsp. salt in a large bowl to combine.
6. Let sit minutes Meanwhile, whisk olive oil, mayonnaise, and mustard in a small bowl to combine.
7. Mix in chicken; set aside.
8. Add pickles, parsley, and hot roasted potatoes and carrots to bowl with onion mixture and toss to combine.
9. Gently mix in reserved dressed chicken just to distribute.
10. To serve, transfer to a platter or divide among plates.

Recipe name: Tangy Vinegar Chicken With Barberries And Orange
Ingredients: '3 lb. skin-on, bone-in chicken legs (thigh and drumstick) or a mix of thighs and drumsticks', 'Kosher salt', '2 Tbsp. vegetable or other neutral oil', '1 medium onion, finely chopped', '4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', ' cup dried barberries or dried tart cherries, rinsed', ' tsp. ground turmeric', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', 'A cup white wine vinegar', '1 medium orange, halved'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Pat chicken dry with paper towels, then season all over with salt.
3. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
4. Cook chicken, skin side down, until golden brown underneath, minutes.
5. Transfer chicken to a plate, leaving any fat in the pan.
6. Remove pan from heat and let fat cool down a little (about 3 minutes should do it).
7. Return pan to medium heat and add onion and garlic.
8. Cook, stirring often and adding a splash of water if needed to keep onion from getting too dark, until onion is softened and golden brown, 6A8 minutes.
9. Stir in barberries, turmeric, and red pepper flakes and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about seconds.
10. Pour in vinegar and 1 cup water, season lightly with salt, and bring to a simmer.
11. Arrange chicken, skin side up, in sauce, then nestle orange halves, cut side down, around chicken.
12. Transfer skillet to oven and bake, uncovered, until sauce is reduced and chicken is cooked through, minutes (or minutes for separated things and drumsticks).
13. Transfer chicken to a platter.
14. Spoon sauce over, then squeeze juice from orange halves on top.

Ingredients: '1 cup coarsely chopped pecans', 'A cup Savory-to-Sweet Coffee Spice Mix', '6 Tbsp. ( g) light brown sugar', '3 Tbsp. ( g) all-purpose flour', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted', 'Nonstick cooking oil spray or room-temperature unsalted butter (for pan)', '2 cups ( g) all-purpose flour', ' tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. baking soda', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', ' cups ( g) granulated sugar', ' cup ( sticks) unsalted butter, room temperature', '3 large eggs, room temperature', ' cup sour cream, room temperature', '2 tsp. vanilla extract', ' cup ( g) powdered sugar', '1 tsp. (or more) orange juice'
Instructions:
1. Whisk pecans, spice mixture, brown sugar, flour, and salt in a small bowl to combine.
2. Drizzle in butter and mix until clumps form.
3. Set aside.
4. Preheat an oven to .
5. Lightly coat an 8A12x4A12" loaf pan with nonstick spray.
6. Line with parchment paper, leaving overhang on long sides.
7. Press paper into pan, then turn over, so greased side is facing up.
8. Whisk flour, baking soda, baking powder, and tsp. salt in a medium bowl to combine.
9. Beat sugar and butter in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with paddle attachment on medium-high speed, scraping down sides of bowl halfway through, until very light and fluffy, 6A8 minutes.
10. Reduce speed to medium.
11. With motor running, add eggs one at a time, beating to incorporate and scraping down sides of bowl after each addition.
12. Reduce speed to low; add half of dry ingredients and mix just to combine.
13. Add sour cream and vanilla and beat to combine.
14. Add remaining dry ingredients and beat until incorporated.
15. Scrape half of batter into prepared pan.
16. Top with streusel (yes, it will look like a lot, but use all of it!), then scrape remaining batter over.
17. Starting at a short side of the pan and working your way to the other short side, drag a butter knife back and forth from one long side to the other through batter to marble streusel; smooth surface.
18. Bake cake until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, minutes.
19. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let cake cool in pan minutes.
20. Run a small knife around edges of cake to loosen if needed, then, using parchment paper, lift out of pan onto rack.
21. Peel away parchment.
22. Let cake cool completely.
23. Combine powdered sugar and a pinch of salt in a small bowl, then mix in orange juice, adding up to a teaspoonful more if needed, until you have a thick but pourable icing.
24. Drizzle over cake and let sit until icing is set before serving, about minutes.
25. Do ahead: Cake can be baked 3 days ahead.
26. Store airtight at room temperature.

Recipe name: Scallop Rice Bowls With Crunchy Spice Oil
Ingredients: '1 lb. dry sea scallops, side muscles removed', '1 tsp. kosher salt, divided', '1 tsp. finely grated peeled ginger', '3 Tbsp. vegetable oil, divided', '2 bunches Tuscan kale, ribs and stems removed', '1 avocado, sliced', 'Steamed rice, Crunchy Spice Oil, lemon wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Pat scallops dry, then season on all sides with tsp. salt.
2. Heat ginger and 1 Tbsp. oil in a large high-sided skillet or medium Dutch oven over medium until oil is hot.
3. Add kale and cup water and toss to coat.
4. Cook until kale is wilted and water is evaporated, 5A7 minutes.
5. Season with remaining tsp. salt.
6. Meanwhile, heat remaining 2 Tbsp oil in large nonstick or cast-iron skillet over medium-high until shimmering.
7. Cook scallops until golden brown and firm to the touch, about 3 minutes per side.
8. Divide scallops among shallow bowls or plates; arrange kale, avocado, and some rice next to scallops.
9. Drizzle with spice oil and serve with lemons for squeezing over.

Recipe name: Coconut Cod Chowder With Seasoned Oyster Crackers
Ingredients: ' lb. skinless, boneless cod or halibut fillets', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided, plus more', '5 Tbsp. ghee or unsalted butter, divided', '2 medium leeks, white and pale green parts only, halved lengthwise, thinly sliced crosswise', '6 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '1 (3") piece ginger, peeled, cut into matchsticks', ' tsp. ground turmeric', ' tsp. ground coriander', ' tsp. ground cardamom', '1 serrano chile, thinly sliced', ' oz. baby Yukon Gold or red potatoes, halved', '2 (.5-oz.) cans unsweetened coconut milk', '2 celery stalks, thinly sliced, plus leaves for serving (optional)', ' cups oyster crackers', '1 tsp. paprika', '2 limes, divided'
Instructions:
1. Season cod all over with tsp. salt; set aside.
2. Heat 3 Tbsp. ghee in a medium Dutch oven over medium.
3. Cook leeks, garlic, and ginger, stirring occasionally, until softened but not browned, about 3 minutes.
4. Add turmeric, coriander, and cardamom and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about seconds.
5. Add chile, potatoes, coconut milk, 1 tsp. salt, and cup water; bring to a boil (still over medium).
6. Immediately reduce heat so soup is at a bare simmer.
7. Cover, leaving lid slightly askew, and cook until potatoes are barely fork-tender, minutes.
8. Carefully nestle reserved fish into pot, spoon some broth over, and cover, leaving lid slightly askew.
9. Cook at a bare simmer 4 minutes, then stir in celery.
10. Cook until fish is opaque and flakes easily, about 5 minutes.
11. Taste chowder and season with more salt if needed.
12. Meanwhile, heat remaining 2 Tbsp. ghee in a small skillet over medium-low.
13. Add crackers and stir well to coat.
14. Cook, stirring often, until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
15. Remove from heat and add paprika; toss to evenly coat.
16. Slice 1 lime in half and squeeze juice into chowder.
17. Taste and season with more salt if needed.
18. Ladle into bowls, breaking fish into large pieces.
19. Scatter celery leaves over chowder if using and top with crackers.
20. Slice remaining lime into wedges and serve alongside.

Ingredients: '3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '2 oz. thinly sliced pancetta (Italian bacon), chopped', '1 medium onion, finely chopped', '4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '1 -oz. can whole peeled tomatoes', ' littleneck clams, scrubbed', '4 oz. ditalini (about 1 cup)', 'Handful of torn basil leaves', 'Country-style bread (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Cook oil and pancetta in a medium Dutch oven over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until pancetta begins to crisp, about 5 minutes.
2. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened but not browned, 6A8 minutes.
3. Add garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and fragrant, about 5 minutes.
4. Stir in red pepper flakes.
5. Add tomatoes, crushing with your hands as you go; increase heat to medium-high.
6. Bring to a simmer and cook, stirring often, until tomato liquid is reduced by half and tomatoes take on a jammy consistency, minutes.
7. Add clams and 2 cups water.
8. Cover pot and cook, stirring occasionally and reducing heat as needed to maintain a simmer, until clams begin to open, minutes.
9. Uncover pot and transfer opened clams with a slotted spoon to a plate.
10. Re-cover pot and continue cooking clams until they open, up to minutes longer; discard any clams that havenAt opened by this time.
11. Add ditalini to pot and cook, stirring often (pasta will want to settle and stick to the bottom of the pot), until al dente, minutes.
12. Add clams back to pot.
13. Ladle clams and sauce among bowls; top with basil.
14. Serve with bread alongside for dipping.

Recipe name: Tamarind-Glazed Black Bass With Coconut-Herb Salad
Ingredients: ' cup unsweetened shredded coconut', '3 Tbsp. virgin coconut oil or a neutral oil (such as grapeseed or vegetable)', '2 medium shallots, thinly sliced into rings', 'Kosher salt', '1 (2") piece ginger, scrubbed, thinly sliced lengthwise', '4 garlic cloves, crushed', '1 Tbsp. double-concentrated tomato paste', ' cup tamarind concentrate', '2 Tbsp. honey', '1 Tbsp. low-sodium soy sauce', '2 (-lb.) head-on whole black sea bass or red snapper, cleaned', '3 Thai chiles or 2 serrano chiles, thinly sliced', '1 cup mint leaves, torn if large', 'Lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Toast coconut in a dry medium saucepan over medium heat, stirring often, until golden brown, 5A7 minutes.
2. Transfer to a small bowl.
3. Wipe out saucepan.
4. Combine oil and shallots in saucepan and set over medium-high heat.
5. Cook, stirring often, until shallots are golden brown and crisp, minutes.
6. Using a slotted spoon, transfer shallots to paper towels to drain; immediately season with salt.
7. Reduce heat to medium and add ginger and garlic to saucepan.
8. Cook, stirring occasionally, until browned in spots, about 4 minutes.
9. Add tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, until paste splits and color darkens slightly, about 2 minutes.
10. Add tamarind concentrate, honey, and soy sauce and cook, stirring constantly, until glaze is smooth and just beginning to bubble, about 1 minute.
11. Remove pan from heat; taste glaze and season with salt.
12. Place a rack in upper third of oven; preheat to .
13. Place 1 fish on a cutting board and thoroughly pat dry inside and out with paper towels.
14. Using a sharp knife, make slashes crosswise on a diagonal about every 2" along length of body (make sure to cut all the way down to the bones as this will help the fish cook evenly).
15. Turn fish over and repeat process on the other side.
16. Transfer fish to a parchment-lined baking sheet, then repeat with remaining fish.
17. Season both fish all over with salt.
18. Brush both sides with about half of the glaze, leaving the garlic and ginger behind.
19. Roast fish, brushing with remaining glaze halfway through, until glaze is sticky and flesh flakes easily with a fork, minutes.
20. Arrange fish on a large platter or 2 smaller platters and scatter toasted coconut, crispy shallots, chiles, and mint evenly on top.
21. Serve with lime wedges alongside for squeezing over.

Ingredients: '1 (2-lb.) piece skinless, boneless pork shoulder (Boston butt)', '7 guajillo chiles, seeds removed', '3 morita chiles, seeds removed', '4 garlic cloves', 'A cup fresh orange juice', ' cup fresh lime juice', '3 Tbsp. achiote (annatto) paste', '1 Tbsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt', '8 (6"-diameter) corn tortillas', '4 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', ' oz. quesillo (Oaxaca cheese), coarsely grated', 'Chopped white onion, coarsely chopped cilantro, and lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Place pork shoulder on a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet and freeze, uncovered, until almost completely frozen, about 2 hours.
2. Using a very sharp knife, thinly slice pork (shoot for about A" thick).
3. Transfer to a large bowl.
4. Meanwhile, bring guajillo and morita chiles and 1 cup water to a boil in a medium saucepan.
5. Cover, remove from heat, and let sit minutes to allow chiles to soften.
6. Transfer chiles and soaking liquid to a blender.
7. Add garlic, orange juice, lime juice, achiote paste, and salt; purAe until smooth.
8. Pour over pork and toss to coat.
9. Cover and let sit at room temperature 2 hours, or chill up to 6 hours.
10. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
11. Evenly space out tortillas on a baking sheet and bake until lightly toasted and very crisp (edges will curl and pucker), minutes.
12. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over high until smoking.
13. Spread about one-fourth of pork across skillet in a single layer; cook, undisturbed, until browned underneath, about 2 minutes.
14. Turn over and cook, undisturbed, until browned underneath and cooked through, about 2 minutes.
15. Transfer to a platter and wipe out skillet.
16. Working in 3 batches, repeat process with remaining pork and 3 Tbsp. oil.
17. Increase oven temperature to .
18. Divide pork among tostadas and top with quesillo; bake until cheese is melted, minutes.
19. Top with onion and cilantro and serve with lime wedges.

Recipe name: Goat Birria Tacos With Cucumber Pico De Gallo
Ingredients: '1 (-lb.) bone-in leg of goat or lamb, untrimmed', 'A cup Diamond Crystal or 3 Tbsp. Morton kosher salt, plus more', '1 large white onion, chopped', ' garlic cloves, peeled', '3 large guajillo chiles, seeds removed', '1 large ancho chile, seeds removed', '2 dried chiles de Arbol', '1 (3") piece ginger, peeled', '2 whole cloves', '1 (.5-oz.) can diced fire-roasted tomatoes', '1 Tbsp. black peppercorns', '1 Tbsp. dried oregano, preferably Mexican', '1 tsp. ground cumin', '5 Persian cucumbers, cut into (") pieces', ' medium red onion, cut into (") pieces', 'A cup fresh lime juice', ' cup chopped basil', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '1A4 habanero chiles, seeds removed, finely chopped', ' (6"-diameter) corn tortillas, warmed'
Instructions:
1. Rub goat leg with A cup Diamond Crystal or 3 Tbsp.
2. Morton kosher salt, working into flesh and fat.
3. Wrap tightly in parchment or wax paper and chill at least 3 hours and up to 3 days.
4. Place a rack in lower third of oven; preheat to .
5. Evenly scatter onion, garlic, guajillo chiles, ancho chile, chiles de Arbol, ginger, cloves, tomatoes, peppercorns, oregano, and cumin across a large roasting pan and place a roasting rack on top.
6. Unwrap goat leg and set on rack.
7. Pour 4 cups water into pan and cover tightly with foil.
8. Bake until meat is pulling away from the bone and shreds easily with a fork, hours.
9. Let leg sit (still covered) until cool enough to handle (about 1 hour).
10. Shred meat into small pieces and place in a large bowl; discard bones.
11. Remove ginger from pan; discard.
12. Working in batches if needed, purAe liquid and aromatics left in pan in a blender until smooth.
13. Pour half of consomA over meat and mix to coat.
14. Taste and season with more salt if needed.
15. Cover and set aside.
16. Pour remaining consomA into a medium bowl and set aside for serving.
17. Do Ahead: Meat can be prepared 3 days ahead.
18. Chill meat and consomA.
19. Toss cucumbers, onion, lime juice, basil, and salt in a medium bowl.
20. Add 1A4 chiles, depending on their heat and how spicy you like things, and let sit minutes.
21. Top warm tortillas with meat, then salsa.
22. Serve with consomA for dipping.

Recipe name: Stuffed Cabbage With Lemony Rice And Sumac
Ingredients: ' large savoy or green cabbage leaves (from 1 large head)', 'Kosher salt', ' cup long-grain white rice (such as basmati or jasmine), rinsed', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling', '1 large onion, finely chopped', ' cup pine nuts', '1 cup finely chopped mixed tender herbs (such as parsley, mint, dill, andor tarragon)', 'A cup chopped golden or brown raisins', '2 Tbsp. sumac, plus more for serving', '1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', '1 large egg, beaten to blend', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter', 'Sour cream (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Line a baking sheet with a clean kitchen towel or a few layers of paper towels; set aside.
2. Working in batches, cook cabbage leaves in a large pot of boiling generously salted water until bright green and pliable, about 2 minutes per batch.
3. Transfer leaves to a bowl of ice water to cool; reserve pot of water for cooking rice.
4. Transfer cabbage leaves to prepared baking sheet and let drain.
5. Return water in pot to a boil and cook rice, stirring often, until grains swell and rise to the surface, 3A6 minutes (depending on quality of rice).
6. Bite into a few grains to test; they should be al dente (rice will finish cooking when baked inside the cabbage).
7. Drain rice and rinse under cold running water to stop it from cooking further.
8. Drain again and transfer to a large bowl.
9. Wipe out pot.
10. Pour in cup oil and set pot over medium heat.
11. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and golden, 7A9 minutes.
12. Add pine nuts and cook, stirring often, until nuts smell toasty and have slightly darkened in color and onion is almost jammy, about 5 minutes.
13. Mix in herbs, raisins, and 2 Tbsp. sumac and cook, still stirring, until herbs have slightly darkened in color and are very fragrant, about 2 minutes.
14. Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice; let cool 5 minutes.
15. Add onion mixture and egg to rice and mix well; season generously with salt and pepper.
16. Wipe out pot; reserve.
17. Working with 1 cabbage leaf at a time, cut out the thickest part of rib by making a thin V-shape; discard.
18. Place 3 heaping Tbsp. filling in the center, running crosswise across leaf.
19. Starting at the base where you cut the V, fold notched side of leaf up and over filling, then fold in sides and roll up leaf like a burrito.
20. Arrange cabbage rolls, seam side down, in a single layer in reserved pot.
21. Add butter and cup water and bring to a simmer over medium-high heat.
22. Reduce heat to low, cover pot, and steam rolls until filling is cooked through and leaves are tender, minutes.
23. Divide cabbage rolls among plates; drizzle with oil and sprinkle with sumac and pepper.
24. Serve with sour cream.

Ingredients: '4 Tbsp. coriander seeds', '6 Tbsp. vinegar powder', '4 Tbsp. dried dill', '2 Tbsp. kosher salt', '4 tsp. celery seeds', '1 tsp. mustard powder', 'A spice mill or mortar and pestle'
Instructions:
1. Toast coriander seeds in a small skillet over medium-high heat, tossing occasionally, until fragrant and slightly darkened in color, about 2 minutes.
2. Transfer to a spice mill or mortar and pestle and let cool; coarsely grind.
3. Transfer to an airtight container and mix in vinegar powder, dried dill, salt, celery seeds, and mustard powder; cover.
4. Do ahead: Spice mix can be made 3 months ahead.
5. Store at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 cup sunflower or other neutral oil', '6 garlic cloves, finely chopped', ' cup sesame seeds', '2 Tbsp. black mustard seeds', '4 tsp. cumin seeds', '1 Tbsp. pink peppercorns, lightly crushed', '1 Tbsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '2 tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. paprika', '1 tsp. pure maple syrup'
Instructions:
1. Heat oil, garlic, sesame seeds, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, pink peppercorns, and red pepper flakes in a small saucepan over medium-high until oil is sizzling and bubbling, about 2 minutes.
2. Immediately reduce heat to low and continue to cook, stirring constantly, until garlic is golden brown and spices are fragrant, about 5 minutes.
3. Remove from heat and stir in salt, paprika, and maple syrup.
4. Let cool.
5. Transfer to an airtight container.
6. Do ahead: Spice oil can be made 1 month ahead.
7. Cover and chill.

Ingredients: '3 Tbsp. bee pollen powder or 3 Tbsp. bee pollen granules, ground in a spice mill', '3 Tbsp. finely ground coffee (not instant)', '2 Tbsp. granulated dried orange peel', '2 Tbsp. ground cinnamon', '2 tsp. ground cardamom', '2 tsp. ground fennel seeds or 1A tsp. fennel seeds, ground in a spice mill'
Instructions:
1. Mix together bee pollen, coffee, orange peel, cinnamon, cardamom, and fennel in an airtight container and cover.
2. Do ahead: Spice mix can be made 1 month ahead.
3. Store at room temperature.

Ingredients: ' cups water', '8 garlic cloves', '2-inch piece fresh ginger, peeled and sliced', '4 whole star anise', '4 bay leaves', '1 tablespoon black peppercorns', '1 (4-pound) chicken, cut into 8 pieces', '4 carrots', '4 celery stalks', '2 unpeeled white or red onions, halved', 'Salt, for seasoning', 'Chopped parsley leaves and lemon wedges, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Wipe the grill grates with oil to prevent sticking.
2. Build a two-zone fire.
3. Your high-heat zone should have embers 1 to 2 inches from the cooking surface, with occasional flames licking it.
4. To create your medium-heat zone, nudge the embers 2 to 3 inches lower than that.
5. Fill a stockpot with the water, garlic, ginger, star anise, bay leaves, and peppercorns and place over medium heat.
6. Place the chicken, carrots, celery, and onions over high heat and cook, turning occasionally, until well charred all over, about minutes total.
7. When each ingredient is done, add it to the pot (itAs important that each ingredient be very well brownedAalmost burntAbefore adding to the pot).
8. Cook the stock for at least 3 hours, adding additional water if necessary to keep the meat and vegetables covered.
9. Season with salt at the end.
10. Remove the pot from heat.
11. Remove the chicken pieces and shred the meat, discarding the skin and bones.
12. Strain the stock, discarding solids.
13. To serve, place a pile of shredded chicken into each bowl and cover with hot stock.
14. Pass the parsley and lemon wedges separately.
15. The stock can be stored in a covered container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week or in the freezer for up to 6 months.

Ingredients: '8 lemons, halved', ' sprigs of thyme', 'Ice cubes', '4 cups ice water', '1 1A2 cups vodka', '1A2 cup agave or simple syrup (see Note)'
Instructions:
1. Wipe the grill grates with oil to prevent sticking.
2. Build a high-heat fire.
3. Your high-heat zone should have embers 1 to 2 inches from the cooking surface, with occasional flames licking it.
4. Place the lemons, cut side down, over high heat until they are charred and soft, about 3 minutes.
5. While the lemons char, using tongs, hold 8 of the thyme sprigs over high heat just until they start to color and give off wisps of smoke.
6. Add the remaining 8 thyme sprigs to a cocktail shaker, squeeze in the lemon juice, and add a couple ice cubes.
7. Shake very well and strain into a pitcher.
8. Add the ice water, vodka, and agave, and stir until the agave is dissolved.
9. Serve the drinks in ice-filled glasses garnished with a charred thyme sprig.

Recipe name: Fragrant Mixed Herb And Flatbread Salad Domaaj
Ingredients: '4-5 pieces Persian flatbread (or toasted tortillas or pitta bread)', ' cup walnuts, roughly chopped', 'Scant cup feta, crumbled', 'I small bunch mint, roughly chopped', '1 small bunch basil, roughly chopped', '1 small bunch tarragon, roughly chopped', '3 tbsp. pomegranate seeds, to garnish', '2 tbsp. balsamic vinegar', '3 tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', ' tsp. golpar (optional)', ' tsp. sea salt', ' tsp. black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Using a pair of scissors, cut the flatbread into small jagged pieces and place them in a large bowl.
2. Toast the walnuts in a small pan over a medium heat for 2 minutes.
3. Add them to the bowl, along with the crumbled cheese and chopped herbs.
4. To make the dressing, whisk the balsamic vinegar, olive oil and golpar (if you are using it) with the salt and pepper.
5. Drizzle the dressing over the salad and then get your hands in there, giving the whole thing a good stir to evenly distribute it.
6. Leave the salad for minutes for the flavors to soak into the bread, then taste and adjust the seasoning.
7. Garnish with a generous sprinkling of pomegranate seeds just before serving.

Recipe name: Torshi Tareh Persian Sour Herb Stew With Marbled Eggs
Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. basmati rice, rinsed', '1 large bunch cilantro', '1 large bunch dill', '1 bunch parsley', '1 small onion, thinly sliced', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', '8 garlic cloves, finely chopped', ' tsp. ground turmeric', ' oz. frozen chopped spinach', ' cup dried cilantro', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', ' cup fresh lime juice (from about 4 limes)', '6 large eggs or 6 large egg whites', 'Cooked rice and smoked white fish (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Combine rice with 3 cups water in a small saucepan.
2. Cover and bring to a boil.
3. Remove from heat; set aside.
4. Meanwhile, tear cilantro, dill, and parsley (including stems) with your hands.
5. Working in batches if needed, stuff into a food processor and process until finely chopped.
6. Combine onion and oil in a cold large high-sided skillet with a lid.
7. Place over medium-high heat and cook, stirring and shaking pan occasionally, until onion is dark brown around the edges, 6A8 minutes.
8. Add garlic and cook, stirring, until garlic is just barely golden, about 1 minute.
9. Add turmeric and cook, stirring, seconds.
10. Add herbs and spinach (no need to defrost or drain) and cook, stirring often, until herbs are wilted and spinach is thoroughly defrosted, about 2 minutes.
11. Add dried cilantro, pepper, tsp. salt, and reserved parcooked rice along with all of the water.
12. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook over medium-low heat until flavors come together, about minutes.
13. Remove lid and continue to cook spinach mixture until greens are glossy and liquid is almost completely evaporated (dragging a rubber spatula or wooden spoon through the mixture should leave a trail with little to no liquid seeping back in), 6A8 minutes.
14. Stir in lime juice; taste and season with more salt if needed.
15. Make 6 divots in mixture and crack an egg into each one (or, if making with egg whites only, separate out egg yolks first and reserve for another use).
16. Working quickly, drag the tip of an offset spatula or butter knife through each egg once or twice to break up the yolks and encourage wisps of egg white to spread throughout the mixture (you want a marbled effectAdonAt scramble).
17. Cover pan and simmer until eggs are cooked to your liking, about 3 minutes for jammy.
18. Serve with rice and smoked fish alongside if desired.

Recipe name: Efo Riro With Kale And Whitefish
Ingredients: '1 medium onion, quartered through root end', '3 plum tomatoes, quartered', '1 red bell pepper, ribs and seeds removed, cut into large pieces', '1 Scotch bonnet or habanero chile', '4 garlic cloves', '2 Tbsp. dried bitter leaf', '4 skinless tilapia or other whitefish fillets (about 1Alb. total), cut into 2" pieces', '4 tsp. Old Bay seasoning', 'Vegetable oil (for frying; about 1 cup)', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '2 Tbsp. double-concentrated tomato paste', '2 tsp. dried thyme', ' tsp. garam masala', ' tsp. ground turmeric', '1 cube Maggi shrimp or chicken bouillon', '3 Tbsp. dried shrimp, rinsed', '4 tsp. powdered irAo (dawadawa; fermented locust beans)', '1 lb. (2 bunches) kale, ribs and stems removed, leaves torn into 2" pieces', 'Steamed puna yams or rice (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. PurAe onion, tomatoes, bell pepper, chile, and garlic in a blender, adding up to cup water as needed, until smooth.
2. Set purAe aside.
3. Place bitter leaf in a small bowl and pour in hot water to cover.
4. Let sit until softened, about minutes.
5. Drain and finely chop.
6. Meanwhile, toss fish with Old Bay seasoning in a large bowl to evenly coat.
7. Pour vegetable oil into a large deep saucepan or medium Dutch oven to come " up sides and heat over medium-high until shimmering.
8. Working in batches, arrange fish in a single layer and fry, turning halfway through, until golden, 6A8 minutes.
9. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to paper towels to drain.
10. Pour oil out from pan and wipe out.
11. Heat olive oil in same saucepan over medium-high.
12. Add tomato paste, thyme, garam masala, and turmeric.
13. Cook, stirring, until spices are fragrant and tomato paste is slightly darkened in color, about 1 minute.
14. Stir in reserved purAe, scraping up any browned bits, then add bitter leaf, shrimp cube, dried shrimp, and irAo.
15. Bring to a boil.
16. Reduce heat so stew is at a simmer and add kale by the handful, stir to wilt slightly before adding more.
17. Once all of the kale has been added, cook (without stirring) until tender, about 5 minutes.
18. Gently fold in fried fish, being careful not to break up too much.
19. Serve with yams or rice alongside.

Ingredients: '2 lb. russet potatoes, peeled, cut into 2" pieces', '1 tsp. kosher salt, divided, plus more', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, divided', '1 head of Savoy cabbage', '3 thick-cut bacon slices (about 3 oz.), finely chopped', ' onion, thinly sliced', '2 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar', '1 tsp. thyme leaves', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', '2 large eggs', ' cup heavy cream', ' cups coarsely shredded sharp cheddar, preferably Irish'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Place potatoes in a medium pot, pour in cold water to cover, and season with salt.
3. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to low and cook until potatoes are fork-tender, minutes.
4. Drain and return potatoes to pot; add 2 Tbsp. butter.
5. Meanwhile, trim very base of cabbage, then pull off outer leaves, being careful not to tear them, until you have total.
6. Cut out any tough ribs; discard.
7. Cook leaves in a large pot of boiling salted water until tender, about 8 minutes.
8. Transfer to a bowl of ice water and let cool.
9. Transfer to clean kitchen towels and pat dry.
10. Thinly slice remaining cabbage.
11. Cook bacon in a "-diameter ovenproof skillet, preferably cast-iron, over medium, stirring occasionally, until bacon is beginning to crisp, about 6 minutes.
12. Add sliced cabbage and onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender and starting to caramelize (vegetables will be golden brown), minutes.
13. Add vinegar, scraping up browned bits, then mix in thyme, pepper, and tsp. salt.
14. Remove from heat.
15. Mash potatoes with a potato masher until soft.
16. Whisk eggs and cream in a small bowl, then add to potatoes and mash to combine.
17. Add bacon mixture, cheese, and tsp. salt and mix well.
18. Taste and add more salt if needed.
19. Wipe out and reserve skillet.
20. Break up 1 Tbsp. butter into smaller pieces and dot around reserved skillet.
21. Line with a single layer of cabbage leaves, overlapping slightly and allowing them to hang over edges of skillet.
22. Scrape in potato mixture and spread out to the edges, flattening out evenly with a rubber spatula.
23. Fold overhanging leaves up and over filling and top with more cabbage leaves, overlapping them slightly to create a single layer.
24. Break up remaining 1 Tbsp. butter and dot over top.
25. Roast cabbage pie until lightly browned, minutes.
26. Let cool minutes before cutting into wedges.

Recipe name: Yogurt And Persian Shallot Dip Mast-O Musir
Ingredients: '1 cups dried musir (Persian shallots, see note)', '4 cups (960g) plain yogurt', '1 teaspoon sea salt', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', '1 cup (85g) shredded fresh mint, or 1 tablespoon dried mint', '1 teaspoon crushed dried rose petals (see note)', '1 tablespoon fresh mint leaves'
Instructions:
1. Soak the musir in water for 3 to hours in the fridge.
2. Drain, rinse in cold water, and pat dry.
3. Inspect the soaked musir, cutting out any stems that remain hard after soaking, and chop finely.
4. In a mixing bowl, combine the chopped musir with the yogurt, salt, pepper, and mint.
5. Mix well.
6. Add the garnish if you like and serve as an appetizer or an accompaniment.

Recipe name: Persian Rice With Fava Beans And Dill Baqala Polow
Ingredients: '2 cups (400g) white basmati rice', '3 lbs (1.3kg) fresh fava beans, or 1 lb (450g) frozen fava beans', ' cup (120ml) olive oil', '1 (4-inch10cm) cinnamon stick', '1 leek (white and green parts), finely chopped', '4 cloves garlic, peeled and crushed', '2 teaspoons salt', ' teaspoon freshly ground pepper', ' teaspoon turmeric', '2 tablespoons ground cardamom', '2 tablespoons rose water', '3 cups (700ml) water', '3 cups (250g) coarsely chopped dill weed', ' teaspoon ground saffron dissolved in 2 tablespoons rose water', 'Fried eggs and Yogurt and Persian Shallot Dip for serving (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Wash the rice by placing it in a large container and covering it with water.
2. Agitate gently with your hand, then pour off the water.
3. Repeat 5 times until the water is no longer cloudy.
4. Drain, using a fine-mesh colander, and set aside.
5. If using fresh fava beans in the pod, shell and remove second skins.
6. If using frozen favas with second skins removed, place in a colander and rinse thoroughly.
7. Set aside.
8. Heat cup (60ml) oil in a large, non-stick pot over medium-high heat until very hot.
9. Add the cinnamon stick, leek, and garlic, and stir-fry for 3 to 5 minutes, or until the leek is wilted.
10. Add the rice, salt, pepper, turmeric, cardamom, and rose water, and stir-fry for another 1 minute.
11. Add the water, tip in the skinned fava beans and bring back to a boil, stirring gently twice with a wooden spoon, to loosen any grains that may have stuck to the bottom of the pot.
12. Cover firmly with a lid to prevent any steam from escaping.
13. Reduce heat to medium and cook for to minutes, or until all the water has been absorbed.
14. Add the dill and fluff using 2 forks.
15. Drizzle the remaining oil and the saffron-infused rose water over the rice.
16. Cover again, reduce heat to low, and cook for another minutes.
17. Remove the pot from heat and allow to cool, still covered, for 5 minutes.
18. Serve with fried eggs and Yogurt and Persian Shallot Dip or alongside roasted lamb or fish.

Recipe name: Chile And Gingerafried Tofu Salad With Kale
Ingredients: '1 package ( to ounces) firm tofu, drained, patted dry, and cut into 8 pieces', 'A cup soy sauce', ' cup hot chile sauce, such as Sriracha or sambal oelek chile sauce', '1 serrano chile, halved, seeded if desired', 'A cup chopped fresh cilantro leaves', '6 garlic cloves, coarsely chopped', '1 tablespoon coarsely chopped peeled fresh ginger', 'Peanut oil, for frying', ' cup cornstarch', '5 ounces baby kale or spinach', '2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil', 'Fine sea salt to taste', 'Sliced scallions (white and green parts), for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Arrange the tofu on a plate or baking sheet.
2. In a blender, combine the soy sauce, chile sauce, chile halves, cilantro, garlic, and ginger; puree until smooth.
3. Using a pastry brush, coat the tofu on both sides with two-thirds of the chile mixture (reserve the rest for serving).
4. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour and up to 6 hours.
5. Fill a -inch skillet with inch of peanut oil and heat it over medium-high heat.
6. Dip the slabs of tofu in the cornstarch, coating both sides, and tap off the excess.
7. Working in batches, add the tofu to the hot oil and cook, turning it over once, until it is golden brown, 1 to 2 minutes per side.
8. Transfer the tofu to a paper-towel-lined plate to drain off the excess oil.
9. In a large bowl, toss the kale with the olive oil and fine sea salt to taste.
10. Add the tofu and toss.
11. Some of the kale will wilt, which is what you want.
12. Serve hot, topped with sliced scallions and cilantro leaves, and with the reserved chile marinade alongside for drizzling.

Recipe name: Spicy Sweet-And-Sour Salmon With Dates Qaliyeh-E Khorma
Ingredients: ' cup coriander seeds', '1 large red onion (about 1lb), diced into A" squares', '2 teaspoons fine sea salt', ' Persian cucumbers, " to A" dice', ' cup apple cider vinegar', '1 teaspoons fine sea salt', ' teaspoon black pepper', '1 teaspoon ground turmeric', '1 tablespoon all-purpose flour', '4A6 thick skinless salmon fillets', ' cup olive oil, divided', '5 cloves garlic, finely grated', '1 cup apple cider vinegar', '2 tablespoons date molasses', '1 scant teaspoon cayenne', '1 tablespoon dried fenugreek leaves', '5 medjool dates, pitted and halved', '1 cup fresh herbs such as cilantro leaves, basil leaves, or chopped spring onions, for serving', 'Kateh, for serving (optional)'
Instructions:
1. In a medium-sized skillet over low heat, toast the coriander seeds, shaking the skillet constantly until the aroma rises.
2. Remove from heat and set aside.
3. In a large mixing bowl, place the onion, salt, and coriander seeds, and toss.
