4. Add the cucumber and pour the vinegar over it.
5. Toss well and set aside.
6. Do Ahead: This pickle can be stored in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days.
7. In a small bowl, mix together all the dusting ingredients and set aside.
8. Rinse the fish and pat dry.
9. Place fillets on a sheet pan lined with parchment paper.
10. Dust both sides of the fish fillets and arrange on the sheet pan.
11. Cover and keep cool until ready to cook.
12. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in wide, non-stick skillet a over high heat until very hot, but not smoking.
13. Sear the fish until brown on both sides (about 1 minute on each side).
14. Remove the fish from heat and set aside.
15. In the same pan, add 2 tablespoons oil, reduce heat to medium heat, add the garlic and stir-fry for 1 to 2 minutes until golden brown.
16. Add the vinegar, molasses, cayenne, and fenugreek leaves, and give it a stir.
17. Return the fish to the pan and simmer for to minutes over low heat until the fish is tender and the sauce has thickened.
18. Adjust seasoning to taste.
19. Add the dates, cover and keep warm until ready to serve.
20. Serve over rice, with a bowl of fresh herbs and the onion and cucumber pickle

Ingredients: '3 cups (600g) white basmati rice', '5 cups (1.3l) water', '1 tablespoon fine sea salt', ' cup (60ml) olive oil'
Instructions:
1. Wash the rice by placing it in a large container and covering it with water.
2. Agitate gently with your hand, then pour off the water.
3. Repeat 5 times until the water is no longer cloudy.
4. Drain, using a fine-mesh colander.
5. If using American long-grain rice, do not wash, and reduce the water to cups instead of cups.
6. In a medium-sized non-stick pot, combine the rice, water, and salt.
7. Gently stir with a wooden spoon to dissolve the salt.
8. Bring to a boil over high heat (this takes about 5 minutes), then reduce heat to medium and simmer, uncovered, for to minutes, or until all the water has been absorbed and the surface of the rice is covered with steam holes.
9. Drizzle the oil over the rice.
10. Cover the pot tightly and steam for minutes over low heat.
11. Keep warm until ready to serve.
12. Just before serving, uncover the rice and fluff gently with a fork.

Recipe name: Pistachio And Pomegranate Meatballs Kufteh-Ye Pesteh-O Anar
Ingredients: '1 large onion, peeled and quartered', '1 cups (180g) raw pistachio kernels', ' cup (15g) fresh plain bread crumbs', '3 cups (250g) chopped fresh parsley', '1 cup (85g) chopped fresh tarragon, or 2 tablespoons dried', '1 cup (85g) chopped fresh cilantro', '1 tablespoon fresh lime juice', '1 teaspoon red pepper flakes', '1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', '1 tablespoon ground cumin', '2 teaspoons fine sea salt', '2 lb (900g) ground dark meat turkey', '1 egg', ' cup olive oil, for brushing meatballs', ' cup (180ml) pomegranate molasses', ' (60ml) cup grape molasses', ' cup (60ml) pomegranate juice', '1 teaspoon salt', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', ' teaspoon red pepper flakes', '1 cup (150g) pomegranate seeds (arils)', 'Lavash, lettuce, and fresh basil for serving (optional)'
Instructions:
1. To make the meatballs: Place all the ingredients, except the ground turkey and egg, in a food processor and pulse until you have a grainy paste.
2. Transfer to a large mixing bowl and add the turkey and egg.
3. Lightly knead with your hands for a few minutes (do not over-mix).
4. Cover and chill in the fridge for at least minutes or up to hours.
5. Preheat the oven to ().
6. Generously oil a wide, non-reactive baking dish, large enough to fit meatballs, (about x in30 x cm) and set aside.
7. Remove the turkey mixture from the fridge and shape into walnut-sized balls (about 1 tablespoons each, using an ice-cream scoop).
8. Place the meatballs in the baking dish and brush well with oil.
9. Bake in the oven for minutes, until bottoms of the meatballs are lightly golden.
10. Meanwhile, in another mixing bowl, combine all the ingredients for the glaze.
11. Spoon the glaze over the meatballs and bake for another 5 minutes to infuse them with the flavor of pomegranate.
12. Garnish with the pomegranate arils and serve warm with the sauce.
13. These meatballs are perfect for wrapping with sprigs of fresh basil in lavash bread or green lettuce.

Recipe name: Sumac Baked Fish With Saffron Quinoa
Ingredients: '3 cups (540g) quinoa', '4 cups (950ml) water', '2 teaspoons fine sea salt', ' cup ( ml) olive oil', ' teaspoon ground saffron', '1 teaspoon ground cardamom', '2 teaspoons fine sea salt', '1 teaspoon freshly ground pepper', '1 teaspoon turmeric', ' cup sumac powder', '4 fillets of salmon, striped bass, or catfish (each in (1cm) thick; about 2 lb (900g) total weight)', '2 tablespoons olive oil combined with 2 tablespoons lime juice', ' cup (55g) shelled walnuts, roughly chopped', '1 cup (85g) basil, fresh, roughly chopped', '1 cup (85g) cilantro, fresh, roughly chopped', '1 cup (85g) mint, fresh, roughly chopped', '2 cloves garlic, peeled and finely chopped', '2 tablespoons lime juice', ' teaspoon fine sea salt', ' teaspoon freshly ground pepper', ' cup (60ml) olive oil'
Instructions:
1. Wash the quinoa by placing it in a fine-mesh colander and rinsing thoroughly.
2. Place all the ingredients in a large pot, stir gently for 1 minute to dissolve the salt, and bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for minutes, without stirring.
4. Fluff the cooked quinoa with a fork and reserve until ready to serve.
5. In a bowl, mix all the ingredients for the sumac rub and set aside.
6. Arrange the fish fillets, skin-side down on a rimmed sheet pan lined with parchment paper.
7. Brush both sides with the olive oil and lime juice mixture.
8. Sprinkle the sumac rub all over the fish.
9. Cover with plastic wrap.
10. Keep chilled in the fridge until ready to cook ( minutes to 1 hour).
11. Shortly before you are ready to serve, preheat the oven to ().
12. Uncover the sheet pan, place it in the oven and bake for to minutes, until fish is cooked through.
13. Meanwhile, mix all the ingredients for the green sauce topping and set aside.
14. Remove the fish from the oven and place 1 tablespoon of the topping on each fillet.
15. Serve with quinoa.

Recipe name: Persian Celery Stew With Mushrooms Khoresh-E Karafs
Ingredients: ' cup (120ml) olive oil', '1 lb (675g) cremini mushrooms, cleaned and thickly sliced', '1 bunch (8 stalks) celery, washed and julienned ( inch6mm-by-2 inch5cm lengths)', '1 medium onion, peeled and thinly sliced', '2 cloves garlic, peeled and thinly sliced', '1 jalapeAo pepper, finely chopped or teaspoon red pepper flakes', '2 teaspoons sea salt', ' teaspoon freshly ground pepper', '3 cups (710ml) water', ' teaspoon ground saffron threads dissolved in 2 tablespoons water (optional)', '3 tablespoons fresh lime juice', '1 cup (80g) chopped fresh parsley', ' cup (40g) chopped fresh mint, or 1 tablespoon dried mint', '3 tablespoons dried fenugreek leaves', 'Persian rice for serving (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Heat the oil in a medium-sized, heavy-bottomed pot over high heat.
2. Add the mushrooms and sautA for 5 minutes.
3. Add the celery, onion, garlic, and jalapeAo and sautA for minutes.
4. Add the salt and pepper, and give it a stir.
5. Add the water and bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat to medium, cover, and simmer for minutes.
7. Add the saffron, lime juice, parsley, mint, and fenugreek, and give it a stir.
8. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for another 5 minutes or until the celery is tender.
9. Adjust seasoning to taste and keep warm until ready to serve.

Recipe name: Jerk Tofu Wrapped In Collard Leaves
Ingredients: '1 cup chopped yellow onion', '3 scallions, thinly sliced', '1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger', '1 tablespoon minced garlic', '2 tablespoons muscovado sugar', '1 tablespoon ground allspice', '1 teaspoon freshly grated nutmeg', '1 tablespoon freshly ground black pepper', 'Pinch of cayenne pepper', '2 Scotch bonnet or habanero chiles, stemmed, seeded, and minced', '2 tablespoons fresh lime juice', '2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar', '6 tablespoons shoyu', ' cup coconut oil', '2 tablespoons minced fresh thyme', 'Fine sea salt', ' cup olive oil', ' large garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '2 garlic cloves, minced', '3 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil', ' teaspoon ground coriander', ' teaspoon coarse sea salt, plus more as needed', '1 cup tightly packed fresh cilantro leaves', '2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice', ' jalapeAo', '2 (-to -ounce) blocks extra-firm tofu', '2 cups Jerk Marinade', '1 tablespoon plus 2 teaspoons fine sea salt, plus more as needed', ' large collard leaves, tough part of the stem removed', '1 cup arrowroot powder', 'Peanut oil, for frying', ' cup Garlic Chips', 'Cilantro Sauce', 'Cilantro leaves, for garnish', 'Crushed peanuts, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Combine all the ingredients except the salt in a food processor, add cup water, and puree until well combined.
2. Season with salt to taste.
3. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator until ready to use, up to 1 week.
4. In a medium skillet, warm the olive oil over low heat.
5. Add the garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until crispy and golden brown, 8 to minutes.
6. Strain the oil through fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, reserving the garlic chips.
7. Use immediately or store in separate airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
8. In a small skillet, combine the garlic, olive oil, coriander, and salt.
9. Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook just until the garlic is fragrant, about 1 minutes.
10. Remove from the heat and let cool.
11. Transfer the oil mixture to a blender.
12. Add the cilantro, lemon juice, jalapeAo, and cup water and blend until smooth.
13. If necessary, season with additional salt to taste.
14. Use immediately or store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
15. First, press your tofu.
16. This procedure extracts excess water, makes the block more uniformly firm, and allows the tofu to absorb the marinade more easily.
17. Just wrap the block of tofu in a clean kitchen towel (or paper towels), place it in a large bowl or a clean kitchen sink, and sit something heavy on top of it (like a -ounce can of tomatoes) for minutes, turning the block over after minutes.
18. Place one block of the tofu on a cutting board.
19. Cut it in half horizontally, then cut vertically down the center to yield four pieces.
20. Repeat with the second block of tofu.
21. Pour the marinade into a large baking dish, adding just enough water to ensure that it is runny, if necessary.
22. Place the tofu in the marinade in one even layer.
23. Cover and refrigerate for 8 hours or overnight, flipping the tofu every minutes for the first 2 hours.
24. Remove the tofu from the marinade, scrape off any excess, and transfer to a large plate.
25. Strain the marinade through a fine-mesh sieve and set aside.
26. In a large pot, bring 2 quarts water to a boil over high heat.
27. Add 1 tablespoon of the salt.
28. Blanch the collard leaves, one at a time, for seconds each, then lay them flat on clean kitchen towels to dry.
29. Combine the arrowroot and the remaining 2 teaAspoons salt in a pie plate.
30. Mix well.
31. Coat each side of the tofu pieces with the arrowroot, shake off any excess arrowroot, and transfer them to a second large plate.
32. Lightly coat the bottom of a large cast-iron skillet with peanut oil and heat over medium-high heat until shimmering.
33. Add half the tofu and fry until golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes.
34. Gently flip each piece with a spatula and fry until golden brown on the second side.
35. Lightly sprinkle both sides of the tofu with salt and transfer to a rack.
36. Repeat to fry the remaining tofu.
37. Wipe the skillet clean.
38. On a clean work surface, arrange two collard leaves lengthwise, overlapping each other by an inch or so, and place one piece of tofu in the center of the leaves.
39. Smear 2 heaping tablespoons of the reserved marinade on top of the tofu, sprinkle a heaping teaspoon of garlic chips on top of the tofu, then fold the leaves around the tofu to create a packet.
40. Repeat with the remaining collard leaves and tofu.
41. In the same skillet, warm 2 tablespoons peanut oil over medium-high heat, tilting the pan to coat it evenly with the oil.
42. Place the packets in the skillet, folded-side up, and cook for about 1 minute.
43. Gently flip each packet with a spatula, cover the skillet with a lid or some aluminum foil, and cook for about 5 minutes to warm through.
44. To serve, spread some cilantro sauce over four plates and place two of the tofu packets on each plate.
45. Garnish with cilantro leaves and peanuts and serve.

Ingredients: '2 tablespoons olive oil, plus more for drizzling', '1 small onion, thinly sliced', '4 garlic cloves, peeled', '2 (.5-ounce) cans butter beans, rinsed', ' cup canned chopped tomatoes', '2 sprigs thyme', 'Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper', '1 cup plus tablespoon water', '1 prepared confit duck leg (5 ounces)', '4 thick slices airy country bread', 'Fresh Italian parsley leaves, chopped, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Heat a medium (-inch) skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add 1 tablespoon of the oil, the onions, and 3 of the garlic cloves.
3. Cook, stirring occasionally, for about 5 minutes, until the onions and garlic start to color.
4. Reduce the heat to medium-low and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, for minutes, or until caramelized.
5. Add the beans, tomatoes, thyme, the remaining 1 tablespoon oil, teaspoon salt, and 1 cup of the water.
6. Turn the heat up to medium- high and bring to a simmer.
7. Smash about one-eighth of the beans against the side of the pan with a fork to thicken the mixture.
8. Cook until thickened, about 5 minutes.
9. Transfer to a bowl, discard the thyme, and smash the garlic cloves.
10. Season with salt and pepper, stir well, and cover with foil to keep warm.
11. Preheat the broiler.
12. Rinse out the pan and return it to the stovetop.
13. Add the confit duck leg skin-side down to the cold pan.
14. Turn the heat to medium-low, add the remaining 1 tablespoon water, cover the pan, and cook for minutes (when the skin softens, scrape it off the duck leg and continue cooking alongside it; this will become the crackling).
15. Remove the lid, turn the duck leg, and continue cooking until the leg is warmed through and starting to crisp up, turning as needed.
16. Continue cooking the duck skin (as if it were a slice of bacon) until nice and crisp, keeping in mind that it will crisp more as it cools (as if it were a slice of bacon), about 3 minutes longer.
17. Drain on a paper towel.
18. Toast the bread on both sides under the broiler until it is crisp on the outside and still soft inside.
19. Rub each slice generously with the remaining garlic clove and top each slice with about cup of the beans.
20. Shred the duck meat and divide it evenly among the toasts.
21. Season with salt and pepper, drizzle with more oil, if desired, and crumble the crackling over the top.
22. Sprinkle with the parsley and serve.

Ingredients: ' tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, room temperature', '2 oz. cream cheese, room temperature', ' cup ( g) sugar', '1 large egg', '1 tsp. finely grated orange zest', ' cup ( g) raw pumpkin seeds (pepitas)', ' cup ( g) raw sunflower seeds', ' cup ( g) poppy seeds, plus more for sprinkling', ' cup ( g) sesame seeds, plus more for sprinkling', ' cup honey', '2 Tbsp. tahini', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '1 large egg', 'A 3"-diameter cookie cutter'
Instructions:
1. Whisk baking powder, salt, and cups ( g) flour in a medium bowl to combine.
2. Set aside.
3. Beat butter, cream cheese, and sugar in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment on medium-high until pale and fluffy, about 5 minutes.
4. Add egg and orange zest and beat, scraping down sides of bowl as needed, just until combined.
5. Reduce speed to low and with motor running, gradually add dry ingredients.
6. Beat until dough comes together and no streaks of dry flour remain.
7. Divide dough in half and place each half on a piece of beeswax or plastic wrap.
8. Pat into a 1"-thick disk.
9. Wrap tightly and chill until firm, at least 2 hours and up to hours.
10. Preheat oven to .
11. Mix pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds, cup poppy seeds, and cup sesame seeds in a medium heatproof bowl.
12. Heat honey in a small saucepan over medium, stirring occasionally with a heatproof rubber spatula, until it bubbles and foams and turns dark amber (an instant read thermometer should register ), about 5 minutes.
13. Remove from heat and stir in tahini and tsp. salt.
14. Pour over seeds and stir to coat.
15. Let cool slightly (you want the mixture to be as hot as possible since it hardens as it cools, but not so hot you could burn your hands).
16. Working quickly, scoop out heaping teaspoonfuls of filling and roll into balls.
17. Roll out dough on a lightly floured surface to a scant " thick.
18. Punch out cookies with cutter.
19. Transfer to 2 parchment-lined baking sheets. (You should ideally have cookies per baking sheet.) They wonAt spread, so donAt worry about getting them close.
20. Gather up and reroll any scraps.
21. Beat egg with 1 Tbsp. water and a pinch of salt in a small bowl.
22. Working one at a time, brush rounds with egg wash and place a ball of filling in the center.
23. Fold sides of dough up to make a triangle, pinching corners to seal.
24. Brush sides of dough with egg wash and sprinkle with more sesame seeds and poppy seeds.
25. Bake hamantaschen, rotating pans top to bottom and front to back halfway through, until crust is golden brown and filling is puffed, minutes.
26. Let cool on baking sheets.
27. Do ahead: Cookies can be baked 3 days ahead.
28. Store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: '2 pounds ground percent lean turkey breast', '1 cup packed fresh spinach leaves, finely chopped', ' cup pitted black olives, finely chopped', 'Zest of 1 lemon', '2 tablespoons finely chopped red onion', '1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh mint or parsley', '1 teaspoon garlic powder, or 3 fresh garlic cloves, finely chopped', '1 teaspoon dried oregano', '1 teaspoon sea salt', ' teaspoon ground black pepper', 'Olive oil or ghee for frying', 'Optional toppings: butter lettuce, sliced tomato, sliced red onion, sliced cucumber, more fresh mint or parsley, basil pesto, or feta cheese'
Instructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, use your hands to combine well the turkey meat with the spinach, olives, lemon zest, onion, mint or parsley, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper.
2. But donAt overmix or the meat with become tough.
3. Divide the mixture into 6 equal portions and shape them into burgers.
4. Warm about a tablespoon of olive oil or ghee in a large skillet set over medium-high heat.
5. Working in batches, add the burger patties and fry them on one side for about 5 minutes, then flip them, frying on the second side for another 4 to 5 minutes, or until theyAre golden brown.
6. If you like, serve each burger on a large piece of butter lettuce and top them with any of the optional ingredients.

Ingredients: '1 (-ounce) package firm tofu', 'A cup finely chopped scallions', '5 A teaspoons mushroom bouillon', '4 tablespoons fresh corn, off the cob', '2 pinches ground white pepper', 'Spicy aioli, for garnish and dip', '1 cup all-purpose flour', '1 cups water', '2 teaspoons mushroom bouillon', '1 (-ounce) package green glutinous rice flakes (aka com dep xanh)', ' cup cornstarch'
Instructions:
1. Pressing the tofu is the most important step, so IAm going to give yAall (I grew up in Texas, so I have the liberty to use AyAallA liberally) three pressing options.
2. The first step before you press is the same: Drain the liquid from the box of tofu.
3. Then press.
4. Method 1: (Easiest) Use a tofu-pressing machine!
5. I spotted one of these in Torrance, California, at a little Japanese grocery store.
6. You place a block of tofu in between two plates and turn a small crank to compress it.
7. Though it will press only one block at a timeAthe boxes of tofu we use come four to a boxAyou can set it and forget it (but do it three more times if you plan on using all the tofu).
8. Method 2: (Most common) Use two similar-size plates or relatively flat surfaces.
9. Lay out the blocks of tofu flat in a single layer on one of the plates, and then put the second plate on top of the tofu.
10. Press the tofu by placing at least pounds of weight on top of the second plate.
11. Method 3: (Most AprofessionalA) We still prepare the tofu this way at our restaurant, which is, without question, the most efficient way to prepare larger servings.
12. Lay baking racksgrates inside baking sheets, then cover racks with clean (lint-free) towels or, as we did successfully for four years, with neatly folded aprons laid in a single layer as level and as flat as possible across the racks.
13. Yup, towels or aprons, plural.
14. Because tofu holds so much moisture, the liquid constantly spills over, and weAve found very few people who just live to clean.
15. To absorb the moisture, and to create much less of a mess, we started using layers of cloth.
16. Lay out the tofu on the covered racks in a single layer, organized as tightly as possible.
17. Make sure the tofu doesnAt hang over the sides of the sheets or it wonAt be pressed evenly.
18. Next, lay another towelapron on top of the layer of tofu, again, flat and level.
19. Then lay another equal-size baking sheet covered rack tofu on top of that.
20. Repeat the same steps as needed.
21. The top layer should be a baking sheet on which you place to pounds of AstuffA to press down on all the layers.
22. No matter how many layers you stack, the tofu should flatten out only slightly, not be completely crushed.
23. Processing the tofu is far simpler than pressing it, though like pressing, there are three different ways to do it.
24. Start with a bowl or a large (-quart) plastic container made by and known as a Cambro (if you have one of those lying around), and do one of the following: Method 1: Use a food mill, which is what we use.
25. This is by far the easiest way to process tofu and get the most consistent texture (and itAs a great method for mashing anything).
26. Method 2: Use a potato masher.
27. Just mash away, but youAll still need to use the third option to smash out any little lumps that the masher doesnAt, um, well, mash.
28. Method 3: Mash everything with your hands. (This is the most common way.) Process the tofu until it is a consistent, almost pasty texture.
29. If you go with No. 2 or No. 3, make sure to smash out all lumps from the tofu or else the seasoning wonAt evenly distribute throughout the mix and, later, the balls.
30. Add chopped scallions, mushroom bouillon, corn, and ground white pepper to the (hopefully) evenly processed tofu.
31. Stick your hands in (maybe with gloves) and mix all the ingredients.
32. Taste some of the mix from different areas of the bowlcontainer to make sure itAs evenly seasoned.
33. It should taste savory, with little bits of scallion and corn in every bite.
34. Now itAs ball-rolling time!
35. Scoop out a tablespoon of the tofu mixture, hand-pack it tightly into the spoon, then level out the spoon with your finger.
36. Assertively shake or scoop out the mix into your hand while mostly keeping it in one piece.
37. Firmly clench the tofu by making a fist, packing it very tightly so it doesnAt fall apart.
38. Continue by shaping into a ball, either with one hand or by lightly tossing the ball back and forth between both hands like youAre playing catch with yourself.
39. Lightly roll the tofu between your hands, moving your hands in an alternating clockwise motion, like you would forming a meatball, only more gently to smooth out any cracks.
40. Tofu is very docile; the more you do it, the better youAll get the feel of it (like most things in life).
41. Place tofu balls on a plate or (ideally) a baking sheet rack.
42. Optional: Refrigerate the balls overnight, uncovered.
43. This will firm them up.
44. Add flour, water, and mushroom bouillon to a bowl.
45. Mix together.
46. It should not be lumpy; the consistency should be not too thin and consistently thick enough to adhere, but not so thick it doesnAt drip off your finger.
47. Empty the green rice flakes into a separate bowl or catering pan, then mix in half of the dry cornstarch with flakes.
48. Next, drop balls into flour paste.
49. Shake off excess paste (we highly recommend using a kitchen AspiderA strainer to do this), then roll balls around in the flakes until theyAre fully coated.
50. Gently clench the balls to pack one last bit of flakes onto the balls.
51. If flakes start to drop off the balls, mix in more of the remaining cornstarch to dry flakes, then try again.
52. Set balls on a rack.
53. Optional: If you plan on making more and hate waste like we do, clean and dry your hands, then sift through all the flakes.
54. Throw out any moist clumps of flakes.
55. Store dry flakes in a closed container, then refrigerate until your next tofu ballArolling party!
56. Optional: Refrigerate the fully flaked balls uncovered (and preferably elevated on a rack) overnight.
57. This will give the flakes more time to adhere and dry out, which is important in giving the balls more puff, crunch, and an overall prettier look after frying.
58. Add at least 2 inches of oil to a pot, bring to over high heat, then fry the balls for about 3 to 4 minutes, or until the flakes puff up bright green and any exposed tofu turns a slight golden color.
59. If the balls start turning brown, the oilAs too hot or youAre frying them for too long.
60. If the balls look oily or wet after frying, either the temperature is too low or youAre frying too many balls at once, which lowers the temperature of the oil too quickly.
61. Remove from oil, shake off excess, drip dry on a rack.
62. Plate and top with spicy aioli and more aioli on the side to dip.

Ingredients: '1 ounces JAgermeister', '1 ounce apple brandy', '2 dashes apple bitters', 'Garnish orange twist'
Instructions:
1. Combine the JAgermeister, brandy, and bitters in an ice-filled rocks glass and stir gently.
2. Garnish with an orange twist.

Ingredients: '1 cup old-fashioned rolled oats', 'Pinch of salt (optional)', '1 tablespoon raw honey or pure maple syrup (optional)'
Instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine the oats and 4 cups cold water and stir to combine.
2. Allow to soak at room temperature for up to 1 hour.
3. Drain the oats (discarding the water) and rinse them.
4. Transfer the oats to a blender, add 4 cups fresh water, and the salt andor sweetener, if using.
5. Blend until completely smooth, about 2 minutes.
6. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve and chill.
7. Keeps up to 5 days in the refrigerator.
8. Shake before using.

Ingredients: ' pounds (1.2 kg) assorted seasonal vegetables, trimmed and peeled', '6 shiso (perilla) leaves', '3 sprigs each thyme, dill, and parsley', ' ounces ( g) ginger, peeled and sliced', '2 cloves garlic, peeled and sliced in halves', '1 teaspoon peppercorns (black or pink) or seeds (cumin, coriander, fennel, or mustard)', '4 dried red chiles or 1 jalapeno pepper, sliced crosswise and seeds removed', '2 cups ( ml) rice vinegar', '2 cups ( ml) filtered water', '1 piece of kombu, 1 x 6 inches (2.5 x cm), cut into pieces', ' cup ( g) cane sugar', '3 tablespoons sea salt', '1 tablespoon julienned orange or lemon peel'
Instructions:
1. Sterilize eight -pint ( ml) mason jars.
2. Slice the vegetables into bite-size pieces, about inch (6 mm) thick.
3. If skinny like asparagus and green beans, leave them whole or cut them in half crosswise.
4. Root vegetables should be blanched first for 1 minute in a pot of boiling water and strained before adding to the jars.
5. Pack the vegetables, shiso, thyme, dill, and parsley in the sterilized jars tightly.
6. Combine the ginger, garlic, peppercorns, chiles, rice vinegar, filtered water, kombu, sugar, salt, and citrus peel in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
7. Remove from heat and pour the hot brine over the vegetables in the sterilized jars.
8. Let cool, then seal the jars and refrigerate.
9. You can start eating the pickles after 1 day.
10. The pickles will keep in the refrigerator for up to 3 weeks.

Ingredients: '4 skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs, patted dry', 'Kosher salt', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', '1 large onion, thinly sliced', '6 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '1 cup red lentils, rinsed', '1 tsp. ground turmeric', ' cup finely chopped parsley', '2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', 'Plain yogurt (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Lightly season chicken thighs all over with salt and set aside.
2. Heat oil in a medium pot over medium-high.
3. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until golden brown, minutes.
4. Add garlic and cook, stirring often, until garlic is golden and onion is deep golden brown and lightly charred around the edges, about 5 minutes.
5. Remove pot from heat and transfer onion and garlic to a plate with a slotted spoon; set aside for serving.
6. Return pot to medium-high heat; pour in lentils and sprinkle turmeric over.
7. Cook, stirring a few times, until fragrant, about seconds.
8. Add reserved chicken and 6 cups water; season generously with salt and some pepper.
9. Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat.
10. Reduce heat to medium-low; cook, stirring occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and lentils are tender, minutes.
11. Using tongs, transfer chicken to a cutting board; let cool slightly.
12. Shred meat with 2 forks or your hands; discard skin and bones.
13. Return shredded chicken meat to pot and add parsley.
14. Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice.
15. Ladle soup into bowls; drizzle with yogurt and top with reserved onion mixture, dividing evenly.

Ingredients: '8 shallots, peeled, halved if large', '1 lb. green beans, trimmed', '1 cup drained Peppadew peppers in brine, torn in half', ' cup white wine vinegar', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 Tbsp. plus cup store-bought or homemade Thai green curry paste', '3 lb. chicken thighs andor drumsticks', '2 tsp. kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in top third of oven; preheat to .
2. Place shallots, green beans, and peppers in a shallow 3-qt. baking dish.
3. Drizzle with vinegar, oil, and 1 Tbsp. curry paste.
4. Using your hands, toss to combine.
5. Season chicken on all sides with salt and rub with remaining cup curry paste.
6. Nestle chicken pieces, skin side up, into shallot mixture in baking dish.
7. Roast, basting chicken with pan juices halfway through, until chicken is cooked through and golden brown on top, minutes.

Recipe name: Ginger-Scallion Meatballs With Green Curry Sauce
Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil, divided', '1 lb. ground pork', '6 scallions, finely chopped', '1 large egg', '2 tsp. finely grated peeled ginger (from about 1" piece)', '2 tsp. kosher salt, divided', '1 (5.4-oz.) can coconut cream', ' cup store-bought or homemade Thai green curry paste', '1 (5 oz.) container baby spinach', '2 Tbsp. fresh lime juice', 'Cooked noodles or rice, unsweetened shredded coconut, and lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in top third of oven; preheat to .
2. Grease a rimmed baking sheet with 1 Tbsp. oil.
3. Vigorously mix pork, scallions, egg, ginger, 1 tsp. salt, and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in a large bowl with a wooden spoon until mixture is shaggy and lightens slightly in color, about 1 minute.
4. Working one at a time, scoop out heaping 1-tablespoonful portions and roll into balls; arrange on prepared baking sheet.
5. Bake meatballs until cooked through and browned on top, minutes.
6. Meanwhile, heat coconut cream and curry paste in a large skillet over medium-high, stirring occasionally, until bubbling, about 2 minutes.
7. Reduce heat to low and stir in spinach and remaining 1 tsp. salt.
8. Cook, stirring often, until spinach is wilted, about 3 minutes.
9. Transfer to a blender and purAe until smooth.
10. Add lime juice and blend to combine.
11. Divide noodles or rice among plates and arrange meatballs on top.
12. Pour sauce over and top with coconut.
13. Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.
14. DO AHEAD: Meatballs can be formed 1 day ahead.
15. Arrange on baking sheet; cover and chill.

Ingredients: 'Nonstick vegetable oil spray', ' cup sliced almonds', '4 oz. bittersweet chocolate (must be at least % cacao in order for the fudge to set properly), broken or chopped into small pieces if needed', ' ripe avocado', 'A cup pure maple syrup', 'A cup almond butter', '1 Tbsp. cocoa powder, preferably Dutch-processed', ' tsp. kosher salt', 'Flaky sea salt'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Coat a 9x5" loaf pan with nonstick spray.
3. Line with parchment paper, leaving plenty of overhang on 2 sides (the spray will help it stick to the pan and stay flat).
4. Spread out almonds on a rimmed baking sheet and toast, tossing halfway through, until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
5. Let cool, then coarsely crush with a flat-bottomed glass.
6. Meanwhile, place chocolate in a small glass or ceramic bowl and microwave in -second bursts, stirring well between each, until melted and smooth, minutes.
7. Let cool slightly. (Alternatively, heat chocolate, stirring occasionally, in a heatproof bowl set over a small saucepan of simmering water until melted; do not let bowl touch water.) PurAe avocado, maple syrup, and almond butter in a food processor until very smooth, about 1 minute.
8. Add cocoa powder, kosher salt, and 2 Tbsp. cold water.
9. Pulse until smooth, then add melted chocolate.
10. Pulse just until combined, scraping down sides of bowl as needed.
11. Transfer chocolate mixture to prepared pan and smooth top.
12. Scatter reserved almonds over, then season with sea salt; press in lightly.
13. Chill until firm, about 1 hour.
14. Remove by lifting up parchment, then cut into squares or bars to serve.
15. Do Ahead: Fudge can be made 1 week ahead.
16. Cover and keep chilled.

Recipe name: Ultimate Green Curry Gaeng Khiaw Wan Gai
Ingredients: '2 tablespoons vegetable oil or coconut cream', '1 cup green curry paste (homemade or storebought)', '1 pound boneless chicken thighs, sliced 1 inch thick', '1 (.5-ounce) can coconut milk', '1 cup cubed eggplant (-inch cubes)', ' cup whole Thai basil leaves', ' cup thinly sliced fresh long red Thai chiles', '4 makrut lime leaves, torn 3 or 4 times to release their oils', '3 tablespoons palm sugar, or to taste', '3 tablespoons fish sauce, or to taste', 'Steamed jasmine rice, rice noodles, or roti'
Instructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, heat the oil over medium heat until sizzling (or if using coconut cream, heat it until bubbling).
2. Add the curry paste and cook, stirring, just until fragrant and the color has intensified, about 1 minute.
3. Watch carefully so it doesnAt burn.
4. Add the chicken and stir to coat in the curry paste, then pour in the coconut milk and bring to a boil.
5. Add the eggplant, lower the heat to maintain a simmer, and simmer until the chicken and eggplant are cooked through, about 8 minutes.
6. Remove from the heat and stir in the Thai basil, chiles, and lime leaves.
7. Stir in the sugar and fish sauce until the sugar has dissolved.
8. Serve hot over jasmine rice or rice noodles or with roti for dipping.

Ingredients: '1 Tbsp. kosher salt, plus more', '6 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', ' large white onion, finely chopped', '2 cups carnaroli or Japanese sushi rice', '1 cup dry white wine', '5 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces', '2 oz. Parmesan, finely grated', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', '1 lb. mushrooms (such as shiitake, crimini, or maitake), trimmed, caps torn into (2") pieces', 'Kosher salt', 'Freshly ground pepper', '5 sprigs thyme', '5 garlic cloves, crushed', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '2 Tbsp. white wine vinegar or fresh lemon juice', '1 oz. Parmesan, finely grated'
Instructions:
1. Combine 1 Tbsp. salt and cups water in a medium pot.
2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium to maintain a bare simmer.
3. Meanwhile, heat oil in a 6-qt.
4. Dutch oven over medium.
5. Add onion and a pinch of salt and cook, stirring often, until onion is translucent and starting to soften, 6A8 minutes.
6. Add cup water and cook, stirring often, until water evaporates and onion is sizzling in oil and tender, about 5 minutes.
7. Taste onion; if itAs still firm at all, add a splash of water and continue to cook until meltingly soft.
8. Add rice and stir well to coat with oil.
9. Cook, stirring constantly, until grains are translucent around the edges and make a clattering sound when they hit against the pot, about 5 minutes. (Coating grains with oil before adding any liquid helps the rice cook evenly so that the outside does not become mushy before the center is tender.) Add wine and another pinch of salt.
10. Bring to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until wine evaporates, about 2 minutes.
11. Reduce heat to medium, then add hot salted water to rice in -cup increments, stirring constantly and allowing liquid to absorb fully before adding more, until rice is al dente and suspended in a fluid, not-too-thick creamy liquid, minutes.
12. It should take 2A3 minutes for each addition to be absorbed; if things are moving faster than this, reduce heat to medium-low.
13. Gradual absorption and constant agitation are key to encouraging starches to release from risotto, creating its trademark creamy consistency.
14. You may not need all of the hot water, but the finished texture should be more of a liquid than a solid.
15. Start checking rice about minutes after the first water addition; the grains should be tender but not mushy, with a slightly firm center that doesnAt leave a chalky residue between your teeth after tasting.
16. Do not overcook!
17. Remove pot from heat, add butter, and stir until melted.
18. Gradually add Parmesan, stirring until cheese is melted and liquid is creamy but very loose; stir in more hot salted water if needed.
19. Taste and season with more salt.
20. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high until shimmering.
21. Add mushrooms; cook, tossing occasionally, until they begin to soften and release some liquid, about 3 minutes.
22. Season with salt and pepper; cook, tossing occasionally, until deeply browned and tender, minutes.
23. Add thyme, garlic, and butter and cook, tossing occasionally, until garlic softens and butter is golden brown, about 3 minutes.
24. Remove from heat and add vinegar.
25. Toss to coat, scraping up any browned bits.
26. Pluck out thyme.
27. Divide risotto among warm bowls.
28. Top each with a few grinds of pepper and spoon mushroom mixture over.
29. Serve with Parmesan alongside.

Ingredients: ' small green Thai chiles, stems removed', '2 lemongrass stalks, bottom third only, tough outer layers removed, smashed with the back of a knife, thinly sliced', '2 shallots, coarsely chopped', '8 small or 6 large garlic cloves', '1 (4") piece galangal, peeled, thinly sliced', 'Zest of 1 makrut (Thai) lime (optional)', '1 cup Thai basil leaves', '1 Tbsp. chopped fingerroot (kra chai) in brine (optional)', ' tsp. shrimp paste'
Instructions:
1. Blend chiles, lemongrass, shallots, garlic, galangal, makrut lime zest, basil, fingerroot (if using), shrimp paste, and cup water in a blender on high speed, scraping down sides and adding more water if needed, until paste is the consistency of a thick salsa.
2. DO AHEAD: Curry paste can be made 5 days ahead.
3. Transfer to an airtight container; cover and chill, or freeze up to 2 months.

Recipe name: Breakfast Sandwich On An English Muffin With Charred Red Onions Herbs And Cheddar
Ingredients: '1 medium red onion, trimmed, cut in half through equator, peeled, separated into rings', '1 tsp. soy sauce', ' tsp. garlic powder', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', 'Kosher salt', ' cup finely chopped soft herbs, such as basil, cilantro, andor dill', '2 Tbsp. green or red hot sauce', '1 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '4 large eggs, lightly beaten', '2 whole-grain English muffins, toasted', '2 oz. sliced sharp cheddar cheese'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Toss onion, soy sauce, garlic powder, and 1 Tbsp. oil on a large rimmed baking sheet to coat.
3. Season with salt.
4. Bake, tossing once halfway through, until onion is browned around the edges and very tender, minutes.
5. Let cool 5 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, toss herbs, hot sauce, and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in a small bowl.
7. Season with salt, if needed.
8. Melt butter in a small nonstick skillet over medium heat.
9. Add eggs; season with salt.
10. Cook, stirring with a rubber spatula, until eggs have set into creamy folds, 1A2 minutes.
11. Spread herb sauce on cut sides of muffins.
12. Transfer bottom halves to a rimmed baking sheet.
13. Top with eggs, then lay cheese over.
14. Bake bottom halves just until cheese is melted, about 5 minutes.
15. Top with onion and close with muffin tops.
16. Do Ahead: Onion can be cooked 5 days ahead.
17. Transfer to an airtight container and chill.
18. To make a sandwich quickly without turning your oven on, fold the cheese into the eggs when they have just begun to set, top with onion (no need to rewarm), and assemble with toasted English muffins.

Ingredients: ' oz. mushrooms, such as shiitake or crimini, stems removed', '1 medium head of cauliflower (about lb.), broken into florets', ' cup plus 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling', '4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, divided', '1 large onion, finely chopped', '6 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '1 chile, such as serrano, Holland, or Fresno, thinly sliced, or tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '1 Tbsp. finely chopped rosemary', 'A cup double-concentrated tomato paste', 'Kosher salt', '1 lb. rigatoni', '2 oz. finely grated Parmesan (about 1 cup), plus more for serving', '3 Tbsp. finely chopped parsley', ' lemon'
Instructions:
1. Pulse mushrooms in a food processor until finely chopped.
2. Transfer to a small bowl.
3. Wipe out food processor bowl.
4. Working in 3 batches, pulse cauliflower in food processor until pieces are about the size of a grain of rice (some smaller and some larger ones are fine), transferring to a medium bowl as you go.
5. Heat cup oil and 2 Tbsp. butter in a large heavy pot over medium-high.
6. Add mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until golden brown, 4A6 minutes.
7. Add onion and 2 Tbsp. oil to pot.
8. Cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is very soft and golden brown, 6A8 minutes.
9. Add garlic, chile, and rosemary and cook, stirring occasionally, until garlic is softened and mixture is very fragrant, about 3 minutes.
10. Add tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, until paste is slightly darkened, about 2 minutes.
11. Add cauliflower and cook, still stirring occasionally, until cauliflower is cooked down slightly and begins to stick to bottom of pot, 6A8 minutes.
12. Season with salt, then keep warm over low heat.
13. Meanwhile, cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until almost al dente, about 1 minute less than package directions.
14. Using a slotted spoon, transfer pasta to pot with sauce.
15. Add 1 cup Parmesan, remaining 2 Tbsp. butter, and 1 cup pasta cooking liquid.
16. Increase heat to medium and cook, stirring occasionally, until pasta is al dente and sauce is clinging to pasta, about 3 minutes.
17. Remove from heat and stir in parsley.
18. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt (it'll probably need another pinch or two).
19. Finely zest lemon over pasta and toss once more.
20. Divide pasta among bowls.
21. Top with more Parmesan, then drizzle with oil.

Ingredients: '1 (-oz.) can chickpeas, rinsed', '1 lime', '8 sprigs cilantro', ' cup plain whole milk Greek yogurt', 'Kosher salt', '2 links fresh chorizo (about 8 oz. total), casings removed', '4 tostada shells'
Instructions:
1. Measure out cup chickpeas; reserve remaining chickpeas for another use.
2. Finely grate zest of lime into a small bowl.
3. Cut lime in half and squeeze juice from one half into bowl with zest.
4. Cut remaining half into wedges; set aside for serving.
5. Gather cilantro on a cutting board.
6. Starting at the stem end, finely chop until you get to the leaves; reserve leaves for serving.
7. Add stems to bowl with lime.
8. Add yogurt to bowl and mix well.
9. Season with salt.
10. Heat a medium nonstick skillet over medium.
11. Cook chorizo, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, until partially cooked, about 3 minutes.
12. Add chickpeas and season with salt.
13. Cook, mashing some of the chickpeas with spoon until chorizo is cooked through and crisp, minutes.
14. Remove from heat.
15. Spread yogurt mixture over tostada shells.
16. Top with chorizo mixture, then with reserved cilantro leaves.
17. Serve with reserved lime wedges for squeezing over.

Ingredients: '1 (-oz.) block firm tofu', ' cup low-sodium soy sauce', '3 Tbsp. pure maple syrup', '3 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '1 (") piece ginger, very thinly sliced', ' cup neutral oil, such as canola or grapeseed', 'Kimchi, toasted sesame seeds, hijiki, daikon, sliced scallions, and steamed rice (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Drain tofu, then sandwich between several layers of kitchen towels to remove excess liquid.
2. Cut into 9 cubes.
3. Whisk soy sauce, maple syrup, rice vinegar, red pepper flakes, and ginger in a small bowl.
4. Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
5. When oil is rippling across the surface, carefully add tofu so it doesnAt splash.
6. Cook, undisturbed, until very crisp and dark brown underneath, 3A4 minutes.
7. Carefully turn and repeat on opposite side.
8. Holding tofu back with a spatula or slotted spoon, pour out oil into a small bowl.
9. Return skillet to medium-high heat and add soy sauce mixture.
10. Cook, reducing heat to medium so it doesnAt over-reduce or burn and basting tofu occasionally, until glaze is thick enough to coat a spoon, about 4 minutes.
11. Divide tofu among plates.
12. Drizzle with glaze, then top with scallions.
13. Serve with rice alongside.

Ingredients: '1 lb. broccoli (about 1 large or 2 medium heads)', '5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', 'Kosher salt', '1 small red onion, cut lengthwise into ("-thick) slices', '4 garlic cloves, sliced', '6 oil-packed anchovy fillets', '1 oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about cup)', 'Lemon wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Trim only the very bottom, woody part of broccoli stem.
3. Peel tough outer layer from stem, from the florets down to the end of the stalk.
4. Starting from stem end, cut broccoli at a angle into "-thick slices until you reach the florets.
5. Break florets apart with your hands into bite-size pieces (this avoids getting bitsy trimmings all over the place).
6. Heat 3 Tbsp. oil in a large heatproof skillet over medium-high until shimmering.
7. Add broccoli; season with salt.
8. Cook, tossing occasionally, until broccoli is bright green and lightly charred, about 3 minutes.
9. Transfer to a plate.
10. Wipe out skillet.
11. Heat remaining 2 Tbsp. oil in same skillet over medium.
12. Cook onion and garlic, stirring often, until onion is beginning to soften, about 3 minutes.
13. Add anchovies and cook, breaking apart with a spoon, until broken down and garlic is beginning to turn golden around the edges, about 2 minutes.
14. Return broccoli to skillet and toss to coat with oil.
15. Transfer to oven and roast, tossing once, until broccoli is browned and tender, minutes.
16. Wrap handle of skillet with a towel before you forget itAs REALLY HOT.
17. Scatter Parmesan over hot broccoli.
18. Divide among plates.
19. Serve with lemon wedges alongside.

Ingredients: '2 (1-lb.) pork tenderloins', '2 tsp. kosher salt', '1 cup dry white wine', ' cup pitted prunes, torn in half', ' cup pitted Spanish green olives', 'A cup (packed) light brown sugar', ' cup capers, plus 1 Tbsp. caper brine', ' cup red wine vinegar', '4 garlic cloves, peeled, smashed', '1 Tbsp. dried oregano', ' cup plus 1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 Tbsp. unsalted butter', ' cup chopped parsley (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Season pork with salt.
2. Let sit minutes.
3. Meanwhile, mix wine, prunes, olives, brown sugar, capers and brine, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and cup oil in a medium bowl to combine; set sauce aside (or use it to marinate pork up to hours).
4. Place a rack in center of oven; preheat to .
5. Heat remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in a large ovenproof skillet over medium-high.
6. Cook pork, turning occasionally, until golden brown all over, 6A8 minutes.
7. Remove skillet from heat and let sit 1 minute so pan can cool down a bit.
8. Pour reserved sauce over pork and give pan a few shakes.
9. Transfer to oven and roast pork, basting with sauce halfway through, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of tenderloin registers , minutes, depending on the thickness of the meat.
10. Transfer pork to a cutting board and let rest minutes.
11. Return pan to medium-high heat.
12. Add butter to sauce and cook, swirling pan occasionally, until butter is melted and sauce is slightly reduced, 3A5 minutes.
13. Stir in parsley if using.
14. Slice tenderloin crosswise against the grain.
15. Transfer to a platter and spoon pan sauce over.

Recipe name: Chicken And Rice Meatballs With Hummus
Ingredients: '6 scallions, thinly sliced', '2 garlic cloves, finely chopped', '1 (1-inch) piece ginger, peeled, finely chopped', '1 lb. ground chicken', ' cups cooled cooked white rice', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', '2 tsp. hawaij, baharat, curry powder, or other spice blend', '1 tsp. Diamond Crystal or tsp. Morton kosher salt, plus more', '1 small white onion, thinly sliced', '4 small dill pickles, sliced', '2 Persian cucumbers, sliced', '2 cups parsley leaves with tender stems', '1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', 'Hummus and lemon wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Gently mix scallions, garlic, ginger, chicken, rice, oil, hawaij, and 1 tsp.
3. Diamond Crystal or tsp.
4. Morton kosher salt in a medium bowl (donAt overwork).
5. Form mixture into balls.
6. Place on a rimmed baking sheet and bake until lightly browned and cooked through, about minutes.
7. Toss onion, pickles, cucumbers, parsley, and lemon juice in a medium bowl to combine; season salad with salt.
8. Spread hummus on plates; top with meatballs and salad.
9. Serve with lemon.

Ingredients: '7 Tbsp. virgin coconut oil', ' cup raw pumpkin seeds (pepitas)', 'A cup raw sesame seeds', '3 Tbsp. chia seeds', ' cups unsweetened natural almond butter', '1 Tbsp. pure maple syrup', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. Aleppo-style pepper'
Instructions:
1. Heat oil and pumpkin seeds in a large skillet over medium, stirring occasionally, until oil is melted and pumpkin seeds are just starting to turn golden, about 3 minutes.
2. Add sesame seeds and chia seeds and cook, stirring constantly, until sesame seeds are golden, about 2 minutes.
3. Transfer seeds and oil to a large bowl and add almond butter, maple syrup, salt, and Aleppo-style pepper.
4. Stir vigorously until smooth; let cool.
5. Do Ahead: Power Butter can be made 2 weeks ahead.
6. Store airtight at room temperature.

Recipe name: Bright And Spicy Shrimp Noodle Salad
Ingredients: 'A cup fresh lime juice', '2 tsp. honey', '1 serrano chile, very thinly sliced', '1 (1-inch) piece ginger, peeled, finely grated', '1 garlic clove, finely grated', '1 Tbsp. plus tsp. fish sauce', '4 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', 'Kosher salt', '1 lb. large shrimp (preferably wild), peeled, deveined', '6 oz. bean thread (cellophane or glass) noodles', '1 English hothouse cucumber, halved lengthwise, thinly sliced crosswise', ' cup salted, roasted peanuts, crushed, divided', '1 cup basil leaves'
Instructions:
1. Stir lime juice and honey in a small bowl until honey dissolves.
2. Mix in chile, ginger, garlic, fish sauce, and 3 Tbsp. oil; season dressing with salt.
3. Toss shrimp and 2 Tbsp. dressing in a medium bowl to coat; let sit minutes.
4. Meanwhile, cook noodles according to package directions.
5. Drain and add to bowl with remaining dressing along with cucumber and cup peanuts; toss well.
6. Heat remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
7. Pour off any liquid from shrimp and pat dry; season all over with salt.
8. Cook shrimp, tossing occasionally, until browned and bright pink, about 5 minutes.
9. Transfer to bowl with noodles, add basil, and toss well to combine.
10. Divide noodle salad among bowls and top with remaining peanuts.

Ingredients: '3 scallions, white and dark green parts separated, thinly sliced', '1 garlic clove, finely chopped', '2 Tbsp. seasoned rice vinegar', '2 Tbsp. toasted sesame oil', '1 Tbsp. soy sauce', '2 tsp. honey', '1 tsp. finely chopped peeled ginger', '1 small kabocha or 2 acorn squash (about 2 lb.)', '1 tsp. toasted sesame seeds'
Instructions:
1. Pour water into a large saucepan, wide skillet, or large wok to come 1" up the sides and place over medium-high heat.
2. Mix white and pale-green parts of scallions, garlic, vinegar, oil, soy sauce, honey, and ginger in a small heatproof ramekin.
3. Set inside a large bamboo steamer off heat (or, if using a metal insert, donAt bring water to a simmer and fit into saucepan before adding dressing.
4. Cover dressing with foil.) Cut squash in half; scoop out and discard seeds.
5. Slice into "-thick wedges.
6. Arrange in steamer basket around ramekin, overlapping a little if needed (for metal insert, add as much squash as will fit without piling up, placing over dressing).
7. Set over saucepan (or turn on heat), cover, and steam until squash is tender (a tester, skewer, or paring knife should easily slide through flesh), minutes.
8. Transfer squash to a platter and top with scallion greens and sesame seeds.
9. Serve with dressing alongside.

Ingredients: '5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '2 bunches Swiss chard, ribs and stems removed and finely chopped, leaves coarsely chopped (keep separate)', '1 large shallot, thinly sliced', '1 (3-inch) piece ginger, peeled, thinly sliced lengthwise', 'Kosher salt', ' tsp. ground turmeric', '1 (-lb.) skinless salmon fillet, preferably center cut', '3 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', ' serrano chile, thinly sliced', ' cup unsweetened coconut flakes', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a large ovenproof skillet over medium-high.
3. Add Swiss chard ribs and stems, shallot, and ginger and cook, stirring occasionally, until shallot is softened but not browned, about 3 minutes.
4. Add Swiss chard leaves and cook, stirring, until partly wilted, about 3 minutes.
5. Remove from heat and season with salt.
6. Combine 2 Tbsp. oil and turmeric in a small bowl.
7. Place salmon, skin side down, on top of greens in skillet.
8. Season salmon with salt and coat with turmeric oil.
9. Transfer to oven and bake until fillet is just opaque in the center and flakes easily and greens are tender, minutes.
10. Meanwhile, heat remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in a small skillet over medium.
11. Add garlic, chile, and coconut flakes and cook, stirring often, until garlic and coconut turn golden, about 5 minutes.
12. Transfer coconut crisp to a small bowl and season lightly with salt.
13. Season fish and greens with pepper; serve with coconut crisp for topping.

Ingredients: '1 small red onion, finely chopped', '4 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar, divided', 'Kosher salt', ' lb. small Yukon Gold potatoes, halved or quartered lengthwise', '6 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '1 (-lb.) skinless, boneless cod fillet, preferably in 1 piece', '1 Tbsp. zaAatar', ' cup pitted Castelvetrano olives', ' cup coarsely chopped mint'
Instructions:
1. Place racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat oven to .
2. Mix onion, 2 Tbsp. vinegar, and a big pinch of salt in a small bowl to combine; let sit at least minutes and up to 1 hour.
3. Drain off vinegar and discard.
4. Meanwhile, toss potatoes and 3 Tbsp. oil on a large rimmed baking sheet; arrange in a single layer.
5. Roast on top rack, undisturbed, until browned and crisp underneath, minutes.
6. Turn potatoes over and reduce oven temperature to .
7. Place cod on a small rimmed baking sheet and drizzle with 1 Tbsp. oil.
8. Season with salt and sprinkle with zaAatar.
9. Slide onto bottom rack in oven and roast until fish flakes easily with a fork and potatoes are browned all over, about minutes.
10. Stir olives, mint, remaining 2 Tbsp. vinegar, and remaining 2 Tbsp. oil into pickled onion.
11. Serve cod and potatoes topped with olive mixture.

Recipe name: Warm Winter Vegetable Salad With Halloumi
Ingredients: '5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '2 garlic cloves, finely chopped', ' tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. Aleppo-style pepper', ' small head of red cabbage (about oz.), cut into 1" pieces', '1 medium red onion, cut into 8 wedges through root end', ' lb. winter squash, preferably delicata, halved, seeds removed, sliced crosswise " thick', '1 large pita bread, split into 2 layers, cut or torn into "" pieces', '8 oz. Halloumi cheese, cut into 1" pieces', '2 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar', ' tsp. ground sumac', 'Mint springs (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Coat a rimmed baking sheet with 1 Tbsp. oil.
3. Mix garlic, salt, Aleppo-style pepper, and 3 Tbsp. oil in a large bowl.
4. Add cabbage, onion, and squash and toss to coat.
5. Spread out on prepared baking sheet (keep bowl handy) and roast until squash is browned underneath, minutes.
6. Toss pita, Halloumi, and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in reserved bowl.
7. Scatter over vegetables, then turn vegetables over with a large spatula.
8. Roast until squash is fork-tender and cabbage, onion, pita, and Halloumi are browned in spots (cheese should be soft inside), minutes.
9. Remove from oven and drizzle with vinegar.
10. Top with sumac and mint.

Recipe name: Kale Pesto With Whole Wheat Pasta
Ingredients: '1 large bunch Tuscan kale, ribs and stems removed', 'Kosher salt', ' oz. farro pasta or whole wheat pasta', 'A cup raw pistachios', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', '1 garlic clove', '1 oz. Parmesan, finely grated, plus more for serving', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Cook kale leaves in a large pot of boiling salted water until bright green and wilted, about seconds.
2. Transfer to a rimmed baking sheet with tongs; keep water boiling.
3. Let kale cool slightly; wring out excess water with your hands.
4. Cook pasta in pot of boiling water, stirring occasionally, until al dente.
5. Blend nuts, oil, garlic, and A cup water in a blender or food processor until very smooth.
6. Add kale and 1 oz.
7. Parmesan.
8. PurAe, adding water 1 Tbsp. at a time as needed, until smooth.
9. Transfer pesto to a large bowl.
10. Using tongs, transfer pasta to bowl with pesto; add butter and A cup pasta cooking liquid.
11. Toss, adding more pasta cooking liquid by the tablespoonful if needed, until sauce coats pasta.
12. Divide among bowls; top with more Parmesan and a few grinds of pepper.

Ingredients: 'A cup plus 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil; plus more for serving', '1 medium onion, finely chopped', '1 fennel bulb, finely chopped', '2 celery stalks, finely chopped', ' garlic cloves, smashed, 1 left whole', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', ' tsp. smoked paprika, plus more for serving', '1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more', ' cup red split lentils', '2 bunches hardy greens (such as mature spinach, kale, andor Swiss chard), stems removed, leaves torn into large pieces', '1 lemon, halved', '4 thick slices country-style bread'
Instructions:
1. Heat A cup oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium.
2. Add onion, fennel, celery, and smashed garlic and cook, stirring often, until softened but not browned, minutes.
3. Add red pepper flakes, tsp. paprika, and 1 tsp. salt and stir to combine.
4. Add lentils and cups water and stir again, scraping down sides of pot so that all of the lentils are submerged.
5. Lay greens on top of liquid, but do not stir or toss them in.
6. Cover pot, reduce heat to medium-low, and cook until lentils are just cooked through and greens are wilted, minutes.
7. Uncover pot and stir mixture a few times to coat greens.
8. Simmer until most of the liquid is evaporated and lentils are saucy (not brothy), about 2 minutes.
9. Squeeze juice of a lemon half into lentils.
10. Taste and season with more salt if needed.
11. Cover and keep warm.
12. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-low.
13. Add 2 slices of bread and cook until golden and crisp underneath, about 5 minutes.
14. Transfer to plates and turn fried side up.
15. Repeat with 1 Tbsp. oil and remaining bread.
16. Rub fried side of each slice with remaining whole garlic clove; season with salt.
17. Generously spoon lentil mixture over toast; drizzle with oil and lightly sprinkle with more paprika.
18. Cut remaining lemon half into wedges and serve alongside for squeezing over.

Ingredients: '1 large sprig rosemary', ' ounce simple syrup or winter herb syrup', '2 ounces blackstrap rum', '1 ounce freshly squeezed lime juice', 'Soda water, to top', 'Garnish lime wheel and rosemary sprig', '1 cup cane sugar', '1 cup water', '2 large sprigs rosemary', '2 bunches thyme', '5 sage leaves', '1 cup sugar', '1 cup water'
Instructions:
1. Combine the sugar and water in a saucepan over low heat.
2. Stir until the sugar dissolves, about 5 minutes.
3. Remove from the heat and let the mixture cool.
4. Store in a glass jar in the fridge for up to 1 month.
5. Makes 1 cups.
6. Wash the herbs and set them aside to dry.
7. Combine the sugar and water in a saucepan over low heat.
8. Add the herbs and stir until the sugar dissolves, about 3 minutes.
9. Remove the pan from the heat and let the mixture cool and macerate for 1 hour.
10. Strain through a cheesecloth into a glass jar and store in the fridge for up to 1 month.
11. Makes 1 cups.
12. Remove the stem from the sprig of the rosemary and combine with the simple syrup in a cocktail shaker.
13. Use a muddler to lightly crush the rosemary and let sit for a few minutes.
14. Add the rum and lime juice.
15. Add ice, shake, and pour through a fine-mesh strainer into a collins glass over ice.
16. Top with soda and garnish with a lime wheel and a sprig of rosemary.

Ingredients: ' cup ( grams) walnut halves', '9 ounces ( gram) bittersweet chocolate', '6 ounces ( grams) unsweetened chocolate', ' cup or 1 stick ( grams) unsalted butter', ' teaspoon pure vanilla extract', '1 cups ( grams) sugar', '4 large eggs (about grams), at room temperature', ' cup ( grams) rye flour', ' teaspoon baking powder', ' teaspoon kosher salt'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to and position a rack in the center of the oven.
2. Place the walnuts on a baking sheet and toast for 8 to minutes, until they start to smell fragrant and are lightly golden brown when you break one in half.
3. Remove them from the oven and let cool.
4. Roughly chop them and set aside.
5. Chop 5 ounces of the bittersweet chocolate and 4 ounces of the unsweetened chocolate into large chunks and place them with the butter in a metal or heatproof glass bowl.
6. Set the bowl over a pot of simmering water and heat, stirring occasionally, until completely smooth.
7. Remove from the heat, whisk in the vanilla, and let cool for about minutes so itAs not piping hot.
8. In a stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, whisk together the sugar and eggs for about 5 minutes on medium-high speed, until light and thick and pale yellow.
9. With the mixer on low, slowly add the chocolate-butter mixture and whisk for about seconds.
10. It will not be completely mixed at this point but thatAs okay, because you will finish combining all the ingredients by hand.
11. Chop the remaining 4 ounces bittersweet chocolate into 1-inch chunks and shave the remaining 2 ounces unsweetened chocolate into fine shavings.
12. In a medium bowl, stir together the rye flour, the chopped bittersweet chocolate, shaved unsweetened chocolate, baking powder, salt, and toasted walnuts.
13. Remove the bowl from the mixer, add the rye flour mixture, and fold it in by hand until the dough is completely homogeneous.
14. For best results, scrape the dough into an airtight container and let it rest in the refrigerator for at least 3 to 4 hours or up to overnight before baking. (The unbaked dough can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.) Preheat the oven to and position a rack in the center of the oven.
15. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
16. Drop the dough in -cup scoops onto the prepared baking sheet, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
17. Bake for to minutes, rotating the baking sheet midway through the baking time, until the cookies are just starting to crack on the edges and the centers are soft but not liquidy when you press them.
18. Remove the cookies from the oven and let them cool on the baking sheet on a wire rack for 5 to minutes, then transfer them to the rack and let cool completely.
19. The cookies can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.

Ingredients: '2 oz. Scotch (The Famous Grouse preferred)', ' oz. sweet vermouth', ' oz. green Chartreuse', "3 dashes Peychaud's bitters", ' oz. absinthe', 'One 1-inch round grapefruit peel, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Pour absinthe into a chilled rocks glass and swirl around the glass's interior to rinse it; set aside.
2. Combine all remaining ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
3. Stir until very well chilled, then strain into the prepared rocks glass without ice.
4. Spritz a 1-inch round of grapefruit peel, skin side down, over the surface of the drink and discard.

Ingredients: '2 oz. Tanqueray gin', '0. oz. lemon juice', '0.5 oz. Luxardo maraschino liqueur', '0. oz. Rothman & Winter crA me de violette', 'Garnish: None'
Instructions: 1. Shake with ice, then fine-strain into a chilled coupe.

Recipe name: Bittersweet Symphony Gin And Aperol Cocktail
Ingredients: '1 ounces London dry gin', '3A4 ounce Punt e Mes', '3A4 ounce Aperol', 'Garnish: 1 generous lemon twist'
Instructions:
1. Stir all the ingredients with cracked ice in a mixing glass, then strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
2. Garnish with the lemon twist.

Ingredients: '1 ounce Rittenhouse Rye (bonded)', ' ounce Del Maguey Chichicapa Mezcal', ' ounce BAnAdictine liqueur', ' ounce Dolin Dry Vermouth', '2 dashes BittermanAs Mole Bitters', 'Garnish: Orange twist', 'Class: Coupe'
Instructions:
1. Stir all ingredients with ice and strain into glass.
2. Garnish.

Ingredients: '2 oz ml rye whiskey', ' oz ml sweet vermouth', ' oz ml Cynar amaro', '2 Luxardo maraschino cherries, skewered on a cocktail pick, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. In an ice-filled mixing glass, combine all the ingredients except the garnish.
2. Stir well, and rain into a chilled coupe glass.
3. Garnish with the cherries.

Ingredients: '1 oz. reposado tequila (El Tesoro preferred)', ' oz. mezcal (Del Maguey San Luis del Rio preferred)', '1 bar spoon agave nectar', '2 dashes Angostura bitters', 'Flamed orange twist, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Mix the tequila, mezcal, agave nectar, and bitters in a mixing glass with ice.
2. Strain over a fresh rock in an Old Fashioned glass.
3. Flame an orange peel over the top of the drink, and rest the twist in the glass as a garnish.

Ingredients: '2 ounces rye or bourbon whiskey', '1 ounce sweet vermouth', '2 dashes Angostura or orange bitters', 'Maraschino cherry for garnish'
Instructions:
1. In mixing glass or cocktail shaker filled with ice, combine whiskey, vermouth, and bitters.
2. Stir well, about seconds, then strain into cocktail glass.
3. Garnish with cherry and serve.

Ingredients: '1 (.5-oz.) can full-fat coconut milk', ' cup unsweetened cocoa powder (preferably Dutch-process)', '3 Tbsp. honey', 'Pinch of kosher salt', '3 Tbsp. dried rose petals, plus more for serving', ' tsp. vanilla extract'
Instructions:
1. Heat coconut milk, cocoa powder, honey, salt, 3 Tbsp. rose petals, and 2 cups water in a medium pot over medium, whisking constantly, until cocoa powder is dissolved, about 5 minutes.
2. As soon as it starts bubbling, reduce heat to maintain a gentle simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until flavors have infused, about minutes.
3. Stir in vanilla.
4. Strain cocoa through a fine-mesh sieve into 2 large mugs.
5. Serve topped with more rose petals.

Ingredients: '2 oz. Banks 5-Island rum', '0. oz. Lustau East India Solera sherry', '0. oz. Campari', 'Garnish: 1 orange twist'
Instructions:
1. Stir with ice, then strain into a chilled rocks glass filled with one large ice cube.
2. Garnish with the orange twist.

Ingredients: '1 ounce Plymouth gin', '1A4 ounce Campari', '1A2 ounce fresh lemon juice', '1A2 ounce simple syrup (1:1 sugar:water)', 'Champagne', 'Garnish: 1 grapefruit twist'
Instructions:
1. Shake the gin, Campari, lemon juice, and simple syrup with ice, then strain into a chilled champagne flute.
2. Top with champagne.
3. Squeeze the grapefruit twist over the drink, then discard.

Ingredients: '2 tablespoons (1 ounce) sweet vermouth', '2 tablespoons (1 ounce) Campari', '2 tablespoons (1 ounce) dry gin', '1 cup ice cubes', '1 navel orange peel twist'
Instructions:
1. In chilled cocktail shaker or pint glass, stir together vermouth, Campari, gin, and ice until well combined.
2. Strain into a martini glass or ice-filled rocks glass and garnish with orange twist.

Recipe name: Roasted Oysters With Warm Butter Mignonette
Ingredients: '1 small shallot, finely chopped', '3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar', ' oysters in shell', 'Coarse sea salt or kosher salt', ' cup ( stick) unsalted butter, melted'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Stir shallot, vinegar, and 1 Tbsp. water in a small bowl; set aside.
3. Line a plate with salt (this will help hold the oysters in place without tipping out their juices) and set aside.
4. Rinse oysters, then arrange flat side up on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet.
5. Roast until oysters crack open, 4A6 minutes. (They wonAt open completely like a cooked clam; the top shell will separate from the bottom with about a " opening at the rounded end.) Some may open before othersAremove from oven as soon as they open.
6. Discard any oysters that donAt open after 7 minutes.
7. To finish opening oysters: Fold a dish towel several times and hold oyster using towel.
8. Slide a butter knife into opening all the way to the hinge.
9. Turn butter knife perpendicular so top shell pops all the way open.
10. Pull off top shell with your hand, being careful not to tip bottom shell and lose any liquid (this should all happen very easily without much force).
11. Gently slide knife under oyster in bottom shell to release the muscle, then transfer to plate with salt.
12. Stir butter into reserved vinegar mixture.
13. Spoon over oysters.

Ingredients: '1 lb. baby Yukon Gold potatoes, halved if larger than 1"', '8 leeks, white and light green parts only, trimmed, cut crosswise into 2" pieces', '8 large eggs', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', ' cup sherry vinegar', '1 tsp. honey', 'Pinch of cayenne pepper (optional)', '1 tsp. kosher salt, divided', ' cup finely chopped parsley', '3 Tbsp. capers, drained', '2 bunches broccolini, trimmed, larger stalks halved lengthwise', 'Mayonnaise (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Set a steamer basket in a large pot filled with about 1" water.
2. Cover pot and bring water to a boil over high heat.
3. Place potatoes, leeks, then eggs in steamer basket, cover, and steam minutes.
4. Using tongs, transfer eggs to a bowl of cold water.
5. Meanwhile, whisk oil, vinegar, honey, cayenne (if using), and 1 tsp. salt in a small bowl.
6. Stir in parsley and capers; set aside.
7. Add broccolini to steamer basket on top of potatoes and leeks; season with remaining tsp. salt.
8. Cover and steam until broccolini is tender, 3A5 minutes.
9. Meanwhile, peel eggs and break or cut in half.
10. Swoosh a bit of mayonnaise in wide shallow bowls or on plates.
11. Divide potatoes, leeks, broccolini, and eggs among bowls or plates.
12. Drizzle with reserved dressing.

Ingredients: '1 tablespoon anise seeds', '1 tablespoon coriander seeds', '1 tablespoon cumin seeds', '1 tablespoon allspice berries', '1 tablespoon ground ginger', '1 tablespoon fennel seeds', '1 tablespoon Sichuan peppercorns', '1 tablespoon dried tamarind pulp', '1 teaspoon whole cloves', '1 (3-pound) chicken, cut into serving pieces', ' cup freshly squeezed lemon juice', '4 cups water', '1 onion, minced', '2 cloves garlic, minced', '2 tablespoons dried bird chiles, or to taste', 'Salt, to taste', '4 cups chicken stock', ' cup Pepper Soup Spice Mixture', '2 tablespoons minced dried smoked shrimp', '2 tablespoons chopped fresh mint', 'Mint sprigs, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Mix all the ingredients together in a bowl.
2. Place them in a spice mill and pulse until they are pulverized.
3. Spoon the peppersoup spice mixture into a clean glass jar and cover tightly.
4. While the peppersoup spice mixture will keep virtually indefinitely, the spices will lose their bite over time, so it is better to make a small batch at first and then increase the amount made as you use it more often. (Makes about cup.) Wash the chicken pieces, rub them with lemon juice, and place them in a large stockpot with the water, onion, garlic, and minced chiles.
5. Salt to taste and bring to a boil over high heat.
6. Lower the heat to medium and simmer for minutes.
7. Add the chicken stock and cup Peppersoup Spice Mixture and continue to cook for an additional minutes, or until the chicken is well cooked.
8. Stir in the shrimp and mint and continue to cook for an additional minutes.
9. Serve hot in soup bowls, garnished with a few sprigs of fresh mint.

Recipe name: Shaved Mushroom Celery And Sesame Salad
Ingredients: '2 stalks celery, ends trimmed', '5 ounces cremini or button mushrooms (about 8), ends trimmed', '1 teaspoons fresh lemon juice', '1 teaspoons white wine vinegar', 'Kosher salt', '2 tablespoons well-stirred tahini', '1 tablespoon olive oil', 'Handful whole parsley leaves', 'Flaky salt', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. To de-string the celery, make a small cut into the outer base end of the celery stalk, no more than A-inch deep, and pull along the length of the stalk.
2. If itAs a wide stalk, repeat, working around the outer edge, until no more strings are released.
3. Using a mandoline, cut the celery and mushrooms into paper-thin shavings.
4. In a large bowl, combine the celery and mushrooms and toss with the lemon juice, vinegar, and teaspoon kosher salt and let stand for 5 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, stir together the tahini and about 2 tablespoons water in a small bowl until smooth.
6. DonAt fret if it looks dry and lumpy; just keep stirring.
7. The consistency should be about that of whipped cream cheese; adjust as needed by adding a bit more water or a bit more tahini.
8. Season with a pinch of kosher salt, then using the back of a spoon, smear the mixture over your serving platter or bowl or divide it among 4 salad plates.
9. Add the olive oil and parsley to the mushrooms and gently toss to combine, then pile on top of the tahini.
10. Sprinkle with a few pinches of flaky salt and a few grinds of black pepper.
11. Serve immediately, making sure to scrape up the tahini smear along with the mushroom-celery salad.
12. Eat within an hour or so, as the mushrooms start releasing liquid as the salad sits.

Ingredients: '1kg (about 2 lbs.) Maris Piper potatoes, peeled and cut into slices about 1.5cm thick', '2 tablespoons sunflower oil', '1 large Spanish onion, thinly sliced', '250g (about 2 cups) pancetta, sliced into strips', '4 garlic cloves, chopped', '100ml (about cup) dry white wine', '1 bay leaf', '200ml (about 1 cup) heavy cream', "450g (about 1 lb.) Reblochon cheese, cut into cm (about in.)-thick slices, don't remove the rind"
Instructions:
1. Cook the sliced potatoes in a large pan of boiling salted water for - minutes, until just tender but not breaking up.
2. Drain and set aside.
3. Meanwhile, heat the oil in a large, ovenproof frying pan (or an ovenproof dish), then add the onion and cook for 8- minutes until translucent.
4. Add the pancetta and cook for another 5 minutes, then stir in the garlic.
5. Continue on the heat, stirring occasionally, until the bacon is cooked through and the onions turn lightly golden.
6. Drain off any excess fat.
7. Raise the heat, then pour the white wine into the pan and add the bay leaf.
8. Cook for a couple of minutes, stirring to deglaze the pan, until the wine has reduced by about a third.
9. Add the cream and simmer until that has reduced by about a third too.
10. Remove the pan from the heat and add the potatoes, stirring carefully to coat them with the creamy mixture - it doesn't matter if they start to break up a little.
11. Remove the bay leaf and season the mixture to taste.
12. Nestle about two-thirds of the chunks of cheese in among the potatoes and onions, then scatter the rest over the top.
13. Transfer the pan to the oven and bake for about minutes at 180ACGas Mark 4, until golden brown and bubbling.
14. Leave to settle for a few minutes, then serve with a salad of vinaigrette-dressed Little Gems and a few cornichons to cut through the creaminess of the Reblochon.
15. Enjoy with a Burgundian oaked Chardonnay such as St VAran.

Recipe name: Broiled Goat Cheese Toasts With Marinated Greens
Ingredients: '2 bunches (about 1 pounds) leafy greens: Swiss chard, kale, mature spinach, beet greens, or a combination of any of the above, washed', '2 tablespoons olive oil, divided', '1 clove garlic, minced', 'Pinch red pepper flakes, or to taste', 'Salt', 'Fresh lemon juice', ' slices of baguette about inch thick, or other bread cut into bite-size pieces', 'Olive oil', ' cup soft goat cheese', '1 cup Marinated Greens, at room temperature', 'Zest of 1 lemon', 'Flaky salt', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Trim off and discard the tough stems from the greens.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon of the olive oil in a wide skillet or saucepan over medium-low heat.
3. Add the garlic and red pepper flakes and stir for about 1 minute, until fragrant.
4. Pile in the greens, in batches if necessary, and add a splash of water (you can also cover with a lid to compress them).
5. Add a big pinch of salt and gently cook, stirring with tongs, until wilted and tender.
6. Cooking times will vary depending on type of green, so watch and taste as you go.
7. If cooking a combination of different greens, cook each type separately.
8. Add a splash of water to the pan if it dries out.
9. Transfer to a colander to drain and cool until safe to handle.
10. Gently squeeze out excess liquid using your hands or a spatula or wooden spoon, pressing against the side of the colander.
11. Coarsely chop the greens, then transfer them to a medium bowl and toss with the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil and a few drops of lemon juice.
12. Taste and add more lemon juice, red pepper flakes, or salt as needed.
13. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator; allow to come to room temperature before serving.
14. Preheat the broiler to high.
15. Arrange the toasts on a baking sheet and brush both sides with olive oil.
16. Transfer to the broiler and broil for 2 to 4 minutes, until lightly golden on the top.
17. Flip the toasts.
18. Smear each slice with about 2 teaspoons goat cheese, drizzle with a bit of olive oil, and return to the broiler.
19. Broil, watching carefully, for just a minute or two, to heat through and lightly brown.
20. Transfer to a serving platter and divide the greens on top.
