21. Zest the lemon over everything, sprinkle with a bit of flaky salt and black pepper, add a drizzle of olive oil, and serve while the toasts are still warm.

Ingredients: '1 cup ( pint) cherry tomatoes, halved', '1 tablespoon soy sauce', '1 teaspoon olive oil', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '1 block silken tofu, about ounces, chilled', 'Toasted sesame oil', 'Thinly sliced scallions (optional)', 'Thinly sliced basil (optional)'
Instructions:
1. In a small saucepan or skillet, combine the tomatoes, soy sauce, olive oil, and many grinds of black pepper.
2. Place over low heat and cook for 5 to 7 minutes, just until the tomatoes start to soften and juices collect in the pan.
3. Carefully cut the tofu into 2 wide slabs (or 4 smaller ones) and place on plates or in shallow bowls.
4. Divide the warm tomatoes on top.
5. Add a judicious drizzle of toasted sesame oil, then sprinkle with scallions and basil, if using.
6. Serve immediately.

Ingredients: '3 tablespoons cayenne pepper', '1 tablespoons roasted peanuts, ground into a A Ane powder', '1 tablespoon paprika', '1 tablespoon garlic powder', '1 tablespoon onion powder', ' tablespoon ground ginger', '2 teaspoons kosher salt', '2 Maggi bouillon cubes, crushed', '1 pound jumbo shrimp, peeled and deveined', '1 pound Wagyu rib eye beef, thinly sliced into strips', '1 pound boneless chicken thighs, cut into 2-inch pieces', '2 medium vine-ripened tomatoes, stemmed', '1 tablespoon olive oil', 'Kosher salt', '2 tablespoons canola oil', '1 medium white onion, thinly sliced', '1 cup heavy cream'
Instructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine all the spice blend ingredients until incorporated.
2. In three separate bowls, place the shrimp, beef, and chicken.
3. Divide the spice blend equally among the bowls and mix well to coat the meats.
4. Cover each bowl in plastic wrap and transfer to the refrigerator to marinate for 1 hour.
5. Preheat oven to .
6. On a rimmed baking sheet, toss the tomatoes with the olive oil and salt.
7. Transfer to the oven and roast for minutes, then remove and let cool.
8. In a large skillet, heat the canola oil over medium-high heat.
9. Add the onion and cook until soft and translucent, 6 to 8 minutes.
10. Add the tomatoes and cook until they have fully broken down, minutes.
11. Stir in the cream and bring to a light simmer.
12. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the cream has reduced and become thick.
13. Transfer to a blender and purAe until smooth.
14. Season with salt and set aside.
15. Light a grill or place a grill pan over medium-high heat.
16. Skewer the shrimp, rib eye, and chicken on soaked -inch bamboo skewers.
17. Grill, turning occasionally, until cooked through, 2 to 3 minutes for the shrimp, 2 to 3 minutes for the rib eye, and 6 to 8 minutes for the chicken.
18. Serve alongside the roasted tomato soubise.

Ingredients: '4 cups diced small onions', '2 tablespoons minced garlic', '2 whole tomatoes, chopped', '2 tablespoons minced ginger', '1 teaspoon minced Scotch bonnet pepper', '1 cup red palm oil', '2 cups ground egusi seeds', '1 teaspoon crayfish powder', '3 Maggi cubes', ' teaspoon dried iru powder', '2 quarts chicken stock', '1 pound goat shoulder, cubed', 'Kosher salt', '2 tablespoons canola oil', '2 cups pumpkin leaf or spinach, strained dry'
Instructions:
1. In a large saucepan, over medium heat, sweat the onion, garlic, tomatoes, ginger, and Scotch bonnet pepper in the palm oil until vegetables are translucent, about minutes.
2. Add the egusi seeds and toast in oil for minutes.
3. Add crayfish powder, Maggi, iru, and chicken stock and simmer for minutes Meanwhile, in a separate pan, over high heat, season the goat meat with salt and sear in canola oil until browned on all sides, about 7 minutes.
4. Add the goat meat to the egusi mixture and simmer together for about an hour, or until the meat is tender.
5. Add the pumpkin leaves during the last minutes of cooking.
6. The sauce should be thick and most of the liquid should have simmered off.
7. Season to taste with salt.
8. Swerve with fufu or pounded yam.

Ingredients: '1 oz. London Dry gin', ' oz. fresh lemon juice', ' oz. maraschino liqueur', '2 dashes orange bitters', 'Garnish: Luxardo cherry'
Instructions:
1. Add all ingredients to a cocktail shaker and shake until well chilled.
2. Strain into a Nick and Nora glass and add cherry.

Ingredients: '1 lb. Chinese broccoli or broccolini, trimmed, cut in thirds crosswise', ' cup soy paste (such as Yu Ding Xing)'
Instructions:
1. Set a steamer basket in a large pot filled with 1" water, cover pot, and bring water to a boil.
2. Add broccoli to steamer basket, cover pot, and steam broccoli until crisp-tender, about 5 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, whisk soy paste and 2 Tbsp. water in a large bowl.
4. Arrange broccoli on a platter and drizzle sauce over.

Recipe name: Alfajores With Coconut Dulce De Leche
Ingredients: '1 (-oz.) can sweetened condensed milk', '1 (.5-oz.) can unsweetened coconut milk', '2 tsp. kosher salt, divided', ' cup ( g) almond flour or meal', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', '1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, room temperature', 'A cup ( g) granulated sugar', '1 large egg yolk', '1 tsp. vanilla extract', ' cup plus 2 Tbsp. honey', ' cups ( g) unsweetened shredded coconut', '1 Tbsp. (or more) any color plant-based food-coloring powder', '1 Tbsp. (or more) second color plant-based food-coloring powder', 'Luster dust (optional)', 'Special Equipment: Assorted (2" or smaller) cookie cutters'
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
2. Bring condensed milk, coconut milk, and tsp. salt to a boil in a small saucepan over medium-high heat.
3. Scrape mixture into a 13x9" baking dish and bake, stirring every minutes or so, until very thick and a light caramel color, minutes.
4. Mixture will look lumpy, broken, and very pastyAthatAs exactly what you want.
5. Let cool in dish 1 hour, then transfer to a food processor and process until smooth.
6. Set dulce de leche aside for serving.
7. Meanwhile, whisk almond flour and cups all-purpose flour in a medium bowl to combine.
8. Using an electric mixer on medium-high speed, beat butter, sugar, and tsp. salt in a large bowl, scraping down sides and bottom of bowl as needed, until pale and creamy, about 3 minutes.
9. Add egg yolk, vanilla, and cup honey and beat until combined, about 2 minutes.
10. Reduce speed to low, add dry ingredients, and mix, scraping down sides and bottom of bowl, until incorporated.
11. Divide dough in half and pat each piece into a 1"-thick disk.
12. Wrap in plastic and chill until firm, at least 2 hours.
13. Preheat oven to .
14. Roll out 1 disk of dough on a lightly floured sheet of parchment paper, dusting with more flour as needed to keep dough from sticking, to about A" thick.
15. Punch out shapes with lightly floured cookie cutters and transfer to 2 parchment-lined baking sheets, spacing 1" apart.
16. Pat scraps into a 1"-thick disk, wrap with plastic, and chill minutes if soft.
17. Bake a sheet of cookies, rotating halfway through, until edges are lightly browned, 7A9 minutes.
18. Let cool minutes on baking sheet, then transfer to a wire rack and let cool completely.
19. Bake remaining sheet of cookies.
20. Line baking sheets with fresh sheets of parchment paper and repeat process with remaining disk of dough.
21. Divide coconut evenly among 3 bowls.
22. Add 1 Tbsp. food-coloring powder (sift if needed) to a bowl of coconut and toss until coconut is coated; add more powder if a more intense color is desired.
23. Repeat with remaining food-coloring powder and another bowl of coconut.
24. Leave 1 bowl of coconut white.
25. If using, add some luster dust to all 3 bowls and toss to combine.
26. Turn half of the cookies over to expose flat side; spread 1 tsp. dulce de leche over (this will be the inside of the sandwiches); set aside.
27. Heat remaining 2 Tbsp. honey in a small bowl in the microwave just until warm (you want it to be loose), then stir in 2 tsp. warm water.
28. Working one at a time, lightly brush honey over the top of remaining cookies (the ones that have not been filled with dulce de leche).
29. Immediately dip tops into a bowl of coconut; press additional coconut onto cookie to cover any bald spots.
30. Close up cookie sandwiches, coconut side up.
31. Do Ahead: Dulce de leche can be made 1 month ahead; place in an airtight container and freeze.
32. Thaw in refrigerator overnight before using.
33. Dough can be made 3 days ahead; keep chilled.
34. Cookies can be baked (but not assembled) 2 days ahead; store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: '3 ounces spinach (ideally Chinese water spinach; about 2 cups packed)', '8 large eggs', ' tablespoons skim milk', ' teaspoons kosher salt', '2 tablespoons vegetable oil', '1 teaspoon sesame oil', '1 teaspoon oyster sauce', '1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', ' Boiled Dumpling Wrappers'
Instructions:
1. Bring a medium pot of water to a boil over high heat.
2. Add the spinach and cook for seconds, until it turns a vivid green, then, using a slotted spoon, transfer it to a bowl of ice water.
3. Chill thoroughly and drain.
4. Wrap the spinach in a clean cheesecloth or tea towel to wring out excess moisture, then roughly chop it and set it aside.
5. In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and teaspoon of the salt and set aside.
6. In a medium nonstick skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium-low heat until a few drops of water added to the pan sizzle and evaporate.
7. Pour in the egg mixture and cook, stirring occasionally, for 4 minutes, just until the eggs form fluffy curds but have not fully set; they should still be slightly runny.
8. Remove from the heat and let cool in a medium bowl.
9. Use your hands to gently fold the eggs, sesame oil, oyster sauce, remaining teaspoons of salt, and pepper together until fully combined.
10. Gently fold in the spinach and mix until fully incorporated.
11. Bring a large pot of water to a boil.
12. Meanwhile, make the dumplings.
13. Holding a wrapper in your palm, use a fork to add about 1 tablespoon of the filling to the center of the wrapper, then lightly pat down the filling with the fork to get rid of any air bubbles.
14. Fold the dumpling into the round yuan bao shape: Cradle the wrapper in your hands and fold the edge closest to you over the filling.
15. Lightly squeeze the dumpling to push out any air bubbles.
16. Clasp one end of the dumpling between your thumb and index finger to pinch it shut; repeat on the other side of the dumpling.
17. Cradle the dumpling in your palms, clasping the sealed edge between your thumbs and index fingers, and squeeze it shut while pushing inward, making sure to squeeze out any air bubbles.
18. The dumpling's belly should form a teardrop shape between your thumbs, which will create the yuan bao shape.
19. Inspect the dumpling for any fissures that could rupture during cooking and pinch them shut.
20. Repeat with the rest of the wrappers.
21. Working in batches, add the dumplings to the pot, 6 at a time.
22. Boil for 2 minutes on high, then reduce the heat to medium-high and cook for 1 minute, then reduce the heat again to medium and cook for 2 more minutes.
23. The dumplings are ready a minute or so after they rise to the surface; their skins will turn puffy.
24. Using a slotted spoon, gently transfer the dumplings to a plate and serve immediately.
25. Bring the water back to a boil over high heat and repeat with the remaining dumplings.

Ingredients: '2 (3-inch) pieces ginger, scrubbed, thinly sliced, divided', ' cup dark soy sauce, divided', '2 tsp. Five-Spice Powder, divided', ' chicken wing pieces (about lb.)', '3 Tbsp. vegetable or sunflower oil', '1 shallot, thinly sliced', '3 scallions, roots trimmed, halved crosswise', '5 garlic cloves, smashed', '1 red finger or Fresno chile, halved lengthwise', '3 oz. Chinese rock or lump sugar or cup raw sugar', '3 whole star anise', '2 pieces dried licorice root (optional)', '1 (4-inch-long) cassia or cinnamon stick', ' tsp. freshly ground white pepper (optional)', ' tsp. Sichuan peppercorns', ' cup low-sodium soy sauce', ' cup unseasoned rice wine', 'Thickly sliced cucumbers (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Mix half of ginger, cup dark soy sauce, and 1 tsp.
2. Five-Spice Powder in a medium bowl.
3. Add wings; toss to coat.
4. Cover and chill at least 1 hour and up to 8 hours.
5. Heat oil in a wok or large deep-sided skillet over medium-high.
6. Add shallot, scallions, garlic, chile, and remaining ginger and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
7. Add rock sugar, star anise, licorice root (if using), cassia, white pepper (if using), Sichuan peppercorns, and remaining 1 tsp.
8. Five-Spice Powder and stir to combine.
9. Pour in low-sodium soy sauce, remaining cup dark soy sauce, and 1 cup water.
10. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low.
11. Lift chicken wings out of marinade; discard marinade.
12. Add wings to wok and pour wine over.
13. Stir gently, then bring to a simmer.
14. Cover and cook over medium-low minutes.
15. Gently stir, then cover again and continue cooking until chicken is cooked through and a deep brown color, about minutes longer.
16. Using a spider or slotted spoon, transfer wings to a plate.
17. Pour liquid in wok through a fine-mesh sieve into a small bowl; discard solids.
18. Return strained liquid to wok and bring to a simmer over medium-high.
19. Cook until sauce thickly coats a spoon, minutes.
20. Return chicken wings to sauce and toss to coat.
21. Using a spider or slotted spoon, transfer to a platter.
22. Serve with cucumbers if using.

Ingredients: ' scallions', ' cup grapeseed or vegetable oil', '1 lb. fresh Shanghai or lo mein noodles or dried lo mein noodles', ' cup soy paste', '2 Tbsp. soy sauce'
Instructions:
1. Cut scallions crosswise into thirds, separating dark green parts from white and pale green parts.
2. Slice lengthwise into very thin strips, keeping dark green and white parts separate.
3. Pour oil into a cold large wok or high-sided skillet.
4. Add white parts of scallions to oil and set over medium-low.
5. Cook until oil starts to bubble, about 5 minutes.
6. Add dark green parts of scallions and stir to combine.
7. Cook, stirring occasionally, until scallions are crisped and deep golden brown, minutes.
8. Do not rush this; slow, gradual browning as the liquid in the scallions evaporates will yield the best flavor.
9. Using a spider or slotted spoon, transfer scallions to paper towels to drain.
10. Let scallions and oil cool.
11. Pour oil through a fine-mesh sieve into an airtight container; discard solids.
12. Cover and chill scallion oil until ready to use.
13. Cook noodles in a large pot of boiling water according to manufacturerAs directions, adding 1 Tbsp. reserved scallion oil to the cooking water when you add the noodles.
14. Drain noodles, reserving cup cooking liquid, and return to pot.
15. Add cup scallion oil and toss to coat.
16. Add soy paste and toss to combine.
17. Add soy sauce and toss again, adding cooking liquid as needed to loosen sauce.
18. Transfer noodles to a bowl or platter and top with three-fourths of crispy scallions.
19. Serve with remaining scallions in a small bowl alongside.
20. Do Ahead: Scallions can be fried 1 day ahead.
21. Keep scallion oil chilled; store scallions between layers of paper towels in an airtight container at room temperature.

Ingredients: '4 ("-thick) boneless pork loin chops (about 1 lb.)', 'A cup soy sauce', 'A cup unseasoned rice vinegar', '2 Tbsp. soy paste', '2 tsp. granulated sugar', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper', ' tsp. freshly ground white pepper (optional)', ' tsp. ground cinnamon', ' tsp. Five-Spice Powder', ' cups Chinese sweet potato starch', 'Vegetable oil (about 3 cups; for frying)', 'Gochugaru (fine Korean red pepper powder; for serving; optional)', 'A deep-fry thermometer'
Instructions:
1. Working one at a time, place pork chops on a cutting board and use the back of a cleaver or heavy chefAs knife to pound meat, moving knife back and forth across the surface, to A"" thick.
2. The surface will be jagged and textured.
3. Whisk together soy sauce, vinegar, soy paste, sugar, black pepper, white pepper (if using), cinnamon, Five-Spice Powder, and A cup water in a large bowl.
4. Add pork chops and turn to coat well.
5. Cover and chill at least 2 hours and up to 6 hours.
6. Remove pork from marinade and pat dry.
7. Place sweet potato starch in a large shallow dish.
8. Working one at a time, dredge pork in starch to coat well on both sides; shake off excess and transfer to a plate.
9. Clip thermometer to the side of a large skillet or pot and pour in oil to come " up sides.
10. Heat over medium-high until thermometer registers .
11. Working in batches, fry pork until just golden, about seconds per side.
12. Transfer to a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet and let drain.
13. Repeat process, frying pork a second time until deep golden brown, about seconds per side.
14. Return to rack and let cool slightly.
15. Transfer pork to a cutting board; slice into strips.
16. Arrange in a single layer on a platter; sprinkle with gochugaru if using.

Ingredients: '3 cups Chex rice cereal (about 3.5 oz.)', '2 cups wasabi peas (about 6 oz.)', '1 (3.5-oz.) bag Dang sticky-rice chips', '1 (3.3-oz.) bag baked snap pea crisps', '1 (3.1-oz.) bag Dang coconut chips', ' cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, melted', '2 (0.-oz.) packages SeaSnax, torn into quarters', '1 Tbsp. plus 2 tsp. garlic powder', '1 Tbsp. finely grated lime zest (from about 2 limes)', ' tsp. cayenne pepper', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. sugar', ' cup dried dill, plus more for serving'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Toss rice cereal, wasabi peas, rice chips, snap pea crisps, and coconut chips in a large bowl.
3. Drizzle butter over and toss gently to coat.
4. Transfer to a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet and bake until lightly golden brown and warmed through, minutes.
5. Meanwhile, wipe out bowl and set aside.
6. Pulse SeaSnax, garlic powder, lime zest, cayenne, salt, sugar, and cup dill in a spice grinder or mini food processor, shaking or scraping down sides as needed, until finely ground.
7. As soon as snack mix comes out of the oven, transfer in 3 batches to reserved bowl, sprinkling each batch after adding to bowl with about one-third of dill-seaweed mixture.
8. Gently toss with your hands (it should still be warm at this point, but cool enough to touch) until thoroughly coated.
9. Let cool completely.
10. Top with more dill to serve.
11. Do Ahead: Turf mix can be made 5 days ahead.
12. Let cool, then store in an airtight container at room temperature.

Ingredients: '2 tablespoons Sichuan peppercorns, toasted', '1 small zucchini, grated', '1 pound ground lamb', '3 scallions, finely chopped, white and green parts', '2 tablespoons sherry cooking wine', '1 tablespoon freshly grated ginger', '1 tablespoon soy sauce', '1 tablespoon kosher salt', ' Panfried Dumpling Wrappers', '2 tablespoons all-purpose flour', '2 tablespoons white vinegar', '1 teaspoon vegetable oil'
Instructions:
1. In a small bowl, soak the peppercorns in A cup of lukewarm water for at least 1 hour, then strain out and discard the peppercorns.
2. Wrap the zucchini in a clean cheesecloth or tea towel and squeeze to wring out excess moisture.
3. In a medium bowl, add the lamb and slowly pour in the peppercorn-infused water, a few drops at a time, and use your hands to mix it in well until the water is fully absorbed.
4. Add the scallions, wine, ginger, soy sauce, and salt, and mix well until fully incorporated.
5. Gently fold in the zucchini and mix until well combined.
6. Holding a wrapper in your palm, use a fork to add about 1 tablespoon of the filling to the center of the wrapper, then lightly pat down the filling with the fork to get rid of any air bubbles.
7. Fold the dumpling into a half-moon, pinching it shut with your thumbs and index fingers, then press the center of the dumpling while pulling on the corners to push out any air bubbles and shape it into a curved crescent.
8. Inspect the dumpling for any holes and pinch them shut.
9. Repeat with the rest of the wrappers to make dumplings.
10. In a small bowl, whisk together the flour, vinegar, and 1 cup of water until combined to make a slurry.
11. Brush the oil in a medium cast-iron or nonstick skillet and heat over medium-high heat until the oil starts to shimmer.
12. Add 6 dumplings with the sealed edges lying flat in the pan, spacing them 1 inch apart, then slowly pour in just enough of the slurry to come one-third of the way up the dumplings.
13. Partially cover the pan, leaving a small gap for steam to escape.
14. Increase the heat to high and cook for 2 minutes for cast iron (1 minute for nonstick).
15. Lower the heat to medium for 2 minutes for cast iron (3 minutes for nonstick).
16. Then lower the heat to low for 2 to 3 minutes for cast iron (3 minutes for nonstick).
17. Cook until the water has evaporated, leaving a paper-thin disk of golden-brown starch on the bottom of the pan.
18. Remove from the heat and slide a thin, flexible spatula around the rim of the pan to loosen the edges of the starch disk, then carefully slide the spatula underneath and flip the disk onto a plate in one piece, crispy side up.
19. Serve immediately, then clean the skillet and repeat with the remaining dumplings.

Ingredients: '2 cups all-purpose flour', '1A8 teaspoon kosher salt', ' cup lukewarm water', '1 egg white'
Instructions:
1. Mix the Dough: Add the flour to a wide mixing bowl and stir in the salt.
2. Stir in the water and egg white with your fingers.
3. The flour will look shaggy, like biscuit dough; as the dough comes together, run an open palm around the edge of the bowl and fold the flour into the center, spinning the bowl with your other hand as you go, until it all forms a rough clump.
4. ItAs fine if there are still pockets of dry flour.
5. Knead the Dough: Coat your work surface with a fine dusting of flour and turn the dough out of the bowl.
6. Dust your hands with flour and shape the dough into a fat log about the width of your hand.
7. Knead the dough by pushing your hands and wrists into the log and rolling it forward.
8. Then roll it back and push again.
9. Repeat a few times until the log moves easily, adding more flour if it sticks, then spin the log degrees, shape it into a horizontal log again, and knead a few more times, adding more flour if necessary.
10. Use no more flour than you need to keep the dough from drying out.
11. As you knead, the dough will get firmer and tougher with a texture reminiscent of a gummy bear.
12. ItAs ready when itAs smooth to the touch, like the surface of a pearl, not tacky, with no cracks or pockets of dry flour.
13. There may be some lumps.
14. Put the dough back in your work bowl and cover it with a sheet of plastic wrap.
15. Let it sleep for to minutes.
16. While it relaxes, you can prepare your filling.Knead Again: Dust your work surface with a little more flour, then knead it as before.
17. Work out all those lumps; after kneading about ten times you should have satin-smooth dough that forms a clean ball you can easily push into, like the gel of a shoe insert.Portion the Dough: Form your dough into a log, dust a dough scraper with a little flour, and cut the dough into four equal sections.
18. Roll each section into a log and chop it into six pieces for a total of twenty-four balls of dough, each about an inch in diameter.
19. Toss the balls with a light coating of flour and cover with a lightly moistened towel.Roll the Dough Into Wrappers: Gently smash the balls of dough into flat disks, then lightly roll an Asian-style rolling pin across them to flatten them out a bit more.
20. Hold one disk by its edge and firmly but gently roll your pin from the diskAs edge to its center.
21. Roll the same edge a few more times, using more pressure at the edge than at the center.
22. Use your other hand to turn the dough disk and reveal a new edge of the disk; roll again.
23. Continue until all edges are rolled out and the wrapper is about 3 to 4 inches in diameter.
24. Hold your rolled-out wrapper up to a light.
25. If you can see through it faintly, your wrapper is ready to go.
26. Otherwise, keep rolling.
27. Roll the edges to half the thickness of the center of the wrappers.
28. As you get faster, youAll notice that all your active hand needs to do is roll the pin back and forth while your other hand just rotates the wrapper.
29. As long as your hands stay in these fixed motions, youAll easily make even, round wrappers though they donAt need to be perfectly uniform.
30. Place freshly rolled wrappers under a lightly moistened towel to keep them from drying out.

Ingredients: '2 ounces Letherbee gin', '2 ounces sweet vermouth', '2 ounces Luxardo Bitter', '1 1A2 ounces fresh grapefruit juice', '1 1A2 ounces fresh orange juice', '1 1A2 cups crushed ice', 'Garnish: frozen orange half wheels'
Instructions:
1. Put all the ingredients in a blender and process at high speed, stopping occasionally to stir the ingredients together.
2. Continue blending at high speed, adding more crushed ice to achieve the desired consistency.
3. Serve garnished with the frozen orange half wheels.

Ingredients: '2 cups raw peanuts, preferably blanched', ' cup peanut oil', ' dried Sichuan chiles or other red Chinese chiles, 8 with seeds removed, cut into strips, left whole', '1 tsp. Sichuan peppercorns', ' tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. sugar', '1 tsp. Five-Spice Powder', '1 tsp. ground Sichuan peppercorns (from tsp. whole)', 'Pinch of freshly ground white pepper (optional)'
Instructions:
1. If using unblanched (skin-on) peanuts, place in a medium bowl and pour in water to cover.
2. Let soak at least 6 hours and up to hours.
3. Drain peanuts, then rub off skins with paper towels if needed.
4. Arrange peanuts on a rimmed baking sheet and freeze at least 2 hours and up to hours (this will dehydrate the nuts so that they crisp faster when fried).
5. If using blanched peanuts, skip the soaking and freeze directly.
6. Place frozen peanuts in a wok or medium pot and pour in oil to cover by ".
7. Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook until peanuts are golden brown and float to the surface, minutes.
8. Using a spider or slotted spoon, transfer to paper towels to drain.
9. Let oil cool.
10. Transfer all but 1 tsp. oil to an airtight container; save for another use.
11. Add sliced chiles, whole chiles, and whole Sichuan peppercorns to skillet with oil and heat over medium-high, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about 2 minutes (donAt let the chiles brownAyou want them to stay red).
12. Mix in peanuts, salt, sugar, Five-Spice Powder, ground Sichuan peppercorns, and white pepper (if using).
13. Transfer to a small bowl; let cool.
14. Do Ahead: Peanuts can be made 3 days ahead.
15. Store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: ' (4"-long) cassia or cinnamon sticks', ' whole star anise', '1 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. whole cloves', ' slices dried sand ginger (optional)', '2 Tbsp. plus tsp. Sichuan peppercorns', ' tsp. sliced dried licorice root or fennel seeds', '1 tsp. black peppercorns', '1 tsp. white peppercorns (optional)', 'A spice mill or coffee grinder'
Instructions:
1. Toast cassia, star anise, cloves, sand ginger (if using), Sichuan peppercorns, licorice root, black peppercorns, and white peppercorns (if using) in a small skillet over medium, tossing once, until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
2. Let cool; grind to a powder in spice mill or coffee grinder.
3. Do Ahead: Five-Spice Powder can be made 1 month ahead.
4. Store airtight at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 lb. ground pork', '1 scallion, finely chopped', '1 Tbsp. soy paste', '1 Tbsp. toasted sesame oil', '1 Tbsp. grapeseed or vegetable oil', '1 tsp. finely grated peeled ginger', '1 tsp. rice wine', '1 tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. freshly ground white pepper (optional)', '1 large egg', ' wonton wrappers', '3 Tbsp. toasted sesame oil', '3 Tbsp. Chinese toasted sesame paste or tahini', '2 Tbsp. soy sauce', '2 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 Tbsp. sugar', '1A3 Tbsp. store-bought or homemade chili crisp; plus more for serving (optional)', 'Thinly sliced scallions (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Using your hands, mix pork, scallion, soy paste, sesame oil, grapeseed oil, ginger, wine, salt, and pepper (if using) in a large bowl until thoroughly combined.
2. Crack egg into center of mixture and stir vigorously in one direction with a wooden spoon or rubber spatula until mixture is shaggy and lightened in color, about 4 minutes.
3. Cover and chill at least minutes and up to hours.
4. Fill a small bowl with cold water.
5. Take a wonton wrapper and lay it in your palm.
6. Using a small offset spatula or butter knife, press about 1 tsp. pork mixture into the center of wrapper.
7. Dip a finger into water and run it around edges.
8. Fold wrapper in half on a diagonal and press edges tightly together to seal.
9. Dip your finger into water again and dampen 2 bottom corners of wonton.
10. Bring corners together and pinch to seal.
11. Place on a parchment-paper-lined baking sheet.
12. Repeat with remaining wonton wrappers and filling.
13. Cover and chill until ready to cook.
14. Do Ahead: Wontons can be assembled 1 day ahead.
15. Keep chilled, or freeze up to 1 month.
16. Freeze in a single layer on baking sheet until frozen, then transfer to an airtight container.
17. Cook from frozen, about 4 minutes.
18. Whisk oil, sesame paste, soy sauce, vinegar, and sugar in a small bowl to combine, then whisk in water, 1-tablespoonful at a time, until you can drizzle sauce easily but itAs not watery.
19. If using chili crisp, stir in 1A3 Tbsp., depending on how spicy you want your sauce; set aside.
20. Bring a large pot of water to a boil.
21. Working in batches of up to at a time, boil wontons until wrappers are puckered and tightly wrapped around filling (filling should be cooked through; slice one open to double-check if unsure), about 3 minutes.
22. Using a spider or slotted spoon, transfer to a plate; let cool slightly.
23. Divide wontons among bowls and spoon reserved sauce over; top with scallions and more chili crisp if desired.

Ingredients: '500g (1lb 2oz 2 1A3 cups) white rice (basmati or patna)', '1 litre (about fl oz4 cups) water', '2 tsp salt', '400ml ( fl oz 1 2A3 cups) coconut milk', 'butter, for greasing'
Instructions:
1. Put the rice into a medium, lidded saucepan and cover with water.
2. Swirl the rice around to wash it, drain and repeat at least twice until the water is clear.
3. Then, add the 1 litre (about fl oz4 cups) of water and bring to the boil.
4. Reduce the heat to medium, half-cover with the lid, and cook until all the water is absorbed, about minutes.
5. Then, add the salt and coconut milk and stir into the rice.
6. Cook uncovered over a low to medium heat until all the milk is absorbed, about minutes depending on the rice you use.
7. If the rice is not cooked, add extra boiling water and cook over a very low heat until soft.
8. Take the rice off the heat and set aside for a couple of minutes to cool very slightly.
9. Transfer the moist rice to a platter, and then smooth and flatten it until it is about 5cm (2 inches) tall and the same shape as the platter.
10. You can either use a spatula, or you can put your hand inside a sandwich bag and use that to shape the rice.
11. Put a little buter on the spatula or the sandwich bag for a smooth effect.
12. Leave the rice to rest for 1A2 minutes, then cut it into diamond shapes or squares while slightly warm so that the pieces do not break.
13. Serve with Pol Sambol, Katta Sambol, or Ambul Thial.

Ingredients: '1 small onion, roughly chopped', '1 garlic clove, halved', '1 medium tomato, cut into quarters', '5 black peppercorns', '1 green chilli, deseeded', ' tsp chilli powder', '5 fresh curry leaves', '200g (7oz 2 cups) freshly grated or desiccated (shredded) coconut', '1 tsp Maldive fish, crushed (optional)', 'juice of lime', 'salt'
Instructions:
1. Place the onion, garlic, tomato, black peppercorns, green chilli, chilli powder and curry leaves in a food processor and blitz until you have a fine paste.
2. Place the paste in a medium bowl and add the coconut, Maldive fish (if using) and mix well by hand.
3. Then add the lime juice and salt to taste, mixing well.
4. A few crushed curry leaves can be used as a garnish.
5. Pictured left.

Recipe name: Steel-Cut Oats With Squash And Tahini
Ingredients: '2 tablespoons dairy butter or vegan butter', '1 cup steel-cut oats', '1 cup whole or unflavored, unsweetened nondairy milk', '3 cups water', '1A2 teaspoon salt', '1 cup Roasted, Mashed Butternut Squash', 'Well-stirred tahini', 'Maple syrup', 'Brown sugar', 'Flaky salt'
Instructions:
1. Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat, then add the oats.
2. Cook, stirring often, until they smell toasty and have darkened a shade, 3 to 4 minutes.
3. Watch that they donAt burn.
4. Pour in the milk and water and add the salt.
5. Bring to a boil, then stir once, partially cover the pan, and reduce to a gentle simmer.
6. Cook for minutes.
7. Stir in the squash, then remove from the heat. (The oatmeal will thicken as it cools; stir in additional water or milk when reheating.) To serve, spoon the hot oatmeal into bowls.
8. For each serving, drizzle with 1 to 2 tablespoons tahini, 1 to 2 teaspoons maple syrup, a small spoonful of brown sugar, and a good pinch of flaky salt.

Ingredients: '1 medium butternut squash, 3 to 4 pounds, or other similar winter squash', 'Olive oil', 'Salt'
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to .
2. Cut the squash in half lengthwise through the stem. (DonAt bother scooping out the seeds.) Rub the squash halves all over with olive oil and place each piece on a baking sheet, cut-side down.
3. Roast for minutes, then flip the squash and continue cooking until tender all over, another to minutesAyou should be able to pierce the neck of the squash with a paring knife and meet very little resistance.
4. Allow to cool, then scoop out the seeds and discard them.
5. Use a spoon to scoop all of the flesh into a bowl and discard the skins.
6. Coarsely mash the squash with a spoon or fork and season with salt.
7. Packed in an airtight container, it will keep in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 days.

Ingredients: '50g (about 3 tablespoons) unsalted butter', '1 small onion, diced', ' a zucchini, diced', '1 carrot (about 100g), diced', ' of a rutabaga (about 100g), diced', '1 small leek (about 100g), diced', '2 sticks of celery, diced', '2 teaspoons medium-hot curry powder', '1 bay leaf', '150ml (about cups) dry white wine', '450ml (about 2 cups) good-quality fish stock', '500ml (about 2 cup) water', '500g (about 1 lb.) mussels, cleaned, debearded and left in a bowl of clean, cold water', '1 small bunch of parsley, chopped', '4 tablespoons Greek yogurt', '4 slices of sourdough bread, toasted', '4 teaspoons mango chutney', '100g (about 7 tablespoons) butter'
Instructions:
1. Melt the butter in a large saucepan over a medium heat and when it starts to bubble, stir in the diced vegetables with a pinch of salt.
2. Cook, stirring regularly for minutes, until the vegetables are soft, but not yet taking on any colour.
3. Stir in the curry powder and cook for a further 2 minutes, then add the bay leaf, white wine, stock and water.
4. Turn the heat up to bring the soup to the boil, then turn the heat down and cook, uncovered, for minutes, to reduce the liquid by a third.
5. When youAre ready to eat, turn the heat up to high again, bring the soup to the boil and stir in the drained mussels, discarding any that are still open and do not close when you tap them.
6. Cover the pot with a lid and cook for 5 minutes, until the mussels have opened.
7. Remove the mussels with a slotted spoon and discard any that have remained closed.
8. Remove the mussels from their shells and return the meat to the soup.
9. Ladle the minestrone into warmed bowls.
10. Stir half of the parsley through the yogurt, and serve with the toasted sourdough topped with mango chutney, butter and the remaining parsley.

Recipe name: One-Pot Pie With Callaloo Plantain Goat Cheese And Cornmeal Crust
Ingredients: '1 stick cold butter', '1 cups all-purpose flour plus more for rolling', ' cup yellow cornmeal', ' teaspoon sea salt', ' cup Parmesan cheese', '2 tablespoons olive oil, divided', '2 cloves garlic, diced, divided', '1 small yellow onion, chopped, divided', '2 tablespoons chopped red bell pepper, divided', '1 or 2 Scotch bonnet peppers, seeded and diced, divided', '2 tablespoons fresh thyme leaves, divided', '2 scallions, diced, divided', '4 cups callaloo chiffonade', ' cup heavy cream, divided', '2 cups cubed ripe plantain (1-inch cubes)', 'Salt and freshly cracked black pepper to taste', ' cup canned corn kernels', '2 tablespoons chopped cilantro', '1 tablespoon butter', '1 tablespoon chopped scallion', ' cup dry white wine', ' cup heavy cream', ' cup whole milk', '1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves', '2 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese', '8 ounces fresh mozzarella cheese, cut into -inch cubes', ' cup grated Parmesan cheese, divided', ' cup goat cheese', '1 egg, beaten'
Instructions:
1. Cut the chilled butter into very small pieces.
2. Mix the flour, cornmeal, and sea salt in a food processor; add the butter, and pulse until the mixture is a sandy texture that resembles breadcrumbs.
3. Transfer to a bowl, and add half the Parmesan cheese, mixing with a fork or knife until combined. (Reserve remaining cheese for topping the pie.) Measure out 4 tablespoons of ice-cold water, and add to the flour mixture a little at a time until the dough sticks together, mixing just long enough to create a mass.
4. Do not overmix.
5. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap or place in a plastic bag; hold it in the refrigerator for at least minutes.
6. Remove one hour before use to bring to room temperature.
7. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large sautA pan over medium heat.
8. Add half the garlic, onion, bell pepper, Scotch bonnet pepper, thyme, and scallion; cook for about 5 minutes until soft.
9. Add the callaloo and cook down for about 8 minutes, until it turns bright green.
10. Stir in cup heavy cream.
11. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
12. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
13. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in another sautA pan over medium heat, and add the remaining garlic, onion, bell pepper, Scotch bonnet pepper, thyme, and scallion; sautA for about 1 minute.
14. Add the plantain and cook, stirring occasionally, for another 5 minutes.
15. Add the corn and cilantro, and sautA for another minute.
16. Add cup heavy cream, and season to taste with salt and pepper.
17. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
18. Add 1 tablespoon chopped scallion.
19. Add the white wine, and cook, stirring, until the wine is evaporated.
20. Stir in the heavy cream and whole milk.
21. Bring to a simmer.
22. Allow to simmer for about 5 minutes, until the sauce is reduced.
23. Stir in the thyme and Parmesan cheese.
24. Preheat the oven to .
25. On a lightly floured surface, roll out two-thirds of the pastry into a large circle, and use it to line the base and sides of an 8-inch pie dish or casserole.
26. Layer half the plantain mixture in the bottom, then distribute half the mozzarella and a sprinkle of Parmesan on top of that.
27. Cover with half the callaloo mixture, then half the goat cheese and more Parmesan.
28. Repeat the layers in the same order with the remaining plantain filling, mozzarella, Parmesan, callaloo filling, goat cheese, and more Parmesan.
29. Pour the sauce over the assembled pie.
30. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the rest of the pastry into an 8-inch round; lay it on top of the pie, trimming away any excess pastry and crimping the edges to seal well.
31. Score an X in the middle of the top crust with a sharp knife.
32. Brush the top of the pie with the beaten egg, scatter with the rest of the Parmesan, and chill for at least minutes.
33. Bake for to minutes until the pastry is golden-brown on top.
34. Allow to cool in the pan for minutes before serving.

Instructions:

Instructions:

Ingredients: ' cup plus 2 Tbsp ml sunflower oil or other neutral oil, plus more as needed', ' yellow onion, finely diced', '2 (.5 oz g) cans cranberry or pinto beans, drained, or 3 cups g cooked homemade beans', '1 tsp kosher salt', ' tsp ground black pepper', '2 Tbsp chopped cilantro', '1 Tbsp chopped dill', '4 large sheets homemade or store-bought (see note) Lavash'
Instructions:
1. To make the filling, in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat, warm 2 Tbsp ml of the oil.
2. Stir in the onion, lower the heat to medium, and cook, stirring occasionally, until translucent, about 4 minutes.
3. Stir in the beans, salt, and pepper and cook until the beans absorb some of the seasoning and are hot all the way through, about 2 minutes.
4. Turn off the heat.
5. Using a potato masher or an immersion blender, mash the beans in the pot to make a paste.
6. Mash in the cilantro and dill and let cool to room temperature.
7. Cut the lavash into strips 3 in 7.5 cm thick and about in .5 cm long.
8. Keep cut lavash covered or in a plastic bag so the strips donAt dry out.
9. To shape each triangle, position a strip of lavash in front of you vertically.
10. Place about 2 Tbsp of bean paste at the bottom end of the strip.
11. Pull the bottom left corner of the strip to the right side, about 3 in 7.5 cm up from the bottom, to create a triangle that encases the beans.
12. Next, take the bottom right corner of the triangle and fold it away from you to the left edge of the lavash to enclose the filling, ArollingA the triangle up the lavash and adding a layer to the triangle.
13. Continue to fold the triangle all the way up the strip until all of the lavash is used up.
14. If any extra lavash hangs off the edge, trim it with scissors.
15. Once finished, place the triangle seam-side down and repeat with the remaining strips and filling.
16. Line a plate with paper towels.
17. Heat 2 Tbsp of oil in a large sautA pan over medium-high heat.
18. Place half of the triangles seam-side down in the oil and fry until golden brown on one side, about 1 minute, adjusting the heat as needed to prevent the lavash from burning.
19. Flip the triangles over to brown the other side, seconds to 1 minute more.
20. Transfer to the paper towelAlined plate and repeat with the remaining oil and triangles.
21. If the pan seems too dry after flipping over the triangles, add more oil.
22. Let cool slightly before serving.
23. Leftovers keep, refrigerated, for up to 3 days and can be reheated in a toaster oven for 3 to 5 minutes until hot in the center.

Ingredients: '1 cup chopped fresh pineapple', '1 cup blackberries', ' cup basil leaves', '1 cup coconut water', 'Juice of lime'
Instructions: 1. In a blender, combine all the ingredients and process until smooth.

Ingredients: '2 tsp. baking powder', ' cups ( g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting', ' tsp. kosher salt, plus more', '2 cups (packed; g) mint leaves', '1 cup ( g) granulated sugar', ' cups ( sticks) unsalted butter, room temperature', '1 large egg', '1 large egg yolk', 'Coarse sanding sugar or Swedish pearl sugar (for decorating)', '1 cup ( g) powdered sugar', '2 Tbsp. ( g) Dutch-process or unsweetened cocoa powder', '1 Tbsp. light corn syrup', 'Special Equipment: A 3"-diameter cookie cutter'
Instructions:
1. Whisk baking powder, cups flour, and tsp. salt in a medium bowl to combine.
2. Pulse mint and granulated sugar in a food processor until mint is finely chopped.
3. Add butter; process to combine.
4. Add dry ingredients and pulse again to combine.
5. Add egg and egg yolk; pulse until a shaggy dough forms.
6. Turn out dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead just until dough holds its shape.
7. Divide dough in half; pat each portion into a 1"-thick disk.
8. Wrap disks in plastic and chill until firm, at least 2 hours.
9. Place racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat to .
10. Working with 1 disk at a time, roll out dough on a lightly floured sheet of parchment paper to about " thick, dusting with more flour as needed to keep from sticking.
11. Punch out rounds with lightly floured cookie cutter and arrange on 2 parchment-lined baking sheets, spacing 1" apart.
12. Pat dough scraps into a 1"-thick disk.
13. Wrap in plastic and chill minutes if soft.
14. Repeat process with scraps.
15. Hold a bench scraper or straightedge upright 1" from edge of a cookie to cordon off a shallow semicircle (do not press down into dough; youAre just making a barrier).
16. Coat semicircle with sanding sugar; gently press to adhere.
17. Remove scraper; you should have a tidy sugared edge.
18. Repeat with remaining cookies and more sanding sugar.
19. Bake cookies, rotating baking sheets top to bottom and back to front halfway through, until edges are lightly browned, minutes.
20. Let cool minutes on baking sheets, then transfer to 2 wire racks set inside 2 rimmed baking sheets; let cool completely.
21. Sift powdered sugar and cocoa powder into a medium bowl.
22. Add corn syrup, a pinch of salt, and 2 Tbsp. warm water and stir until smooth and glossy.
23. Position bench scraper to make another shallow semicircle on the other side of the cookie, angling it so that it forms a V with the sugared edge, lightly pressing scraper into cookie to make a barrier.
24. Spoon 1 tsp. glaze onto exposed semicircle and use a toothpick to help coat evenly; excess will drip over sides.
25. Lift scraper straight up and over chocolate side to create a neat edge.
26. Repeat with remaining cookies and glaze.
27. Let sit until glaze is set, at least 2 hours.
28. Do Ahead: Dough can be made 1 month ahead; freeze.
29. Thaw in refrigerator before using.
30. Cookies can be baked 1 day ahead; store tightly wrapped on a rimmed baking sheet at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 cup ( g) all-purpose flour', ' tsp. baking soda', ' tsp. ground cinnamon', ' tsp. ground cloves', ' cup ( g) plus 6 Tbsp. ( g) rye flour, divided', '3 tsp. ground ginger, divided', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', 'A cup ( g) finely chopped crystallized ginger', 'A cup robust-flavored (dark) molasses', ' cup (packed; g) dark brown sugar', ' cup ( sticks) unsalted butter, melted, plus 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature', '2 large egg yolks', '6 Tbsp. ( g) raw sugar'
Instructions:
1. Whisk all-purpose flour, baking soda, cinnamon, cloves, cup rye flour, 2 tsp. ground ginger, and 1 tsp. salt in a medium bowl to combine; add crystallized ginger and toss until coated and evenly distributed.
2. Whisk molasses, brown sugar, and cup melted butter in a medium bowl to combine.
3. Add egg yolks and whisk just until egg yolks are absorbed but mixture is still very dark, about seconds.
4. Add dry ingredients and fold until no streaks of flour remain.
5. Let sit, uncovered, at room temperature minutes (batter will thicken as it sits).
6. Meanwhile, place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to .
7. Whisk raw sugar, remaining 6 Tbsp. rye flour, remaining 1 tsp. ground ginger, and remaining tsp. salt in a small bowl to combine.
8. Add remaining room-temperature butter and smash together until mixture resembles clumpy wet sand; this is your streusel topping.
9. Using a 1-oz. ice cream scoop, portion out dough (about 2 Tbsp. each) and roll into balls between your palms.
10. Working one at a time, toss balls in streusel mixture to coat and place on 2 parchment-lined baking sheets, spacing 3" apart.
11. Bake 1 sheet of cookies until they have spread and are slightly cracked and just set around the edges and streusel is lightly browned, minutes.
12. Let cool minutes on baking sheet.
13. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool completely.
14. Bake remaining sheet of cookies.
15. Do Ahead: Dough can be rolled into balls 2 weeks ahead.
16. Freeze on a baking sheet; transfer to resealable plastic freezer bags.
17. Let sit at room temperature minutes before rolling in streusel and baking.

Recipe name: Malaysian Chicken Curry With Buttermilk Beer Beignets
Ingredients: '1 A tablespoons Yeo brand Malaysian curry powder', '1 teaspoon sugar', '1 teaspoon kosher salt', '1 teaspoon chicken bouillon', '1 pound dark meat chicken (cut, uncut, bone-on, boneless)', 'A cup coconut milk', '2 A tablespoons whole milk', '2 teaspoons sambal badjak (an Indonesian chili paste with minced onions and dried shrimp)', ' yellow or white onion, sliced', '2 makrut lime leaves, ripped in half widthwise', 'A pound Okinawan sweet potatoes (aka Hawaiian purple sweet potatoes), peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes', 'A pound carrots, cut into 1 -inch-long pieces', 'Buttermilk Beer Beignets (optional)', 'Scallions, coarsely chopped, for garnish'
Instructions:
1. Combine curry powder, sugar, salt, and chicken bouillon in a mixing bowl and hand mix together until itAs all one love, one mix.
2. Rinse chicken under water, pat dry with paper towels, then transfer to a mixing bowl.
3. Next, pour the beautiful curry spice mix all over that chicken.
4. Mix and rub the spices all over the chicken until no chicken remains uncovered with spice.
5. Cover it all up, like itAs your only goal in life.
6. Marinate overnight.
7. Alternatively, if youAre in a hurry, marinate for 1 hour, though the curry mix wonAt fully penetrate the chicken or touch the souls of others, which is what eating is all about.
8. When ready to cook, youAll need a pan to sautA with and a pot for stewing and braising.
9. Combine coconut milk, whole milk, and sambal badjak in pot.
10. Next, heat the sautA pan to medium-high heat.
11. Add a little bit of cooking oil, add some onions and some lime leaves, and sear batches of chicken until chicken turns golden brown all around, while sweating onions and leaves.
12. Do not cook the chicken through; just brown and sear.
13. The lime leaves will fill your kitchen with their incredible citrus scentAa pretty amazing smell.
14. After each batch is evenly seared, transfer into pot.
15. To ensure the chicken cooks evenly, do not heat up the pot until all chicken is seared.
16. Continue searing onions, lime leaves, and chicken.
17. Once all chicken is seared, sautA any remaining lime leaves, and onions, and transfer to the pot before cooking.
18. If there is any excess spice mix in the chicken marinade bowl, also transfer into the pot because the spice mix is measured specifically to give the best flavor for the curry sauce itself and not just the marinade.
19. Bring pot to a boil over high heat, then simmer covered with a slight opening for minutes, or until the chicken is tender but not falling apart.
20. The curry consistency should be thin and almost milky.
21. Once chicken is done, turn off heat.
22. While waiting for the chicken to finish cooking, prepare an ice water bath in a bowl.
23. Bring 2 inches of water to a rolling boil in a pot over high heat.
24. Cook the sweet potatoes in the boiling water for 6A8 minutes.
25. Check often to see when the potatoes are cooked through; they should be crunchy, still firm and not mushy.
26. Once theyAre done, remove the potatoes from pot (but do not dump the boiling water; save it for blanching the carrots) and shock them immediately in the ice water bath for 2 minutes, or until they are completely cool to the touch.
27. Strain and set aside cooked potatoes in a fresh bowl.
28. Next, blanch the carrots by repeating the steps as for the potatoes, checking for nonmushy doneness.
29. If you use baby carrots, they take about 4A5 minutes to blanch.
30. Adjust time accordingly for the type of carrots youAre using and how theyAre cut.
31. Cook, shock, strain, then set aside with the potatoes.
32. Chicken can be served immediately, but it will be most flavorful if cooled down, stored, and covered overnight in the fridge, and then reheated and served the next day.
33. Blanched potatoes and carrots can also be stored overnight.
34. When ready to serve (not long beforeAjust right when you are ready to serve), add carrots and potatoes to the hot chicken curry.
35. Garnish with scallions.
36. Serve with steamed rice, and if you want to soak up that curry deliciousness (my favorite part), sop it up with some Buttermilk Beer Beignets.
37. And thatAs the best experience with chicken curry that you can haveAreaching the epitome of chicken curry enlightenment.
38. Enjoy, kids!

Ingredients: '6 jasmine green tea bags', ' cup honey', ' sprigs mint, divided', '6 lemons, divided'
Instructions:
1. Bring 3 quarts water to a boil in a medium pot.
2. Remove from heat and add tea bags, honey, and 6 mint sprigs.
3. Cover and let sit 8 minutes.

5. Meanwhile, slice 4 lemons in half and squeeze juice into a small bowl.

7. Add lemon juice, lemon slices, and remaining 6 mint sprigs to hot toddy.
8. Ladle into mugs.

Recipe name: Almond Pad Thai With Shiritaki Noodles
Ingredients: '2 tablespoons coconut oil', '2 cups broccoli florets', '2 cups shredded peeled carrots', ' red pepper, thinly sliced', ' cup peas', '6 tablespoons coconut aminos', '2 tablespoons smooth almond butter', '2 tablespoons filtered water', '1 (7-ounce) package fettuccine shirataki noodles, prepared according to package instructions', ' cup thinly sliced green onions', '2 tablespoons sesame seeds'
Instructions:
1. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat the coconut oil.

Ingredients: '4 limes', '7 navel oranges, divided', '2 small jalapeAos, divided', '1 cup sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt', 'Club soda (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Juice limes and 6 oranges into a large pitcher. (You should have about cup lime juice and cups orange juice.) Discard lime rinds and half of the orange rinds.
2. Cut remaining orange rinds into quarters and place in a large saucepan.
3. Coarsely chop 1 jalapeAo and add to orange rinds along with sugar and salt.
4. Using a muddler or dowel-style rolling pin, muddle rinds until thoroughly smooshed and much of the sugar and salt is dissolved, about 3 minutes.
5. Stir in 1 cup warm water and let sit at least minutes and up to 1 hour.
6. Strain orange mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into pitcher with juice, gently pressing with a spoon to extract as much liquid as possible; discard solids.
7. Thinly slice remaining orange and add to pitcher.
8. Remove seeds from remaining jalapeAo; thinly slice into rounds and add to pitcher.

10. Top off with club soda; stir to combine.

12. Cover and chill.

Ingredients: '3 (3A) cinnamon sticks', '1 (4A) piece ginger, thinly sliced lengthwise', ' whole cloves', '6 cups pomegranate juice', 'A cup sugar', 'Club soda and lemon twists (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Bring cinnamon sticks, ginger, cloves, pomegranate juice, and sugar to a boil in a medium saucepan.
2. Cook until reduced to cups, about minutes.

4. Let cool.
5. To serve, pour 1 oz. pomegranate syrup into a tall ice-filled glass.
6. Top off with club soda and stir to combine; garnish with a lemon twist.
7. Do Ahead: Syrup can be made days in ahead.
8. Chill in an airtight container.

Recipe name: Pine Nut And White Bean Dip
Ingredients: ' cup pine nuts', ' cup grapeseed or vegetable oil', '1 garlic clove', '1 (-oz.) can cannellini (white kidney) beans, rinsed', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:

12. Let come to room temperature before serving.

Ingredients: '1 tablespoon freshly squeezed lime juice', ' cup packed cilantro leaves', '1 cup unsalted cashews, dry-roasted, raw, or toasted', ' cup avocado oil', '4 garlic cloves, roughly chopped', '1 tablespoon tamari', '1 tablespoon coconut aminos', '1 jalapeAo pepper, stemmed, seeded, and roughly chopped', '2 boneless, skinless chicken breasts, pounded thin (see tip below)', '1 large broccoli head, cut into florets', '2 tablespoons avocado oil'
Instructions:

Recipe name: Instant Pot Coconut Green Curry Shrimp

Ingredients: '3 Granny Smith apples, scrubbed, coarsely chopped', ' bunch parsley, coarsely chopped (leaves and stems)', '5 celery stalks with leaves attached, coarsely chopped, plus more for serving', ' cup apple cider vinegar', 'Pinch of kosher salt', ' tsp. freshly ground black pepper, plus more for serving'
Instructions:
1. PurAe apples, parsley, 5 celery stalks, and 3 cups water in batches in a blender.
2. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a pitcher, gently pressing with a spoon to extract as much liquid as possible (alternatively, run apples, parsley, and celery through a juicer and mix with 3 cups water).

Ingredients: '1 large daikon (about 1 lb.), peeled, thinly sliced into (3"-long) matchsticks', ' tsp. kosher salt, divided', '1 red finger or Fresno chile, seeds removed, finely chopped', '2 garlic cloves, finely chopped', '2 Tbsp. toasted sesame oil', '2 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 Tbsp. granulated sugar', ' tsp. store-bought or homemade chili crisp (optional)', 'Toasted white andor black sesame seeds (for serving)'
Instructions:

Recipe name: Okonomiyaki Aas You Like Ita Pancakes With Bonito Flakes
Ingredients: '1 cups ( g) all-purpose flour', '1 teaspoon baking powder', ' teaspoon sea salt', '1 large egg, beaten', ' cups ( ml) whole milk or milk of your choice', '8 ounces ( g) cabbage, thinly sliced', '2 scallions, white and light green parts chopped', ' yellow, green, or red bell pepper, thinly sliced', '4 tablespoons vegetable oil', '8 ounces ( g) boneless chicken, shrimp, crab, or sukiyaki-style beef or pork, cut into -inch ( mm) pieces', '2 tablespoons mayonnaise', '2 tablespoon or more homemade or store-bought Tonkatsu Sauce', ' cup (4 g) bonito flakes', ' cup (4 g) crumbled nori'
Instructions:
1. Whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt in a small bowl.In a medium bowl, whisk the egg and milk.
2. Add the flour mixture and mix until just blended.
3. The batter should be quite thin.
4. Add the chopped vegetables to the batter and mix well.Heat 1 tablespoon of the oil in a medium non- stick skillet over medium-high heat.
5. Pour cup ( ml) of the batter to make a 6-inch ( cm) pancake.
6. Cook until medium brown, about 1 minute.
7. Place of the meat on top of the pancake and then flip the meat side down.
8. Turn heat to low and cook until the bottom of the pancake is browned, the meat is thoroughly cooked, and the vegetables are tenderAabout minutes.
