Ingredients: 'Canola oil', '1 pound hard paneer, cut into -inch cubes', '3 tablespoons unsalted butter', '1 large yellow onion, finely chopped', '-inch piece of ginger, peeled and grated', '6 cloves of garlic, crushed', '2 (-ounce) cans of crushed tomatoes', '1 tablespoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves)', '1 teaspoon ground cinnamon', ' teaspoon ground cloves', ' teaspoon ground red chile', '2 tablespoons honey', ' teaspoons salt', ' cups peas (fresh or defrosted)', ' cup heavy cream (plus extra to serve)', 'A handful of toasted flaked almonds'
Instructions:
1. Put a tablespoon of oil into a large lidded frying pan over medium heat and, when hot, add the cubes of paneer.
2. Fry for a couple of minutes until golden on all sides, turning regularly, then remove to a plate.
3. Put the butter into the same pan over medium heat.
4. When hot, add the onion and fry for around minutes, until translucent and turning golden.
5. Add the ginger and garlic, stir-fry for 2 to 3 minutes, then add the crushed tomatoes.
6. Cover with the lid and cook for to minutes, until reduced to a lovely thick sauce.
7. Add the fenugreek leaves, cinnamon, cloves, ground red chile, honey, and salt to the pan.
8. Stir, then add the fried paneer, cover with a lid, and cook for 5 minutes, or until cooked through.
9. Add the peas and cream and cook for a further 5 minutes.
10. To serve, scatter with the almonds and drizzle with a little extra cream.
11. This curry is perfect with steamed basmati rice.

Ingredients: '8 large eggs', ' cup whole milk or cream', ' cup grated Parmesan cheese', 'Kosher salt', 'Freshly ground black pepper', '1 cup (about 4 ounces) cooked pasta', ' cup pesto or Roasted GarlicAHerb Sauce, plus more for serving', '1 tablespoon unsalted butter'
Instructions:
1. Set a rack in the upper third of the oven and preheat it to .
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, Parmesan, teaspoon kosher salt, and a few grinds of black pepper.
3. In a separate bowl, toss the pasta with the pesto or herb sauce.
4. Melt the butter in a -inch cast-iron skillet (or other nonstick oven-safe pan) over medium heat.
5. Pour the egg mixture into the skillet, then nestle in the pasta.
6. Cook, undisturbed, until the frittata is set around the edges but still looks wet on the surface, about 5 minutes.
7. Slide the frittata into the oven and bake until the top is set, about minutes.
8. Serve the frittata warm or at room temperature, with more pesto or herb sauce on the side.

Recipe name: Salmon Confit With Lime Juniper And Fennel
Ingredients: '2 limes', ' teaspoon fine sea salt, plus more as needed', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper', '4 dried juniper berries, lightly crushed with a mortar and pestle or the side of a heavy knife', ' teaspoon fennel seeds, lightly crushed with a mortar and pestle or the side of a heavy knife', '4 (6- to 8-ounce) skinless salmon fillets', '6 sprigs fresh marjoram or thyme', 'Extra-virgin olive oil, as needed', '1 large fennel bulb, with fronds', 'Flaky sea salt, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Heat the oven to .
2. Finely grate the zest from 1 of the limes and place the zest in a bowl (reserve the lime for later).
3. Stir in the salt, pepper, and crushed juniper berries and fennel seeds.
4. Sprinkle the mixture all over the salmon fillets and place them, packed close together, in a small baking dish (a large loaf pan or an 8-inch cake pan will work).
5. Thinly slice the remaining lime and lay the slices on the fish.
6. Tuck the marjoram sprigs around the fish.
7. Cover the fish with olive oilAyouAll need at least cup, possibly even 1 cup, to submerge it.
8. Bake the fish until itAs just cooked through, to minutes. (Thinner fillets may take less timeAstart checking at minutes.) Remove the fennel fronds from the bulb and chop enough to make cup.
9. Trim the fennel bulb and thinly slice it on a mandoline or with a very sharp knife.
10. In a bowl, toss the fennel fronds and slices with a pinch of fine sea salt.
11. Juice the zested lime and add juice, to taste, to the fennel.
12. Drizzle the fennel with oil.
13. Serve the salmon and fennel sprinkled with flaky sea salt.

Recipe name: Coconut-Braised Chickpeas With Sweet Potatoes And Greens
Ingredients: ' cup slivered almonds', '2 tablespoons coconut or vegetable oil', '1 onion, thinly sliced', '2 garlic cloves, finely chopped', '1 tablespoon grated or finely chopped fresh ginger', ' teaspoons curry powder', '1 large bunch (about 8 ounces) greens, such as Swiss chard, kale, or collard greens, stems removed and leaves torn into medium-size pieces, washed, and shaken dry (there should be a little water still clinging to the leaves)', '1 (-ounce) can chickpeas, rinsed and drained', '1 (.5-ounce) can coconut milk', '2 teaspoons kosher salt', '4 Roasted Sweet Potato halves, cut into -inch pieces', '1 tablespoon fresh lime juice, plus additional wedges for serving', ' cup chopped fresh cilantro leaves and tender stems, plus more for serving', 'Plain yogurt, for serving'
Instructions:
1. Toast the almonds in a small, dry skillet set over medium heat, tossing and stirring frequently until the almonds take on a golden color, 3 to 5 minutes. (Be vigilantAthe almonds go from golden to burned like that.) Transfer the almonds to a plate and set aside to cool.
2. Warm a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and add the oil.
3. Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened a little, about 5 minutes.
4. Add the garlic, ginger, and curry powder and cook, stirring frequently, until it all gets really fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes more.
5. Add the greens to the pot and stir them into the onion mixture with a wooden spoon, scraping up any browned bits at the bottom of the pot while you do so. (If there are a lot of brown bits and theyAre sticking, add a teaspoon or two of water.) Add the chickpeas, coconut milk, 2 cups water, and the kosher salt.
6. Let the whole thing simmer, partially covered, for about minutes.
7. Add the sweet potatoes to the pot and cook for a minute or twoAjust enough to warm them through.
8. Stir in the lime juice and cilantro and taste for seasoning; add more salt or lime juice if necessary.
9. Serve the braised chickpeas in shallow bowls drizzled with yogurt, topped with the almonds and a few cilantro leaves, and with lime wedges on the side.

Ingredients: '3 large heads broccoli (3 to 4 pounds total), trimmed, cut into florets with some stalk attached (cut the florets in half if large)', ' cup olive oil', 'Kosher salt', 'Freshly ground black pepper'
Instructions:
1. Set the racks in the upper and lower thirds of the oven and preheat it to .
2. Line two large rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper.
3. Divide the broccoli between the sheets, arranging them in a single layer.
4. Drizzle each sheet of broccoli with cup oil, then season each with teaspoon salt and a few grinds of pepper.
5. Roast, stirring once and rotating the sheets halfway through, until the broccoli is crispy and charred in spots, to minutes.
6. Taste and adjust the seasoning, if desired.

Ingredients: '6 green chillies', '55ml (about cup) vegetable oil', '2 tsp. mustard seeds', ' fresh curry leaves', '300g (2 cups) Spanish white onions, sliced (a little chunky is good)', '15g (1 Tbsp.) garlic paste', '15g (1 Tbsp.) ginger paste', '2 tsp. fine sea salt', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper', ' tsp. ground turmeric', '25g ( cup) fresh root ginger, cut into matchsticks', '400ml (about 2 cups) coconut milk', '250ml (about 1 cup) coconut cream', ' large prawns', '300g (about 1 cups) medium tomatoes, cut into small bite-sized wedges', 'Lemon wedges, to serve'
Instructions:
1. Remove and discard the stalks from the chillies, then slice each one into 3 or 4 long strips.
2. Set to one side.
3. Place a large saucepan over a medium heat.
4. Add 40ml of the oil, let it warm for a few seconds, then add the mustard seeds and curry leaves.
5. Let them crackle for a few seconds.
6. Add the onions and sautA lightly for minutes, until soft but not coloured.
7. Add the garlic and ginger pastes, salt, black pepper and turmeric and sautA for 3 minutes, stirring regularly.
8. Add the sliced chillies and ginger matchsticks and cook for 3 minutes.
9. Pour in the coconut milk and cream and simmer for minutes, stirring occasionally.
10. While the curry is simmering, place a small frying pan over a medium-high heat and add the remaining 1 tbsp oil.
11. Toss in the rest of the curry leaves and fry for 1 minute, until crisp.
12. Drain on kitchen paper and set aside.
13. Add the prawns and tomatoes to the sauce and simmer gently for a further 5A6 minutes, until the prawns are cooked; do not overcook or they will be tough.
14. Serve scattered with the fried curry leaves, with lemon wedges on the side.

Recipe name: Spinach And Feta Cooked Like Saag Paneer
Ingredients: ' cup plus 2 tablespoons ghee or olive oil, divided', '2 tablespoons coriander seeds', '2 green cardamom pods, or teaspoon ground cardamom (freshly ground is best)', '1 small yellow onion, diced into -inch pieces', '1 tablespoon roughly chopped fresh ginger', '1 garlic clove, minced', '1 pound fresh baby spinach ( to cups)', ' tablespoon fresh lime juice (from about a quarter of a lime), plus more if needed', '1 small Indian green chile or serrano chile, roughly chopped', '1 teaspoon kosher salt', '6 ounces feta cheese, cut into -inch cubes (a little over cup)', '1 teaspoon cumin seeds', ' teaspoon asafetida (optional, but really great)', ' teaspoon red chile powder'
Instructions:
1. In a large pan over medium heat, warm cup of the ghee (or oil).
2. Once the ghee has melted (or the oil begins to shimmer), add the coriander and cardamom and cook, stirring, for about 2 minutes, until the seeds start to brown.
3. Add the onion and cook until it is translucent, 5 to 6 minutes.
4. Stir in the ginger and garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
5. Add the spinach and cook until it is just wilted, 4 to 5 minutes.
6. Remove the pan from the heat and add the lime juice, green chile, and salt.
7. Let cool for 5 minutes.
8. Transfer to a blender and blend into a chunky paste.
9. Return the spinach mixture to the same pan and set it over low heat.
10. Stir in cup water, then gently fold in the feta, being careful not to break up the cubes.
11. Cook for 5 to 7 minutes more to soften the feta slightly and allow it to soak up some of the spinach sauce.
12. While the feta cooks, in a small pan or butter warmer over medium-high heat, warm the remaining 2 tablespoons ghee (or oil) for 1 minute.
13. Add the cumin seeds.
14. As soon as (emphasis on as soon asAyou donAt want your cumin to burn!) the cumin seeds start to sputter and brown, about 1 minute max, remove the pan from the heat.
15. Immediately add the asafetida (if using) and red chile powder.
16. Pour all of the ghee (or oil) mixture into the spinach and feta once that is done cooking.
17. Serve with rice or roti.

Ingredients: '2 large egg whites, room temperature (reserve yolks)', ' tsp. cream of tartar', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' cup granulated sugar, divided', 'Unsweetened cocoa powder (for dusting)', '2 oz. chopped bittersweet chocolate', '6 oz. raw pistachios', '1 cups whole milk', '1 tsp. unflavored powdered gelatin', '5 large egg yolks (from meringues and frosting)', ' cup granulated sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. almond extract', '3 large egg whites (reserve yolks)', ' cup granulated sugar', ' tsp. kosher salt', '8 oz. chopped bittersweet chocolate', '1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, room temperature, cut into pieces', '1 tsp. vanilla extract', 'Neutral oil or unsalted butter (for pan)', '6 large eggs, room temperature, separated', '1 cups granulated sugar, divided', '1 tsp. vanilla extract', '1 cups cake flour', ' tsp. baking powder', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' tsp. ground cardamom, divided', '1 tsp. cream of tartar', 'Powdered sugar (for dusting)', ' cup brandy', '8 oz. chopped dark chocolate', '2 Tbsp. unsweetened cocoa powder', '2 oz. raw pistachios, finely ground', 'Powdered sugar (for dusting)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Using an electric mixer on high speed, beat egg whites, cream of tartar, and salt in a large bowl until foamy and the yellowish tinge has disappeared, about 2 minutes.
3. With the motor running, add granulated sugar about 1 Tbsp. at a time until mixture is very stiff and shiny, about 5 minutes.
4. Transfer meringue to a piping bag fitted with a " round pastry tip (or a freezer bag with a " opening cut from one bottom corner).
5. Pipe mounds ranging in size from 1" " in diameter for the mushroom caps on a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet.
6. Pipe cones straight up about 1 " high for the mushroom stems.
7. With a lightly damp finger, tamp down points on rounds.
8. Sprinkle rounds with cocoa powder.
9. Pipe extra tops and stems with remaining meringue as backups.
10. Bake meringues until dry and set, about 2 hours.
11. Open oven door minutes to release any steam.
12. Turn oven off, shut door, and let meringues dry out completely, about 2 hours longer or up to overnight.
13. When ready to assemble, melt chocolate in a medium heatproof bowl in microwave, stirring every seconds, until melted, about seconds.
14. Using an offset spatula, release meringues from parchment.
15. Using a paring knife, make a small hole in the center of flat side of each mushroom cap.
16. Spread some chocolate on flat side, then stick top of stem into hole.
17. Return to parchment and repeat with remaining tops and stems.
18. Let mushrooms stand at room temperature until chocolate is firm.
19. Combine pistachios and milk in a small saucepan.
20. Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook, stirring occasionally, until pistachios are very tender, about minutes.
21. Remove and skim off any loose pistachio skins.
22. Meanwhile, sprinkle gelatin over 2 Tbsp. water in a small bowl and let stand 5 minutes.
23. Transfer pistachio mixture to a food processor and blend until smooth.
24. Return mixture to saucepan and whisk in egg yolks, granulated sugar, and salt.
25. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring, until mixture begins to thicken but do not let boil, 3A5 minutes.
26. Remove from heat and stir in gelatin mixture and almond extract.
27. Transfer to a small bowl and let cool.
28. Whisk egg whites, granulated sugar, and salt in a large heatproof bowl.
29. Set bowl over a pot of simmering water (do not let bowl touch water) and cook, whisking constantly, until mixture becomes fluffy and opaque and you canAt feel any grains of sugar when you rub mixture between two fingers, about 5 minutes.
30. Remove bowl from heat.
31. Melt chocolate in a medium heatproof bowl in microwave, stirring every seconds, until melted, about 1 minute.
32. Let cool slightly.
33. Using an electric mixer on high speed, beat egg white mixture until cool, glossy, and fluffy, about minutes. (A stand mixer fitted with the whisk attachment makes this job much easier!) Add butter one piece at a time, beating until fully incorporated before adding the next, until fluffy and smooth, another 4 minutes.
34. If mixture looks curdled, switch to the paddle attachment andor apply a little bit of heat to underside of bowl with a washcloth dipped in hot water and continue to beat until smooth.
35. Beat in chocolate and vanilla extract until combined.
36. Do Ahead: Frosting can be made 3 days ahead.
37. Transfer to an airtight container and chill.
38. Bring to room temperature and beat to desired consistency.
39. Place a rack in center of oven; preheat to .
40. Grease an 18x13" rimmed baking sheet with oil.
41. Line with parchment paper and grease parchment.
42. Using electric mixer on high speed, beat egg yolks and cup water in a large bowl until doubled in size and thick and fluffy, about 5 minutes.
43. With the motor running, gradually add 1 cup granulated sugar, a little at a time, until mixture is very thick.
44. Beat in vanilla.
45. Sift flour, baking powder, salt, and tsp. cardamom over egg yolk mixture and fold gently to combine (it will be very thick).
46. Using electric mixer on high speed, beat egg whites and cream of tartar in another clean large bowl until foamy and the yellowish tinge has disappeared, about 1 minute.
47. Beat in cup granulated sugar, about 1 Tbsp. at a time, until shiny, medium stiff peaks form.
48. Fold about one-quarter of egg white mixture into egg yolk mixture to loosen.
49. Fold remaining egg white mixture into egg yolk mixture until combined.
50. Transfer batter to prepared pan; smooth top.
51. Bake cake until puffed and set and springs back when gently poked in the center, minutes.
52. Transfer cake to a wire rack and let cool 5 minutes.
53. Set a clean dish towel on a work surface and dust completely with powdered sugar.
54. Cut around edges of cake with a sharp paring knife.
55. Invert cake onto prepared towel; remove parchment.
56. Without squishing the cake and starting on long side, gently roll up cake in towel.
57. Transfer cake to a wire rack and let cool completely.
58. While cake cools, make the brandy syrup.
59. Cook remaining cup granulated sugar and tsp. cardamom with 2 Tbsp. water in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until sugar is dissolved, about 2 minutes.
60. Remove from heat and stir in brandy.
61. Let cool.
62. Melt chocolate in a medium heatproof bowl in microwave, stirring every seconds, until melted, about 1 minute.
63. Using an offset spatula, spread melted chocolate across a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet.
64. Sift cocoa over and freeze bark until solid, about minutes.
65. Gently unroll cake.
66. With a pastry brush, evenly brush with brandy syrup until all syrup is absorbed.
67. Spread filling evenly over cake and roll it back up without towel.
68. Transfer to a platter.
69. Trim ends.
70. Cut off about 3" from both ends on a diagonal.
71. Place angled side of each small piece against roll to create branches, positioning one on top and the other on the side, using a large dab of frosting to adhere.
72. Cover any exposed cake on sides with more frosting.
73. Break chocolate bark into long shards and press into cake in a decorative pattern.
74. Top and surround cake with meringue mushrooms.
75. Use pistachios to create AmossA on and around log.
76. Dust with powdered sugar.

Ingredients: ' large eggs', '1 (6-oz.) can oil-packed tuna', '2 oil-packed anchovy fillets', ' cup mayonnaise', '2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', '2 Tbsp. capers, drained, divided', ' tsp. kosher salt', '4 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided', '2 Tbsp. salmon roe (optional)'
Instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil over medium-high heat.
2. Gently lower cold eggs into water.
3. Cook minutes, then drain and transfer to a large bowl of ice water.
4. Carefully peel eggs and cut in half lengthwise. (To get clean cuts, use a very sharp knife and wipe the blade between each cut.) Pop out yolks and transfer to a food processor.
5. Arrange whites on a platter.
6. Add tuna, anchovies, mayonnaise, lemon juice, and 2 tsp. capers to food processor and purAe until smooth.
7. Add salt and 2 Tbsp. oil and pulse just until combined.
8. Transfer to a pastry bag or resealable plastic bag and snip the corner.
9. Pipe into egg whites.
10. Cook remaining 4 tsp. capers and 2 Tbsp. oil in a small skillet over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until capers are crispy, 3A4 minutes.
11. Transfer to a paper-towel-lined plate and let cool.
12. Top eggs with crispy capers and salmon roe (if using).
13. Do Ahead: Whites and filling can be made 1 day ahead.
14. Tightly cover egg whites on baking sheet or platter and chill.
15. Store filling in tightly sealed bag and chill.

Ingredients: ' cup red or black quinoa, rinsed', 'Kosher salt', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil, divided, plus more for drizzling', '2 (.5-oz.) cans chickpeas, rinsed, drained', '1 tsp. garlic powder', ' tsp. smoked paprika', '8 oz. crimini mushrooms, trimmed, cut into " pieces', ' cup tahini', ' cup white miso', ' cup oat flour or all-purpose flour', '8 slices sharp cheddar or vegan cheese', 'Mayonnaise, mustard, hamburger buns, shredded iceberg lettuce, and sliced pickles (for serving; optional)'
Instructions:
1. Cook quinoa in a large saucepan of lightly salted boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender, minutes.
2. Drain well and let cool at least minutes.
3. Meanwhile, heat cup oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
4. Add chickpeas (make sure they are well drained) and cook, tossing occasionally, until lightly browned, minutes.
5. Add garlic powder and paprika and toss a few times to coat.
6. Transfer to a large bowl and let cool about 5 minutes.
7. Add mushrooms, tahini, miso, and cup oil to bowl with chickpeas and mash with a potato masher or fork to combine.
8. Add quinoa and flour and mash until mixture holds together when squeezed in your hand.
9. Taste and season with salt if needed.
10. Form into 8 patties and arrange on a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet.
11. Freeze until firm, about 2 hours.
12. At this point, the patties can be baked, or they can stay frozen up to 3 months.
13. Transfer to resealable freezer bags or layer in an airtight container with sheets of parchment between patties.
14. Preheat oven to .
15. Drizzle frozen patties generously with oil on both sides on a rimmed baking sheet.
16. Bake patties until browned and crisp, minutes.
17. Place a slice of cheese on top of each patty and bake until cheese is melted, about 1 minute.
18. Spread mayonnaise and mustard on cut sides of buns (if using).
19. Place burgers on bottom buns.
20. Top with lettuce and pickles (if using) and close up burgers.
21. Do Ahead: Patties can be made 3 months ahead.
22. Transfer to a resealable freezer bag and freeze.

Recipe name: Kimchi Soup With Tofu And Clams
Ingredients: '2 Tbsp. neutral oil (such as canola or grapeseed)', '1 small onion, thinly sliced', '6 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '1 lb. kimchi, juices reserved, thinly sliced', '1 Tbsp. white miso', ' tsp. (or more) kosher salt', '1 lb. littleneck clams (), scrubbed', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '1 lb. silken tofu, drained, cut into 1" pieces', '2 scallions, white and light green parts only, thinly sliced'
Instructions:
1. Heat oil in a 4A6-qt.
2. Dutch oven or large heavy pot over medium until hot.
3. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring often, until onion is softened, 3A5 minutes.
4. Add kimchi along with its juices and cook, stirring often, until kimchi begins to caramelize and stick to bottom of pot, minutes.
5. Add 4 cups water and increase heat to medium-high to bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat as needed to maintain a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until some of the liquid is evaporated and flavors have melded, about minutes.
7. Spoon a few tablespoons of soup into a small bowl.
8. Add miso and salt.
9. Stir until miso loosens to a smooth sauce-like consistency, then stir back into soup (this helps avoid any lumps of undissolved miso).
10. Add clams to soup.
11. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until clams open, 5A7 minutes depending on size (discard any clams that do not open after minutes).
12. Mix in butter, then add tofu.
13. Give soup one very gentle stir just to incorporate tofu (be careful not to break it up too much).
14. Continue to cook soup, undisturbed, just until tofu is warmed through, 1A2 minutes.
15. Taste and season with salt if needed.
16. Divide soup and clams among bowls.
17. Top with scallions.

Recipe name: Roasted Cabbage Steaks With Crispy Chickpeas And Herby Croutons
Ingredients: '2 medium purple or green cabbages (4A5 lb. total; second cabbage optional)', '2 tsp. (or more) kosher salt, divided', '1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper, divided', '2 (.5-oz.) cans chickpeas, drained, rinsed', '1 tsp. ground cumin', ' cup plus 1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided; plus more for drizzling', '1 tsp. ground coriander, divided', ' loaf sourdough bread (about lb.)', '2 garlic cloves, finely grated, divided', '2 tsp. Dijon mustard, divided', '2 Tbsp. finely chopped parsley, plus whole leaves for serving', ' cup vegan or regular mayonnaise', '2 Tbsp. (or more) fresh lemon juice'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Slice 1 cabbage into 1"-thick steaks.
3. Cut the second cabbage in half (if using).
4. Arrange on a large rimmed baking sheet and drizzle with oil.
5. Turn steaks and halves and drizzle second sides with oil; season with 1 tsp. salt and tsp. pepper.
6. Arrange cabbage halves cut side down and roast until golden brown on the bottom and tender, minutes. (Reserve cabbage halves for another use.
7. Let cool, then cover and chill.) Meanwhile, place chickpeas on another rimmed baking sheet and pat dry with a clean towel.
8. Add cumin, cup oil, 1 tsp. coriander, tsp. salt, and tsp. pepper and toss to combine.
9. Roast, stirring once halfway through, until golden and crisp, minutes.
10. Transfer to a medium bowl and set aside; reserve baking sheet (no need to wash).
11. Tear bread into bite-size chunks.
12. Whisk cup oil, 1 grated garlic clove, 1 tsp. mustard, 2 Tbsp. chopped parsley, tsp. salt, tsp. pepper, and remaining tsp. coriander in a large bowl to combine.
13. Add torn bread and toss well to coat.
14. As soon as chickpeas are done roasting, transfer bread to reserved baking sheet and roast until golden and crispy, minutes.
15. Whisk mayonnaise, lemon juice, and remaining grated garlic, 1 Tbsp. oil, 1 tsp. mustard, and tsp. pepper in a small bowl.
16. Taste and season with salt if needed.
17. Smear mayonnaise sauce on plates and top with cabbage steaks.
18. Top cabbage with chickpeas and croutons.
19. Drizzle with more mayonnaise sauce, thinning with lemon juice or water if needed to make it pourable.
20. Top with parsley leaves.

Recipe name: Creamy Squash Risotto With Toasted Pepitas
Ingredients: '2 large butternut squash (about 4 lb. total)', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', '1 Tbsp. white miso', '1 tsp. smoked paprika', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' cup plus 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '1 medium onion, finely chopped', '2 tsp. (or more) kosher salt, divided', '2 cups arborio rice', '1 cup dry white wine or hard or sweet cider', ' cup raw pumpkin seeds (pepitas)', '2 tsp. smoked paprika', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '2 tsp. finely grated orange zest', ' cup coarsely chopped dill or parsley (from about bunch)', 'Orange wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Cut squash in half lengthwise and scoop out seeds.
3. Place squash (cut side down) on a rimmed baking sheet and roast until it gives easily when pressed, minutes.
4. Set aside until cool enough to handle.
5. Scoop flesh from squash and transfer to a food processor.
6. Add oil, miso, paprika, and salt and purAe, scraping down sides as needed, until very smooth.
7. Do Ahead: Squash purAe can be made 4 days ahead.
8. Transfer to an airtight container and chill.
9. Bring 8 cups water to a boil in a medium pot.
10. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a large deep-sided skillet or braising dish over medium.
11. Cook onion, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent but not browned, 5A7 minutes.
12. Season with 2 tsp. salt, add rice, and stir to combine.
13. Continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until rice is translucent around the edges, about 4 minutes.
14. Add wine and cook, stirring constantly, until evaporated, 1A2 minutes.
15. Add cup (about a ladleful) boiling water to rice mixture and cook, stirring constantly, until water is absorbed, about 1A2 minutes.
16. Continue adding water cup at a time and stirring constantly until rice is al dente and all water is absorbed, about minutes.
17. Stir in 2 cups squash purAe and continue to cook, stirring, until heated through, 3A5 minutes.
18. Taste and add more salt if needed.
19. Meanwhile, cook pumpkin seeds, paprika, red pepper flakes, and remaining cup oil and tsp. salt in a large skillet over medium heat, stirring often, until seeds are puffed and starting to brown and oil is fragrant, 2A3 minutes.
20. Remove from heat and stir in orange zest (zest will sizzle).
21. Transfer risotto to a platter.
22. Pour pepitas and oil over, then top with dill.
23. Serve with orange wedges alongside for squeezing over.

Ingredients: '4A6 large eggs', '5 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar', '1 Tbsp. honey', '1 Tbsp. tamari or soy sauce', ' tsp. kosher salt', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling', '1 bunch curly kale, stems removed, torn into bite-size pieces', '1 lb. brussels sprouts, trimmed, thinly sliced', '1 cup store-bought or homemade hummus', '1 cup Seedy Power Sprinkle', 'Flaky sea salt'
Instructions:
1. Bring a medium pot of water to a boil over high heat.
2. Gently lower eggs into water and cook 7 minutes.
3. Transfer to a large bowl of ice water and let sit until cool enough to handle.
4. Peel and slice in half lengthwise.
5. Meanwhile, whisk vinegar, honey, tamari, kosher salt, and cup oil in a large bowl until smooth.
6. Add kale and brussels sprouts.
7. Toss and massage with your hands until kale is darker in color and vegetables are coated in dressing.
8. Swoosh about cup hummus onto side of each bowl.
9. Top with salad and egg halves; season eggs with sea salt.
10. Drizzle oil over and top with seedy sprinkle.
11. Do Ahead: Unpeeled eggs can be cooked 2 days ahead.
12. Transfer to an airtight container and chill.
13. Kale and brussels sprouts salad can be made 8 hours ahead.
14. Cover and chill.

Ingredients: '3 eggs', 'Zest of 1 lime', ' ounces evaporated milk', ' ounces sweetened condensed milk', '1 cups white rum', ' tsp. grated nutmeg, plus more for garnish', '2 tsps. Angostura bitters'
Instructions:
1. Ready a large bowl or wide-mouth pitcher and fine mesh sieve.
2. In an electric mixer fitted with the whisk attachment, beat the eggs and lime zest on medium-high speed until very frothy, about 3 minutes.
3. Reduce mixer speed to low, add the evaporated and condensed milk, and mix for 2 minutes.
4. Slowly stream in the rum, then mix for another 2 minutes.
5. Add the nutmeg and angostura bitters and stir for 1 minute.
6. Turn off the mixer, and strain the ponche a crema through the sieve into the pitcher or bowl.
7. Serve immediately on the rocks with a dusting of nutmeg.

Ingredients: '1 large egg white', '2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil', '2 Tbsp. honey', '1 Tbsp. Aleppo-style pepper (optional)', '2 tsp. kosher salt', '1 cup sunflower seeds', ' cup chia seeds', ' cup flaxseeds', ' cup raw sesame seeds'
Instructions:
1. Whisk egg white, oil, honey, pepper (if using), salt, and 1 Tbsp. water in a medium bowl to combine.
2. Add sunflower seeds, chia seeds, flaxseeds, and sesame seeds and stir to coat.
3. Let sit until liquid is absorbed, about minutes.
4. Preheat oven to .
5. Spread seed mixture in an even layer on a rimmed baking sheet.
6. Bake, stirring very gently after minutes to encourage the mixture to form small clusters, until golden brown and crisp, minutes.
7. Let cool.
8. Do Ahead: Sprinkle can be made 2 weeks ahead.
9. Store in an airtight container at room temperature.

Ingredients: '1 (2.2-oz.) can oil-packed anchovy fillets', '1 head of garlic, cloves separated, thinly sliced', ' cup extra-virgin olive oil', ' tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '1 lb. penne pasta', 'Kosher salt', '3 Tbsp. cold unsalted butter', '3 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice', '1 head of radicchio, trimmed, quartered, leaves separated', '1 (5-oz.) package baby arugula', '1 cup basil leaves, torn if large', 'Freshly ground black pepper'

8. Add pasta and sauce and toss to coat, adding more pasta cooking liquid as needed.
9. Add arugula and basil but donAt toss; season with salt and black pepper.
10. Divide pasta among plates or bowls, very gently tossing so arugula gets coated in sauce but doesnAt wilt too much.

Ingredients: '2 1A4 ounces gin or vodka', '3A4 ounce dry vermouth', 'Olives'

Recipe name: Chard-Wrapped Fish With Lemon And Olive
Ingredients: '2 lemons', 'A cup pitted mixed olives, plus whole pitted olives', '4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil', '3 cups cilantro leaves, finely chopped (about cup)', '4 garlic cloves, finely minced (4 teaspoons)', '1 teaspoon dried red pepper flakes', '5 very large Swiss chard leaves (or more as needed), tough ends trimmed', ' pounds skinless, center-cut white fish fillets, such as halibut or cod', '1 teaspoon kosher salt, plus more for seasoning', ' teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, plus more for seasoning', '1 small onion, diced', ' tablespoons all-purpose flour', ' cups chicken stock', ' cup yellow cherry tomatoes, halved', 'Rice, coucous, or other cooked grain'
Instructions:
1. Finely zest and juice both lemons.
2. Reserve the juice and half the zest.
3. In a small bowl, combine the remaining zest with the olives, 1 tablespoon of the olive oil, 2 tablespoons of the chopped cilantro leaves, 1 teaspoon garlic, and the red pepper flakes until incorporated.
4. Bring a small pot of water to a boil over medium-high heat.
5. Use a knife to separate the leaves from the chard stems so you have 2 leaves from each stem.
6. Thinly slice the stems crosswise.
7. Dip the chard leaves in the boiling water for seconds to soften; drain, cool slightly, and pat dry.
8. Cut the fish into 8 equal-sized pieces, season with salt and pepper, and spread 2 teaspoons of the olive mixture on each piece of fish.
9. Place 1 piece of fish on a piece of chard and roll it up; use the extra 2 leaves to patch any parts that need extra wrapping.
10. Heat the remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
11. Add the onion and cook, stirring, until tender and lightly golden, 8 to 9 minutes.
12. Add the chard stems with the remaining 3 teaspoons garlic and cook, stirring, until softened, 3 to 4 minutes.
13. Add the flour and cook, stirring, until absorbed, 1 minute.
14. Add the reserved lemon zest and juice with the stock, salt, and pepper and cook, stirring, until the liquid thickens and bubbles, 2 to 3 minutes.
15. Stir in most of the remaining cilantro.
16. Nestle the fish in the skillet, scatter the tomatoes and whole olives over the fish, reduce the heat to medium-low, cover, and cook until the fish is opaque and the chard can be cut easily, minutes.
17. Serve over the grain of your choice with sauce spooned over the top; garnish with cilantro.

Recipe name: Chilaquiles With Bacon Eggs And Cheese
Ingredients: ' lb. thick-cut bacon, cut into small pieces', '6 garlic cloves, crushed, peeled', '1 serrano chile, thinly sliced', '1 (.5-oz.) can pinto beans, drained, rinsed', '2 cups jarred or homemade salsa verde, plus more for serving', '8 cups thick tortilla chips, plus more', 'Kosher salt', '4 oz. Monterey Jack or pepper Jack, shredded (about 1 cup)', ' oz. Cotija or Parmesan, finely grated (about cup)', '8 large eggs', ' cup cilantro leaves with tender stems', 'Hot sauce and lime wedges (for serving)'
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to .
2. Cook bacon in a large (") cast-iron or other ovenproof skillet over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until fat is rendered and bacon is beginning to crisp, 5A7 minutes.
3. Add garlic and chile and continue to cook, stirring, until bacon is crisp and garlic is golden brown, 2A3 minutes more.
4. Add beans, stir to combine, then stir in 2 cups salsa verde.
5. Taste by dipping a tortilla chip into mixture; season with salt if needed.
6. Transfer bean mixture to a large bowl (trying to add the chips to the skillet will make a mess, trust us).
7. Gently stir in 8 cups chips with a rubber spatula until thoroughly coated without breaking chips.
8. Return to hot skillet.
9. Stick a handful of fresh chips vertically into mixture here and thereAthey will get extra crunchy as the chilaquiles bake.
10. Sprinkle Monterey Jack and Cotija over.
11. Make 8 evenly spaced indentations in filling and carefully crack an egg into each.
12. Bake until egg whites are opaque and cheese is bubbly, minutes.
13. Top chilaquiles with cilantro.
14. Serve with hot sauce, lime wedges, and more salsa verde alongside.

Ingredients: '2 - to 3-pound boneless pork butt roast, cut into 3 or 4 large pieces', '2 tablespoons chile-lime seasoning (like TajAn)', ' cup chicken or beef broth', ' cup % agave tequila', '4 tablespoons fresh lime juice, divided', '2 tablespoons avocado oil', 'Fine Himalayan pink salt', ' cup chopped fresh cilantro', '1 teaspoon grated lime zest', '2 small heads butter lettuce', 'Toppings, such as queso fresco, avocado, sour cream, andor hot sauce'
Instructions:
1. Rub the pork pieces evenly with the chile-lime seasoning.
2. Place the pork in the Instant Pot.
3. Add the broth, tequila, and 2 tablespoons of the lime juice.
4. Secure the lid on the pot and close the pressure-release valve.
5. Set the pot to HIGH pressure for minutes.
6. At the end of the cooking time, use a natural release to depressurize.
7. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the pork from the pot to a bowl.
8. Use two forks to shred it into large bite-size pieces.
9. Pour the cooking juices out of the pot into a separate bowl.
10. Wipe out the pot.
11. Select SAUTA and when the pot is hot, add 1 tablespoon of the avocado oil.
12. Add about half of the pork to the hot oil and cook, stirring occasionally, until the pork is browned and slightly crisp in some places, about minutes.
13. Remove the pork from the pot.
14. Repeat with the remaining pork and 1 tablespoon avocado oil.
15. Return all the pork to the pot and season to taste with salt.
16. Select CANCEL.
17. Stir the cilantro, lime zest, and remaining 2 tablespoons lime juice into the pork.
18. Skim the fat from the reserved cooking juices and add the cooking juices as desired to moisten the carnitas.
19. Serve the carnitas with butter lettuce leaves and desired toppings.

Ingredients: '1 large egg', '2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese (8 ounces)', ' cup grated Parmesan cheese (2 ounces)', ' cup crushed pork rinds or almond meal', '2 tablespoons chopped fresh basil, plus cup thinly sliced fresh basil for garnish', '2 cloves garlic, minced', '1 pounds ground chicken', '4 tablespoons olive oil, divided', '1 tablespoon tomato paste', '1 (.5-ounce) can whole peeled plum (Italian) tomatoes, undrained', '1 teaspoon dried basil, crushed', '1 teaspoon garlic powder'
Instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine the egg, 1 cup of the mozzarella, the Parmesan, pork rinds, chopped basil, and garlic.
2. Add the chicken and mix gently using your hands to combine.
3. Form into meatballs about 1 inches in diameter.
4. Select SAUTA on the Instant Pot.
5. When the pot is hot, add 2 tablespoons of the olive oil.
6. Add about half of the meatballs to the hot oil and cook until browned on all sides, turning the meatballs as needed, 6 to 8 minutes.
7. Transfer the meatballs to a plate.
8. Repeat with the remaining meatballs and 2 tablespoons olive oil.
9. Add the tomato paste to the pot.
10. Cook and stir for 1 minute.
11. Add the canned tomatoes and their juice, breaking them apart as you do so.
12. Stir in the dried basil and garlic powder.
13. Return the meatballs to the pot.
14. Select CANCEL.
15. Secure the lid on the pot and close the pressure-release valve.
16. Set the pot to HIGH pressure for minutes.
17. At the end of the cooking time, use a natural release to depressurize.
18. Top the meatballs with the remaining 1 cup mozzarella and let stand until melted before serving with the basil leaves.

Ingredients: '2 heads of garlic (about cloves)', '3 fresh bay leaves', ' cup ( mL) canola oil', '1 tablespoon fish sauce', ' teaspoon ground white pepper', '2 teaspoons ground Sichuan peppercorns', '2 teaspoons chile flakes', ' teaspoon ground white pepper', ' teaspoon ground coriander', ' teaspoon ground kombu', ' teaspoon ground cumin', ' teaspoon five-spice powder', ' teaspoon light brown sugar', ' teaspoon sea salt, plus more as needed', 'Canola oil, for frying', '1 (-ounce425 g) can chickpeas, drained and peeled', '1 tablespoons buttermilk', '1 tablespoon plus cup ( g) all-purpose flour', '2 teaspoons garlic powder', ' teaspoon baking soda', ' teaspoon ground white pepper', ' teaspoon fine sea salt', 'A good handful fresh Thai basil leaves', '1 garlic clove, grated', '2 teaspoons Garlic Confit Sauce (see below)'
Instructions:
1. Gently smash the garlic cloves so they are cracked but not completely broken.
2. Peel off the skins.
3. In a small saucepan, combine the garlic cloves, bay leaves, and oil.
4. Simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the garlic is evenly golden brown on all sides, about minutes.
5. Remove the bay leaves and transfer the rest of the mixture to a blender (or use an immersion blender).
6. Add the fish sauce and pepper.
7. Blend until the mixture is smooth.
8. DO AHEAD: Keep in an airtight jar in the fridge for up to 2 weeks, or 2 months in the freezer.
9. Thaw andor stir before use.
10. Even though some of the spices are already ground, we will process them a second time to get a finer powder that will stick well to the chickpeas.
11. In a spice grinder, grind the spice mix ingredients to a fine powder.
12. Set aside.
13. In a deep frying pan or Dutch oven, pour enough oil to reach a depth of 2 inches (5 cm).
14. Heat over medium-high until the oil bubbles up immediately around an inserted wooden chopstick.
15. Meanwhile in a large bowl, toss the chickpeas with the buttermilk and the 1 tablespoon of flour until they are evenly coated in this light batter.
16. In a separate large bowl, mix the remaining cup ( g) flour, the garlic powder, baking soda, white pepper, and salt.
17. Add the coated chickpeas to the flour mixture and toss until you have a slightly wet but crumbly mixture.
18. It may look like the chickpeas are kind of sticking together, but itAs okay.
19. In a large bowl, mix the garlic with 2 teaspoons of the Garlic Confit Sauce (save the rest for another day).
20. Set aside.
21. Now, in several small batches as needed, add the breaded chickpeas to the hot oil and start breaking them apart with a chopstick.
22. ItAs okay if they are in small clumps of 2 to 4 chickpeas.
23. Fry for a couple of minutes, until the chickpeas are golden brown all around.
24. Transfer with a slotted spoon to the bowl with the garlic sauce and repeat to fry the rest of the chickpeas.
25. Add the basil leaves to the frying oil and immediately cover the pot with a lid, leaving a small slit for the steam to escape.
26. When the popping sound has subsided, remove the lid and fry the basil until crispy.
27. Transfer with a slotted spoon to the bowl with the chickpeas.
28. Toss the chickpeas to coat them evenly with the garlic mixture, then add the spice mixture and toss until evenly coated.
29. Season with more salt if needed.
30. Serve immediately.

Instructions: 1. Cook dosa batter and fill with spiced potato mixture.

Ingredients: Pav Bread, Potatoes, Tomatoes, Butter, Spices
Instructions: 1. Cook mashed vegetables with butter and spices. Serve with pav bread.

Ingredients: Gram Flour, Yogurt, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves
Instructions: 1. Steam gram flour batter and season with mustard seeds.

Ingredients: Semolina, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves, Green Chilies, Carrots, Peas
Instructions: 1. Cook semolina with mustard seeds, green chilies, and vegetables.

Ingredients: Puffed Rice, Tamarind Chutney, Onions, Peanuts, Sev
Instructions: 1. Mix puffed rice with tamarind chutney, onions, and sev.

Ingredients: Chicken, Butter, Tomato, Cream, Cashews, Spices
Instructions:
1. Marinate chicken in yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, and spices.
2. Grill or cook the chicken in a pan.
3. Prepare a sauce with tomato puree, butter, cream, and spices.
4. Add the cooked chicken to the sauce and let it simmer.
5. Serve with naan or rice.

Ingredients: Phyllo Dough, Honey, Nuts, Butter, Sugar
Instructions: 1. Layer phyllo dough with honey and nuts. Bake until golden.

Ingredients: Rice, Seafood, Saffron, Tomato, Paprika, Garlic
Instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a pan and saute onions, garlic, and tomato.
2. Add seafood and cook for a few minutes.
3. Stir in rice and saffron, adding broth gradually.
4. Simmer until rice absorbs liquid.
5. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve warm.

Ingredients: Flour, Yeast, Butter, Salt, Baking Soda
Instructions: 1. Mix flour, yeast, butter, and salt. Bake until golden.

Ingredients: Fish, Lime Juice, Red Onion, Chili, Cilantro
Instructions:
1. Cut fresh fish into bite-sized pieces.
2. Marinate in lime juice with red onions, salt, and chili.
3. Let sit for minutes before serving with cilantro garnish.

Ingredients: Rice, Vegetables, Beef, Egg, Gochujang, Soy Sauce
Instructions:
1. Cook rice and place in a bowl.
2. Arrange stir-fried vegetables, beef, and a fried egg on top.
3. Serve with gochujang sauce and mix before eating.

Ingredients: Flour, Butter, Sugar, Yeast, Milk, Salt
Instructions:
1. Mix flour, butter, yeast, sugar, and milk to form dough.
2. Roll and fold several times, then refrigerate.
3. Cut into triangles and roll into croissants.
4. Bake at for minutes until golden.

Ingredients: Noodles, Pork, Soy Sauce, Miso, Egg, Green Onion
Instructions:
1. Cook ramen noodles in boiling water.
2. In a separate pot, simmer pork, miso, soy sauce, and broth.
3. Pour broth over cooked noodles.
4. Top with sliced pork, boiled egg, and green onions.

Ingredients: Rice Paper, Shrimp, Vermicelli, Herbs, Peanut Sauce
Instructions:
1. Soak rice paper in warm water until soft.
2. Fill with shrimp, vermicelli noodles, and fresh herbs.
3. Roll tightly and serve with peanut dipping sauce.
